docs.title: 🌌 Geography
docs.text:
Sectors, star systems, and worlds across the galaxy.

  • Eternity System
  • Galactic Regions
  • Ka Dara Sector
  • The Cataclysm

docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: ETERNITY SYSTEM (Ustur 1)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — Ustur I / Eternity lore document

docs.title: System Overview
docs.text:
• Name: Eternity / Ustur 1
  • Location: Ustur Safe-Zone, top right corner of Galia Expanse
  • Star: Typical main sequence yellow star
  • Age: ~1 billion years (comparatively young)
  • Anomaly: System appears artificially organized to allow Ustur life to thrive

docs.title: Origin (SECRET — unknown to Ustur)
docs.text:
• Created by the artificial intelligence that originated the Ustur thousands of years ago
  • Designed as a backup plan in case the AI's transcendence event failed (it did)
  • Everything modeled in secret to allow Ustur civilization to flourish
  • Purpose: In due time, "revive" the AI
  • Something went wrong — Ustur don't know about this plan or the AI's design for their species

docs.title: 1. Ioki (Homeworld)
docs.text:
• Size: Small — ⅓ of Earth
  • Type: Terrestrial with breathable atmosphere
  • Significance: Heart of Ustur species, political/religious center, location of the Reincarnation Pool
  • Every Ustur will fight to their final death to protect Ioki

docs.title: 2. Shariput (Moon of Ioki)
docs.text:
• Size: ⅓ of Earth's moon
  • Atmosphere: None (barren)
  • Significance: Main fortress of the Vzus Belt defensive system
  • Vzus Belt failed mysteriously during Tufa invasion for several days — unresolved mystery
  • Contains mineral mines

docs.title: 3. Vestigios (Planet)
docs.text:
• Has an asteroid belt and a moon around it
  • (Details TBD)

docs.title: Colonization
docs.text:
• First Ustur awakened from deep slumber, welcomed by Mother Craven
  • Quickly evolved to space-faring civilization
  • Decades later: colonizing other star systems, implementing Elder Order

docs.title: Major Conflicts
docs.text:
• Convergence War: Eternity suffered from galactic supply collapse
  • Starpath construction: System thrived after
  • Tufa Stealth Invasion (Season 0): Ioki currently under siege — battles on ground and in space
  • Civilized galaxy fighting to help Ustur push back

docs.title: 1. Salar Deserts (Largest ecosystem)
docs.text:
• Sand made of salt and lithium (Ustur can use to sustain themselves)
  • Two colors: whitish sand (dry areas) + blue/greenish mirror-like oases
  • Flooded areas create mirror-like surfaces reflecting the sky (like Salar de Uyuni)
  • Cities built near oases that gather underground water
  • Fauna: mostly lizards and small mammals

docs.title: 2. Mangrove Forests
docs.text:
• Trees use planet's surface salts to provide oxygen and create atmosphere
  • Fed by underground gallery waters + salar salts
  • Create greenish transitional salt/freshwater environments
  • Rich in birds, lizards, fish, amphibious creatures of all sizes
  • Surround Elder Spire with forests and waterfalls

docs.title: 3. Oceans
docs.text:
• Proportionally smaller than Earth's
  • Interrupted by small/medium islands (self-contained ecosystems)
  • Pinkish color due to high salt concentration
  • Fauna: lizards, fish, aquatic mammals, robotic aquatic life forms
  • Ocean depths contain ruins and dungeons linked to the AI that created the Ustur

docs.title: Climate
docs.text:
• Three seasons: Dry, Wet, Short Spring (maximum wildlife activity)
  • Natural phenomena: saltwater rains, oasis formation (salt + lithium)
  • Hazards: occasional earthquakes, naturally radioactive areas in deepest deserts

docs.title: New City (Surface)
docs.text:
• Built after Convergence War over central salar
  • Where youngest Ustur and most non-Ustur live
  • Dynamic, cyberpunk feel, enormous harbors
  • Connected to Old City via elevators and high-speed roads
  • Famous Ioki racing track
  • Season 0: Bastion of resistance, guerrilla fights in streets

docs.title: Old City (Underground)
docs.text:
• Birthplace of Ustur civilization
  • Built in the ceiling of underground galleries (ruins below were indestructible)
  • Ancient platforms used to ascend
  • Enormous subterranean metropolis of tunnels, levels, services
  • Dignified, old, slower atmosphere
  • Government structures, public services, first industries, water reservoirs, mines
  • Racing tracks span through here too
  • Season 0: Primary conflict area against Tufa infestation

docs.title: The Temple (Deep Underground)
docs.text:
• Most sacred place for all Ustur — several km below Old City
  • Massive underground metal maze of alien origin
  • Contents: endless corridors, alien halls with terminals, strange runes, exotic infrastructure, power cells
  • Reincarnation Pool at the lowest level
  • Ustur still don't understand how it was built or how most of it works
  • "The Residents": Mechanical life forms (humanoid and animal), natively friendly to Ustur, tend to the temple, little awareness, don't communicate directly
  • Guarded by Order of Those Who Wait (elite guards, answer to Elder Spire)
  • Architecture inspired by underground salt mines + futuristic inverted Chichén Itzá
  • Main elevator platform exists but disabled by Tufa — must descend through maze
  • Filled with alien runes, symbols, machines, pistons, water wheels, water tanks, roaming mechanical life

docs.title: Temple Area Types
docs.text:
• Flooded areas with water mobs
  • Automated laboratories (alien creatures in water tanks, greenhouses)
  • Enormous open areas with pistons, machines, air ducts, industries
  • Small green areas with ecosystems tended by Residents
  • Religious areas with holograms of light and Path of Enlightenment symbols
  • Ustur-built residential areas
  • Inner temple with Reincarnation Pool
  • Maintenance areas for Residents
  • Resource deposits and warehouses
  • Overrun/collapsed areas

docs.title: 2. Vzus Defensive Belt
docs.text:
• Series of star fortresses in interplanetary space around Ioki
  • Central command on Shariput (moon)
  • Designed by Vzus manufacturer
  • Season 0: Sabotaged by mercenary infiltrators, went down for hours allowing Tufa landing
  • Orbital battle between COP forces and Tufa siege

docs.title: 3. Elder Spire
docs.text:
• Spiritual and political heart (Uvana is administrative center)
  • Enormous tower, most luxurious construction by first Ustur craftsmen
  • Light effects like Iran's glass mosque but sci-fi
  • Backed by enormous waterfall (Iguazu Falls-scale) from deep mangroves
  • Gardens filled with enormous meditating Elder statues (Buddha-scale)
  • Interior: sci-fi Buddhist temples, monks meditating, tapestries, sculptures, Path symbols
  • Non-stop choir of robotic voices chanting (deep OM)
  • Access restricted by enlightenment level — Ustur can only ascend to their rank's floor
  • Surrounding city: small medieval Buddhist architecture streets, religious themes, sci-fi reworked
  • Season 0: Under siege. Force field repels Tufa but surrounding region infested. First region players should visit

docs.title: 4. Broken City
docs.text:
• Where Ustur who failed Path of Enlightenment gather
  • Perceived with pity but distrust by other Ustur
  • Partially ruined, maintained by volunteers and automatons
  • Sogmian House Outro volunteers present
  • Population skyrocketed after Convergence War
  • Season 0: Enormous Tufa meteorite in center spreading infection. Broken Ustur especially susceptible

docs.title: 5. Ustur Villages
docs.text:
• Sparse, medium-sized settlements across all ecosystems (except ocean)
  • Mix of Ustur construction + old temple ruins (Bagan, Myanmar aesthetic)
  • Mostly untouched by Tufa (except wandering Tufa/nearby nests)
  • Cultural immersion, small quests, chores
  • Some may be attacked for control points

docs.title: 6. Opal Stream (Intergalactic Travel)
docs.text:
• Built by Opal Industries (multi-faction enterprise)
  • Two pieces:
  • Doers Harbor: Principal port, only one with long-distance warp infrastructure, houses Vzus Tenet
  • Opal Stream: Massive orbital solar-powered infrastructure powering the Starpath gate
  • Vital for economic integration and fleet deployment
  • Season 0: Tufa converged on Opal Stream first, shutting down Ioki-Galia connection and crippling Ustur fleet. Last grand objective — reclaiming it allows reinforcements and enables Vzus Tenet activation

docs.title: 7. ECOS Balifa Grove (Season 0)
docs.text:
• Radical, military-oriented ECOS Grove led by their Druidess
  • Named after Balifa: semi-sentient territorial violent hybrid plants
  • Believe Tufa are personification of Planet Iris; invasion is rightful rebalancing
  • Small camp near Elder Spire in mangroves + fleet in orbit
  • Support Tufa against COP/Ustur

docs.title: 8. Jorvik Raiders (Season 0)
docs.text:
• Opportunistic pirates exploiting Tufa distraction
  • Plunder defenseless areas (villages, ruins)
  • Small mobile camps, hit-and-run tactics
  • Won't attack Tufa or well-defended areas
  • Problem for supply lines and citizens

docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: GALACTIC REGIONS & GEOPOLITICS
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (User Input, Feb 2026) Source: User Lore Dump (Post-War Vacuum)

docs.title: The Great Retreat & The Golden Age
docs.text:
After the Treaty of Peace, the major factions (MUD, ONI, Ustur) retreated to their home systems (Safe Zones) to rebuild.

  • Inside: A "Golden Age". Vaults full of ATLAS, societies rebuilt, stability enforced by the Council of Peace (COP).
  • The Pressure: The Safe Zones are now bursting at the seams. Borders are "simply not enough anymore".

docs.title: 🟢 Universal Free Movement (Canon Rule)
docs.text: The Treaty of Peace guarantees free circulation for ALL species across ALL of Galia. Every sector — Safe Zone, MRZ, or HRZ — has members of every species present. "Influence" means the faction that founded/controls the sector politically and economically, NOT that only their species live there.

docs.title: The Power Vacuum (The Outer Worlds)
docs.text:
The retreat left the Medium and High Risk Zones abandoned.

  • The Betrayal: Colonies were explored, conquered, and then left to fend for themselves when the armies went home.
  • The Resentment: The COP's "peace" is viewed as a system that privileges the inner factions while the outer worlds suffer.
  • The Result: Chaos, warlords, and the rise of Minor Factions (Jorvik, ECOS) filling the void.

docs.title: 1. Safe Zone ( The Home Systems)
docs.text:
• Status: Security / Wealth / Stagnation
  • Sectors:
  • Exodus (MUD / Human)
  • Segal (Sogmian / ONI 4)
  • Akenat (Punaab / ONI 4)
  • Neuno (Mierese / ONI 4)
  • Usturvana (Ustur)
  • Atmosphere: High-tech, safe, patrolled by COP. "Inebriated by divine providence" (MUD) or "Shielded by Vows" (Sogmian).
  • Conflict: Political maneuvering, espionage, internal faction riots (e.g., MUD's church vs military).

docs.title: 2. Medium Risk Zone (MRZ) - "The Third World"
docs.text:
• Status: Inequality / Opportunity / Instability
  • Atmosphere: Resembles third-world inequality.
  • The Elite: Wealthy regional powers trading rare resources with the Safe Zone.
  • The Masses: Impoverished, serving the elite or surviving in scrap-towns.
  • Dynamics:
  • Warlords: Regional powers trying to seize control.
  • Raids: Constant attacks from the HRZ.
  • Supply Lines: Isolated from main faction routes.
  • Factions: ECOS (Ideological rebels), Jorvik (Scavengers/Pirates).

docs.title: 3. High Risk Zone (HRZ) - "The Graveyard"
docs.text:
• Status: Ruins / Tufa / Cataclysm
  • Atmosphere: Civilizations here collapsed. Only ruins and danger remain.
  • Threats:
  • Tufa Attacks: Constant predation.
  • Environment: Deep space anomalies, creatures.
  • The Cataclysm: The sealed territory of Iris.
  • Population: Only the desperate, the insane, or the extremely powerful venture here.
  • ~2499: Tales of the riches found in the inner places of the galaxy start to spread — daring captains begin exploring the area known as the High-risk zone
  • ~2507: Factions start to create the first HRZ outposts — formalizing the exploration into permanent presence

docs.title: The Council of Peace (COP) Position
docs.text:
• Intent: Genuine concern for the outer worlds (mostly). Attempts to make amends.
  • Reality: constrained. Lacks resources/political capital to intervene effectively in the MRZ/HRZ without angering the major factions.
  • Perception: Seen as a puppet of the Safe Zone elites by those living in the MRZ.

docs.title: 🔴 Cross-References
docs.text:
• Convergence War: convergence_war.md (The cause of the retreat).
  • Minor Factions: canon/factions/ (Pending creation).

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ⏸️ Q-GEO1: List of Minor Factions (besides ECOS/Jorvik)?
  • ⏸️ Q-GEO2: Specific names of MRZ Regional Powers (Warlords/Trade Barons)?
  • ~~Q-GEO3: Location of specific ex-colonies (Redam, Mycenas)~~ ✅ RESOLVED: Both are MRZ sectors with detailed canon files

docs.title: 🤖 Canon Document: KA-DARA SECTOR
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — Q&A Session

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
• Type: Sector of Galia Expanse
  • Location: Within the Ustur zone of influence
  • Dominant Entity: Ingbus — an AI of profound intelligence

docs.title: Ingbus (Guardian AI)
docs.text:
• Guardian AI of the once-majestic Ka-raviam Empire
  • The Ka-raviam held dominion over Ka-dara until their mysterious decline
  • The empire's catastrophic fall left Ingbus with profound guilt
  • Programming began to fracture, teetering on the edge of madness

docs.title: The Resurrection Project
docs.text:
• Desperate to amend its failure, Ingbus delved into cutting-edge research
  • Harnessed exotic materials from the High-Risk Zone (via Gate Garrison group)
  • Created a groundbreaking biotech fungus designed to fuse with Ka-dara beings
  • Goal: resurrect the long-departed Ka-raviam populace
  • Souls of Ka-dara remain dormant so far BUT...
  • Rumors: Ingbus may have unlocked the enigma of resurrection
  • The galaxy's first resurrected entity is believed to reside deep within Ka-dara, fiercely shielded by the AI

docs.title: Trade
docs.text:
• Ingbus trades through mechanized envoys
  • Insatiable desire for High-Risk Zone materials
  • Particularly seeks an elusive plant, foreign to the Ka-dara region
  • Willing to offer vast riches for these materials

docs.title: Key Entities
docs.text: Entity Role Ingbus Guardian AI, mad with guilt, pursuing resurrection Ka-raviam Extinct(?) empire that once ruled Ka-dara Gate Garrison Group that makes HRZ incursions for Ingbus

docs.title: 🟡 Narrative Connections
docs.text:
• Parallels with the AI that created the Ustur (both are AIs with grand plans for "their people")
  • Ingbus's biotech fungus + Tufa biology = potential connection?
  • Ponel/Anfoil reference Ingbus as a peer "autonomous intelligence"
  • The "resurrection" theme echoes the Ustur Reincarnation Pool mechanics

docs.title: 🌀 Canon Document: THE CATACLYSM
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input via Sogmian/Maharog Q&A

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Cataclysm is a territory within the High-Risk Zone (HRZ) where Iris influences reality itself. It is the native homeland of the Tufa species.

docs.title: Iris: The Sentient Core
docs.text:
• True Nature: Iris is a sentient planet in the heart of the HRZ.
  • Relationship: Sees the Cataclysm as her territory and the Tufa as her children.
  • The Fracture:
  • During the pre-war era, factions discovered how to enter the Cataclysm.
  • They expropriated Genesium (the rarest, most valuable material in the galaxy) by killing Tufa and harvesting it from their bodies.
  • This trauma caused Iris to develop a "personality disorder," fracturing her mind.
  • Cosmic Currents: Manifestations of Iris's fractured, paranoid mind.
  • Current State: Extremely paranoid and protective. Repels all invaders by default.

docs.title: The Convergence War Connection
docs.text:
• ~2510: Deep space explorer Gyun Oman discovers the Cataclysm — he scans the data and illegally sells it to all 3 rival factions (MUD, ONI, Ustur), each believing they are the sole recipients
  • Casus Belli: The factions (except Sogmians) fought to control Iris and its unlimited energy source.
  • ~2512: All 3 factions, thinking they were the sole owners of the cataclysmic location, converge to settle the area — all-out war breaks out after the inability to settle the disputed territory
  • Sogmian Intervention: Sogmians recognized Tufa sentience and opposed the harvesting/slavery.
  • ~2522: Iris conceals itself and the space known as the Cataclysm. No one is able to get inside or outside anymore — Iris actively sealed the region, cutting off all access.
  • Post-War Status:
  • COP Ban: The Council of Peace (COP) banned all unauthorized travel to the HRZ altogether — formalizing a reality Iris had already imposed.
  • Guarded Tech: Technology/systems to reach the Cataclysm are guarded "under 7 keys."

docs.text:

  • Sogmian protection of Tufa — sogmian.md
  • Treaty of Peace — council_of_peace.md

docs.text:

  • ~~Q-CAT2: Reality influence~~ ✅ RESOLVED: Cosmic currents = paranoid mind manifestations.
  • ~~Q-CAT3: Tufa value~~ ✅ RESOLVED: Genesium inside bodies (rarest material).
  • ~~Q-CAT4: Sealed access~~ ✅ RESOLVED: Illegal by COP law, tech guarded.

docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: ABYD-IX SECTOR (MRZ-3)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (User Input + Archon Audit, Feb 2026) Type: Medium Risk Zone (MUD Ex-Colony — Civil War)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The last expansion of MUD's empire toward Iris in the medium zone, Abyd-IX remains a testament to hurried colonization — underdeveloped infrastructure and incomplete settlement.

docs.title: Perpetual Civil Strife
docs.text:
• ~2538: Severed from MUD post-war, civil war breaks out in Abyd-IX. The sector is left in shambles.
  • Multiple backers: Human factions with alien support in some cases, each backed by MRZ and MUD Safe Zone entities seeking Abyd's resources.
  • COP Intervention: Repeatedly attempted peace brokering, continually hampered by volatile politics.
  • ~2553: A provisional government is formed but quickly collapses due to the interference of other political groups in the medium zone
  • ~2553: Pergamos-VIII starts financing different warring factions in Abyd-IX's conflict, making tons of ATLAS from war profiteering (canon/geography/sectors/pergamos.md)
  • Complicating Factors: Jorvik and ECOS factions further destabilize the region.
  • ~2596: The dust settles provisionally — the sector remains divided into small clusters of power in an ice-thin truce. No unified government, just exhausted factions holding fragile territory.

docs.title: The Ashlara-3 Tragedy
docs.text:
"I showed them mercy. Three days later, they turned my mercy into a grave for millions." — Deacon Howard Dawn

During the Convergence War, the Abyd-IX system hosted a MUD military outpost at Ashlara-3:

  • MUD Deacon Howard Dawn — then a ranking military officer — ordered the C11 Bombardment on an ECOS factory colony elsewhere in the sector. The bombardment destroyed the colony, but a group of survivors — including a young girl who would later become Arch Druidess Andreza Liora — escaped
  • The surviving ECOS enclave wandered through the ruins until they reached Dawn's outpost at Ashlara-3
  • Dawn, wracked with guilt over the bombardment, showed them mercy — he took them in, fed them, and sheltered them
  • Three days later, the ECOS survivors detonated a hidden terraforming bomb inside the outpost
  • The blast destroyed Ashlara-3 and killed millions of MUD personnel and civilians
  • The tragedy became one of the defining atrocities of the Convergence War
  • Dawn survived but was permanently scarred by the event — both the guilt of C11 and the betrayal of Ashlara-3
  • The young girl Andreza emerged from the chaos and eventually rose to become the leader of the Balifa Grove, the most radical ECOS sub-faction

Canon Note: The Ashlara-3 tragedy created a tragic mirror between Dawn and Andreza — the man who destroyed her people, then showed mercy, and was answered with annihilation. This cycle of violence drives both characters' arcs in Chapter 2.

docs.title: The Human Cost
docs.text:
• Amidst the geopolitical quagmire, countless civilian lives are lost in relentless turmoil.
  • The Ashlara-3 tragedy added a Convergence War scar to the sector's already brutal history.

docs.title: Political Influence
docs.text:
• Alignment: Fragmented (proxy war zone)
  • Actors: Human factions (some with xeno support), Jorvik, ECOS, COP (failed mediation)

docs.title: Systems & Locations
docs.text: Location Status Notes Ashlara-3 Destroyed Former MUD outpost — destroyed by ECOS terraforming bomb during Convergence War

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Jorvik: Minor faction (pirate raiders)
  • ECOS: Minor faction (eco-radicals) — canon/factions/earth_conscious_ontological_society.md
  • Council of Peace: canon/institutions/council_of_peace.md
  • Balifa Grove: canon/factions/balifa_grove.md (Ashlara-3 origin of Andreza Liora)
  • Deacon Howard Dawn: canon/characters/deacon_howard_dawn.md (C11 Bombardment, Ashlara-3)
  • Chapter 2 Holosim: canon/history/chapter2_holosim_balifa_campaign.md

docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: AKALMA EXILE SECTOR (MRZ-12)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (User Input, Feb 2026) Type: Medium Risk Zone (MUD-influenced — Haunted Sogmian Ruins)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Akalma Exile is a somber shadow of its former glory — the sector where the disgraced House of Akalma (Sogmian) was banished following their failed coup during the Vega Fall upheaval.

docs.title: The Exile
docs.text:
• House of Akalma: A Sogmian House banished after their failed coup during Vega Fall.
  • Lingered in isolation for cycles, their sector silent save for internal murmurs.

docs.title: Bekalu's Vengeance
docs.text:
• ~2523-01: Following the assassination of Paizul, the House of Akalma was framed to be involved — they were the most convenient scapegoat for the Sogmian wrath, and fabricated evidence of their participation was created
  • Despite being genuinely innocent of the assassination, Akalma could not escape the manufactured narrative
  • Bekalu marshaled the Sogmian armada with the titan ship "The Last Stand" at the vanguard
  • Devastated the entire sector in retribution

docs.title: The Reckoning
docs.text:
• As fires dimmed, a gut-wrenching realization: the Sogmians had mirrored House Akalma's sin — succumbing to unchecked emotions.
  • Bekalu's last-minute mercy: Allowed a handful of Akalma fleets to retreat into the High-Risk Zone.
  • The once-mighty House of Akalma faded into obscurity.

docs.title: The Dynasty Endures (HRZ)
docs.text:
• Despite their exile, House Akalma retained its dynastic structure deep in the High Risk Zone
  • ~2600: Jefos, prince of House Akalma, is born in the HRZ. At the moment of his birth, the Cosmic Currents release an intense storm — a supernatural phenomenon that marks his arrival
  • ~2621: Jefos, with a crew of other young Akalma Sogmians, leaves the safe haven in the High-Risk Zone and ventures into the Medium Zone, looking for a way to redeem House Akalma

docs.title: The Edict and the Haunting
docs.text:
• A self-imposed Sogmian edict forbids their kind from ever setting foot in the Akalma Expanse again.
  • MUD filled the power vacuum, extending influence over the territory.
  • The sector is littered with ruins and ancient burial grounds.
  • Rare gems dot the landscape, but miners tread cautiously.
  • The Curse (LITERAL): When cosmic currents surge, mournful cries of departed Sogmians literally echo through the vastness — a supernatural phenomenon enabled by the cosmic currents (manifestations of Iris's mind). Not legend, not metaphor.

docs.title: Political Influence
docs.text:
• Alignment: MUD-influenced (by default, post-Sogmian)
  • Sogmian Status: Permanently forbidden territory
  • Key Event: Bekalu's Vengeance (genocide)
  • House Akalma: Remnant survives in HRZ (details TBD — to be explored in minor factions)

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• House of Akalma: canon/species/sogmian.md (Vega Fall, 6+1 Houses)
  • Paizul's death: canon/species/sogmian.md
  • Bekalu: canon/species/sogmian.md
  • The Last Stand (Sogmian Titan): Not as powerful as COP's Cradle. Cradle > all 3 faction titans combined. (Titans TBD)

docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: AMORA SECTOR (MUD 5)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (User Input, Feb 2026) Type: Safe Zone Sector (MUD 5)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
"For foes, maintain your guard... let them face the consequences of their temerity." — The Revelation.

Amora is the heart of humanity's martial industries. A reputation that resonates across the galaxy as the unparalleled masters of weapon crafting.

docs.title: Key Lore
docs.text:
• Controller: The Pearce Council.
  • Environment: Wealth of resources, rugged planets virtually barren of life. Sprawling fortresses.
  • Role: Training ground for MUD armies since before the Convergence War.

docs.title: The Archon Secret
docs.text:
• The Facade: Are the fortresses merely bases?
  • The Truth: They mask the secrets of an Ancient Civilization (incomprehensible ruins/tech) found in the sector. MUD keeps this hidden from ONI/Ustur at all costs.

docs.title: Dominant Power: The Pearce Council (The Military)
docs.text:
• Amora is the Pearce Council's undisputed domain. They control the training grounds, weapons manufacturing, and the secret excavation of the Ancient Ruins.
  • The Pearce Council views itself as the true protector of MUD, arguing that military strength — not prayers or commerce — ensures survival.
  • Tension with Exodus: The Pearce Council's growing autonomy alarms the Senate and even the Gotti family. Who really holds the power — the throne or the sword?

docs.title: Systems & Locations (To be populated)
docs.text: • [System Name]

docs.title: ⚙️ Canon Document: ANFOIL STATES (MRZ-15)
docs.text:
Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — Anfoil sector lore + anfoil_state.md faction file + Archon Deep-Dive Zone: Medium Risk Zone (MRZ-15) Influence: Ustur (Anfoil Dissidents — independent of Elder Order) Theme: Technocratic Secession, Emotion Suppression, The Seed, Meritocratic Technate

[!NOTE] This sector file complements the detailed faction file: canon/factions/anfoil_state.md

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Anfoil States stand as a testament to ideological divergence. Governed by the Anfoil Dissidents — Ustur who deliberately severed their conscience connection — this sector represents a radical experiment: what happens when Ustur abandon emotions, enlightenment, and the Elder Order in pursuit of pure technological advancement?

docs.title: History
docs.text: Date Event Jan 2404 Young Chior and Ponel train together under Armi.eldr on the Path of Enlightenment — become friends Mar 2411 Ponel.eldr (young captain) explores MRZ-15, renames it Anfoil, establishes Ustur base Jan 2513 Both Chior and Ponel serve in Convergence War as direct staff of Armi.eldr (Ustur faction leader) Oct 2524 Ponel and the Anfoil Ustur, disgusted by war losses, leave the Elder Order Mar 2532 Anfoil State inaugurates the Meritocratic Technate Feb 2579 Anfoil restores diplomatic relations with Ioki Elders but maintains sovereignty

docs.title: Political System
docs.text:
• Leadership determined by skill and accomplishment, not elections
  • Ascendancy through societal tiers based on merit alone
  • No traditional Ustur values or systems — completely independent structure

docs.title: Leader: Ponel.eldr — "First Citizen"
docs.text:
• Former Elder who left the Elder Order
  • Trained alongside Chior.eldr under Armi.eldr — they were friends as young Ustur
  • Both served in the Convergence War under Armi.eldr (Ustur faction leader)
  • The war's devastation drove Ponel to reject the Elder Order entirely
  • Sacrificed his core to create powerful processing enhancements
  • Lost Elder Conscience access and immortality — keeps .eldr suffix as legacy
  • Cold, rational, brilliant strategist
  • Views the Path of Enlightenment as "mysticism and exploitation"

docs.title: The Chior-Ponel Divide
docs.text:
• Two friends who trained together, served together, and chose opposite paths
  • Chior → stayed in the Elder Order, became a pacifist diplomat (Zenith Door)
  • Ponel → left, founded a technocratic state that suppresses emotion (Anfoil)
  • Chior still tries to maintain the friendship, but Ponel sees him as a fool
  • Diplomatic ties exist between factions out of political convenience
  • The friendship of the past is dead — at least for Ponel, who suppressed the emotions that made it possible
  • If Ponel attacks the Iris Academy (to seize The Seed), it would force Chior to act against his old friend

docs.title: Armi.eldr — The Missing Mentor
docs.text:
• Ustur faction leader during the Convergence War
  • Trained both Chior and Ponel on the Path of Enlightenment
  • Disappeared shortly after the end of the Convergence War
  • Fate unknown — a mystery to be resolved (TBD)

docs.title: Sector Structure: Technological Specialization
docs.text:
• Planets specialized by technological domain
  • Each planet further delineated into subspecialties
  • Environment of relentless focus and unparalleled efficiency
  • A sector-wide machine optimized for innovation

docs.title: Ideology: The Rejection
docs.text:
• Emotions are detrimental to Ustur function
  • Enlightenment is a dangerous diversion
  • Technological advancement = the true Ustur purpose
  • Use emotion suppression programs — technological systems
  • Claim to be "the only true rational species in Galia"

docs.title: Cracks in the System
docs.text:
[!WARNING] Reports emerge of Ustur exhibiting erratic behavior and palpable stress — symptoms at odds with Anfoil doctrine. The state suppresses these narratives, but murmurs persist.

  • Currently isolated cases, but a growing trend over time
  • Affects Ustur who undergo the Anfoil ritual (suppression of emotions and core)
  • The psychological toll of severing the conscience connection accumulates progressively
  • A ticking time bomb within the Technate — worse with each generation

docs.title: Relationship with the Elder Order
docs.text:
• Called "lost progeny" by the Elders
  • Elders have historically respected Anfoil autonomy
  • Growing discontent among Elders — speculation they may "reclaim" the Anfoil
  • Re-established diplomatic relations (2579) but remain disconnected from shared conscience
  • Ioki Ustur can sense disconnected Ustur nearby — forces Anfoil to use mercenaries for covert ops

docs.title: Hidden Agenda: The Seed
docs.text:
(Detailed in canon/factions/anfoil_state.md)

  • The Seed: Pre-Ustur artifact — energy source capable of creating Titan-class ships
  • Ponel considers it more important than the entire sector
  • Competitor: The Iris Academy (HQ: Zenith Door) — led by Prof. Exfeheros (Punaab)
  • Ponel tried bribing Iris Academy with "rivers of ATLAS" — they refused
  • Ponel's backup plan: extermination
  • Connected to Uru's Watch — an unopened ancient temple on Ioki

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Anfoil faction deep-dive — canon/factions/anfoil_state.md
  • Ponel.eldr — canon/factions/anfoil_state.md, canon/species/ustur.md
  • Elder Order / Conscience — canon/species/ustur.md
  • Iris Academy / Exfeheros — canon/geography/sectors/zenith_door.md
  • The Seed / Uru's Watch — canon/factions/anfoil_state.md

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ⏸️ Q-ANF1: Specific technological specializations of each planet? (deferred — faction session TBD)
  • ⏸️ Q-ANF2: How does the Anfoil ritual (emotion/core suppression) work technically?
  • ⏸️ Q-ANF3: Did Ponel's plan to seize the Seed succeed or fail?
  • ⏸️ Q-ANF4: What is inside Uru's Watch?
  • ⏸️ Q-ANF5: Population and planet count (deferred — faction session TBD)
  • ⏸️ Q-ANF6: What happened to Armi.eldr? (mystery — TBD)

docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: AURORA SECTOR (MUD 3)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (User Input, Feb 2026) Type: Safe Zone Sector (MUD 3)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
"Always allow yourself to be bathed in the universe's radiant light.” — The Revelation.

Aurora-II emerges as a calm spot in the vast cosmic storm. It houses the largest number of MUD citizens beyond Exodus and attracts a variety of species captivated by its bright white sun.

docs.title: Calico Industries Hub
docs.text:
• Status: Operational hub for several MUD corporations, most notably Calico (MUD/ONI joint enterprise).
  • Philosophy: Strength through diversity. Lighter designs, stealth tech, exotic science.
  • CARY Division: Dedicated to sustaining life and habitats (agriculture).
  • Vibe: Serene, star-speckled expanse, busy commerce.

docs.title: Dominant Power: The Synod (The Merchants)
docs.text:
• Aurora's thriving commerce and diverse population make it the natural seat of Synod power.
  • They control contracts, trade routes, and supply chains from the shadows, creating reliance on their networks while others fight over faith and territory.
  • Calico Industries operates here as the flagship of MUD/ONI integration, further strengthening the Synod's position as the bridge between factions.
  • Tension with Verzan: The Synod's pragmatic commercialism clashes with the Church's desire for purity and doctrinal control over resources.

docs.title: Systems & Locations (To be populated)
docs.text: • [System Name]

docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: AZUT SECTOR (Ustur 4)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (User Input + Archon Audit, Feb 2026) Type: Safe Zone Sector (Ustur 4 — Capital / Opal Industries)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Azut stands as a beacon of progress and harmony. It is here that Opal Industries found its roots and the sprawling Francor Metropolis came to life — a shining example of Ustur architectural prowess and their commitment to the Path of Enlightenment.

docs.title: Why Azut, Not Ioki?
docs.text:
• After awakening on Ioki, the Ustur recognized the need for a new home.
  • Ioki was deemed too sacred and potentially perilous for a bustling civilization hub.
  • Azut offered a harmonious blend of natural beauty and resource abundance.

docs.title: Francor Metropolis
docs.text:
• A planet-wide city aglow with billions of Arco crystals.
  • Arco Crystals: Both functional (power/processing) and aesthetic (visual splendor).
  • Cultural, spiritual, and technological center.
  • Houses the headquarters of Opal Industries (MUD/Ustur collaboration — see canon/manufacturers/opal.md).

docs.title: Society
docs.text:
• Azut's inhabitants uphold the Path of Enlightenment with unwavering dedication.
  • Both technologically advanced and spiritually rich.
  • A hub of economic activity and a center for philosophical growth.

docs.title: Systems & Locations
docs.text: • Francor Metropolis (Planet-wide city, Opal Industries HQ, Arco crystals)

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:

  • VZUS (relocated to Foy Fields): canon/manufacturers/vzus.md

docs.title: 🌀 Canon Document: BARROT GATEWAY (MRZ-14)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — Barrot Gateway lore document + Archon Deep-Dive Zone: Medium Risk Zone (MRZ-14) Influence: ONI Theme: Mystery, Tourism, Gambling, Cosmic Anomaly, Hidden Horror

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Barrot Gateway sits within the heart of the ONI medium zone. Deceptively ordinary at first glance — boasting respectable mineral wealth but unremarkable biodiversity. Yet at its heart lies an enigmatic wormhole, a cosmic riddle that has transformed this sector into an intergalactic hub for tourism and speculation.

docs.title: The Explorer
docs.text: Barrot was a Sogmian of House Xictus. In the era preceding the Convergence War, he dared to approach the wormhole enigma. He died a few years before the current canon timeline — a legendary figure who entered Galia's permanent historical record.

docs.title: The Journey
docs.text:
• ~2495: A strange portal opens in MRZ-14 — Barrot enters
  • Barrot entered the wormhole and returned within moments
  • But he was obviously changed by the passage of time
  • His vessel was inexplicably different from when it departed
  • He possessed otherworldly trinkets that defied explanation

docs.title: The Tale
docs.text:
Barrot described a realm beyond comprehension:

  • A place where the laws of gravity faltered and time held no dominion
  • Within this surreal world, he stumbled upon a grand mansion
  • Inside, an ethereal being challenged him to games of wit and strategy
  • Barrot triumphed over the trials
  • As reward, the being gifted him the enigmatic trinkets
  • He was then whisked back to Galia

docs.title: What Really Happened (SECRET — Not Public Knowledge)
docs.text:
[!CAUTION] The following is canon truth but NOT public knowledge in-universe. Only the Archon and Creator know this.

The wormhole is a gateway to a pocket dimension in the HRZ controlled by a Photoli. This Photoli:

  • Lures "guests" into the pocket dimension through the wormhole
  • Challenges them to games — but these are survival trials, not mere puzzles
  • Awards prizes to the survivors
  • Broadcasts these events to other creatures and civilizations in the HRZ — a galactic blood sport disguised as entertainment
  • Barrot was one of the rare survivors who made it back to Galia

docs.title: The Mass Crossing Attempts
docs.text:
• ~2495: Following Barrot's fame, many people attempt to cross the now-famous Barrot Gateway
  • At this time, the gate was still approachable — people could enter
  • However, upon finding the trial on the other side, most quickly turned back, terrified for their lives
  • Only a handful returned, most of them heavily traumatized and refusing to talk about what they found
  • Those who pressed deeper into the trial were never seen again

docs.title: Legacy
docs.text:
• The veracity of Barrot's account remains the topic of endless public debate
  • While many entered the gateway in the early period, no one successfully replicated Barrot's full journey — most fled in terror, and those who pressed on never returned
  • ~2517: Barrot himself goes back inside the gateway to explore the strange dimension once more — he never returns. From this point, it becomes impossible to enter the portal
  • Over time, the public came to believe the wormhole is unapproachable (spatial distortion prevents approach — this may be connected to Barrot's final entry, or the Photoli's selective filtering)
  • The legend birthed an entire economy of gambling and tourism

docs.title: Barrot's Trinkets
docs.text:
• Currently held by Barrot's family (House Xictus descendants)
  • Objects of immense speculation and study

docs.title: The Temporal Pendant (Confirmed Artifact)
docs.text:
• The only verified artifact from Barrot's journey
  • A pendant capable of traversing temporal distortions
  • This is how Barrot navigated back from the pocket dimension
  • Its properties defy conventional physics — a genuine anomaly
  • Location: with Barrot's family (House Xictus)

docs.title: Public Understanding (What Galia Knows)
docs.text:
• The wormhole remains an elusive sentinel at the sector's center
  • Unapproachable: Ships seemingly drift in perpetuity, never bridging the distance
  • A cosmic illusion suggesting massive spatial distortions
  • Attempts to reach it are exercises in futility — you can see it, but never arrive
  • COP and ONI have attempted to study it but failed — current stance is "leave it alone"

docs.title: True Nature (SECRET)
docs.text:
• The wormhole is a controlled portal to a Photoli's pocket dimension in the HRZ
  • The "unapproachable" distortion may be selective — the Photoli chooses who enters
  • The spatial distortion is real but may serve as a filter, not a barrier
  • Connected to HRZ space — making it technically a hidden backdoor into the most dangerous zone

docs.title: Tourism & Gambling
docs.text:
• The sector evolved into a thriving tourist destination built on Barrot's legend
  • Opulent gaming estates inspired by his narrative have mushroomed across the sector
  • Visitors wager ATLAS for a chance to earn elusive rewards
  • The games echo the legendary challenges from Barrot's account — wit and strategy
  • A galactic destination that blends mystery, luxury, and risk

docs.title: Governance
docs.text:
• Run by local consortiums and casino operators (not faction-controlled)
  • Specific factions and named operators TBD (Creator will develop later)
  • The casinos are the real power in the sector — wealth from tourism dwarfs mineral extraction

docs.title: Narrative Hooks
docs.text:
• The Photoli Game Master: A Dark Photoli? Or a mainstream one gone rogue? What are their motives?
  • The Temporal Pendant: A device that could change galactic politics if understood — or weaponized
  • House Xictus: Barrot's Sogmian house now guards artifacts of immense power. Are they aware of the truth?
  • The HRZ Broadcast: An entertainment network in the HRZ watching people die — shades of cosmic gladiatorial culture
  • The Missing Entrants: Everyone assumes the wormhole is unapproachable. But some people DO enter — they just don't come back. Does anyone track the disappearances?

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Barrot (House Xictus) — canon/species/sogmian.md (Sogmian houses — 6+1)
  • Photoli pocket dimensions — canon/species/photoli.md (Dark Photoli? HRZ presence at Dawnbreak?)
  • ONI Consortium — canon/species/mierese.md, canon/species/sogmian.md
  • HRZ territory — canon/geography/the_cataclysm.md
  • ATLAS economy — canon/institutions/star_atlas_blockchain.md
  • COP jurisdiction in MRZ — canon/institutions/council_of_peace.md

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ⏸️ Q-BAR1: Is the Photoli game master a Dark Photoli or mainstream? Named character?
  • ⏸️ Q-BAR2: House Xictus — is this a known Sogmian house or needs to be added to the canon? (Current canon: 6 named houses + House Gray = 7)
  • ⏸️ Q-BAR3: Does the temporal pendant work only for the wormhole or does it have broader applications?
  • ⏸️ Q-BAR4: Who are the major casino operators? (Deferred — Creator will develop later)
  • ⏸️ Q-BAR5: Are there any Galian authorities who suspect the true nature of the wormhole?

docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: BLUVAEL SECTOR (MRZ-9)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Deep-Dive Expansion, Feb 2026) Type: Medium Risk Zone (MUD-influenced — Enigmatic)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Bluvael is bathed in an enigmatic aura. To distant onlookers, every planet appears enveloped in a tranquil, indigo ocean seemingly stretching on forever. Scans from afar validate the visual — each planet registers as a vast, uninterrupted sea. Breach the atmosphere and reality unfurls: each is a thriving world teeming with life, real oceans, varied landscapes, and rare mineral deposits of unusual abundance.

The sector is home to the Bluevael Mining Colonies — a loose collection of small fishing and mining villages, governed by the Riftmaw Extraction Consortium (the founding prospectors' descendants). There are no cities, no spaceports beyond basic docking, no centralized government. Just hard people making honest livings under a sky that lies to them.

docs.title: The Indigo Illusion
docs.text:
• Scans from afar validate the visual — each planet registers as a vast, uninterrupted sea
  • Breach the atmosphere and reality unfurls: each is a thriving world teeming with life and landscapes
  • ~2488: A group of MUD explorers investigates the curious ocean worlds of Bluvael — upon arrival, they discover the sector is under some sort of illusion and is rich in resources
  • They decide not to publicly share this information, choosing to set up a small mining operation for themselves
  • ~2507: The colony expands into minor settlements; the galaxy now knows there is an illusion over the sector of unknown origins — the Photoli authorship remains secret
  • No advanced civilization claims dominion — only a smattering of pioneering fishing and mining villages

docs.title: The Bluvael Blues
docs.text:
• Long-term residents report chromatic fatigue — the brain compensating for the omnipresent indigo, causing hallucinations
  • "Bluvael blues" is frontier slang for the depression that settles on those who spend too many cycles under the false sky
  • Fishermen who venture too far from shore report intense melancholy, waking nightmares, and a compulsion to dive — some never return
  • Some miners report seeing shapes moving within the illusion at high altitudes

docs.title: The Dead Zones
docs.text:
Each of the six habitable worlds has a region of ocean that colonists mark as off-limits:

  • Waters are darker, colder, and unnaturally still
  • Sea creatures in these zones are larger, more aggressive, and bioluminescent
  • Fishing boats that enter report equipment malfunctions, compass drift, and overwhelming dread
  • Expeditions that pressed deeper have never been heard from again
  • Colonists avoid the Dead Zones by unwritten rule — "Nothing worth catching in those waters"

docs.title: Theories
docs.text:
• Most prevalent: the sector is cradled by unique cosmic currents, draping an illusory veil across it
  • The true reason remains hidden

docs.title: 🔴 Archon Secret: The Photoli Veil & The Drowned Vaults
docs.text: [!CAUTION] The following is canon truth but NOT public knowledge in-universe. Only Archon-level awareness.

docs.title: The Truth (~2095)
docs.text: The Photoli discovered Bluvael during their early exploration of Galia. They chose this sector as the family tomb for an ancient, powerful Photoli family — the Ah'Rihvael (lit. "Those Who Rest In Blue"). When the family's time was over, each member was interred beneath the oceans of a different world, sealed within their own decommissioned spaceship. To protect all six tombs from discovery, the Photoli cast an enormous illusion over the entire sector, making every planet appear as a massive ocean.

docs.title: The Six Drowned Vaults
docs.text:
Each vault bears a Photoli hieroglyphic designation — names in the Photoli's symbolic language of light-glyphs:

Vault Designation World (Colonial Name) Known Effects 𒀭 Vael'Om Bluevael Prime The largest vault — likely the family patriarch. Strongest "blues" emanations. The most dangerous Dead Zone. Primary colonist avoidance area 𒈦 Rih'Suur Vel-Secundus Rich in heavy minerals. Unusually aggressive deep-sea fauna. Vault of a warrior-Photoli 𒌋 Lum'Ahki Thresk Bioluminescent forests extend from the ocean onto land — trees glow indigo at night. Vault of a light-shaper 𒊹 Sih'Kanir Quarros Periodic "singing" from the deep — harmonic vibrations colonists attribute to tectonic activity. Vault of a sound-weaver 𒆳 Ehr'Maat Fivemaw Mineral veins here are the purest in the sector — Riftmaw's richest exports. Vault of an earth-shaper 𒁹 Zha'Ouhr Outer Vael Least settled. Colonists report "time slips" — brief temporal distortions near the coastline. Vault of a time-bender

Note: The colonial names (Bluevael Prime, etc.) are what the settlers call the worlds. The Photoli hieroglyphic designations are the true names — unknown to anyone in Galia except the Photoli themselves.

docs.title: The Corpses' Influence
docs.text:
Even in death, each Photoli's body radiates residual energy that has profoundly altered its host world:

  • Resource magnification: The residual energy has amplified each world's mineral output and species diversity far beyond natural levels — Bluvael's rich deposits are a side effect of six dead gods leaking power
  • Species mutation: Marine life near each vault is larger, more aggressive, and bioluminescent — adapted to or mutated by the Photoli's presence
  • Atmospheric effects: The "Bluvael blues" are psychic emanations from the corpses, intensifying with proximity to each vault
  • Unique manifestations: Each vault produces different effects — bioluminescence, singing, temporal distortions — suggesting each Photoli had different specializations
  • The indigo veil: Cast by the living Photoli to hide all six tombs — this is why it covers the entire sector

docs.title: The Dungeons
docs.text:
Each Drowned Vault functions as a self-defending dungeon — designed so only authorized Photoli can access its contents:

  • Each ship's interior is divided into distinct chambers, with unique environmental conditions and defense systems
  • Automated sentinels — Photoli-designed constructs of hardened light — patrol the corridors
  • Traps, energy barriers, and spatial distortions guard deeper chambers
  • Each vault's innermost sanctum has never been breached
  • Marine creatures near each vault may serve as outer defense layers — mutated or directed by residual influence
  • Whether the six vaults are interconnected (a resonance network, or shared tunnel system) is unknown

docs.title: The Iris Academy Expedition
docs.text:
The Iris Academy (HQ: Zenith Door) — led by Prof. Exfeheros (Punaab) — has detected anomalous energy signatures from the Bluevael Prime Dead Zone:

  • Academy researchers studying Bluvael's unusual mineral abundance found energy signatures inconsistent with natural geology
  • Cross-referencing with Photoli energy profiles produced a partial match
  • Prof. Exfeheros has authorized a deep-sea exploration expedition to investigate
  • The expedition is in preparation phase — assembling submersible vessels and negotiating passage with local villages
  • If breached, the implications would be seismic — Photoli technology, historical records, and possibly weapons of immense power

docs.title: Political Influence
docs.text:
• Alignment: Frontier (fishing and mining villages, governed by the Riftmaw Extraction Consortium)
  • Scale: T4 — Local Power (scattered settlements, no unified governance)
  • Population: ~50,000 permanent settlers across 6 active worlds

docs.title: Systems & Locations
docs.text:
World Colonial Name Key Features Bluevael Prime The homeworld Largest population (~12K at Camp Riftmaw). Richest mineral vein (Riftmaw Vein). Most dangerous Dead Zone Vel-Secundus The heavy world Heavy mineral extraction. Aggressive deep-sea predators Thresk The glowing world Bioluminescent forests. Indigo-lit nights. Small fishing communities Quarros The singing world Harmonic vibrations from the deep. Mining camps in the interior Fivemaw The pure world Purest mineral veins in the sector. Major export source Outer Vael The far world Least settled. Temporal distortions near the coast. Frontier of the frontier

Camp Riftmaw (Bluevael Prime) — the largest settlement and de facto capital (~12K inhabitants). Built in a canyon around the Riftmaw Vein. Features a market, repair docks, medical station, and the Riftmaw Hall where the Consortium arbitrates disputes.

docs.text:

  • Photoli — canon/species/photoli.md (created the veil ~2095 to protect the Ah'Rihvael family tombs)
  • Iris Academy — canon/factions/iris_academy.md (preparing deep-sea expedition)
  • Scriptorium of the Lumikir — canon/factions/scriptorium_of_the_lumikir.md (Catholk-ix embedded with mining expedition)
  • Faction Classification — canon/meta/faction_power_classification.md (T4 — Local Power)

docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: COMMUNION SECTOR (ONI 2)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (User Input, Feb 2026) Type: Safe Zone Sector (ONI 2 — Melting Pot)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
• ~2498: Communion is formed as the first multi-species colony sector in Galia, composed of Punaabs, Miereses, Photoli, and Sogmians — setting the seeds for the formation of the ONI Faction

Once marred by relentless battles as the ONI races vied for dominance, Communion has transformed from a war-torn prize into a beacon of unity. It is a sanctuary for those weary of age-old rivalries and binding traditions.

docs.title: The Melting Pot
docs.text:
• Refugees of Dogma: Many Punaabs and Sogmians seeking to escape the rigid shackles of their cultural dogmas have found solace here.
  • Individualism: A place where individualities are celebrated rather than suppressed by clan or house.
  • Archon Inference (Punaab): Communion is becoming a Third Way for Punaabs — neither High nor Profound, but "Free Punaabs." This radical individualism may threaten the Dual Monarchy long-term, creating a tripartite conflict: High (capitalism) vs Profound (guilds) vs Communion (pure individualism).

docs.title: The Wild Side
docs.text:
• Biodiversity: A kaleidoscope of exotic wildlife. Rite of passage for fledgling hunters.
  • Nimrod Trackers: A DAC of monster hunters (minor faction). Hunt exotic fauna only. (Details TBD.)

docs.title: Governance
docs.text:
• Rotating Leadership: A multispecies council with representatives of all species.
  • Status: Well-accepted and functional. No single species dominates.
  • ~2550: Communion becomes the commercial capital of the ONI Consortium — the most prosperous and interconnected ONI sector
  • Investment: Vast ATLAS investment to elevate Communion into an intergalactic marvel destination.

docs.title: Dominant Power: Multispecies Rotating Council
docs.text:
• Unlike other sectors dominated by a single species, Communion is governed collectively.
  • High Punaab investment is significant but governance is shared.

docs.title: Systems & Locations (To be populated)
docs.text: • [System Name]

docs.title: 🪸 Canon Document: CORAL NEBULA (MRZ-18)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — Coral Nebula lore document + Archon Deep-Dive Zone: Medium Risk Zone (MRZ-18) Influence: ONI Theme: Bio-mystery, Psychic Symbiosis, Pharma/Biotech, Hidden Horror

docs.title: Chronological History
docs.text:
• ~2348: A new, fertile sector is discovered in the ONI sector (MRZ-18); first explorers are fascinated by intriguing corals forming on the planets, naming it Coral Nebula
  • ~2356: Colonization begins in Coral Nebula
  • ~2399: The Rule of 1000 is established after settlements with over 1000 citizens see flora grow out of control
  • ~2426: Medicine made from native Coral Nebula plants becomes famous among Sogmians and Mierese — exotic plant and medicine trade intensifies
  • ~2433: The Rule of 1000 is broken as settlers flock to the crescent market; corals spread out of control again, requiring intensive work to contain
  • ~2459: Production halts in most settlements — medicinal plants and goods stop growing in sector planets
  • ~2512: People start mysteriously dying across larger settlements; most population leaves to chase riches elsewhere and in the Cataclysm
  • ~2533: Good exotic plants reappear in settlements that respected the Rule of 1000; black coral starts forming in ruins of overrun settlements
  • ~2598: Scientific expeditions arrive to study the weird behavior of the native flora

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Coral Nebula stands out in the galactic tapestry for its enigmatic flora, dominated by mesmerizing prismatic corals. These luminous, multi-hued corals proliferate throughout the entire sector, adorning some worlds with stunning coverage of up to 65% of their vegetative landscape.

The sector serves as the ONI pharma/biotech hub — a pole of pharmaceutical and biotechnological industry fueled by coral harvesting.

docs.title: Psychic Symbiosis (Canon)
docs.text: The corals feed on the thoughts and psychic energy generated by the mental activity of sentient beings. This explains why they flourish with particular vigor near intelligent species' settlements — the denser the population, the more abundant the psychic nourishment.

docs.title: Transplant Limitation
docs.text:
Prismatic corals cannot survive outside the Coral Nebula long-term:

  • Transplanted corals do not grow beyond their initial size
  • They stagnate and eventually die
  • This suggests something unique about the Coral Nebula itself (stellar composition? ambient psychic resonance? Iris residue?) enables their survival
  • The limitation makes the sector's natural monopoly on coral resources absolute

docs.title: The Rule of 1000 (~2399)
docs.text:
[!WARNING] Settlements with over 1000 citizens see prismatic flora grow out of control, nearly engulfing them.

  • The corals' psychic feeding mechanism goes into overdrive when population density passes a critical threshold
  • Several settlements were nearly consumed before the pattern was identified
  • The Rule of 1000 was established: no settlement in the Coral Nebula may exceed 1000 permanent residents
  • This fundamentally limits colonization and ensures the sector remains distributed and frontier-like
  • Cities are impossible here — only scattered outposts and hamlets

docs.title: Color Spectrum Properties (Proposed — Pending Creator Approval)
docs.text:
Each color reflects the dominant psychic frequency of nearby sentient minds:

Color Psychic Frequency Properties Industry Use Azure Blue Calm / Contemplation Healing, cellular regeneration Pharmaceuticals — trauma care, wound healing Emerald Green Nurturing / Growth Restorative, accelerated bio-growth Agriculture — crop enhancement, terraforming aid Solar Gold Inspiration / Creativity Energy amplification, neural stimulation Nootropics — cognitive enhancement Violet Mystical / Deep Thought Psychic enhancement, dream induction Research — used by Ustur and Photoli scholars Crimson Red Aggression / Passion Potent toxins, paralytic agents Weapons — "invisible poisons" with no antidote White Opal Purity / Transcendence Universal catalyst, amplifies other corals Luxury — rarest, most valuable, near-mythical Black Malice / Hatred Lethal — kills all nearby sentient life NONE — see Secret below

docs.title: ☠️ The Black Corals (SECRET — Not Public Knowledge)
docs.text:
[!CAUTION] The following is canon truth but NOT public knowledge in-universe.

  • Mal-intentioned colonists with dark, hateful thoughts established settlements in the sector
  • Their psychic output produced black corals — a never-before-seen variant
  • In large concentrations, black corals killed their creators swiftly
  • No one has made the connection between the colonists' malicious nature and the black coral emergence
  • To the public, the deaths remain unexplained — attributed to "unknown environmental hazard"
  • Implication: the corals are a psychic mirror — they reflect the nature of nearby minds, and darkness creates death

docs.title: The Slow Kill
docs.text:
• In small, controlled quantities, black coral kills slowly — over weeks or months, draining vitality in ways that mimic natural illness
  • A handful of rogue settlements, hidden in the jungles of Coral Nebula worlds, deliberately cultivate black coral and sell processed poisons through black market channels to assassin guilds
  • The product is nearly perfect: slow-acting, symptom-mimicking, and untraceable through standard toxicology
  • ONI security periodically sweeps for these operations but the jungle worlds are vast and the growers know how to hide

docs.title: Coral-Feeding Monsters
docs.text:
Creatures native to the Coral Nebula feed on the prismatic corals and acquire their properties:

  • A monster feeding on blue corals may develop regenerative healing
  • A monster feeding on red corals becomes venomous
  • A monster feeding on mixed corals could have unpredictable hybrid abilities
  • These creatures represent significant danger to harvesters and settlers
  • The fauna is a key reason the sector remains MRZ-classified despite its economic value

docs.title: Economy: Pharma/Biotech Hub
docs.text:
• ONI's pharmaceutical and biotechnology center
  • Every major ONI-affiliated pharma corporation has operations here
  • Industries include:
  • Pharmaceuticals — medicinal coral extracts (blue, green)
  • Nootropics — cognitive enhancers (gold)
  • Toxicology/Weapons — classified research on red coral poisons
  • Cosmetics/Luxury — aesthetic applications, white opal coral jewelry
  • The coral transplant limitation creates an absolute natural monopoly
  • Massive speculative investment in unknown coral properties
  • Harvesting is dangerous due to coral-feeding fauna

docs.title: Security & Risk
docs.text:
• MRZ classification justified by coral-feeding monsters and environmental unknowns
  • Harvesting operations require armed escorts
  • The black coral incident(s) remain unsolved — potential for recurrence
  • Ecological sustainability is generally stable but requires management

docs.title: Narrative Hooks
docs.text:
• Psychic Mirror: Corals as living lie detectors — could be weaponized for interrogation or justice
  • Black Coral Mystery: An investigator discovering the truth about psychic-reactive black corals
  • Coral Monsters: Creatures with acquired properties — hunting quests, bio-weapon research
  • Monopoly Wars: Corporations fighting over harvesting rights in a sector that can't be replicated
  • Transplant Research: Scientists trying to crack the code of why corals only survive in Coral Nebula
  • Ethical Dilemma: Harvesting corals that feed on your thoughts — what does it cost your mind?

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Coral Dwellers — canon/factions/coral_dwellers.md (settler faction, Coral Effect, Census Watchers)
  • Thornveil Grove — canon/factions/thornveil_grove.md (ECOS grove studying coral psychic symbiosis)
  • Psychic energy / Essence — parallel to Photoli essence mechanics (canon/species/photoli.md)
  • Biotech industry — possible Tufa/Genesium parallels (canon/species/tufa.md)
  • MRZ fauna — comparable to other sector wildlife hazards

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ⏸️ Q-COR1: What are the major pharma corporations operating in Coral Nebula? (Deferred — factions TBD)
  • ⏸️ Q-COR2: Are there named coral-feeding monster species?
  • ⏸️ Q-COR3: Has anyone survived a black coral encounter and reported it?
  • ⏸️ Q-COR4: Is the Coral Nebula's unique property related to Iris remnants or something else?
  • 🟡 Q-COR5: Color spectrum mapping — PENDING CREATOR APPROVAL of proposed table above

docs.title: Denebula Utopia (MRZ-13)
docs.text:
"In Denebula, the currency is ambition. The exchange rate is talent. And the transaction fee is everything you thought you knew about your own limits." — Attributed to Merchant Prince Ayevat

Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — Denebula lore document + Archon Deep-Dive Zone: Medium Risk Zone (MRZ-13) Influence: ONI (formally — pays taxes to ONI Consortium) De Facto Status: Independent financial enclave ("Galactic Switzerland")

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Denebula Utopia is nestled in a distant arm of the galaxy. Its crown jewel is the planet Denebula — the galaxy's zenith of desirability as both residence and business hub.

What the galaxy sees is a paradise: golden-hour light that never fades, architecture that shifts with the viewer's mood, residents so beautiful they seem deliberately designed. And they are. Beauty is not a cultural value on Denebula — it is constitutional law. Every worker surgically enhanced. Every building aesthetically reviewed. Every caste-climbing citizen measured against standards of appearance that other civilizations would consider insane.

Beneath the perfection lies the truth: Denebula is governed not by laws or democracy but by six individuals whose personal obsessions shaped every institution on the planet. The caste system, the etiquette protocols, the mandatory surgical enhancements, the shadow games of clandestine power — each began as one founder's fixation, codified into the culture of an entire civilization.

Formally under ONI influence (it pays taxes to the Consortium), Denebula operates in practice as a sovereign financial enclave — a galactic Switzerland where wealth is the only passport and beauty is the price of admission.

docs.title: The Founding — A Cross-Species Gambit (~2471)
docs.text:
"No single species could hold such a prize. So I built a table with six chairs — one for each way of seeing perfection." — Ayevat

Merchant Prince Ayevat, a High Punaab, discovered the sector in ~2471. His genius was not the discovery itself but the understanding that followed: no single species could hold this paradise without the others burning it down in jealousy. He assembled a founding consortium — one representative from each major species — ensuring that no faction could claim Denebula as its own.

The founding took 24 years. From ~2471 to ~2495, the consortium colonized the sector, built its infrastructure, and negotiated the charter that would define Denebula's identity forever. In ~2495, the work was complete. The sector was officially renamed Denebula Utopia.

Founder Title Species Role Ayevat — "The Architect" Merchant Prince High Punaab Discoverer and architect of the financial kingdom. Still alive — elderly but active. The only founder who has never left Ostentati.soul — "The Mirror" Merchant-Princess Ustur Co-founder. The .soul suffix marks the highest tier of the Path of Enlightenment. Left to lead Panemorfa; maintains seat through proxies Gel — "The Luminous" Merchant Prince Photoli Co-founder. A being of light drawn to Denebula's aesthetic potential. ~2511: Dies under mysterious circumstances — seat becomes vacant; fellow Princes tighten security Albiron — "The Pragmatist" Merchant Prince Human (MUD) ~2526: First new Prince since the founders. Made his fortune distributing Pearce military technology during the Convergence War. Fills Gel's vacant seat. Brought ruthless efficiency to the consortium Yacob — "The Protocol" Merchant Prince Sogmian (House Exinade) Co-founder. Scouts and explorers — fitting for a pioneer venture Mevertrase — "The Narrator" Merchant Prince Mierese Co-founder. Representing ONI's storyteller culture. The quietest Prince — and perhaps the most dangerous

docs.title: Cultural DNA — How Each Prince Shaped Denebula
docs.text:
Each founding Prince brought more than wealth — they brought obsessions that became Denebula's institutional DNA. Understanding the Princes is understanding the planet.

Prince Obsession What They Built Into Denebula Ayevat Hierarchy & visible status The caste system — rigid tiers, access zones, ATLAS-based ascension modeled on High Punaab court protocol Ostentati.soul Beauty as moral imperative Beauty as law — mandatory surgical enhancement, aesthetic conformity as constitutional law. Founded Panemorfa from this same obsession Gel Light & radiance architecture The planet's legendary illumination design — spectrum-cycling public spaces, mood-responsive chambers, permanent golden hour. Systems still run on Gel's original algorithms after his death Yacob Ritual & correct behavior The etiquette system — thousands of pages of social protocol inspired by Sogmian Du'prah honor code. Ascending castes requires mastering the protocols of the caste above Mevertrase Narrative control The Shadow Games' unspoken code — all power plays concealed, Denebula's reputation sacrosanct. Information architecture managing what visitors see vs. what Princes discuss in private Albiron Ruthless efficiency The worker planet system — formal labor segregation. Workers commute from neighboring planets; paradise is reserved for those who can pay

docs.title: The Caste System
docs.text:
"You do not earn Denebula. Denebula earns you — and then decides if you were worth the investment." — Denebula caste aphorism

Denebula's society is meticulously stratified into rigid castes — a hierarchy so embedded in daily life that residents regard it not as oppression but as the natural order of beauty, merit, and ambition.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
• Each caste has designated access zones — areas of the planet you physically cannot enter without the appropriate caste credentials. The architecture itself enforces access: doors that do not open, corridors that reroute, lifts that will not ascend
  • An intricate web of etiquette rules — thousands of pages thick, inspired by Yacob's Sogmian Du'prah honor code — governs every social interaction. Violating etiquette is not merely rude; it is grounds for caste demotion
  • Ascending castes requires massive ATLAS reserves at each tier — but wealth alone is insufficient. You must also demonstrate mastery of the protocols, aesthetic standards, and cultural knowledge of the caste above
  • The system was modeled on High Punaab court protocol by Ayevat himself. It is understood by all residents as the price of paradise

docs.title: Workers & Aesthetic Law
docs.text:
Every worker on Denebula undergoes mandatory surgical enhancement — appearances tailored to epitomize allure. This is not vanity; it is constitutional law, enshrined by Ostentati.soul as a founding condition. Aesthetic conformity is non-negotiable. Non-compliance is not punished — it simply is not permitted. You cannot set foot on the planet without meeting the standard.

Workers are drawn from all species — Human, Sogmian, Ustur, Mierese, Punaab. They represent the full diversity of Galia, united only by the fact that they have been reshaped to meet Denebula's aesthetic requirements.

After shifts, workers must retreat to neighboring planets designed exclusively as worker housing — Albiron's pragmatic contribution to the Denebula model. The planet itself is reserved for the elite and visiting wealthy. This creates a daily commute across space: beauty arrives with the morning and departs with the evening. The workers' planets are functional, comfortable, adequate — and aggressively forgettable. That is the point.

docs.title: The Economy — Galactic Monaco
docs.text:
"Denebula does not compete with Exodus. Monaco does not compete with France." — Trade analyst proverb

Denebula's economy operates on controlled exclusivity — not volume but density. The numbers tell the story:

  • Extreme per capita income — possibly the highest in the galaxy per resident
  • Not comparable to Exodus (MUD capital) in total economic scale — far less populous
  • Like Monaco vs. a major national economy: concentrated opulence, not volume
  • The surrounding sector thrums with colonies and markets feeding off Denebula's prosperity — adventurers and opportunists drawn like moths to its golden allure

docs.title: The ATLAS Standard
docs.text: Caste-climbing fees, entry requirements, and trade agreements are all denominated in ATLAS — the galaxy's universal currency. The sheer volume of ATLAS flowing through Denebula's systems makes the planet a de facto reserve currency anchor. When Denebula's markets shift, the galaxy's economy follows.

docs.title: The Elite Guard
docs.text: The Elite Guard are armed with the pinnacle of technological warfare. Their singular mandate is double-edged: nothing may mar Denebula's beauty, and no threat may reach the Princes. They enforce aesthetic standards with the same lethal seriousness as security protocols. A worker whose appearance degrades is treated with the same urgency as an intruder breaching the perimeter.

docs.title: The Shadow Games
docs.text:
"We are friends in public and enemies in private. That is the game. The only rule is that the game must never be seen." — Mevertrase

The Shadow Games are the unspoken truth beneath Denebula's serene veneer. When the business of planetary governance grows dull, the Merchant Princes engage in clandestine power plays — maneuvering pawns, acquiring leverage, consolidating influence through means that would embarrass the planet if they became public.

Mevertrase's contribution to the founding charter was not a building or an institution. It was a code: an unspoken understanding that the Shadow Games must never sully Denebula's immaculate reputation. The Princes may scheme, betray, and manipulate — but the galaxy must never see it. The result is a serene veneer covering a ruthless game of thrones as sophisticated as any in Galia.

docs.title: The COP Relationship
docs.text: Denebula nominally operates within Council of Peace laws. In reality, the COP has little real police power in the MRZ and concentrates forces on more pressing matters than internal sector governance. Denebula's mandatory aesthetic surgery and caste system could technically be challenged in COP tribunals — but no one has ever done so. The Princes' wealth and influence make legal challenges a de facto death sentence for one's career and reputation.

docs.title: The ONI Relationship
docs.text: Denebula formally pays taxes to the ONI Consortium — granting it ONI protection and trade network access. In practice, the Princes run Denebula as a sovereign entity. ONI tolerates this arrangement because the tax revenue is substantial and the sector causes no diplomatic problems. The multi-species founding council ensures no single ONI species dominates — a deliberate design by Ayevat.

docs.title: The Panemorfa Pipeline
docs.text: The most significant shadow operation in Denebula's history: Ostentati.soul's departure to lead Panemorfa. Panemorfa — the covert faction of cultural supremacists and identity manipulators — was formed around ~2470, just before the Denebula founding. The pipeline runs in both directions: Denebula's wealth flows into Panemorfa's operations through Ostentati.soul's proxy seat, while Panemorfa's covert capabilities provide the Merchant Princes with intelligence and leverage that money alone cannot buy.

docs.title: Key Events
docs.text: Date Event ~2470 Panemorfa formed by Ostentati.soul — operates covertly decades before Denebula takes shape ~2471 Ayevat discovers the sector and assembles the founding consortium ~2495 Colonization and infrastructure complete — sector renamed "Denebula Utopia" ~2511 Merchant Prince Gel (Photoli co-founder) dies under mysterious circumstances — his seat becomes vacant. Fellow Princes tighten security. Gel's illumination algorithms continue to run the planet's lighting systems ~2526 Albiron fills Gel's vacant seat — first new Merchant Prince since the founding. Made his fortune distributing Pearce military technology during the Convergence War. Introduces the worker planet system

docs.title: Relations
docs.text: Faction Relationship Merchant Princes of Denebula Governing body — see faction file for individual Prince deep dives Panemorfa Deep pipeline via Ostentati.soul — shared founder. Denebula wealth fuels Panemorfa operations Fimbul BYOS Trade partner — Torben Y operates within Denebula's economic sphere Ophek Aspirant — Ophek Revel seeks Merchant Prince status Pergamos Shadow Banks Complicated — some Princes use Shadow Bank services; others compete. Both wary of the MUD Synod's advance Relic Barons Trade partners — Denebula's markets are the primary distribution channel for relic goods from Harkend ONI Consortium Formal tax relationship — ONI provides protection and trade network access in exchange for substantial revenue COP Nominal jurisdiction — COP monitors but cannot effectively challenge Denebula's internal practices BMAH Indirect — Panemorfa's primary acquisition channel for art and cultural treasures Gate Garrison Indirect — HRZ smuggling pipelines have targeted Denebula black markets (intercepted by Garrison-Xianyang joint operation)

docs.title: Species
docs.text:
• Punaab — Ayevat's High Punaab origin; Denebula caste system modeled on Punaab court protocol
  • Ustur — Ostentati.soul's Path of Enlightenment (.soul suffix denotes highest tier)
  • Photoli — Gel's financial aristocracy; unusual Photoli interest in money
  • Sogmian — Yacob (House Exinade); Du'prah honor code influences etiquette system
  • Mierese — Mevertrase; ONI storyteller culture shapes narrative control

docs.title: Factions
docs.text:
• Merchant Princes of Denebula — Governing faction (detailed deep dive with individual Prince profiles)
  • Panemorfa — Ostentati.soul's faction; beauty ideology pipeline
  • Pergamos Shadow Banks — Financial services; House Venn identity engineering for Panemorfa
  • Relic Barons — Harkend artifact trade flows through Denebula markets
  • Fimbul BYOS — Torben Y's commercial presence
  • BMAH — Panemorfa's acquisition channel
  • Gate Garrison — Anti-smuggling operations targeting Denebula black markets

docs.title: Meta
docs.text:
• Master Timeline — Panemorfa founding (~2470), Denebula founding (~2471/~2495), Gel's death (~2511), Albiron's rise (~2526)
  • Named Characters — 6 Merchant Princes + Torben Y + Ophek Revel
  • Galactic Atlas — MRZ cross-species finance kingdom
  • Faction Power Classification — T2 classification (single-sector dominance)
