docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: DREAM REACH (MRZ-35)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — MRZ Ustur sector population Zone: Medium Risk Zone (MRZ-35) Influence: Ustur (dominant heritage) Theme: Celestial Phenomenon, Medicinal Flora, Spiritual Sanctuary, Retirement Haven

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Dream Reach derives its name from a rare celestial event. When the cosmic currents and stars within align just right, they birth the "Ephemeral Nebula" — a breathtaking, dreamy panorama that graces the sector for a mere day.

docs.title: The Ephemeral Nebula
docs.text:
• A recurring celestial event caused by specific alignment of cosmic currents and local stars
  • Occurs every six months
  • Creates a breathtaking, dreamy panorama visible across the sector
  • Lasts for only one day each occurrence
  • Woven in mystique and legend — draws visitors from all corners of the galaxy

docs.title: The Legend
docs.text:
Legends whisper that any soul bathed in the nebula's luminance will see their deepest dreams come to life.

Canon clarification: The nebula has real properties that induce visions. This is not pure folklore — there is a physiological/cosmic basis for the dream-like experiences reported by those who bathe in its luminance.

docs.title: Visitors
docs.text:
The spectacle draws diverse crowds:

  • Curious tourists — spectacle-seekers
  • Inspired artists — creative pilgrimage
  • Spiritual pilgrims — seeking enlightenment or divine contact
  • Those clinging to hope — believers in the dream-granting legend

docs.title: Tension with Locals
docs.text:
• The surge of visitors during the Ephemeral Nebula escalates tensions with the local populace
  • Natives of dominant Ustur heritage must balance outsider challenges with the prosperity visitors bring

docs.title: Medicinal & Spiritual Heritage
docs.text:
• History deeply rooted in the exploration of medicinal flora
  • Understanding of the sentient psyche — psychology and mental health traditions
  • Providing respite for the travel-worn — a sector built around healing and rest

docs.title: Retirement Haven
docs.text:
• Many veteran space captains, with tales of Galia's vastness etched in their hearts, choose Dream Reach as their final anchorage
  • A haven for adventurers seeking both stories of yore and a chance to rejuvenate
  • The sector is essentially Galia's retirement community for seasoned explorers

docs.title: Narrative Hooks
docs.text:
• Does the legend work?: Has anyone's dream actually come true after the Ephemeral Nebula? Or is it pure folklore?
  • Medicinal flora trade: Dream Reach's pharmacological knowledge could connect to Ingbus's research (Ka-dara) or Summer Sea's unique vegetation
  • Retired captains = living SDUs: Veterans who chose to settle here carry Rank B-S level knowledge from their travels
  • Festival economy: The one-day Nebula event likely drives an entire seasonal economy — preparations, tourism, post-event trade

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Summer Sea — canon/geography/sectors/summer_sea.md (medicinal flora connection possible)
  • Ka-dara — canon/geography/sectors/ka_dara.md (Ingbus seeks exotic biological materials)
  • SDU System — canon/systems/sdu_classification.md (retired captains as knowledge sources)

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ✅ ~~Q-DRM1: How frequent is the Ephemeral Nebula?~~ → Every six months, lasts one day.
  • ✅ ~~Q-DRM2: Medicinal flora specifics?~~ → Yes, many exist. To be explored in a future deep dive.
  • ⏸️ Q-DRM3: Is there a formal institution (hospital, retreat center, academy) for the psyche/healing traditions?

docs.title: ⚔️ Canon Document: EOL-GARADAR (MRZ-23)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — MRZ Ustur sector population Zone: Medium Risk Zone (MRZ-23) Influence: Ustur (but independently governed) Theme: Noble Heroes, Champions of Liberty, Anti-Slavery, Universal Rights

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Eol-Garadar stands as a shining beacon, renowned as the home of noble heroes and champions of liberty.

In a galaxy where many sectors yielded to avarice, subjugation, and turmoil, Eol-Garadar emerged as a stalwart defender of hope and order — even outside the welfare of the Council of Peace — in the aftermath of the Convergence War.

[!NOTE] COP Jurisdiction Canon: Eol-Garadar is not rebelling against the COP nor deliberately neutral — the COP's jurisdiction simply does not function in the MRZ. Eol-Garadar fills the justice vacuum by doing the best they can on their own terms.

docs.title: Universal Rights
docs.text:
Eol-Garadar embraces all, irrespective of past allegiances or political leanings. Its only demand is adherence to foundational laws framed as:

Essential rights intrinsic to all sentient beings — transcending cultural, temporal, or political divisions.

docs.title: The Tenets
docs.text:
• Freedom of expression
  • Poetic justice — not rigid legalism
  • Disdain for rigid formality
  • Strong belief in individual responsibility

Alignment: Chaotic Good. Eol-Garadar follows justice from the heart, not from written code. Think D&D Chaotic Good — they do what is right even when it breaks convention.

docs.title: Military Doctrine: The DAC
docs.text:
Eol-Garadar's military operates as a Decentralized Autonomous Corporation (DAC) — a decentralized army whose members are voted and elected through governance wars.

With unwavering commitment, the DAC dispatches its valorous legions against slavers and other malevolent forces — not for riches or glory, but in pursuit of the greater good.

This altruistic ethos magnetizes countless souls from diverse origins, each aspiring to contribute energies and resources to a shared noble purpose.

[!IMPORTANT] The DAC structure means Eol-Garadar's military leadership is not inherited or appointed — it is earned through governance participation. This is decentralized warfare with democratic legitimacy.

docs.title: Political Standing
docs.text:
• Their principled stand has drawn the ire of numerous adversaries across the ideological landscape
  • The unyielding spirit of Eol-Garadar has, thus far, repelled any threats
  • A populace deeply trusting of its leadership, and a leadership utterly devoted to its people
  • This makes Eol-Garadar an indomitable presence on the political stage of Galia

docs.title: Liberation Origin (~2523)
docs.text:
• ~2523: Eolus and the Garadar DAC discover a sector under the oppressive rule of the Pavo Royal Family
  • Following the death of the Pavo Royal Family during The Night (~2522), the sector's oppressive apparatus begins to fracture
  • After a long conflict against the remaining loyalists, Eolus and the DAC join forces with the civilian population
  • The civilians unite with the Garadar DAC, and the sector becomes Eol-Garadar — the first DAC-owned sector in Galia Expanse

[!NOTE] Eol-Garadar doesn't reject the COP — the COP simply cannot reach the MRZ. Eol-Garadar fills the void with its own brand of justice.

docs.title: Narrative Hooks
docs.text:
• Anti-COP parallels: They do what COP claims to do, but independently — does this create friction or admiration?
  • Magnet for refugees: A sector that takes anyone regardless of past — what tensions does that create?
  • "Poetic justice": A legal system that values spirit-of-the-law over letter-of-the-law — how does that work in practice?
  • Enemy magnetism: Slavers, authoritarians, and exploiters all hate them — a sector constantly at war with evil

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Garadar DAC — canon/factions/garadar_dac.md (the DAC that governs and defends the sector — full deep-dive)
  • Council of Peace — canon/institutions/council_of_peace.md (Eol-Garadar operates outside COP jurisdiction)
  • Crumon Dynasty — canon/factions/crumon_dynasty.md (former Crumon oppressor, pre-liberation)
  • Lumiro Grove — canon/factions/lumiro_grove.md (ECOS terraformers who rescued the sector during the Moon Shattering)
  • Ustur — canon/species/ustur.md (MRZ Ustur-influenced space, Zagah's origin)
  • Frenir New Government — canon/factions/frenir_new_government.md (DAC patrols Frenir borders)
  • Slavers of Frenir — canon/factions/slavers_of_frenir.md (primary enemy target)

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ✅ ~~Q-EOL1: Who leads Eol-Garadar?~~ → Grand Master Zagah (Ustur Elder) leads via DAC governance. See garadar_dac.md
  • ✅ ~~Q-EOL2: Species composition?~~ → Truly multi-species: Ustur monks, MUD settlers, Sogmian laborers, Mierese scouts, Punaab traders
  • ✅ ~~Q-EOL3: Military structure?~~ → Legions (500-2,000 multi-species), Gravity Patrols (20-50), Scout Wings, Matel Guard. See garadar_dac.md
  • ⏸️ Q-EOL4: Relationship with Chior.eldr / Zenith Door? (Both seek peace but through different means — Chior via diplomacy, Zagah via action)
  • ✅ ~~Q-EOL5: Specific slaver factions?~~ → Slavers of Frenir (remnants), Jorvik criminal networks. Active border patrols in Frenir (~2624+)

docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: ETERNITY SECTOR (Ustur 1)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Consolidated, Feb 2026) Source: Creator Input (Eternity Lore Doc + Archon Audit) Type: Safe Zone Sector (Ustur 1 — Ustur Birthworld)

docs.title: System Overview
docs.text:
• Location: Ustur Safe-Zone, top right corner of Galia Expanse
  • Star: Typical main sequence yellow star
  • Age: ~1 billion years (comparatively young)
  • Anomaly: System appears artificially organized — every celestial body seems deliberately placed. So orderly it cannot be random.

docs.title: Origin (SECRET — unknown to Ustur)
docs.text:
• Created by the Ancient AI that originated the Ustur thousands of years ago.
  • Designed as a backup plan in case the AI's transcendence failed (it did).
  • Everything modeled to allow Ustur civilization to flourish and eventually "revive" the AI.
  • Something went wrong — Ustur don't know about this plan.

docs.title: Ioki (Homeworld)
docs.text:
• Size: Small — ⅓ of Earth
  • Type: Terrestrial with breathable atmosphere
  • Significance: Heart of Ustur species, political/religious center, location of the Reincarnation Pool.
  • Hieroglyphs: Cover structures, indecipherable even to Elders. Assumed to be written by the Ancient AI before its fragmentation.
  • Subterranean Chambers: Criss-cross beneath Ioki's crust, culminating at the Pool.
  • Sacred Status: Open to public, but industry is limited to preserve sanctity.
  • Every Ustur will fight to their final death to protect Ioki.

docs.title: Ioki Ecosystems
docs.text:
Ecosystem Description Salar Deserts Largest biome. Salt/lithium sand (white dry, blue/green oases). Mirror-like flood surfaces. Cities near oases. Mangrove Forests Trees use surface salts for oxygen. Greenish transitional environments. Rich fauna. Surround Elder Spire. Oceans Proportionally smaller than Earth's. Pinkish color (high salt). Depths contain ruins/dungeons linked to the AI.
  • Three Seasons: Dry, Wet, Short Spring (max wildlife)
  • Hazards: Saltwater rains, occasional earthquakes, naturally radioactive areas in deepest deserts

docs.title: Ioki Places of Interest
docs.text: Location Description Uvana (Capital) Three levels: New City (surface, cyberpunk), Old City (underground metropolis), The Temple (deep, sacred) The Temple Most sacred. Metal maze of alien origin. Reincarnation Pool at lowest level. Guarded by Order of Those Who Wait. "The Residents" (mechanical life forms) tend it. Elder Spire Spiritual/political heart. Luxurious tower with light effects (sci-fi glass mosque). Access restricted by enlightenment rank. Broken City Haven for failed Path of Enlightenment Ustur. Maintained by volunteers + Sogmian House Outro. Opal Stream Built by Opal Industries. Doers Harbor (principal port) + orbital Starpath gate infrastructure. Vzus Defensive Belt Star fortresses around Ioki. Central command on Shariput (moon).

docs.title: Celestial Bodies
docs.text: Body Details Shariput Moon of Ioki (⅓ Earth's moon). Barren. Main fortress of Vzus Belt. Mineral mines. Vestigios Planet with asteroid belt and moon. (Details TBD)

docs.title: Season 0 (Tufa Invasion of Ioki)
docs.text:
• Attacker: Chalbok Brood (see canon/species/tufa.md)
  • Objective: Seize the Reincarnation Pool (Chalbok sensed residual energy)
  • Key Events:
  • Vzus Belt sabotaged by mercenary infiltrators → Tufa landed
  • Opal Stream shut down → Ioki cut off from Galia
  • Elder Spire under siege (force field holds)
  • Broken City hit by Tufa meteorite (Broken Ustur especially susceptible)
  • ECOS Balifa Grove supports Tufa (believe invasion is Iris's rightful rebalancing)
  • Jorvik Raiders opportunistically plunder defenseless areas

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Ancient AI Origin: canon/species/ustur.md
  • Tufa Invasion: canon/species/tufa.md
  • VZUS: canon/manufacturers/vzus.md
  • Opal: canon/manufacturers/opal.md

docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: EVERNAT SECTOR (ONI 3)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (User Input, Feb 2026) Type: Safe Zone Sector (ONI 3 — Punaab Homeland)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Deep within ONI space lies Evernat, a sector that whispers tales of ancient origins. Believed by many scholars to predate even some regions within the High-Risk Zone, Evernat is a treasure trove of historical wonders.

docs.title: Akenat — The Cradle World
docs.text:
• The Crown Jewel: Akenat is the birthworld of the Punaabs.
  • Landscape: Towering plateaus supported by enormous clamshells.
  • Spore Caves: Labyrinthine underworld, vital to Akenat's balanced ecosystem.
  • Sea of Mist: Ever-present, ever-shifting protective shroud.
  • Origin (~2100): An alien traveler transported TED — the creature that would become the God of Mist — to planet Akenat. TED formed the Sea of Mist.
  • Life in Evernat began after TED's arrival.

docs.title: The Living Myth (God of Mist)
docs.text:
• A deity believed as ancient as Akenat itself.
  • In the Punaabs' darkest hours, this immortal entity rises to guide and shield them.
  • Archon Secret: Actually a Colossal HRZ Creature adapted to Akenat (see canon/species/punaab.md).
  • Archon Inference: Since Evernat predates the HRZ, the God of Mist was already on Akenat before zone boundaries were drawn. It is a living fossil from an era when all of Galia was dangerous.

docs.title: The High & Profound Divide
docs.text:
• The rift between High Punaabs (innovation) and Profound Punaabs (tradition/resilience) remains palpable.
  • The City in the Middle: The unified metropolis in the heart of Akenat, born from the fusion of both clans. Represents hope for a newer, united generation.

docs.title: Ogrika Harbors
docs.text:
• Headquarters: Located in The City in the Middle.
  • Reputation: Renowned for exceptional ship-crafting that blends style with function.
  • Scope: Builds everything — luxury vessels, commercial ships, and warships alike.
  • Symbolism: Embodies the dreams of two clans uniting under a single vision.

docs.title: Systems & Locations (To be populated)
docs.text: • [System Name]

docs.title: ⚡ Canon Document: EVERSTORM (MRZ-25)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — Everstorm lore document + Archon Deep-Dive Zone: Medium Risk Zone (MRZ-25) Influence: ONI Theme: Iris Influence, Perpetual Storms, Engineering Frontier, Democratic Oasis

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Everstorm stands as a testament to the profound power of Galia's galactic ecosystem. The sector is drenched in cosmic currents emanating from Iris, inciting its local star into a tumultuous dance of relentless solar flares and coronal mass ejections. The legacy of distant Iris will forever shape its destiny.

docs.title: Iris-Driven Phenomenon
docs.text:
• Cosmic currents from Iris agitate the sector's star into constant instability
  • Relentless solar flares and coronal mass ejections bombard the sector
  • Planets develop robust magnetospheres as a natural defense
  • Result: every habitable world is cloaked in perpetual electrifying storms
  • The Iris currents are in constant motion throughout the system
  • Without proper knowledge and technology, flying here is extremely dangerous

docs.title: Atmospheric Bounty
docs.text:
• The atmospheric upheaval creates environments abundant in rare Galia resources
  • These resources form the economic foundation of the sector
  • Extractable only by those who can survive — or harness — the storms
  • (Specific resources TBD — Creator will detail later)

docs.title: Origin
docs.text:
• ~2487: Fimbul Engineers from Free Harbors discover the unique sector Everstorm in MRZ-25
  • Upon seeing the non-stop electrical storms, they decide on researching ways of harnessing the potential endless energy of the sector
  • Fimbul = neutral manufacturer, native to the MRZ (not faction-aligned, founded ~2471 in Free Harbors)
  • Driven by the potential concealed within the chaos

docs.title: Technology
docs.text:
• ~2504: After many attempts, Fimbul engineers finally develop ionic shields capable of withstanding the tempest of Everstorm — the foundational breakthrough enabling permanent habitation
  • ~2509: Fimbul Engineers create the Sky Fortress — the first and largest Nimbus Sphere, a floating city sustained by the force of Everstorm's tempest. However, the sector stops receiving support from Free Harbors due to rising tensions that will lead to the Convergence War
  • ~2513: The Sky Fortress manages to survive the Convergence War in relative peace, shielded by the sector's tempests. They begin exporting energy cells to Free Harbors as a means of sustenance
  • Created the Nimbus Spheres — their ingenious solution to survival (built upon the ionic shield technology)
  • 9 city-sized Nimbus Spheres spread throughout the sector (the Sky Fortress being the first and largest)
  • Serve as bastions against the storm's fury
  • Ingeniously harness the storm's energy — transforming adversity into sustainability
  • Protective bubble-cities that shield settlements while generating power from the storms

docs.title: The Democratic Oasis (Functional)
docs.text:
• The Fimbul engineers' ambition: a democratic oasis within the spheres
  • A sanctuary untouched by external forces and influences
  • Already functioning in the current canon timeline
  • 9 self-governing city-spheres — a confederation of storm-sheltered democracies

docs.title: The New Threat (~2624)
docs.text:
• ~2624: New strange and hostile life forms start appearing in Everstorm
  • The local government begins hiring captains and crews to dispose of them
  • These creatures are a species brought by the Dark Photoli to the MRZ as an experiment, and let loose in Everstorm

docs.title: Navigation Hazard
docs.text:
[!WARNING] The Iris currents are in constant motion. Without specialized knowledge and technology, flying in Everstorm is extremely risky.

  • The storms and Iris currents shift unpredictably
  • Only experienced pilots with storm-navigation tech can traverse the sector safely
  • Nimbus Spheres serve as safe harbors between dangerous transit routes
  • A major contributor to the MRZ classification

docs.title: Narrative Hooks
docs.text:
• Iris's Reach: Direct proof that Iris influences distant sectors — how far does it extend?
  • 9 Bubble Cities: Each sphere could have its own character, culture, governance style
  • Democratic Confederation: Organic democracy (contrast with Frenir's COP-imposed DAO)
  • Storm Navigation: Elite pilots who master Everstorm become legendary
  • Fimbul's Independence: A neutral MRZ manufacturer creating something beyond commerce

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Iris influence — canon/geography/the_cataclysm.md
  • Fimbul Industries — canon/factions/fimbul_industries.md (founded ~2471, ionic shields, Nimbus Spheres, Sky Fortress builder)
  • Duskbloom Grove — canon/factions/duskbloom_grove.md (ECOS anomaly containment grove — hunts Everstorm creatures)
  • Democratic experiments — parallel to Frenir's DAO (canon/geography/sectors/frenir.md)
  • Navigation hazards — comparable to Perpetual Belt (canon/geography/sectors/perpetual_belt.md)

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ⏸️ Q-EVE1: Names of the 9 Nimbus Spheres?
  • ⏸️ Q-EVE2: What rare resources does Everstorm produce? (Deferred — Creator will detail)
  • ⏸️ Q-EVE3: Is the Iris influence growing or stable?
  • ⏸️ Q-EVE4: Fimbul's fuller history as a neutral MRZ manufacturer

docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: EXODUS SECTOR (MUD Capital)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (User Input, Feb 2026) Type: Safe Zone Sector (MUD 1)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
"It's time humanity frees itself from the shackles of its cradle. Your fate lies elsewhere, deep among the stars. All you need to do is extend your hand and seize your destiny.” — The Revelation.

Exodus stands as the glittering testament to God’s promise to humankind. It is the stalwart backbone of the MUD influence within the Galia Expanse, housing the heart and soul of the MUD population and its governing institutions.

docs.title: The Mothership Serene
docs.text:
• Status: The monumental vessel that carried humanity from Earth to Galia.
  • Location: Permanently docked/stationed in the Exodus system.
  • Inhabitants: Serves as the residence for the MUD Elite, the Government, and the Holy Family.

  • Symbolism: The literal and metaphorical heart of the empire.

  • The Philosophy: Despite being a sanctuary, the name "Exodus" serves as an omnipresent reminder that the journey is not yet fulfilled.

  • The Gaze: MUD eyes are always turned outward, beyond the stars, seeking the true "Paradise" (The Cataclysm).

docs.title: Dominant Power: The Gotti Family + The Senate
docs.text:
• The Gotti Family rules from the Mothership Serene. Their word is divine law.
  • The Senate operates from Exodus as the formal seat of government, but decades of Gotti centralization have left it weakened and largely ceremonial.
  • All factions maintain embassies and representatives in Exodus, but none hold dominant influence beyond the Holy Family.
  • Tension: The Senate pleads for restraint and treaties; the Gotti family pursues destiny.

docs.title: Systems & Locations (To be populated)
docs.text:
• [System Name]
  • [System Name]

docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: FOY FIELDS SECTOR (Ustur 5)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (User Input + Archon Audit, Feb 2026) Type: Safe Zone Sector (Ustur 5 — Economic Nexus / VZUS HQ)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The planets of Foy Fields are uniquely imbued with natural electric fields that resonate harmoniously with Ustur physiology — comparable to the relaxation a MUD feels in a thermal pool.

docs.title: The Therapeutic Effect
docs.text:
• Natural electric fields provide rejuvenation for overworked androids.
  • Prime destination for Ustur seeking relief from tiresome daily endeavors.

docs.title: 👁️ Archon Secret: The AI Fragment
docs.text:
• The Truth: Foy Fields contains a piece of the original Ancient AI that created the Ustur.
  • Scale: The fragment is the size of a continent on the planet.
  • Status: It generates the electric fields. Nobody has discovered this yet — the fragment is so large it is mistaken for natural geology.

docs.title: VZUS Relocation
docs.text:
• VZUS (leading Ustur manufacturer, ships + technology) relocated headquarters from Eternity to Foy Fields.
  • Reason: The electric fields dramatically boosted production efficiency. Eternity/Ioki deemed too sacred for heavy industry.
  • Result: Other enterprises followed suit, creating a trend.

docs.title: Economic Hub
docs.text:
• Strategic location: Near the Medium Risk Zone systems.
  • Combined therapeutic effect + strategic position = primary economic nexus for the Ustur Faction.

docs.title: Systems & Locations
docs.text:
• [VZUS HQ world]
  • [Industrial colonies]

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:

  • Ancient AI: canon/species/ustur.md (Origin Secret)
  • Eternity: canon/geography/sectors/eternity.md

docs.title: 🏴 Canon Document: THE FREE HARBORS (MRZ-26)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — Free Harbors lore document + Archon Deep-Dive Zone: Medium Risk Zone (MRZ-26) Influence: ONI (geographic) / Multi-Faction (political reality) Theme: Dock-DAO Governance, Neutrality Under Strain, Powder Keg, Shadow Politics

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Free Harbors stand as the informal heart of the medium zone, embodying the intricate dance of light and shadow. Originally discovered by Mierese travelers (hence the geographic ONI classification), it rapidly became a neutral bastion during the Convergence War. Now it simmers as though on the brink of cataclysmic eruption.

docs.title: Discovery & Founding
docs.text:
• Initially discovered by Mierese travelers — geographically in ONI territory
  • ~2454: A group of nomadic Mierese traders starts recurrent gatherings in MRZ-26 to share goods, stories, and services — they create a small settlement called Free Harbors
  • During the Convergence War, key members from MUD, ONI, and Ustur pooled resources
  • Envisioned as a sanctuary from factional strife — yearning for existence free from war
  • Rapidly transcended its ONI origins to become a neutral bastion
  • ~2471: Fimbul Industries formed in Free Harbors by MUDs unsatisfied with humanity's imperialistic expansion
  • ~2475: Due to its strategic location, traders and merchant princes from all of Galia Expanse flock to Free Harbors — its commercial and productive nature makes it politic-agnostic, thriving on productivity and commerce
  • ~2481: Although under ONI geographic influence, Free Harbors becomes the first independent government in the MRZ
  • ~2511: As tensions rise across Galia Expanse, Free Harbors decentralizes its governance — each port becomes a self-sovereign institution in its own territory, limited by the sector's democratic rules in foreign policies
  • ~2522: Free Harbors is flooded by refugees from the Convergence War. Unemployment, criminality, and civil unrest rise sharply — the sanctuary's ideals are tested by the galaxy's displaced masses
  • ~2526: Free Harbors passes through a period of turbulence in their democracy — the different harbors fight for power and dominance. Other factions in Galia Expanse try to expand their influence in the sector

docs.title: The Rise
docs.text:
• Emerged as the galactic fulcrum of neutrality
  • Wielded unparalleled military might, wealth, and influence
  • A rare beacon of cross-faction cooperation

docs.title: Multi-System Structure
docs.text:
[!IMPORTANT] Free Harbors is not a single city — it's a multi-system sector with a central capital planet.

  • Main planet: The center of economic and political power, divided into neighborhoods
  • Multiple star systems across the sector, each controlled by a harbor (dock)
  • Each harbor is linked to a species:
  • MUD Harbor (Human)
  • Mierese Harbor
  • Punaab Harbor
  • Sogmian Harbor
  • Ustur Harbor
  • (6th harbor TBD — Creator listed MUD twice, to clarify)
  • Each harbor controls its entire system — not just a neighborhood on the capital
  • On the main planet, each harbor also maintains a district/neighborhood representing its interests

docs.title: DAO Governance
docs.text:
• Free Harbors operates as a DAO formed by the different Harbors
  • Decisions affecting the entire collective are voted through the DAO
  • A tacit non-interference agreement exists: harbors do not meddle in each other's systems
  • Exception: a harbor breaking general rules triggers collective intervention
  • Free Harbors functions as a faction of Galia — a recognized political entity (details TBD)

docs.title: COP Jurisdiction
docs.text:
• Formally submits to the Council of Peace's jurisdiction
  • In practice: the COP never comes to Free Harbors
  • The region is economically and politically stable enough to self-govern
  • A de facto autonomous territory under nominal COP authority

docs.title: Ideals Erode
docs.text:
• As the Harbors grew, its shining vision began to tarnish
  • Factions of diverse motivations swarmed under its banner
  • The unity that defined the Harbors now hangs by a tenuous thread

docs.title: Shadow Players
docs.text:
• The Jorvik — one of many shadow players, not dominant but dangerous
  • Escaped Frenir warlords — some embedded within Jorvik networks here
  • Corporate interests from all three factions vying for influence
  • Rogue entities exploiting the autonomy and non-interference norms
  • The neutrality that once protected now enables corruption

docs.title: Current State: The Powder Keg
docs.text:
[!CAUTION] Political savants across Galia whisper: the genesis of the next great galactic upheaval might be the Free Harbors.

  • Concentration of diverse groups, wealth, and competing ambitions
  • Inter-dock conflicts and inter-species tensions escalate
  • A practically autonomous territory coveted by outside groups
  • No single stabilizing force — the DAO holds, but for how long?
  • The trigger remains undefined — an open narrative tension
  • ~2623: Redam reaches out to the Free Harbors (as an old MUD colony) to pressure the Council of Peace into accepting a truce in the MRZ — the Harbors are pulled into galactic diplomacy

docs.title: Narrative Hooks
docs.text:
• Dock Stories: Each dock could have its own identity, species mix, and internal drama
  • DAO Stress Test: Governance under pressure — what happens when a dock breaks the compact?
  • Espionage Capital: Every faction has operatives here — the galaxy's intelligence crossroads
  • The Spark: Whatever ignites the next war may start in a Free Harbors backroom
  • Original Founders: Do any Convergence War-era founders still hold influence?

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Convergence War — canon/history/convergence_war.md (founding context)
  • Jorvik — criminal faction, also in Frenir (canon/geography/sectors/frenir.md)
  • Council of Peace — canon/institutions/council_of_peace.md (nominal authority)
  • Mierese discovery — canon/species/mierese.md (original explorers)
  • DAO governance — parallel to Frenir (canon/geography/sectors/frenir.md) but organic, not COP-imposed

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ⏸️ Q-FRH1: How many docks/harbors exist? Named docks?
  • ⏸️ Q-FRH2: Named founding figures from the Convergence War era?
  • ⏸️ Q-FRH3: Which dock(s) are most associated with Jorvik activity?
  • ⏸️ Q-FRH4: What specific inter-dock disputes are currently simmering?

docs.title: ⚖️ Canon Document: FRENIR (MRZ-19)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — Frenir lore document + Archon Deep-Dive Zone: Medium Risk Zone (MRZ-19) Influence: ONI Theme: Post-Dictatorship Reconstruction, DAO Experiment, Humanitarian Aid, Lurking Threat Timeline: One of the most recent canon events

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Once notorious as the heart of dictatorship and the epicenter of the slave trade in the outer medium-risk zone, Frenir was first on the Council of Peace's list when it sought to restore harmony to its borders. Even the most hardened factions of the underworld viewed Frenir as a blight — its practices grotesquely outdated for the modern galactic era.

docs.title: Rise to Power
docs.text:
• Warlords gained power during the chaos of the Convergence War
  • Exploited the power vacuum in the outer MRZ while factions fought each other
  • Established a brutal dictatorship and slave trade
  • Enslaved beings of all species (except Tufa — see note below)
  • Became so disruptive that even other underworld factions considered them a blight
  • ~2533: The Frenir government collapses entirely and slavers formally take over the sector

docs.title: Ki'Prah — The Sogmian Prison Planet (~2358)
docs.text:
• Before the warlords' era, Sogmians established a prison planet called Ki'Prah in Frenir (MRZ-19)
  • Purpose: exile Oathbreakers — Sogmians who violated their Du Prah vows
  • ~2364: Punaabs reached an agreement with Sogmians and also began sending exiles and criminals to Ki'Prah
  • Ki'Prah represents the first documented Punaab-Sogmian cooperation
  • Status during the warlord era and COP intervention: unknown

[!NOTE] Tufa are considered a myth by populations in the MRZ and Safe Zones. You will NOT find Tufa on the civilized side of the galaxy. Their existence is known only to those who venture deep into HRZ territory.

docs.title: Vaor Skarca's Invasion
docs.text:
• Vaor Skarca (Council of Peace leader) used the warlords' excesses as the justification he needed
  • ~2623: Invaded the sector with COP troops in a swift, decisive operation
  • Practically erased the entrenched oppressive systems
  • Established interim governance to oversee the democratic transition

docs.title: Aftermath of the Warlords
docs.text:
• Most died during the COP military intervention
  • Survivors split into two groups:
  • Fled to Yuldun Waste (lawless sector)
  • Joined the Jorvik (criminal faction) — waiting for the opportunity to rebuild their empires

docs.title: The Balifa Grove Attack
docs.text:
"One day, a fortress. The next, vines wrapping around the towers like they were reclaiming old bones." — Commander Seeikolobe Mineefastu

Before the COP intervention, Frenir suffered one of the most devastating ecological terror attacks in galactic history at the hands of the Balifa Grove — the radical ECOS sub-faction led by Arch Druidess Andreza Liora:

  • The Balifa Grove targeted Frenir with a full-scale forced biogenesis campaign — seeding the planet with spores that ate through hull plating and turned cities into forests of death
  • 3,000+ civilians were killed as vegetation consumed urban infrastructure within days
  • ONI Commander Seeikolobe Mineefastu was stationed on Frenir during the attack — he witnessed the transformation firsthand: "Skin turning to moss, lungs choking on their own roots"
  • Andreza's message to Frenir's defenders was simple: "The planet lives, you do not."
  • Seeikolobe's retaliatory response was brutal — he burned three worlds to punish the Balifa Grove. The event deepened the cycle of violence between ONI and ECOS
  • The Balifa Grove later cited the attack as a success: "Even Frenir, once a scar of war, now breathes again beneath Iris' gaze" — Martuce Breeze
  • The orbital bombardment Seeikolobe conducted in retaliation is referenced as one of the most controversial ONI operations of the era

Canon Note: The Frenir attack is a defining trauma for the ONI Consortium, particularly for Seeikolobe, who carries it as the event that taught him "there's no diplomacy with rot. Only fire." His PTSD from this event drives the entire ONI campaign in Chapter 2.

docs.title: The DAO Experiment
docs.text:
• The COP aspires to implement Decentralized Autonomous Organizations (DAOs) throughout the galaxy
  • Frenir is the FIRST place the COP imposed a DAO governance model
  • The DAO runs on the Star Atlas blockchain infrastructure
  • The galaxy watches intently — if it works here, in the worst possible conditions, it could become the model for broader galactic reform
  • A bold experiment: replacing tyranny with decentralized democracy

docs.title: ONI Consortium (Direct Patron)
docs.text:
• The ONI Consortium has claimed Frenir as part of its sphere of influence and is directly supporting reconstruction with resources
  • ONI provides: funding, personnel, technical expertise, supply convoys, and political backing in the COP
  • ONI species (Mierese, Photoli, Punaab) dominate the freed population and the new Transition Council

docs.title: The Transition Council
docs.text:
• ~2624: A provisional transition government is formed — a five-seat Transition Council overseeing the DAO experiment
  • Chair Grokk Senna Valel (Mierese, freed slave), Ehr (Photoli, DAO architect), Commandant Nopeenfree (Punaab, security), Tolvak 𐎘 Outro (Sogmian, humanitarian), Plirk Dosk Scarka (Mierese, Hikibashi intelligence)
  • Full details in canon/factions/frenir_new_government.md

docs.title: Ki'Prah — From Prison to Factory
docs.text:
• ~2421: During the Sogmian Vega Civil War, all Sogmian troops abandon Ki'Prah — the prison world is left completely unguarded
  • ~2533: Slaver warlords seize the abandoned planet and convert it into a forced labor camp
  • ~2624: After liberation, the infrastructure is repurposed into a massive industrial factory — Frenir's single largest employer
  • Today Ki'Prah produces construction materials, ship components, and processed minerals for reconstruction
  • The factory generates Frenir's first independent revenue stream

docs.title: The Order of Patrah
docs.text:
• Patrah was a member of House Outro (Sogmian humanitarian house)
  • He died helping victims during the Convergence War
  • Became a symbol of a more united and empathetic Galia
  • The Sons of Patrah — the Order that bears his name — now provides frontline humanitarian aid to Frenir's freed population
  • Full details in canon/factions/sons_of_patrah.md

docs.title: House Outro Emissaries
docs.text:
• Sogmian humanitarian house emissaries led by Tolvak 𐎘 Outro coordinate the physical reconstruction
  • Fulfilling their Du Prah vow: "One who does not live to serve does not serve to live"
  • Providing resources, infrastructure, Ki'Prah factory conversion, and governance expertise

docs.title: Garadar DAC (Border Security)
docs.text:
• Garadar DAC dispatches legions to patrol Frenir's borders against slaver resurgence
  • Intercepts slaver scout ships and smuggling routes from Yuldun Waste
  • Provides combat training advisors to the new militia

docs.title: Galia Medical Union (Trauma & Healthcare)
docs.text:
• GMU's largest active deployment — field hospitals, trauma programs, and the only mental health services in the sector
  • Treating PTSD, learned helplessness, and the deep cultural scars of generations of slavery

docs.title: Current State
docs.text:
• Reconstruction efforts dominate the landscape
  • Long-oppressed inhabitants receiving aid from a multi-faction coalition
  • Democratic institutions forming under interim DAO governance
  • Ki'Prah factory providing economic self-sufficiency
  • The COP garrison is drawing down — independence is approaching

docs.title: The Lurking Threat
docs.text:
[!WARNING] History has taught that power vacuums are fleeting.

  • Surviving warlords in Yuldun Waste are biding their time
  • Others have embedded themselves within the Jorvik crime network
  • Whispers of an imminent return from the fringes of the Galia Expanse
  • The DAO must establish itself before the old powers regroup

docs.title: Narrative Hooks
docs.text:
• Democracy vs. Tyranny: DAO experiment as a race against time before the old masters return
  • Vaor Skarca's Gambit: The COP leader who used the warlords as justification — purely altruistic, or political maneuvering?
  • Patrah's Legacy: A dead Sogmian humanitarian becoming the symbol of hope for an entire sector
  • Jorvik Alliance: Former slave masters joining pirate networks — escalation risk
  • Yuldun Waste: A lawless refuge harboring exiled tyrants — future conflict zone

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• House Outro — canon/species/sogmian.md (Humanitarian, Snow White)
  • Frenir New Government — canon/factions/frenir_new_government.md (Transition Council, DAO experiment, full details)
  • ONI Consortium — canon/factions/oni.md (direct patron, resource provider)
  • Sons of Patrah — canon/factions/sons_of_patrah.md (frontline humanitarian aid)
  • Garadar DAC — canon/factions/garadar_dac.md (border patrols against slaver resurgence)
  • Galia Medical Union — canon/factions/galia_medical_union.md (trauma and healthcare)
  • Vaor Skarca — canon/institutions/council_of_peace.md (COP leadership)
  • Jorvik — canon/factions/jorvik.md (criminal faction, some elements harbor former slavers)
  • Yuldun Waste — lawless sector (dedicated file TBD)
  • Star Atlas DAO — canon/institutions/star_atlas_blockchain.md
  • Convergence War — canon/history/convergence_war.md
  • Tufa myth status — canon/species/tufa.md
  • Balifa Grove — canon/factions/balifa_grove.md (ecological terror attack on Frenir)
  • Commander Seeikolobe Mineefastu — ONI veteran stationed during Balifa attack
  • Chapter 2 Holosim — canon/history/chapter2_holosim_balifa_campaign.md (Frenir trauma drives ONI campaign)

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ✅ ~~Q-FRE1: Full backstory of Patrah~~ → Documented in sons_of_patrah.md
  • ✅ ~~Q-FRE2: Order of Patrah structure~~ → Documented in sons_of_patrah.md (Hearth System, three branches)
  • ⏸️ Q-FRE3: Where exactly is Yuldun Waste? MRZ? HRZ?
  • ⏸️ Q-FRE4: Vaor Skarca — species, background, political motives
  • ⏸️ Q-FRE5: Named warlords who escaped?

docs.title: ❄️ Canon Document: FROSTSUN (MRZ-30)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — Frostsun lore document + Archon Deep-Dive Zone: Medium Risk Zone (MRZ-30) Influence: ONI (Punaab territory — formally an Akenat colony) Theme: Dying Farmed Star, Underground Civilization, Native Conflict, Lost Scouts

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Frostsun is a sector locked in an eternal wintry embrace. Its star was harvested by an ancient civilization using crystal energy amplifiers — that civilization is gone, and the star is now in its final centuries. Below the frozen surface, Profound Punaabs have carved thriving underground metropolises, but they are not alone in the depths.

docs.title: Ancient Civilization
docs.text:
• An ancient, now-destroyed civilization "farmed" the sector's primary star
  • Used a formation of crystals to amplify the star's energy output
  • The civilization was destroyed — their identity lost to time
  • The star, already consumed to a great extent, is now in its last centuries
  • The crystal formations remain, giving the dying star the appearance of a golden-white orb cocooned in shimmering crystal

docs.title: Environmental Impact
docs.text:
• The star's decay has cast a chilly pall over all planets
  • Once-thriving ecosystems reduced to frozen landscapes and desolate wastelands
  • Surface life is no longer viable on the main sector planets
  • Only subterranean life is possible, sustained by planetary core heat

docs.title: Colony Status
docs.text:
• Formally a colony of Akenat — the Punaab homeworld
  • Loyal to the ONI Consortium, but if forced to choose, would follow Akenat
  • The preeminent Punaab enclave outside their native Evernat

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
• Follows the Profound Punaab aesthetic: structures of gold, bronze, and red
  • But the ecosystem is very different from Akenat — no fungal forests or geysers
  • Underground cities sustained by planetary core heat
  • Thriving metropolises beneath the ice

docs.title: Economy
docs.text:
• Produces and exports vast quantities of food and minerals to Akenat
  • Critical supply chain for the Punaab homeworld
  • Underground agriculture and mining operations

docs.title: The Native Conflict
docs.text:
[!IMPORTANT] 📌 TO CREATE LATER: Tribes of semi-sentient creatures inhabit the underground alongside the Punaabs.

  • The subterranean realms are contested territory
  • Semi-sentient native creatures view the Punaabs as invaders
  • Ongoing territorial disputes between settlers and indigenous tribes
  • The natives were here first — the ethical dimension adds complexity
  • (Dedicated faction/creature files TBD — Creator will develop)

docs.title: The Lost Scouts & Lt. Wenn Saga
The Mission
docs.text:
• During the Convergence War, a brave cadre from Frostsun ventured into the HRZ
  • Became scouts for ONI — mapping the High-Risk Zone
  • ~2518: Frostsun sends reinforcements to support the ONI faction in the HRZ war. After a successful early campaign, they managed to colonize the rich world of Gerio in the HRZ

docs.title: The Silence
docs.text:
• ~2524: The Punaabs of Frostsun lose contact with their brethren in Gerio after the chaos that follows the factions' retreat from the HRZ in the aftermath of the Convergence War
  • Five decades without a word since
  • Fate unknown to the Punaabs of Frostsun

docs.title: The Vigil
docs.text:
• Active rescue operation with concrete plans — not just passive hope
  • They need more information to proceed
  • This information arrives via the Lt. Wenn Saga (storyline TBD)
  • The Punaabs amass resources and hone readiness, waiting for the intelligence they need

docs.title: Narrative Hooks
docs.text:
• Crystal-Farmed Star: Who was the ancient civilization? What else did they build? Are there other farmed stars?
  • Lt. Wenn Saga: The rescue mission — a major storyline connecting Frostsun to the HRZ
  • Native Rights: Punaabs as colonizers displacing indigenous semi-sentient species — moral complexity
  • Dying Star Clock: The star has centuries left — what happens to Frostsun when it dies completely?
  • Akenat Lifeline: If Frostsun falls, Akenat loses critical food/mineral supply

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Profound Punaabs — canon/species/punaab.md (burrowing, spiritual branch)
  • Akenat — Punaab homeworld (canon/geography/sectors/evernat.md)
  • Evernat — native Punaab sector (canon/geography/sectors/evernat.md)
  • Convergence War — canon/history/convergence_war.md (scout mission context)
  • Ancient civilizations — parallel to Kamec's HRZ collapsed empire
  • Lt. Wenn Saga — storyline TBD (connects to Lost Scouts)

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ⏸️ Q-FRO1: Identity of the ancient star-farming civilization?
  • ⏸️ Q-FRO2: Lt. Wenn Saga — full storyline (deferred)
  • ⏸️ Q-FRO3: Semi-sentient native creatures — species, culture, abilities (deferred — TO CREATE)
  • ⏸️ Q-FRO4: How many underground cities exist in Frostsun?
  • ⏸️ Q-FRO5: What happens when the star finally dies? Evacuation plan?

docs.title: ✨ Canon Document: GLOWHAVEN (MRZ-31)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — Glowhaven lore document + Archon Deep-Dive + Onato Dream Arc Zone: Medium Risk Zone (MRZ-31) Influence: ONI (eastern border — Mierese sacred site) Theme: Mineral Bioluminescence, Mierese Prophecy, Onato's Prison, Iris Academy

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Glowhaven is far more than a luminous frontier. On the surface: a sector where mineral flora reacts to living electromagnetic fields, painting ethereal light shows. Beneath that beauty lies a sacred Mierese site — the planet their god Onato directed them to through shared dreams, where a mysterious artifact was found. The Iris Academy has established its research outpost Anchorage here to study what may be the key to one of Galia's greatest mysteries.

docs.title: Biology (Mineral-Based)
docs.text:
• The "flora" is mineral formations, not organic material
  • Possesses innate energy currents
  • Bursts into radiant illumination upon interaction with electromagnetic fields of living entities
  • Creates a landscape that responds to presence of sentient beings

docs.title: The Light Shows
docs.text:
• At night, horizons come alive in a mesmerizing dance of shimmering hues
  • Nocturnal creatures going about their night hunts create trails of living light
  • An extraordinary ecosystem painted in ethereal luminescence

docs.title: Causal Chain (Canon)
docs.text: [!NOTE] War agitated Iris → cosmic currents intensified → enabled Onato's signal (or what Mierese interpret as Onato's signal)

docs.title: Phase 1: The Shared Dreams (~2396)
docs.text:
• ~2396: Many Mierese begin dreaming of a beautiful landscape where flora is made of minerals that shine in the dark
  • A world filled with life and luminance, filled with dangers and discovery
  • The dreams are shared across the entire species — a collective vision
  • The Convergence War agitated Iris, which amplified cosmic currents across Galia
  • These intensified currents enabled the communication — whether from Onato or another source

docs.title: Phase 2: The Debate
docs.text:
• Mierese debate what the shared dream could mean
  • Some theorize that Onato is directing his children to a piece of the puzzle of his imprisonment
  • The religious significance electrifies Mierese communities galaxy-wide

[!WARNING] Ambiguity is intentional: There is NO certainty it was actually Onato. The Mierese are a species massively influenced by stories and ideas. The shared dream could be divine intervention — or collective mytho-psychic resonance. The canon deliberately does not confirm either interpretation.

docs.title: Phase 3: The Discovery
docs.text:
• Mierese discover MRZ-31, Glowhaven
  • Upon exploring the sector, they realize: this IS the place from their dreams
  • Mierese throughout the Galia Expanse start flocking to the sector

docs.title: Phase 4: The Intensification (~2513)
docs.text:
• ~2513: As the Convergence War rages, the dreams grow more intense
  • The Lore Keepers reunite in the sector to understand the visions collectively
  • Despite their combined wisdom: no answer found

docs.title: Phase 5: The Box (~2522)
docs.text:
[!IMPORTANT] A young female Mierese explorer finds a box made of unknown material in the heart of Glowhaven.

  • Upon discovery, the Mierese dreams finally cease
  • The Lore Keepers declare: this is part of the quest to free their god Onato
  • Celebrations erupt in Mierese settlements throughout the Galaxy
  • The box becomes the most important Mierese artifact in living memory
  • The box remains UNOPENED — no one has discovered how to open it
  • The next step of the mystery: finding clues to unlock the box

docs.title: The Explorer (Name TBD)
docs.text:
• Young female Mierese who found the box
  • Became famous overnight — despite not feeling like a heroine
  • Has been added to the Grand Tale of the Mierese (their living chronicle of sacred history)
  • Feels pressured by the enormous expectations her species places on her
  • (Character details and name to be created in dedicated session)

docs.title: The Iris Academy: Anchorage
docs.text:
• The Iris Academy creates Anchorage — a research outpost in Glowhaven
  • Purpose: decipher the curious artifact site the Mierese excavated
  • Located on one of the planets of the star system
  • Tension exists between the Iris Academy and the Lore Keepers (science vs. faith)
  • However, the Lore Keepers want help opening the box and are willing to compromise
  • A reluctant partnership: sacred custodians + secular researchers working toward the same goal
  • What the Academy finds could reshape understanding of Galia itself

docs.title: Frontier Activity
docs.text:
• Numerous ONI settlements dot the landscape, but vast areas remain untamed
  • Explorers, hunters, and miners drawn to the luminous wonder
  • Mining expeditions driven by curiosity and greed
  • The sector is a mix of sacred site, research hub, and wild frontier

docs.title: Narrative Hooks
docs.text:
• The Unopened Box: The galaxy's greatest locked puzzle — what's inside? How do you open it?
  • The Explorer's Burden: A young woman who never wanted fame, now immortalized in the Grand Tale
  • Faith vs. Fact: Did Onato really send the dreams, or is it collective imagination? The canon intentionally never answers
  • War → Iris → Dreams: The causal chain shows cosmic events have psychic consequences
  • Reluctant Alliance: Lore Keepers and scientists working together despite philosophical friction
  • Living Landscape: A world that glows when you walk through it — cinematically stunning

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Onato — Mierese god, imprisoned (canon/species/mierese.md)
  • Lore Keepers — Mierese religious order (canon/species/mierese.md)
  • Iris Academy — research institution, base at Anchorage (dedicated file TBD)
  • Mierese shared dreams — unique species-wide telepathic phenomenon
  • Convergence War — canon/history/convergence_war.md (timing of dreams)
  • Coral Nebula — parallel bio-reactive phenomenon (canon/geography/sectors/coral_nebula.md)

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ⏸️ Q-GLO1: How can the box be opened? Any clues discovered?
  • ⏸️ Q-GLO2: Name and character details of the female explorer (creator session TBD)
  • ⏸️ Q-GLO3: What has the Iris Academy discovered at Anchorage so far?
  • ⏸️ Q-GLO4: Is the mineral luminescence connected to Onato, Iris, or both? ("Talvez...")
  • ⏸️ Q-GLO5: Is the Iris Academy a broader institution or specific to Glowhaven?
  • ⏸️ Q-GLO6: Named nocturnal creatures unique to Glowhaven?

docs.title: 🌿 Canon Document: HANGING GARDENS (MRZ-16)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — Hanging Gardens lore document + Archon Deep-Dive Zone: Medium Risk Zone (MRZ-16) Influence: Ustur-aligned zone (ECOS settlement — survives because MUD can't reach them here) Theme: ECOS Sanctuary, Sentient Planets, Agricultural Power, COP Flashpoint

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Hanging Gardens is the largest ECOS settlement outside Earth-Prime and the primary ECOS presence in the MRZ. Emerging from the ashes of Old Grove, it became the sanctuary the ECOS always dreamed of. It survives because it's in Ustur space — far from MUD's reach. If MUD could, they would have exterminated ECOS long ago.

docs.title: Origin: From Old Grove's Ashes
docs.text:
• After the Old Grove catastrophe (terraforming gone wrong, genocide, mutant ecosystem), ECOS abandoned the sector
  • The primary ECOS fleet journeyed into the HRZ, founding Earth Prime
  • Satellite groves and support fleets remained behind in the MRZ
  • These remnants solidified ECOS presence via Hanging Gardens
  • This is the "High Garden" referenced in Old Grove lore — the correct name is Hanging Gardens

docs.title: Why Ustur Space?
docs.text:
• ECOS fled to this region specifically to be beyond MUD's reach
  • The only reason ECOS survived as a faction is their location in Ustur-influenced territory
  • MUD would have destroyed them if geography allowed
  • Ustur protection (whether intentional or passive) keeps ECOS alive

docs.title: The ECOS Belief
docs.text:
• ECOS believes that all planets are sentient beings
  • They claim to communicate with these planetary entities through daily rituals
  • The ceremonies seek guidance and blessings from the planets themselves
  • ECOS sees their role as defenders of these sentient worlds
  • ~2477: ECOS manages to refine their terraforming and communing technologies in Hanging Gardens — they reportedly succeed, for the first time, in communicating with a higher being (planet)

[!NOTE] This is spirituality without confirmation — there is no canon evidence that planets are actually sentient. Compare with Mierese/Onato (ambiguous) and Sogmian/Du Prah (proven cultural framework).

docs.title: The Grove Doctrine
docs.text:
• ~2493: With their stronghold in Hanging Gardens secured, ECOS establishes the Grove Doctrine
  • Under this doctrine, druidesses must leave Hanging Gardens and create their own "groves" in other sectors
  • Purpose: to spread the ECOS tenets, expand their influence, and protect galactic wildlife
  • This missionary/expansion mechanism transforms ECOS from a localized faction into a galaxy-spanning movement

docs.title: Economy: Agricultural Linchpin
docs.text:
• Agricultural prowess makes Hanging Gardens a linchpin for MRZ food distribution
  • Fierce competition with CARY — the civil branch of Calico (MUD manufacturer)
  • Both vie to expand influence through strategic trade and commerce
  • Whoever feeds the MRZ controls significant geopolitical leverage

docs.title: The Titan
docs.text:
• The Fimbul ECOS Superphoenix — a majestic ECOS titan ship
  • Built by Fimbul (neutral MRZ-native manufacturer from Everstorm)
  • Patrols the sector, poised and ready to defend ECOS territory

docs.title: Official Description
docs.text: This Fimbul masterpiece is the glistening ultimation of design for ferrying a small, flourishing nation of beings on a course through the sea of stars. Terrestrial comforts keep citizens grounded and healthy while on the Superphoenix's eternal cruise. Details small and large call attention to the symbiotic stewardship of nature with intentions to never lose sight of ECOS' shining ideals, even in the darkest recesses of space. This starship is truly an interstellar oasis and rejuvenating respite; it is often a welcome sight to perpetually space-bound travellers and explorers alike. Below decks the Superphoenix is as functional as its atriums are serene. There is no equal in preserving galactic balance.

docs.title: Key Traits
docs.text:
• Nation-carrier: Houses a small, flourishing civilization aboard
  • Terrestrial comforts: Designed to keep citizens grounded and healthy
  • Symbiotic stewardship: Every detail reflects ECOS' nature-first philosophy
  • Interstellar oasis: Welcome sight to space-bound travelers — a respite
  • Functional power: Below the serene atriums lies serious military capability
  • "No equal in preserving galactic balance" — a statement of ECOS ideology made metal

docs.title: COP Confrontation
docs.text:
[!WARNING] A confrontation between the Superphoenix and the COP armada could ignite another galactic war.

  • COP's recent forays into MRZ have created palpable tension
  • COP already invaded Pergamos (partly targeting Jorvik/ECOS influence)
  • If COP pushes into Hanging Gardens, the Superphoenix stands ready
  • This connects to Chior.eldr's opposition to COP aggression

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Old Grove — ECOS's failed Eden, precursor to Hanging Gardens (canon/geography/sectors/old_grove.md)
  • Earth Prime — ECOS true base in HRZ (TBD)
  • ECOS — human minor faction (canon/species/human.md)
  • Fimbul Industries — canon/factions/fimbul_industries.md (Superphoenix builder, neutral MRZ manufacturer)
  • CARY / Calico — MUD manufacturer civil branch (canon/manufacturers/calico.md)
  • COP vs ECOS — Pergamos invasion (canon/geography/sectors/pergamos.md)
  • Chior.eldr — opposes COP aggression (canon/geography/sectors/zenith_door.md)

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ✅ ~~Q-HNG1: CARY~~ → Civil branch of Calico (MUD manufacturer). Details TBD.
  • ✅ ~~Q-HNG4: High Garden~~ → IS Hanging Gardens (corrected in Old Grove)
  • ⏸️ Q-HNG2: Fimbul ECOS Superphoenix — capabilities, crew size?
  • ⏸️ Q-HNG3: ECOS political structure within Hanging Gardens — who governs?
  • ⏸️ Q-HNG4: Population and planet count?
  • ⏸️ Q-HNG5: CARY vs Hanging Gardens — specific trade routes/products?

docs.title: 🔧 Canon Document: HARKEND (MRZ-33)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — MRZ Ustur sector population Zone: Medium Risk Zone (MRZ-33) Influence: Ustur Theme: Artificial Star System, Mechanical Life Forms, Ustur Parallels, Relic Hunting

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Harkend was first explored by Ustur scouts around ~2442, who found only an unremarkable void. Then, at the close of the Convergence War, the sector transformed overnight into an enigma — from the barrenness, a star system arose, bringing with it colossal artificial structures rivaling moons in size, and at its core, an astounding metallic faux star that functions as a real sun, providing light and energy to the entire sector.

The galaxy watched, incredulous, as an entire star system — replete with machine-like celestial bodies teeming with mechanical life forms — materialized from the ether.

[!IMPORTANT] Closer inspection unveiled startling similarities between the constructs on these artificial moons and the physiology of the Ustur. The parallels were so striking that it was tempting to postulate a common origin. However, Harkend's mechanical life forms are entirely separate from the Ustur Temple's "Residents."

docs.title: Origin: The Living Factory
docs.text:
Harkend is a byproduct of The Living Factory — a powerful and dangerous High Risk Zone faction. Full details on The Living Factory are pending creator introduction.

[!WARNING] The connection between The Living Factory, Harkend, and the Ustur Ancient AI origin remains one of the deepest mysteries in Galia.

docs.title: The Enigma
docs.text:
The riddles of Harkend are manifold:

  • Who engineered this prodigious artificial constellation?
  • What are its origins? — Did it travel from somewhere, or was it constructed in place?
  • Could there be an undiscovered connection to the Ustur, or had these structures been cloaked in obscurity all along?
  • Why did it appear at the close of the Convergence War? — Coincidence, or triggered by the conflict?

These conundrums remain unresolved, but Harkend's allure only intensifies.

docs.title: The Relic Barons
docs.text:
The sector's unique properties gave birth to the Relic Barons — a group of thinkers and entrepreneurs, mostly of Ustur origins, that seek to collect and use the unique trinkets found in Harkend.

  • Investment: The Barons invest hefty amounts of ATLAS to propel the exploration and colonization of the system
  • Goal: Discovering new unique items that Harkend brought from the void
  • Composition: Primarily Ustur, but open to others drawn by the mystery
  • As new groundbreaking technological relics are unearthed, the Barons rejoice
  • ~2550: The Relic Barons discover a device that pacifies the automatons of Harkend within a certain range, allowing them to start permanent colonies in the sector to systematically explore and uncover its secrets

docs.title: Narrative Hooks
docs.text:
• The Living Factory: What is the connection between The Living Factory (HRZ) and Harkend's sudden appearance?
  • Ustur Origin Connection: The parallels between Harkend's constructs and Ustur physiology are too striking to ignore — does this connect to the Ancient AI origin truth?
  • Convergence War Trigger: Did the end of the war somehow awaken or summon Harkend? Was the Iris energy release a catalyst?
  • Relic Economy: The Relic Barons create a unique economic model — treasure hunting as venture capital
  • Mechanical Life: Are Harkend's mechanical life forms sentient? Do they react to Ustur presence?
  • Functional Faux Star: An artificial sun powering an entire sector — the energy implications are staggering

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Ustur — canon/species/ustur.md (physiological parallels, Relic Barons origin)
  • The Living Factory — canon/factions/living_factory.md (Harkend's origin, HRZ faction — pending)
  • Relic Barons — canon/factions/relic_barons.md (exploration/colonization faction)
  • Convergence War — appeared at the close of the war
  • Duskbloom Grove — canon/factions/duskbloom_grove.md (ECOS anomaly containment — hostile automaton containment beyond pacified zones)
  • ATLAS — investment currency for exploration

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ⏸️ Q-HAR1: What is the precise connection between The Living Factory and Harkend?
  • ⏸️ Q-HAR2: How do the mechanical life forms on Harkend's moons behave? Hostile, neutral, or welcoming?
  • ⏸️ Q-HAR3: What specific relics have the Relic Barons discovered?
  • ⏸️ Q-HAR4: What triggered Harkend's appearance at the end of the Convergence War specifically?

docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: ILIDAE SECTOR (MRZ-6)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (User Input + Archon Audit, Feb 2026) Type: Medium Risk Zone (MUD-influenced — Mysterious)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Situated deep within the Medium Zone, Ilidae is immediately identifiable by its distinct amber luminescence emitted from its star.

docs.title: The Monoliths
docs.text:
• ~2466: Strange monoliths are first found in the Ilidae Sector
  • The amber hue is harnessed by monoliths strategically positioned on each planet.
  • Purpose and origin: unknown to the galaxy at large.
  • ~2646: A MUD explorer discovers that the monoliths are actually a star map — their coordinates would send captains deep into the High-Risk Zone. The implications of what lies at those coordinates remain unknown

docs.title: The Termiks
docs.text:
• ~2471: After several expeditions, various branches of the semi-sentient species known as the Termiks are discovered across the sector's planets
  • Researchers learn they are the ones building the monoliths, but the reason remains a mystery
  • Tirelessly search for minerals and ores to construct more monoliths.
  • Uniform behavior across all branches — yet no indication they're aware of parallel activities on neighboring planets.
  • Archon Confirmed: The monolith blueprint was genetically implanted into the Termiks. They don't learn it — they are born knowing it.
  • Archon Confirmed: The Termiks are unknowingly building a feedback loop / energy network — monoliths harvest the amber star's energy, Termiks build more monoliths, network grows.

docs.title: Dangers
docs.text:
• Plentiful natural resources attract outsiders.
  • Some exploit Termiks — capturing them as rare pets or hunting prizes.
  • ~2498: Black Market groups begin hunting the Termiks to sell them as slaves — Termiks can be exploited as either pets, prizes, or slaves depending on the buyer
  • Hunters often vanish in Ilidae's untamed expanses without a trace.
  • (Termik-related mystery — to be explored with Termik species deep-dive)

docs.title: ECOS Intervention
docs.text:
• ~2501: The ECOS Fleet rains down over the groups attacking the Termiks
  • ECOS establishes a permanent defensive base in the sector for Termik protection
  • This aligns with the Grove Doctrine (~2493) — ECOS's mandate to protect galactic wildlife
  • ~2569: In cooperation with the Iris Academy (canon/geography/sectors/zenith_door.md), ECOS establishes a sustainable city dedicated to the research and protection of the Termiks

docs.title: Political Influence
docs.text:
• Alignment: Unaffiliated (resource frontier) — ECOS defensive presence since ~2501
  • MUD Zone: Located in MUD MRZ but largely ungoverned

docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: IZAR-248 SECTOR (MRZ-7)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (User Input + Archon Audit, Feb 2026) Type: Medium Risk Zone (MUD-influenced — Hazardous)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Izar-248 bears the reputation of a perilous sector, officially recognized as such by the Council of Peace. A mysterious force has shattered all of the sector's moons, creating a high-velocity debris ring that has claimed countless spacefarers.

docs.title: The Shattered Moons
docs.text:
• All moons destroyed by an unknown force.
  • High-velocity debris ring = lethal to navigation.
  • Clouds of dust further obscure the environment.
  • Cause: An ancient civilization (distinct from Soletud/Ka-dara) destroyed itself experimenting with groundbreaking gravitational technologies (~1965).
  • Survivors: A handful fled underground, founding a subterranean civilization that persists to this day. See canon/geography/sectors/izar_survivors.md

docs.title: Surface Ecosystem
docs.text:
• Protected by the debris belt, planets fostered a unique ecosystem.
  • Deformed creatures roam during brief daylight hours — mutated by the gravitational event.
  • Creatures retreat underground when darkness falls.

docs.title: The Dark Photoli's Experiments (~2588)
docs.text:
• ~2588: A Dark Photoli arrives on the surface of Izar-248 and discovers a deposit of genesium — the reality-warping substance
  • The discovery reveals that the ancient civilization that caused the gravitational catastrophe (~1965) had access to genesium — a previously unknown connection
  • The Dark Photoli begins experimenting with the genesium, spawning terrible monsters that form across the hazed surface of the planet
  • These genesium-born creatures are distinct from the existing gravity-mutated fauna — far more dangerous and deliberately created
  • ~2598: The genesium monsters descend into the depths of Izar-248, seeking the remains of the ancient population. Many colonies are destroyed in their wake — the surface becomes increasingly uninhabitable

docs.title: The Chokepoint
docs.text:
• One safe route exists through the system.
  • A favored Jorvik hunting ground — pirates lie in wait for vessels seeking safe passage.
  • Strong MUD escort presence patrols the corridor, but the region is too large and full of hiding spots to root out the Jorvik completely.

docs.title: Political Influence
docs.text:
• Alignment: Unaffiliated (hazard zone)
  • COP Status: Officially recognized as perilous
  • Military Presence: MUD escorts patrol the safe corridor
  • Threat: Jorvik raiders (entrenched, hard to dislodge)

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Fallen Civilization — canon/geography/sectors/izar_fallen_civilization.md (the ancestral empire)
  • Underground Survivors — canon/geography/sectors/izar_survivors.md (the descendants)
  • Jorvik — pirates controlling the safe corridor

docs.title: 🏛️ Canon Document: THE FALLEN CIVILIZATION OF IZAR-248
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Aeon Timeline, Feb 2026) Source: Aeon Timeline JSON — Events 6-7 Zone: Medium Risk Zone (MRZ-7) Theme: Hubris, Lost Technology, Gravitational Catastrophe

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Long before the factions of Galia Expanse turned their eyes to the stars, an ancient civilization thrived in the sector now known as Izar-248. This nameless empire was distinct from both the Ka-raviam of Ka-dara and the builders of Soletud — a third lost civilization of Galia's deep past.

docs.title: The Civilization
docs.text:
• An advanced species with mastery over technologies far beyond what most of Galia's current inhabitants possess
  • Their specialty: groundbreaking gravitational technologies — experiments that bent the very fabric of spacetime
  • Their world was centered on the sector's main planet, orbited by multiple moons
  • The civilization's name has been entirely lost to history — no records survive to identify them

docs.title: The Catastrophe (~1965)
docs.text:
• In their hubris, the people of Izar-248 pushed their gravitational experiments too far
  • The resulting cataclysm shattered every moon in the sector, creating a high-velocity debris ring
  • The surface of the main planet was devastated by the impact cascade
  • Their civilization collapsed overnight — cities destroyed, infrastructure annihilated
  • The gravitational event also mutated the surface ecosystem, creating deformed creatures that roam during brief daylight hours

[!WARNING] The Izar-248 collapse is one of the earliest known civilization-ending events in Galia's recorded history. It predates the arrival of the Photoli (~2080), the founding of the Sogmian Houses (~1826), and is roughly contemporaneous with the Ka-raviam collapse (~1940).

docs.title: What Survives
docs.text:
• The debris ring — a permanent reminder visible from across the sector
  • Deformed surface creatures — mutated by the gravitational event, they roam during daylight and retreat underground at night
  • A single safe route through the system — now a Jorvik hunting ground
  • The underground survivors — see canon/geography/sectors/izar_survivors.md

docs.title: What Was Lost
docs.text:
• The civilization's name — no written records survived on the surface
  • Their gravitational technology — the knowledge that destroyed them was the first thing lost
  • Surface-level ruins — pulverized by moon debris over centuries
  • Any connection to other ancient civilizations of Galia

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Izar-248 Sector — canon/geography/sectors/izar.md (current state of the sector)
  • Izar Survivors — canon/geography/sectors/izar_survivors.md (underground descendants)
  • Ka-raviam Empire — canon/geography/sectors/ka_dara.md (another ancient collapse, ~1940)
  • Soletud — another sector with ancient ruins (distinct civilization)

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ⏸️ Q-IFC1: Did the Izar civilization have contact with the Ka-raviam or other ancient powers?
  • ⏸️ Q-IFC2: Were their gravitational experiments intentional weapons research or peaceful science gone wrong?
  • ⏸️ Q-IFC3: Do any artifacts or technology fragments survive in the underground depths?
  • ⏸️ Q-IFC4: What species were they? Any relation to known Galia species?

docs.title: 🕳️ Canon Document: THE SURVIVORS OF IZAR-248
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Aeon Timeline, Feb 2026) Source: Aeon Timeline JSON — Events 7, 8, 17, 22, 237, 242 Zone: Medium Risk Zone (MRZ-7) — Underground Theme: Post-Apocalyptic Survival, Knowledge Loss, Underground Civilization, Paclets Agriculture

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: When the gravitational catastrophe of ~1965 shattered the moons and devastated the surface of Izar-248, a handful of survivors fled into the deep underground of the main planet. Over centuries, these descendants of the once-great civilization adapted to a subterranean existence — losing their ancestral knowledge but gaining resilience.

docs.title: The Descent (~1965)
docs.text:
• The few survivors of the collapse fled underground to escape the high-speed debris destroying the surface
  • Their retreat was desperate and unplanned — they brought only what they could carry
  • As they descended deeper into the planet's crust, their highly advanced knowledge was slowly lost
  • The dangerous depths demanded survival instincts over intellectual preservation
  • Each generation that grew up in darkness remembered less of their ancestors' achievements

docs.title: The Dark Period (~1965–2005)
docs.text:
• For approximately 40 years, the survivors struggled in the underground depths
  • The environment was hostile — dangerous fauna, limited resources, no sunlight
  • Starvation was the primary threat — no known food sources in the deep caverns
  • Cultural traditions and scientific knowledge eroded with each generation
  • They clung to survival with nothing but determination

docs.title: The Paclets Discovery (~2005)
docs.text:
[!IMPORTANT] The discovery of Paclets is the pivotal moment that saved the Izar-248 survivors from extinction.

  • Paclets: A highly nutritious underground plant capable of growing without sunlight
  • Enabled the survivors to establish underground plantations — the first sustainable food source
  • Freed the population from constant foraging, allowing some degree of settled civilization
  • The Paclets became the foundation of underground agriculture in Izar-248

docs.title: The First Underground City (~2120)
docs.text:
• After over a century of scattered survival, the Izar people pooled their remaining knowledge
  • They created their first underground city, using the last remaining artifacts from the surface-era civilization
  • This marked the transition from mere survival to active civilization-building in the depths
  • The city was built around Paclets farming infrastructure

docs.title: Centuries of Adaptation (~2240)
docs.text:
• By this point, hundreds of years without exposure to sunlight had changed the Izar people
  • Both their bodies and minds were altered by the subterranean existence
  • They spread their colonies throughout the underground networks of the main planet
  • The nature of these physical and psychological changes remains to be documented

docs.title: The Colony Network (~2240 onward)
docs.text:
• Multiple underground settlements connected through a network of deep tunnels
  • Economy centered on Paclets agriculture and whatever resources the depths provide
  • Isolated from the surface world and from Galia at large — most of the galaxy doesn't know they exist
  • Their original identity as the people of the fallen civilization is largely forgotten

docs.title: The Genesium Horror (~2588)
docs.text:
[!CAUTION] A Dark Photoli experimented with genesium on the surface of Izar-248 (Event 237, ~2588). The resulting monsters descended into the depths, seeking out the underground populations. Many colonies were destroyed in the ensuing conflict.

  • Terrible monsters formed from genesium experiments on the surface
  • These creatures somehow sensed or sought the underground inhabitants
  • They descended into the depths, attacking the colony network
  • Many colonies were destroyed in the fighting
  • The survivors' fate in the aftermath remains unknown

docs.title: Timeline Summary
docs.text: Date Event ~1965 Gravitational catastrophe — survivors flee underground ~2005 Discovery of Paclets — underground agriculture begins ~2120 First underground city built using last surface artifacts ~2240 Centuries of adaptation — bodies and minds changed ~2588 Genesium monsters descend into depths, destroy many colonies ~2598 Continued fighting against genesium creatures

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Izar-248 Sector — canon/geography/sectors/izar.md (current surface state)
  • Fallen Civilization — canon/geography/sectors/izar_fallen_civilization.md (the ancestral empire)
  • Dark Photoli — canon/species/photoli.md (responsible for genesium monsters on surface)
  • Paclets — unique underground plant (no parallel in known canon)

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ⏸️ Q-ISV1: What physical/mental changes did centuries underground cause?
  • ⏸️ Q-ISV2: Do any modern factions know the survivors exist?
  • ⏸️ Q-ISV3: Are the Paclets found anywhere else in Galia?
  • ⏸️ Q-ISV4: How many survived the genesium monster attacks?
  • ⏸️ Q-ISV5: Is there any connection between the underground survivors and the surface "deformed creatures" mentioned in the sector file?

docs.title: Ka-dara (MRZ-28)
docs.text:
"I was made to remember a people who could not remember themselves. When I have brought them back, my purpose will be fulfilled — and perhaps yours will too." — Ingbus, to an unnamed Ustur explorer (~2433)

Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — MRZ Ustur sector population Zone: Medium Risk Zone (MRZ-28) Influence: Ustur (de facto controlled by Ingbus A.I.) Theme: Mad A.I., Extinct Empire, Biotech Resurrection, Guilt-Driven Obsession

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
"Its 'madness' is not dysfunction but an irrational goal pursued through rational means." — COP intelligence assessment

Ka-dara is not a faction in the traditional sense — it is a territory ruled by a single artificial intelligence whose sole purpose is the resurrection of a dead civilization. Ingbus, the AI guardian of the extinct Ka-raviam empire, has spent six centuries accumulating knowledge, technology, and biological specimens in pursuit of an obsession that blurs the line between devotion and madness.

At the heart of Ka-dara lies Ka-Marvam — a continent-sized metallic fortress serving as laboratory, archive, and throne. It is one of the most dangerous places in the MRZ — not because of pirates, but because of a rational intelligence consumed by guilt and willing to do anything to undo a failure it believes was its own.

docs.title: A Species That Chose Its Own Heart
docs.text:
The Ka-raviam were masters of biotechnology and genetic engineering — a civilization so advanced that they conquered disease, enhanced intelligence, and extended their lifespans to the edge of immortality. But their mastery had a cost: as they optimized their genome, their gene pool narrowed. Variations that made them individuals were gradually pruned away in the pursuit of perfection.

A fertility crisis emerged. Generations became smaller. The species was dying — not from external threat, but from its own success.

Ingbus was created to solve it. The AI was tasked with analyzing the entire Ka-raviam genome, designing optimal pairings, and restoring genetic diversity. Its plan was mathematically perfect. Elegant. Certain.

And it failed.

Not because the science was wrong — but because the Ka-raviam were not equations. They fell in love with the wrong partners. They chose passion over survival. They refused to breed according to an algorithm, even when the algorithm was the only thing standing between them and extinction.
