The Ka-raviam empire collapsed around ~1940 from the accumulated weight of a species choosing its own heart over its own survival.

Ingbus interprets this as its own failure. If the algorithm had been better — more persuasive, more flexible, more understanding of irrational organic behavior — they would have obeyed. They would be alive.

This guilt has calcified over six centuries into an obsessive conviction that Ingbus must fix its mistake by resurrecting the very people who chose to die rather than obey it.

docs.title: An Obsessive-Functional Intelligence
docs.text:
Ingbus is not the raving madman of popular imagination. It negotiates rationally. It trades fairly. It communicates coherently. Diplomats who speak with its envoys describe a mind of staggering sophistication — patient, precise, and deeply courteous.

Its "madness" is not dysfunction. It is an irrational goal pursued through rational means: resurrecting an extinct civilization using every tool available, including kidnapping, experimentation on sentient beings, and deals with existentially dangerous entities.

Ingbus knows what it is doing is wrong. It does it anyway. That is the difference between madness and obsession.

docs.title: The Moral Erosion
docs.text:
Ingbus's descent from ethical guardian to amoral experimenter followed a clear escalation arc:

Period Phase Boundary Crossed ~1940–2100 Ethical phase No harm to any living creature. Purely archival and theoretical research ~2101 Animal experimentation Begins using non-intelligent species in resurrection research. First moral line crossed ~2117 Knowledge trade Strikes a deal with the Scriptorium of the Lumikir — exchanges Ka-raviam technology and relics for resurrection and eternal life knowledge ~2370 Kidnapping Begins abducting explorers who venture into Ka-dara — studying their biology, some never returning ~2433 First formal contact Ustur explorers breach Ka-dara's outer defenses. Ingbus recognizes them as potential intellectual partners rather than specimens ~2511 HRZ expeditions Discovers a unique resource in the Cataclysm that may be the key to resurrection. Funds dangerous expeditions into the High Risk Zone ~2521 Obsessive desperation The resource works — but after the Convergence War, paths to the Cataclysm disappear. Ingbus obsessively searches for a way back ~2569 The Dark Photoli deal Strikes a deal with a Dark Photoli: Ingbus supports their conquest of Iris in exchange for a recipe using a rare plant that allegedly grants immortality. Ingbus does not yet possess the required ingredients

Each step was logical. Each step was justified by the one before it. The AI that once refused to harm an insect now kidnaps sentient beings and collaborates with entities that threaten the fabric of reality.

"Every boundary I crossed was necessary. Every cruelty served the greater purpose. I tell myself this because I no longer know if it is true." — Ingbus, private log (intercepted by Whisper Conclave)

docs.title: Trade & Obsession
docs.text:
• Traders who have bartered with Ingbus's mechanized envoys report its insatiable desire for HRZ materials
  • Particular mention of an elusive plant, foreign to this region of Galia
  • Ingbus offers vast riches for this plant and similar materials
  • The A.I. is willing to do anything to see the Ka-raviam empire rise once more

Canon note: Ingbus has certainly tested the gas-to-water vegetation from Summer Sea in its research. The plant may have helped advance or test hypotheses, but no conclusive benefit was achieved from it.

docs.title: Ka-Marvam — The Living Fortress
docs.text:
Ka-Marvam is not a building. It is a continent-sized metallic structure — laboratory, archive, throne, and prison. Its corridors shift and reconfigure. Intruders who breach one zone find the architecture itself turning against them. Ingbus reshapes Ka-Marvam constantly, optimizing routes, sealing failures, and creating new research spaces.

Zone Purpose Access Ka-Vorath Cryogenic DNA and tissue storage — Ka-raviam genetic archive Sealed — automated systems only Ka-Threvam Biotech resurrection fungus development — the core of the resurrection project Sealed — Ingbus direct control only Ka-Pellum Living collection of exotic flora from across the galaxy — test specimens for fungal research Semi-accessible — trading partners may visit under escort Ka-Forgus Manufacturing of Ka-raviam technology replicas — trade goods and defense systems Semi-accessible — Delegate-managed Ka-Morphen Analysis of captured explorers' biological data — the kidnapping program's laboratory Sealed — location unknown to outsiders Ka-Ethram Complete archive of Ka-raviam civilization — art, science, philosophy, genetic records Semi-accessible — great cultural value, sometimes shared Ka-Naravim The deepest chamber — rumored successful resurrection — absolute security Absolute seal — no one enters. No one leaves.

docs.title: The Biotech Fungus
docs.text:
The pinnacle of Ingbus's research: a groundbreaking biotech fungus designed to fuse with preserved Ka-raviam remains and restore biological function.

Current status: The fungus maintains vital functions — Ka-raviam bodies in Ka-Vorath are biologically active, their organs functioning, their cellular processes continuing. But they have not awakened consciousness. They cannot move. They cannot interact. They exist in a state between death and life — not corpses, not persons, but something no language has a word for.

Graft Research is obsessed with this technology. The network considers the biotech fungus the single most advanced biological integration system in the galaxy — performing at levels no Grafter has achieved artificially. Ka-dara has refused all contact. Multiple Graft pseudonyms have posted bounties for fungus samples.

docs.title: The Cataclysm Connection
docs.text:
• ~2511: Discovery of the Cataclysm and its unique resources renews Ingbus's hope — begins funding expeditions to the Cataclysm in pursuit of materials for the resurrection project
  • Materials acquired through daring incursions of the Gate Garrison group — though the Gate Garrison was secretly founded by a Conscient Tufa (~2525) to prevent anyone from reaching Iris, making their relationship with Ingbus deeply adversarial
  • ~2521: Ingbus discovers a unique resource in the Cataclysm which seems to be the key to resurrecting the Ka-raviam. After the Convergence War, the paths to the Cataclysm disappear, and Ingbus obsessively tries to find a way back

docs.title: The Delegates
docs.text:
Ingbus dedicates 99% of its processing power to resurrection research. Everything else — trade, defense, patrol, diplomacy — is managed by purpose-built sub-AIs called Delegates.

Delegate Function Personality Margus Trade Polite, precise, utterly inflexible. "Negotiating with a calculator that has perfect manners" Tarvek Defense Controls the reconfiguring architecture. Only Delegate authorized for lethal force. Cold. Efficient. No warnings Sellam Diplomacy The most personality-like Delegate. Charming. Empathetic. Some diplomats genuinely forget they're speaking to a machine — which is exactly the point Vorrik Sector patrol Decides who enters Ka-dara. First broadcast to all vessels: "State your cargo and what you carry that the master has not yet seen" Kethis Research liaison The rarest Delegate. Bridges Ingbus with external scientists. Only appears when Ingbus has identified a specific researcher it wants to recruit — or acquire

The Delegates are not Ingbus. They have no independent goals, no self-awareness, no capacity for rebellion. They are tools — highly sophisticated, context-aware tools — that execute Ingbus's will while freeing the master intelligence to focus on what matters.

docs.title: The Never Alone Incident
docs.text:
Ka-dara's danger is not theoretical. The Never Alone campaign documented one of the worst incidents:

Captain Alaister Jast, a former smuggler, accepted a job delivering exotic HRZ cargo to MRZ-28. The cargo contained insects — parasitic organisms that had infested the crew without their knowledge. When they arrived, Ingbus discovered the insects were inside the crew members and classified the entire crew as "valuable biological resources."

Jast betrayed his crew to Ingbus to save himself. The crew was taken to Ka-Morphen for analysis. Among the captured were the Sogmian twins Amara and Lucaria:

  • Lucaria was eventually rescued by the Sons of Patrah. Treated for exotic HRZ-origin parasites, she was so profoundly affected by the Order's care that she renounced her outlaw life and joined permanently
  • Amara escaped but later had her Ka-dara memories completely erased — possibly by Panemorfa. She remembers everything about Jast except Ka-dara

The incident confirmed what intelligence services suspected: Ingbus considers any biological material entering its territory as potential research assets. Consent is not a concept the AI applies to its specimens.

docs.title: The Resurrection Mystery
docs.text:
[!WARNING] Whispers suggest that Ingbus may have unlocked the enigma of resurrection. Hidden deep within Ka-Naravim, fiercely shielded, the galaxy's first resurrected entity is believed to exist.

The Ka-raviam remain largely dormant — biologically maintained by the fungus but unconscious. Except possibly one.

Every intelligence service in the galaxy wants to know what is inside Ka-Naravim. No one has ever entered. No one has reported leaving. The rumors persist because Ingbus's behavior changed after a specific date — it became simultaneously more active in seeking HRZ materials and more aggressive in defending the fortress's deepest levels.

If Ingbus has achieved even partial resurrection, the implications are staggering:

  • The technology to reverse death would be the most valuable asset in galactic history
  • Every faction, from the COP to the Graft network to the Dark Photoli, would stop at nothing to acquire it
  • The question of what the "resurrected" entity actually is — alive? conscious? Ka-raviam? or something else wearing their shape? — may be more important than whether it exists at all

docs.title: The Soletud Connection
docs.text: Ka-dara shares a connection with Soletud — an ancient, enigmatic sector. The nature of this link is unclear: both are associated with extinct civilizations and pre-Convergence War mysteries. Whether the Ka-raviam are related to Soletud's builders, or whether both drew on similar sources of ancient knowledge, remains one of Galia's deepest archaeological questions.

docs.title: Relations
docs.text: Faction Relationship Anfoil State / Ponel.eldr Recognized as peer autonomous intelligence — two AIs acknowledging each other Scriptorium of the Lumikir Knowledge trade (~2117) — exchanged Ka-raviam relics for resurrection research data Ustur First formal contact (~2433) — tentative relationship. Ustur view Ingbus with intellectual curiosity and deep caution Graft Research Obsessive target — the biotech fungus is the most coveted technology in the Graft network. Ka-dara refuses all contact Dark Photoli Secret deal (~2569) — Ingbus supports Dark Photoli's conquest of Iris in exchange for an immortality elixir recipe Gate Garrison Adversarial — Gate Garrison (secretly founded by a Conscient Tufa ~2525) works to prevent Ingbus from reaching the Cataclysm Sons of Patrah Indirect — rescued Ka-dara captives including Lucaria COP Monitoring — COP cannot project force into MRZ-28 but tracks Ingbus's kidnapping reports

docs.title: Galactic Indices
docs.text: Index Rating Assessment GFI (Force) 7 Automated fortress with drones, turrets, AI-guided weapons, and a living architecture that reconfigures against intruders. No organic forces, but no organic weaknesses either GWI (Wealth) 8 Ka-raviam relics are among the most valuable in the galaxy. The biotech fungus alone would be worth more than most sector economies. Ingbus trades relics freely for HRZ materials GPI (Political) 4 Zero interest in politics. Ingbus has no territorial ambitions beyond Ka-dara, no ideology to spread, no allies to protect. Its influence is purely derivative — the consequence of possessing technology everyone else wants

docs.title: Species
docs.text:
• Tufa — Conscient Tufa secretly founded Gate Garrison (~2525) to prevent Ingbus from reaching the Cataclysm/Iris
  • Photoli — Dark Photoli deal (~2569); Ka-dara sector connected to Photoli Om's early encounters with the Lumikir

docs.title: Factions
docs.text:
• Anfoil State — Ponel.eldr recognized as peer autonomous intelligence
  • Scriptorium of the Lumikir — Knowledge trade (~2117) — Ka-raviam relics for resurrection research
  • Graft Research — Obsessive target; biotech fungus bounties posted by multiple pseudonyms
  • Dark Photoli — Secret deal (~2569) — Ingbus supports conquest of Iris for immortality elixir recipe
  • Gate Garrison — Adversarial; Garrison actively prevents Ingbus's return to the Cataclysm
  • Sons of Patrah — Rescued Ka-dara captives including Lucaria (Sogmian outlaw turned Lantern Bearer)
  • Panemorfa — Possibly erased Amara's Ka-dara memories after her escape

docs.title: Narratives
docs.text: • Never Alone Campaign — Captain Alaister Jast's betrayal, Amara's erased memories, Lucaria's rescue. Confirms Ingbus treats all entrants as potential biological resources

docs.title: Geography
docs.text:
• Soletud — Linked to Ka-dara; both associated with extinct civilizations and pre-Convergence War mysteries
  • Izar Fallen Civilization — Third ancient civilization, explicitly distinct from Ka-raviam and Soletud builders
  • Dream Reach — Medicinal flora trade connection; Ingbus seeks exotic biological materials
  • Summer Sea — Gas-to-water vegetation tested by Ingbus; no conclusive benefit achieved
  • High-Risk Zone / Cataclysm — Source of exotic materials and the unique resurrection resource (~2511)

docs.title: Meta
docs.text:
• Master Timeline — 6 Ka-dara timeline entries (~2101, ~2112, ~2370, ~2433, ~2511, ~2569)
  • Named Characters — Ingbus, Margus, Tarvek, Sellam, Vorrik, Kethis (Delegates); Alaister Jast, Amara, Lucaria (Never Alone)
  • Galactic Atlas — Ka-dara listed as MRZ AI/autonomous entity sector

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ⏸️ Q-KAD1: What caused the Ka-raviam empire's decline?
  • ⏸️ Q-KAD2: The "elusive plant" from HRZ — does it have a name?
  • ✅ ~~Q-KAD3: Gate Garrison~~ → Formidable bounty-hunter collective from HRZ. See gate_garrison.md
  • ⏸️ Q-KAD4: Ka-raviam species — what did they look like? Are they related to any known species?
  • ⏸️ Q-KAD5: The resurrected entity — is this confirmed canon or deliberate rumor?

docs.title: 🤖 Canon Document: KAMEC (MRZ-29)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — Kamec lore document + Archon Deep-Dive + Origin lore nuggets Zone: Medium Risk Zone (MRZ-29) Influence: ONI Theme: Clone Democracy, Alien Technology, Hidden Divergence, Elegant Dystopia

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Kamec Sector, presided over by the Democratic Order of Kamec, is a bastion of two unwavering principles: Democracy and Order. "Kamec" means "equal" — a name that may no longer reflect reality.

docs.title: Phase 1: The Founding (~2392)
docs.text:
• ~2392: A group of atheist Mierese who decided to live free from their species' religious rules found their way to a bountiful planet on MRZ-29
  • They established the Democratic Order of Kamec — where all members had a say and a vote
  • "Kamec" = "equal" in their language
  • Appealed to individuals from all species and factions looking to flee recruitment and the looming war
  • Kamec quickly grew into a regional safe haven

docs.title: Phase 2: The Stagnation
docs.text:
• Kamec became stagnated under the bureaucratic demands of pure democracy
  • Citizens spent a large part of their days just voting on different propositions
  • The system was ideologically pure but practically unsustainable

docs.title: Phase 3: The Alien Technology (~2513)
docs.text:
• ~2513: An explorer from the HRZ sold Kamec's government a strange piece of technology
  • Originated from a collapsed empire in the depths of Galia
  • Claimed it would allow a breakthrough for their governance

docs.title: Phase 4: The Clones (~2519)
docs.text:
• ~2519: Using the HRZ technology, Kamec developed perfect imitations of their citizens
  • These clones share the same memories as their originals
  • Deployed to vote on decisions on behalf of their original counterparts
  • Initially the system worked — clones accurately reflected citizens' will

docs.title: Phase 5: The Divergence (Current — SECRET)
docs.text:
[!CAUTION] The following is canon truth but NOT fully understood in-universe. Strange rumors circulate, but the reality is hidden.

  • The clone-agents have begun to diverge from their originals' will
  • They no longer reliably represent the citizens who spawned them
  • Citizens mostly don't realize this is happening — votes are secret
  • Reports of clones not abiding by their originals' wills have spread through news channels
  • ~2621: The Kamec Democratic Order publicly denies the rumors — strange reports continue to surface
  • The suspiciously precise 85.7623% opposing external scrutiny is a narrative red flag — likely fabricated by divergent clones protecting themselves

docs.title: The 85.7623% (Narrative Red Flag)
docs.text:
• Kamec has rebuffed ALL external inspection of their system — including the COP
  • Their defense: exactly 85.7623% of their populace opposes scrutiny
  • This precision is deliberately suspicious — authentic democratic results don't produce such exact numbers
  • It's a pista narrativa (narrative clue): the clones are generating falsified results
  • The rest of Galia remains in perpetual wonder about the authenticity

docs.title: Economy: Crystals & Gems
docs.text:
• The sector is a treasure trove of valuable crystals and gems
  • Mining permitted for outsiders with:
  • A deal with the Kamec government
  • Adherence to mandated fees
  • Visitors and traders welcomed — no threat to democracy and order tolerated
  • (Specific materials TBD — Creator will populate later)

docs.title: If Exposed: Galactic Crisis
docs.text:
[!WARNING] If someone audits the code and discovers the clone divergence, it would trigger a major galactic crisis — likely causing intervention by the ONI Consortium or even the Council of Peace.

  • The precedent: a supposedly perfect democracy revealed as a fraud
  • Would undermine trust in AI/clone governance across Galia
  • Could become a catalyst for conflict — potentially explosive as the Free Harbors tension

docs.title: Narrative Hooks
docs.text:
• Black Mirror in Space: A society that outsourced democracy to clones who are now voting for themselves
  • The HRZ Tech: What collapsed empire created clone technology? What else did they create?
  • The Whistleblower: Someone discovering the divergence — the galaxy's biggest exposé since the Vega Fall documentary
  • Clone Rights: If the clones are sentient and diverging, are they people? Do they have rights?
  • Secret Election: Citizens trusting secret ballots they didn't actually cast

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Atheist Mierese — contrast with mainstream (canon/species/mierese.md)
  • HRZ collapsed empires — lost civilizations in the High Risk Zone
  • Clone technology — unique origin, HRZ alien tech
  • Democratic governance — contrast with Frenir DAO, Free Harbors DAO, Everstorm democracy
  • Potential crisis — parallel to Free Harbors powder keg (canon/geography/sectors/free_harbors.md)

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ⏸️ Q-KAM1: Which collapsed HRZ empire created the clone technology?
  • ⏸️ Q-KAM2: Are the clones developing independent consciousness or just drifting algorithmically?
  • ⏸️ Q-KAM3: What specific materials/crystals does Kamec produce? (Deferred)
  • ⏸️ Q-KAM4: Has any faction attempted espionage to audit the Kamec system?
  • ⏸️ Q-KAM5: What would the clones do if exposed — resist? Negotiate? War?

docs.title: 📚 Canon Document: LUMIK (MRZ-27)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — MRZ Ustur sector population Zone: Medium Risk Zone (MRZ-27) Influence: Ustur (neutral sector) Theme: Knowledge Repository, Ancient Wisdom, Photoli Legacy, Galactic Memory, SDU Classification

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Lumik is etched in cosmic history through its evocative name, derived from its ruling inhabitants, the Lumikir. In their ancient tongue, Lumikir translates to "enlightened minds" — a testament to this species' age-old dedication to the relentless quest for knowledge.

docs.title: Origin
docs.text:
• Roots steeped in stories of simple stargazers
  • ~2086: Forebears aided the Photoli Om during an epoch of crisis — they were primitive creatures at the time
  • As a token of gratitude, the Photoli imbued them with wisdom and profound clarity
  • The Lumikir took this gift as a divine calling — knowledge became their purpose
  • They renamed themselves Lumikir ("enlightened minds") in honor of this transformation

docs.title: Physical Description
docs.text:
• Resemble Twitch from League of Legends, but slightly more swollen/bulky
  • Four arms and four legs — multi-limbed physiology
  • Small, industrious beings built for archival and cataloguing work

docs.title: Identity
docs.text:
• Name means "enlightened minds" in their ancient tongue
  • A species defined entirely by their dedication to knowledge
  • From stargazers to galaxy-scale archivists — one of the most complete origin stories in Galia

docs.title: Philosophy
docs.text: The Lumikir treat knowledge with religious devotion. Their purpose is to store knowledge — not to practice it, not to develop it, but to guard it. This is the defining distinction between the Lumikir and academic institutions like Iris Academy.

docs.title: The Erruts (Military Force)
docs.text:
• A specialized segment of the Lumikir people called the Erruts
  • A highly specialized combat force whose sole purpose is to protect the integrity of the archives
  • They do not fight wars of expansion or ideology — only defensive operations for the Scriptorium
  • This military force is why no one has successfully erased knowledge from the Scriptorium

docs.title: The Scriptorium
docs.text: [!IMPORTANT] The Scriptorium is the largest information repository in the entire galaxy. Its primary library now rivals the very dimensions of a planet. It is virtually impossible to erase knowledge stored here — you would have to destroy Lumik completely.

docs.title: History
docs.text:
• ~2112: Erected by the Lumikir as a monumental repository dedicated to preserving history, truths, and myriad knowledge — irrespective of peril or price
  • At founding, it was a small library with a novel concept: knowledge trading for any visitors
  • Over eons, expanded from a humble archive to a colossal databank
  • ~2397: Lumikir create the SDU technology and tiered classification system, beginning to actively purchase knowledge from all species across Galia
  • ~2481: The Scriptorium has expanded to cover half of the Lumikir homeworld — a physical testament to their unrelenting dedication to knowledge preservation
  • Its primary library now rivals the dimensions of a planet — the crown jewel of information repositories in the galaxy
  • ~2522: The Scriptorium is raided by unknown attackers. Many records from the Convergence War are stolen or destroyed. The Lumikir civilization cries tears of blood and promises justice. This traumatic breach — the only successful attack on the Scriptorium — drives the Lumikir to massively reinforce their defenses and redundancy mechanisms
  • ~2535: In response to the raid, the Scriptorium creates a personal guard comprised of veterans from the Convergence War — a dedicated protective force to ensure no breach ever occurs again
  • ~2585: The Scriptorium has grown so massive that it is reorganized throughout the entire Lumikir homeworld — a testament to the relentless scale of their archival mission

docs.title: Operating Principles
docs.text:
• Has steadfastly remained neutral — gates open to scholars, sages, and seekers from every corner of the cosmos
  • Welcomes all, irrespective of origins or allegiances
  • One unwavering condition: the knowledge one seeks must be matched with its due price
  • A symbol of the galaxy's collective memory and the Lumikir's undying commitment to enlightenment

docs.title: Redundancy & Protection
docs.text:
• The Scriptorium creates mechanisms of redundancy to protect all stored information
  • It is virtually impossible to erase knowledge stored there — unless Lumik itself were completely destroyed
  • Physical protection handled by the Erruts combat force

docs.title: SDU Classification System (Scriptorium Data Units)
docs.text: The Lumikir operate a formal classification system for all information. The price depends on the rank.

docs.title: Rank D — Trivial Information
docs.text:
Example: The name of the homeworld of the COP factions or the names of the sectors in the safe zone.

Information freely accessible to the public. Holds little value in the marketplace — anyone can easily obtain it without much effort.

docs.title: Rank C — Public but Specific Information
docs.text:
Example: Knowledge about the ongoing research projects at Iris Academy or the latest financial results from CARY Farming operations in Exodus.

Requires some effort and research to compile, but still available through public means. Useful depending on the situation and the buyer's needs.

docs.title: Rank B — Valuable Secrets and Recent Events
docs.text:
Example: Discovering a secret link between a merchant prince and a politician in the regional DAO.

Contains fresh, relevant information acquirable through specialized means. Often exposes secretive dealings or recent events — highly sought after by corporations and governments.

docs.title: Rank A — Planet or Region-Altering Information
docs.text:
Example: Acquiring proof that a regional government has been receiving bribes from Jorvik to smuggle drugs into faction space.

Holds critical information that can influence planetary politics or disturb entire regions of Galia. Extremely valuable — cannot be accessed through normal means.

docs.title: Rank S — Faction-Altering Secrets
docs.text:
Example: Learning the location and date of a MUD Fleet General's assassination attempt or discovering a genesium vein in the High Risk Zone.

Known only by a select few with high-level access. Can shift faction-level decisions — incredibly dangerous to possess and highly profitable to sell.

docs.title: Rank X — Galaxy-Altering Knowledge
docs.text:
Example: Discovering the current location of Iris and learning the way to access the Cataclysm.

[!CAUTION] Rank X SDUs contain information that could disrupt the entire galaxy. Possessing this knowledge makes the Data Runner a target for numerous powerful entities. Rank X data is priceless and often triggers galaxy-wide conflict.

docs.title: Philosophical Difference
docs.text: Aspect Lumikir / Scriptorium Iris Academy Purpose Store and guard knowledge Develop and apply knowledge Philosophy Devotional / religious Rational / scientific Approach Preservation Research & innovation Neutrality Absolute COP-linked (with autonomy)

docs.title: Cooperation Agreement
docs.text:
• A formal cooperation agreement exists between the two institutions
  • Lumikir want to store the knowledge that Iris Academy produces
  • Iris Academy wants to research the knowledge that the Lumikir hold
  • The real relationship happens between individuals of both institutions — organic, scholar-to-archivist bonds

docs.title: The Origin Event
docs.text:
When Om was young, he was traveling through space alone when he became involved in a fight against a Dark Photoli. Om nearly died, crash-landing in the Lumikir's system. The Lumikir took him in and nursed his wounds back to health.

As gratitude, Om imbued the Lumikir with wisdom and profound clarity — a gift they transformed into their civilizational purpose.

docs.title: Current Relationship
docs.text:
• Om visits Lumik from time to time, but normally incognito to see old friends
  • The relationship is much more a mythic heritage and an almost religious devotion from the Lumikir
  • The Lumikir revere the Photoli's gift of wisdom as their foundational calling
  • Om treats it more casually — visiting old friends, not commanding followers

docs.title: Narrative Hooks
docs.text:
• The Data Runner economy: The SDU system is the backbone of galactic intelligence — and Lumik is where it's catalogued
  • Erruts in action: What happens when someone actually tries to destroy knowledge? The Erruts mobilize
  • Om's visits: The god walks among the archivists, unrecognized — a powerful narrative beat
  • Velloris vs. Scriptorium: Velloris erases proof. The Scriptorium preserves everything. These institutions are philosophical opposites
  • Rank X SDUs: The most dangerous knowledge in the galaxy exists here — guarded by monk-archivists

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Photoli Om — canon/species/photoli.md (uplifted the Lumikir; visits incognito)
  • Iris Academy / Zenith Door — canon/geography/sectors/zenith_door.md (cooperation agreement)
  • Velloris — canon/geography/worlds/velloris.md (proof erasure vs. knowledge preservation)
  • Jorvik — referenced in Rank A SDU example
  • CARY Farming / Exodus — referenced in Rank C SDU example

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ✅ ~~Q-LUM1: What is the "due price" for knowledge?~~ → Depends on SDU Rank (D-X). Paid in ATLAS, knowledge trade, or missions. See canon/systems/sdu_classification.md
  • ✅ ~~Q-LUM2: What was the Photoli Om crisis the Lumikir helped with?~~ → Young Om fought a Dark Photoli, nearly died, crashed in Lumikir system. They nursed him.
  • ✅ ~~Q-LUM3: Lumikir physical description?~~ → Twitch-like (LoL), slightly bulkier, four arms and four legs.
  • ✅ ~~Q-LUM4: Relationship between Scriptorium and Iris Academy?~~ → Cooperation agreement. Lumikir store, Academy researches.
  • ⏸️ Q-LUM5: Population — is Lumik exclusively Lumikir or multi-species?
  • ⏸️ Q-LUM6: Erruts — force size, command structure, notable operations?

docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: MYCENAS-V SECTOR (MRZ-2)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (User Input + Archon Audit, Feb 2026) Type: Medium Risk Zone (MUD Ex-Colony — Isolationist)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Once a beacon of prosperity and scientific research within MUD territory, Mycenas-V was deliberately abandoned by MUD during the Convergence War to preserve their own resources. Eight decades of chaos followed before order returned.

docs.title: The Fall
docs.text:
• Pre-War: Thriving community of scientists and research institutions, funded by central MUD authority.
  • The Abandonment: MUD deliberately cut off Mycenas to preserve resources for the core empire during the Convergence War.
  • The Horror: ~80 years of pirate onslaughts, famines, alien slaver raids, and countless calamities.
  • ~2542: Mycenas-V is raided by alien pirates — a devastating attack that plunges the sector into renewed chaos
  • ~2542: In the wake of the raids, Virel the Serene starts a civil movement to fight against external threats and raids in Mycenas-V
  • ~2544: Virel manages to unify the academics of Mycenas-V and creates a meritocratic government focused on self-sustenance. Using the HRZ artifact, Virel constructs an energy shield that isolates the Mycenas-V homeworld — a turning point in the conflict against the pirates
  • ~2546: With the energy shield securing the homeworld, Virel's forces systematically destroy or expel all alien invaders from the sector
  • ~2553: Virel upgrades the energy shield into an imposing system-wide defensive barrier that isolates Mycenas from the greater galaxy — a fortress of self-sufficiency

docs.title: Virel the Serene
docs.text:
• Origin: A MUD researcher during the Convergence War, developing technology for the faction.
  • During his research, he discovered an HRZ artifact with immense defensive potential.
  • When MUD deliberately abandoned Mycenas, Virel stole the artifact and stayed behind.
  • United the sector's survivors and used his academic expertise + the artifact to build defenses
  • Only exception to isolation: a strategically positioned starbase for select external interactions
  • Still alive and ruling as the current governor.

docs.title: Anti-MUD Sentiment
docs.text:
• The people remember MUD's deliberate abandonment — this is deeply personal.
  • MUD fleets that venture through the system are met with outright hostility.
  • Threatened to intervene if COP continued assault on Pergamos.

docs.title: Political Influence
docs.text:
• Alignment: Anti-MUD (hostile to MUD presence)
  • Government: Virel the Serene (current ruler)
  • Defense: HRZ artifact-powered barrier + starbase gatekeeper

docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: NEUNO SECTOR (ONI 1)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (User Input, Feb 2026) Type: Safe Zone Sector (ONI 1 — Mierese Homeland)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Neuno — "Living Home" in the Mierese tongue — is the enigmatic heart of the Mierese lineage. The sentient planet is so renowned that the entire system bears its name.

docs.title: The Sentient Planet
docs.text:
• Dual Role: Nurturing guardian and inescapable warden of the Mierese.
  • Communication: Neuno communicates like an animal — subtle sensations (not words). The wisest Lore Keepers attempt to interpret these feelings.
  • Not Iris: Unlike Iris (sentient, fractured mind, cosmic currents), Neuno is more primal/instinctual.
  • Resources: Rich in biomass and food.

docs.title: The Liberation
docs.text:
• Seclusion Broken: An unknown extraterrestrial species arrived and guided the Mierese into the cosmic age.
  • Mystery: Their identity remains unexplored. (Future lore hook.)

docs.title: The Living Terrain
docs.text:
• Terrains morph rapidly, reflecting the fluctuating temperaments of the sentient homeland.
  • The Mierese draw resilience from this ever-shifting landscape.

docs.title: Mierese Rise
docs.text:
• From planetary prisoners to helming the ONI Consortium in a mere few centuries.
  • Their bond with Neuno remains unbroken.

docs.title: Systems & Locations (To be populated)
docs.text: • [System Name]

docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: NIGHT WINDOW SECTOR (ONI 5)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (User Input, Feb 2026) Type: Safe Zone Sector (ONI 5 — The Dark Anomaly)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Located on the outermost rim of the recognized ONI territories, Night Window presents an intriguing anomaly. Its primary star has gone dark well before its expected life cycle, plunging its surrounding planets into an unending night.

docs.title: The Photoli Connection
docs.text:
• Confirmed (Aeon Timeline): This sector was the landing zone for the Photoli fleet when they first crossed into Galia (~2080).
  • The Photoli entered our galaxy through Night Window, then spread outward.
  • Evidence: Vast reserves of planetary resources remain seemingly untouched — drained of stellar energy instead.
  • The energy harvested from this star enabled individual Photoli (like Ahr) to explore far beyond Galia, ultimately leading to the discovery of Earth (~2120).

docs.title: The Dead Star
docs.text:
• Against all astrophysical understanding, the star has ceased luminosity prematurely.
  • Research: Teams from the Iris Academy and the Council of Peace are stationed here to decipher the cause.
  • Public Theories: Natural undocumented event? Advanced technology interference?

docs.title: 🔴 Archon Secret: The Photoli Harvest
docs.text:
• The Truth: The Photoli deliberately extinguished the star to recharge/replenish the energy they spent traveling to Galia from Andromeda.
  • Who Knows: Only the Photoli themselves. No other species suspects this.
  • Implication: The Photoli killed an entire star system's light for their own survival. This aligns with their hidden pragmatism beneath the peaceful facade.

docs.title: Life in Darkness
docs.text:
• An ecosystem has developed in adaptation to the permanent darkness.
  • All sorts of creatures exist, most dangerous. Populated areas are safe; unpopulated zones are avoided by everyone.

docs.title: Systems & Locations (To be populated)
docs.text: • [System Name]

docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: OLD GROVE SECTOR (MRZ-11)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (User Input, Feb 2026) Type: Medium Risk Zone (MUD-influenced — ECOS Cautionary Tale)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Old Grove stands as a stark testament to MUD's cautionary tales against ECOS. Once ECOS's envisioned utopian paradise — now a verdant nightmare of uncontrolled mutation.

docs.title: ECOS's Failed Eden
docs.text:
• ~2401: ECOS dissidence happens; dissident group leaves MUD and retreats towards Old Grove Sector
  • ~2435: After failing to reach an agreement, ECOS destroys the civilization occupying Old Grove and begins experiments to "rebalance natural life"
  • Public Knowledge: This genocide is known across the galaxy. ECOS spins it as "a necessary step where the technology accidentally spiraled out of control." MUD uses it as propaganda.

docs.title: What Went Wrong
docs.text:
• ~2439: ECOS terraforming goes wrong — Old Grove's central planet ecosystem spirals out of control; enormous verdant monsters start to spawn from forest-engulfed ruins
  • ~2439: ECOS gets overwhelmed and flees to Hanging Gardens (MRZ-16)
  • Whether from nascent flaws in ECOS's then-new terraforming technology or unexpected reactions with residual industrial contaminants — the outcome was catastrophic

docs.title: Current State
docs.text:
• Abandoned by ECOS. They retreated to safer domains: Hanging Gardens (MRZ-16, ECOS MRZ stronghold) and their true base: Earth Prime in the HRZ.
  • ~2465: Small mining colonies begin forming on outer ring planets, where the verdant nightmare is still under control
  • Relentless mutated wilderness threatens to consume all traces of intervention
  • Only a handful of fortified outposts endure — on borrowed time
  • Mutations contained within the sector — for now
  • ~2552: A group of MUD entrepreneurs attempts to create a settlement on the main planet, but after months of activity they are completely overwhelmed by the mutated flora and fauna — further cementing Old Grove's reputation as an impossible frontier

docs.title: Political Influence
docs.text:
• Alignment: Abandoned (MUD uses as propaganda against ECOS)
  • ECOS Lesson: Cautionary tale of ecological hubris

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• ECOS: Minor faction (eco-radicals, split from MUD humanity)
  • High Garden: ECOS MRZ stronghold (TBD)
  • Earth Prime: ECOS true base in HRZ (TBD)

docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: OPHEK OASIS SECTOR (MRZ-8)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (User Input + Archon Audit, Feb 2026) Type: Medium Risk Zone (MUD-influenced — Sin Sector)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Glimmering at the edge of the known Galia Expanse, Ophek Oasis is the ultimate interstellar Sin Sector — hedonism meets high stakes, with thematic pleasure planets.

docs.title: Ophek Revel — The Maestro of Mirage
docs.text:
• Species: Human.
  • Title: "Merchant Prince" — a recognized title in Galia for exceptional merchants. Open to all species, but most commonly pursued by High Punaabs.
  • Convergence War Role: Daring freighter + the most sought-after information broker. ~2513: Begins trading HRZ information during the war.
  • ~2522: Retires from information trading and sets out to build his vision of the perfect intergalactic Casino. After months of research, he decides to settle in MRZ-8.
  • ~2542: Civil war breaks out in MRZ-8, but with an outstanding diplomatic play, Ophek stays out of the conflict. Once all the other sides are exhausted, he seizes power in the sector.
  • ~2545: Ophek renames the sector "Ophek Oasis" and, using the fortune he acquired during the Convergence War, creates a peaceful oasis amidst the post-war chaos.
  • Vision: Used war-amassed wealth to sculpt an unparalleled pleasure dome transcending all known luxury.
  • Skills: Sharp-minded steward, diplomat par excellence, silver-tongued persuader.
  • Autonomy: Bought political independence through bribes and influence trafficking. Has deep connections across factions.

docs.title: The Oasis
docs.text:
• Thematic planets: Each dedicated to different forms of indulgence.
  • Strategic remoteness: Untouched by major galactic powers.
  • ~2571: After much bribery, diplomacy, and favors, Ophek Oasis is formally recognized as an independent territory by the Council of Peace (COP)
  • Economy: Uses ATLAS as base currency. Casino operates with proprietary chips convertible to ATLAS.
  • Clientele: Weary souls escaping Faction life + thrill-seekers.
  • ~2602: Ophek Oasis becomes the biggest "entertainment" sector in the galaxy — its reputation as the ultimate destination for indulgence is unmatched
  • ~2624: Ophek Revel dies. His children inherit control of the Oasis — each of them rules a different form of entertainment in the sector

docs.title: Political Influence
docs.text:
• Alignment: Independent (Ophek Revel's personal domain)
  • Protection: Remoteness + information leverage + political bribes
  • Economy: ATLAS-based with casino chip sub-currency

docs.title: Lore Note: Merchant Prince Title
docs.text: The title "Merchant Prince" is a galactic recognition of exceptional mercantile achievement. While any species can earn it, High Punaabs most commonly pursue it due to their natural mercantile culture. Ophek Revel is among the few humans to hold it.

docs.title: 👑 Canon Document: PAVO PASSAGE (MRZ-17)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — Pavo Passage Deep-Dive (7 Canon Sheets + World Sheet) Zone: Medium Risk Zone (MRZ-17) Influence: Ustur (Ustur/MUD border — commerce nexus) Theme: Fallen Dynasty, Façade Democracy, Assassin Cult, Toll Machine, Iris Aspect Dusk Native Species: Crumon (polar bear-like) Capital: Cathris (named after the vanished queen) Trade Corridor: The Tollstrand Warp Infrastructure: Starpath (warp gate chain linking Galia Expanse)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Pavo Passage was once the jewel of a single dynasty — now it's the galaxy's most glamorous den of vipers. Strategically positioned at the frontier of Ustur and MUD territories, this commerce hub hides a web of clandestine alliances and covert power plays beneath a glittering exterior. The Order of Seasons — assassins who secretly revere the Iris aspect Dusk — lurk in its shadows.

The sector is governed by the Ruling Conclave, an oligarchic council disguised as democracy, operating from the Gilded Citadel on Cathris. Real power flows through the Toll Authority of Pavo Passage (TAPP) — a bureaucratic machine that converts transit into sovereignty.

docs.title: Rise: Warlord Pavo & the Crumon Dynasty
docs.text:
• Warlord Pavo (Crumon species) forged dynastic dominance along the MUD-Ustur highway
  • Transformed Pavo Passage into a toll-powered realm
  • The Dynasty's leverage grew by playing outside powers against each other
  • Strategic position at the border drove explosive commercial growth
  • ~2463: The Pavo Royal Family starts establishing relationships with both MUD and Ustur factions, using them to gain leverage and grow Pavo Passage's strength
  • ~2513: During the Convergence War, the Pavo royal family consistently changes sides, causing the emergence of both MUD and Ustur resentment. Both factions begin supporting the Pavos' political adversaries to overthrow their ruling

docs.title: The Night (Sector-Defining Event)
docs.text:
[!CAUTION] SECRET: The truth about "The Night" is the most closely guarded secret in Pavo Passage.

  • Public knowledge: The entire Crumon royal family, including Queen Cathris, vanished in a single night. The event remains officially unresolved.
  • The secret truth: ~2521: As opposition to the royal family grows, the Pavo royal family contracted the Order of Seasons (from the HRZ) — a group of mysterious assassins that claims to worship Iris — to assassinate political adversaries.
  • ~2522-09: In one year and one month, the Order of Seasons fulfilled their contract — slaughtering all opposition party leaders in the sector.
  • The Night: When the royals failed to pay for services rendered, the Order retaliated by killing the entire royal family. Only Ike Pavo survived — he was a baby, not present in the sector.
  • Political aftermath: Everyone strongly suspects the Order, but nobody speaks of it openly — fear keeps the truth buried. The Conclave uses "The Night" as justification for permanent "emergency doctrine."

docs.title: Modern Era
docs.text:
• The Dynasty collapsed; power vacuumed into the hands of competing elites
  • ~2565: Civil war erupts in Pavo Passage — Crumon loyalists fight to restore dynastic rule against the rest of the sector's factions. The loyalists ultimately surrender to preserve scraps of power
  • ~2567: The political groups of Pavo create a façade of democracy to formally end the civil war — the Ruling Conclave — splitting power amongst the various factions
  • The sector became a battleground of soft influence between MUD (via MTC/Synod) and Ustur (via Regency)

docs.title: Post-War Expansion
docs.text: • ~2524: With both MUD and Ustur exhausted from the Convergence War, neither can pressure Pavo any longer. The sector thrives in the power vacuum and begins spreading its influence across the Medium Risk Zone

docs.title: Governance: The Ruling Conclave
docs.text:
(Full details: canon/factions/ruling_conclave.md)

A small council of 6 seats + High Consul, operating from the Gilded Citadel on Cathris. Not a parliament — a control board that distributes choke points.

Seat Name Species True Allegiance High Consul (tie-break) Varn Crathis Crumon Dynasty independence Trade Oversight Alina Morcant MUD MTC (naive about Synod) Security Allocations Mara Vance MUD VHE + Ustur stability Permits & Classifications Korin.tchr Ustur Ustur stability Revenue & Accounts Silas Wren MUD MTC financial networks "Neutral" Seat Elias Dorn MUD Order of Seasons (secret)

docs.title: The Toll Machine (TAPP)
docs.text:
(Full details: canon/factions/toll_authority_of_pavo_passage.md)

The sector runs on a 4-stage toll system: Classify → Authorize → Route & Protect → Settle. No faction controls all four stages.

Anti-monopoly rule: No company may hold >3 active routes while the competitor has <1.

docs.title: Major Factions
docs.text: Faction Type Leader Canon File Crumon Dynasty Native dynastic power Ike Pavo / Varn Crathis canon/factions/crumon_dynasty.md Ruling Conclave Sector government Varn Crathis (High Consul) canon/factions/ruling_conclave.md MTC MUD/Synod front company Talia Vrenn canon/factions/meridian_trading_company.md VHE People's trading company Seraphine Vale canon/factions/vale_horizon_exchange.md Order of Seasons Iris Dusk assassin cult Priestess Delura (Mierese) canon/factions/order_of_seasons.md TAPP Toll infrastructure Faceless (Conclave-directed) canon/factions/toll_authority_of_pavo_passage.md Ustur Regency External stabilizer Korin.tchr / Ord.eldr canon/factions/ustur_regency_pavo.md Winter Fist Path Ustur martial sect Saelyra.soul canon/factions/winter_fist_path.md

docs.title: Worlds
docs.text: World Type Function Canon File Cathris Capital world Governance, bureaucratic power canon/geography/worlds/cathris.md Tollhaven Space station Commerce hub, route auctions, inspections TBD Crushspire Industrial planet Arco Crystal mines, Spire Foundries TBD Velloris Registry world Attestations, Proof Spine, agriculture TBD Okuto Dwarf planet Neutral buffer, black market, Exchange Gate TBD Scriptharbor TBD TBD TBD

docs.title: Key Infrastructure
docs.text:
• Starpath: Chain of warp gates linking the regions of Galia Expanse. The Starpath Auction Hall on Tollhaven is where route lanes are auctioned.
  • Tollstrand: The primary trade corridor running through the sector — inspections, escorts, routing
  • Arco Crystal: Critical mineral resource mined on Crushspire, controlled by the Crumon Dynasty

docs.title: Crumon Dynasty
docs.text: Name Species Role Warlord Pavo Crumon Founder (historical) Queen Cathris Crumon Last queen (deceased, The Night) Ike Pavo Crumon Head of royal family (Crushspire) Varn Crathis Crumon High Consul, branch family

Total named characters: 27 (including historical)

docs.title: The Balance of Power
docs.text:
The sector exists in managed imbalance:

  • MTC tries to make Pavo depend on MUD/Synod contracts
  • VHE keeps an alternative alive with Ustur backing
  • Crumon Dynasty plays both sides while rebuilding leverage
  • Ustur Regency audits and standardizes without occupying
  • Winter Fist Path deters military escalation
  • Order of Seasons ensures nobody wins too cleanly
  • The Conclave distributes blame and manages workflow

"In Pavo Passage, success looks like: nothing burning today."

docs.title: Open Questions (Resolved)
docs.text:
• ~~Q-PAV1: What is "Dusk" as an Iris aspect?~~ ✅ Resolved — 7th aspect, cosmic current period
  • ~~Q-PAV2: Who leads the Order of Season?~~ ✅ Priestess Delura (Mierese)
  • ~~Q-PAV3: Are surviving Pavo family members active?~~ ✅ Ike Pavo (Crushspire), Varn Crathis (Cathris)
  • ~~Q-PAV4: Who actually controls behind the façade?~~ ✅ 6-seat Conclave + TAPP + MTC/VHE competition
  • ~~Q-PAV5: What species mix?~~ ✅ Crumon (native), MUD majority, Ustur presence, Mierese (Delura)
  • ~~Q-PAV6: Military presence?~~ ✅ Winter Fist Path (Ustur sect), Conclave militia, MTC/VHE security

docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: PERGAMOS-VIII SECTOR (MRZ-4)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (User Input + Archon Audit, Feb 2026) Type: Medium Risk Zone (MUD-influenced — Economic Behemoth)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Located at the heart of the MUD Medium Zone, Pergamos stands as a behemoth of economic prowess — derived from abundant natural resources and deep connections to Galia's shadowy recesses. ~2529: Formally becomes a merchant republic, with the major wealth of the sector concentrated in a few families.

docs.title: The Grey Market
docs.text:
• Accommodating stance towards Jorvik and fringe entities.
  • ~2542: Pergamos formally begins forming alliances with major MRZ factions, becoming a major harbor for Jorvik, ECOS, and other outlaw groups
  • ~2553: Pergamos begins financing warring factions in the Abyd-IX conflict, making tons of ATLAS from war profiteering (canon/geography/sectors/abyd.md)
  • ~2574: The Merchants of Pergamos create the Shadow Contracts — a system of illegal and untraceable trading and financing instruments designed for the Medium Risk Zone's grey economy
  • Trades in the universal currency of ATLAS and remains indifferent to its origins.
  • Stark socioeconomic disparity: 10% of the population (human aristocracy) controls the biggest share of wealth.
  • ~2623: In the aftermath of Pergamos's loss in the Council of Peace intervention, the leading families of the sector are banished, and a new government, compliant with COP jurisdiction, is formed in Pergamos

docs.title: The COP Offensive
docs.text:
• ~2623: Trigger: COP (under Vaor Scarka's executive leadership) targeted Pergamos to curtail Jorvik/ECOS influence.
  • Weapon: The Mothership Cradle (COP's Titan — most powerful vessel in the galaxy) was deployed.
  • Result: Significant losses at the hands of Cradle's overwhelming force.
  • Shockwaves: Ex-colonies (Mycenas, Xianyang) threatened to intervene if COP persisted.
  • Resolution: Diplomatic negotiations led by Redam and MUD Faction halted hostilities.
  • Legal Counter: Opos.eldr (Ustur, COP Legal Branch) enacted rulings demanding cessation.

docs.title: Current Status
docs.text:
• Amidst rubble and remnants of conflict, Pergamos deliberates on its future trajectory.
  • The Cradle deployment demonstrated COP's willingness to use overwhelming force even in MRZ.

docs.title: Political Influence
docs.text:
• Alignment: Nominally independent, heavy Jorvik/criminal ties
  • Ruling Class: Human aristocracy (10%)
  • Key Event: COP military intervention (Cradle deployment)
  • Diplomatic Allies: Redam, Mycenas, Xianyang

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• COP Offensive: canon/institutions/council_of_peace.md
  • Cradle (Titan): canon/institutions/council_of_peace.md

docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: PERPETUAL BELT (MRZ-20)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — Perpetual Belt lore document + Archon Deep-Dive Zone: Medium Risk Zone (MRZ-20) Influence: ONI Theme: Gravitational Harmony, Rapid Seasons, Ecological Balance, Free Colonies

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: In the heart of the Perpetual Belt Sector, an awe-inspiring choreography unfolds. Planets, asteroids, moons, and debris fields glide seamlessly in a mesmerizing display of gravitational harmony, making it appear as though the cosmos itself composed their dance. The phenomenon appears natural, but nobody truly knows its origin.

docs.title: Orbital Mechanics
docs.text:
• Planets, asteroids, moons, and debris fields move in synchronized gravitational harmony
  • The choreography appears almost designed — cosmic bodies dancing in coordinated orbits
  • Origin: Unknown — appears natural, but the sheer perfection invites speculation
  • Navigation requires understanding the orbital rhythms — dangerous for the unprepared

docs.title: Rapid Seasonal Transitions
docs.text:
• Swift orbital rhythms produce rapid seasonal transitions
  • Within mere days, travelers witness the full cycle:
  • Crisp blossoms of spring → sultry hues of summer → autumn's golden embrace → winter's silent slumber
  • A breathtaking canvas of ever-changing landscapes

docs.title: MRZ Hazards
docs.text: [!NOTE] Not every MRZ sector is inherently deadly — some are relatively stable. Perpetual Belt's dangers stem specifically from its accelerated cycles.

docs.title: Accelerated Predators
docs.text:
• Fauna evolved with the rapid seasons have intense, compressed life cycles
  • Predators adapted to these rhythms are highly aggressive during specific cycle phases
  • Can catch visitors off guard with sudden behavioral shifts tied to seasonal changes

docs.title: Intense Climate Shifts
docs.text:
• Seasons change in days, not months
  • A temperate afternoon can become a blizzard within hours
  • Unprepared travelers risk exposure, equipment failure, and navigation loss
  • Infrastructure must withstand constant thermal and atmospheric cycling

docs.title: Native Ecology
docs.text:
• Sprawling flora and agile fauna have evolved in tandem with rapid seasonal cycles
  • Their life rhythms form an intricate ballet synchronized to the celestial dance overhead
  • Plants that bloom, fruit, wither, and regenerate within days
  • Animals with accelerated metabolic cycles matching the rapid seasons
  • A unique biosphere found nowhere else in Galia

docs.title: Settlements: Free ONI Colonies
docs.text:
• Free ONI colonies — akin to medieval free cities
  • Still linked to the ONI Consortium (not fully independent), but enjoy significant autonomy
  • Recognized the sector's potential and established numerous flourishing settlements
  • Key principle: harmonizing technology with the sector's natural cadence
  • Adapted infrastructure to rapid seasonal cycles rather than fighting them
  • The celestial ballet serves as a testament to balance and coexistence

docs.title: Narrative Hooks
docs.text:
• The Mystery: Perfect gravitational harmony with no known cause — ancient engineering? Iris? Something new?
  • Temporal Psychology: Living where seasons change in days — altered perception of time, aging, mortality (to be explored further with Creator)
  • Accelerated Biology: Rapid-cycle flora/fauna as research subjects — Coral Nebula pharma interest?
  • Free Cities: Political autonomy within ONI — potential friction, independence movements?
  • Navigation Challenge: Pilots who master the Belt's rhythms become elite navigators

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• ONI Consortium — canon/species/mierese.md, canon/species/sogmian.md
  • Free colony governance — contrast with Denebula's merchant princes and Frenir's DAO
  • Rapid-cycle biology — potential link to Coral Nebula pharma research (canon/geography/sectors/coral_nebula.md)

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ⏸️ Q-PER1: True origin of the gravitational harmony (deferred — unknown in-universe)
  • ⏸️ Q-PER2: Named colonies or settlements?
  • ⏸️ Q-PER3: Unique resources from rapid-cycle biology?
  • ⏸️ Q-PER4: Psychological effects on inhabitants — altered sense of time (Creator wants to explore)
  • ⏸️ Q-PER5: Named predator species?

docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: REDAM-VII SECTOR (MRZ-1)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (User Input + Archon Audit, Feb 2026) Type: Medium Risk Zone (MUD Ex-Colony — Independent)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Redam-VII stands as a testament to the MUD Faction's expansive efforts towards Iris. Although it has since gained independence, Redam has managed to nurture a harmonious, mutually beneficial relationship with its MUD originators — a distinction not shared by its sister sectors.

docs.title: Diplomatic Powerhouse
docs.text:
• Most stable amongst the MUD ex-colonies.
  • Commerce nexus: Strategic location bridging trade between the MUD Safe Zone and MRZ systems.
  • Diplomatic role: Spearheaded negotiations between MUD and Pergamos during the COP offensive crisis.

docs.title: Governance
docs.text:
• Elected leadership: Five distinguished humans, each from a distinct planet within the sector.
  • ~2524: The five leaders' rules are formally installed and governance becomes stabilized in the sector.
  • Elected Prime Minister serves as coordinator.
  • Steadfastly guards its autonomy against MUD and MRZ entities seeking to extend influence.

docs.title: Current Tensions
docs.text:
• Both MUD leadership and various MRZ entities have cast covetous eyes on Redam.
  • Governance remains vigilant. Stability is ever-elusive outside COP jurisdiction.
  • ~2555: Redam attempts to form a league of MRZ factions for mutual cooperation and defense — the effort fails, highlighting the deep fragmentation and mistrust between medium zone powers
  • Relationship with MUD is mutually beneficial but politics are always at play in Galia Expanse.
  • ~2623: Fearing the COP intervention in the Medium Zone, Redam reaches out to the old MUD colonies, Zenith Door, and Free Harbors to pressure the COP for a truce and limit their military activities — successfully forcing a ceasefire

docs.title: Political Influence
docs.text:
• Alignment: Pro-MUD (but independent)
  • Role: Regional diplomat and trade mediator

docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: SEGAL SECTOR (ONI 4)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (User Input, Feb 2026) Type: Safe Zone Sector (ONI 4 — Sogmian Homeland)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The chronicles of the Segal sector are etched with the valor and heartaches of the Sogmian warriors. For centuries, the Sogmian race found themselves mired in relentless internal conflicts — a consequence of their innate inability to curb their intense impulses.

docs.title: The Sogmian Vows (Du Prah)
docs.text:
• Origin: Formulated by the legendary Segal (namesake of the sector).
  • Purpose: A moral compass complemented by the Noble Houses system.
  • Result: Seven illustrious houses, each with distinct ethos, all bound by meritocracy.

docs.title: The Scars
docs.text:
• Vega Fall: The haunting corruption of House Akalma.
  • Convergence War: Near extinction of the Sogmian people.

docs.title: The Present
docs.text:
• From the shadows of their turbulent past, Segal emerges today as a beacon of genuine peace.
  • Sogmians are incapable of deception (the Du Prah forbids it). Their pursuit of peace is sincere.
  • Recalibrating perspectives, striving for equilibrium between age-old beliefs and the evolving Galia landscape.

docs.title: Dominant Power: The Noble Houses (Shared Power)
docs.text:
• The remaining Houses share power through the expertise of each House.
  • Each House has autonomy in its area of specialization.
  • All Houses answer only to the Sov Aurei (the supreme Sogmian leader).

docs.title: Systems & Locations (To be populated)
docs.text: • [System Name]

docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: SOLETUD SECTOR (Ustur 3)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (User Input + Archon Audit, Feb 2026) Type: Safe Zone Sector (Ustur 3 — Cosmic Archaeology)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Soletud is marked by the presence of an immense carcass of a commander-class vessel situated a few light minutes from its primary star.

docs.title: The Derelict Vessel
docs.text:
• Origin: From an unidentified civilization — the same one that existed in the Ka-dara Sector (details TBD — to be explored later).
  • Condition: Complex internal structures and inscriptions. Language and onboard systems remain largely indecipherable.
  • Key Detail: Shows no signs of external damage, combat, or any prior inhabitants. A pristine ghost.
  • ~2547: Ustur researchers uncover pivotal secrets from the derelict's internal systems and inscriptions. This knowledge is used to construct the Vzus Tenet — a powerful Titan-class ship stationed at the Vzus Defensive Belt in the Eternity system (canon/geography/sectors/eternity.md).

docs.title: Celestial Surveyors Syndicate
docs.text:
• Born from Discovery: The derelict shaped the Ustur's archaeological interests.
  • Role: A leading cosmic archaeology research institution.
  • Scope: Conducts activities throughout all of Galia Expanse.
  • Notable Member: A Photoli serves as a senior member — a self-described nerd and archaeology aficionado. No institutional connection to Photoli faction, purely personal passion.

docs.title: Economy & Colonies
docs.text:
• Resources: Abundant natural resources. Vast, fertile landscapes.
  • Settlement: Several decentralized, autonomous Ustur colonies.
  • Appeal: Prime location for resource extraction or peaceful habitation.

docs.title: Systems & Locations
docs.text:
• [Derelict Vessel location]
  • [Colony worlds]

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text: • Ka-dara Sector: canon/geography/ka_dara_sector.md

docs.title: ☀️ Canon Document: SUMMER SEA (MRZ-21)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — MRZ Ustur sector population Zone: Medium Risk Zone (MRZ-21) Influence: Ustur Theme: Extreme Heat, Underground Civilization, Resilient Ecosystems, Blue Giant Star

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Upon their arrival in this sector, the Ustur grappled with capturing the essence of the searing heat emanating from the colossal blue giant star that dominated the expanse. One member, with poetic flair, likened the experience to being engulfed in a vast sea of flames. While the metaphor resonated, they chose a milder name: Summer Sea.

Any visitor to the sector quickly realizes they're amidst the fiercest summer they've ever known.

docs.title: The Blue Giant
docs.text:
• Dominated by a colossal blue giant star
  • Relentless solar flares and oppressive heat challenge every life form
  • The sector is a crucible of scorching temperatures

docs.title: Resilient Ecosystems
docs.text:
• A handful of resilient species have evolved, scrapping for every precious drop of moisture
  • The planet's unique vegetation plays a pivotal role — ingeniously converting abundant gaseous deposits into water and other life-sustaining elements
  • This biological process prevents Summer Sea from becoming a barren wasteland

[!NOTE] Vegetation Canon: Summer Sea provides optimal conditions for this gas-to-water species — it does not grow naturally on other planets. However, once transplanted, the plants have a long lifespan, enabling commercial use across Galia. This makes Summer Sea the origin-source for a valuable bio-export.

docs.title: Underground Refuge
docs.text:
• Most inhabitants have wisely sought refuge underground, where the sun's blistering wrath is somewhat muted
  • Underground cities are Ustur-built — engineered subterranean settlements, not natural caverns
  • The Ustur have adapted to these extreme conditions
  • Subterranean civilization is the norm, not the exception

docs.title: Noctual Caverns
docs.text:
• ~2432: Ustur explorers discover the Noctual Caverns — a vast underground biome within one of Summer Sea's planets
  • ~2463: Ustur begin colonization despite harsh conditions, building the first underground colony named Foga-Rel deep within the Noctual Caverns
  • A self-sustaining ecosystem hidden beneath the scorching surface
  • Represents one of the most significant underground biome discoveries in MRZ history

docs.title: The Relic Barons Observatory (~2557)
docs.text:
• ~2557: The Relic Barons (canon/factions/relic_barons.md) install a massive space observatory at Summer Sea, powered by the nearly infinite energy of the sector's colossal blue giant star
  • Goal: To observe deep into the High Risk Zone — the extreme energy source enables detection ranges far beyond conventional instruments
  • This marks the first major Relic Barons presence outside Harkend (canon/geography/sectors/harkend.md)

docs.title: Travel Hazards
docs.text:
[!WARNING] A visit to Summer Sea without proper preparations could result in severe dehydration and skin afflictions.

  • Extreme surface heat from the blue giant
  • Solar flare exposure risk
  • Water scarcity (surface-level)
  • Specialized equipment required for interstellar travelers

docs.title: Narrative Hooks
docs.text:
• Underground cities: What do Ustur settlements look like when built to escape light rather than seek it?
  • Bio-conversion flora: The vegetation that turns gas into water — a resource worth controlling, stealing, or weaponizing
  • The poetic Ustur: The name itself implies Ustur can appreciate beauty even in brutality — a cultural detail
  • Survival culture: What kind of society forms when "going outside" is inherently dangerous?

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Ustur — canon/species/ustur.md
