docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: VALIANT SECTOR (MUD 2)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (User Input, Feb 2026) Type: Safe Zone Sector (MUD 2)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
"The road ahead is paved with trials... But remember, you are resilient, you are strong. Your destiny is solely your own." — The Revelation.

Valiant stands as a proud monument to human resilience. It was the first expansion frontier of the MUD civilization.

docs.title: The Translucid Pox
docs.text:
• The Event: An insidious alien disease hidden in ore veins ravaged the first settlers.
  • The Toll: 8% of the MUD population perished.
  • The Cure: MUD scientists ventured into the heart of affected planets to find a cure.
  • The Legacy: Survivors and their descendants are marked by extreme paleness, a badge of survival recognized across the sector.
  • Archon Inference: The Pox may be a by-product of ancient civilization mining that spread across multiple regions. The same civilization whose ruins lie in Amora may have left biological contamination in ores throughout the MUD sectors.

docs.title: Dominant Power: Scientia (The Scientists)
docs.text:
• The scientists who cured the Translucid Pox became the sector's heroes. Their legacy birthed a culture of "science above dogma".
  • Valiant is the center of MUD research, medical innovation, and technological advancement.
  • Tension with Verzan: Scientia's rationalism clashes directly with the Church's faith-based orthodoxy. Valiant citizens tend to view Verzan pilgrims with suspicion.

docs.title: Systems & Locations (To be populated)
docs.text: • [System Name]

docs.title: ⚔️ Canon Document: VEGA FALL (MRZ-24)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — Vega Fall lore document + Archon Deep-Dive + sogmian.md Zone: Medium Risk Zone (MRZ-24) Influence: ONI (Sogmian territory) Theme: Civil War, Tyranny, Shame, Redemption through Lutavira

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Vega Fall is a grim chapter in the annals of Sogmian history. A tale so heart-rending that to this day, uttering the names "Vega" or "House Akalma" brings visible discomfort to any Sogmian — a cultural shame comparable to how Germans view Nazism. The sector bears the name of the exposé that toppled a tyrant.

docs.title: The Luminous Gem
docs.text:
• In his prime, Vega was hailed as the most luminous gem of the Sogmian species
  • Member of House Akalma (Crimson Red — motto: "Embrace your emotions")
  • Achieved a meteoric rise through a series of dazzling military victories
  • Almost half of the upcoming Sogmian generation aspired to join House Akalma
  • Unanimously chosen as Sov Arei (Supreme Leader) by peers from all noble houses

docs.title: The Consolidation
docs.text:
• First act as Sov Arei: Relocated the Sogmian capital to this sector
  • Amassed unprecedented power — effectively a dictatorship
  • Introduced a heretical Du Prah interpretation: Vows applied exclusively to Sogmians; other species were "uncivilized" and unworthy

docs.title: The Exposé: "Vega Fall"
docs.text:
• A journalist from House Outro infiltrated Vega's citadel
  • Published the exposé "Vega Fall" — revealing enslavement, atrocities, and cannibalism of other species
  • The revelations united the fragmented houses into a coalition

docs.title: The Civil War
docs.text:
• Devastating conflict across Sogmian space
  • Culminated in Vega's last stand in his citadel — killed by coalition forces
  • House Akalma survivors banished to MRZ-12 (Akalma Exile)
  • This banishment led to another great tragedy a hundred years later — the Convergence War

docs.title: House Lutavira Headquarters
docs.text:
• The sector is majority Sogmian today
  • Vega's citadel has been reformed and restored
  • It now serves as the headquarters of House Lutavira (Violet — "Rise, win, conquer")
  • Lutavira = the house of natural-born leaders, the most fundamentalist Du Prah followers
  • Powerful symbolism: the legitimate leaders reclaiming the throne of the greatest tyrant

docs.title: Cultural Significance
docs.text:
• No formal prohibition against visiting or mentioning the sector
  • The discomfort is purely cultural — deep collective shame, not law
  • Comparable to how Germans view their Nazi past: acknowledged, studied, never celebrated
  • A living reminder of what happens when the Du Prah is corrupted

docs.title: Post-War Refuge
docs.text: • ~2523: House Outro opens the doors of Vega Fall for Convergence War refugees — the sector that once symbolized Sogmian shame now becomes a beacon of compassion, sheltering the displaced from across the galaxy

docs.title: The Akalma Exile's End (~2544)
docs.text:
• ~2544: The fleeing remnants of House Akalma encounter the Oxir — an enigmatic species in the HRZ
  • High Matron Odette of the Oxir directs them to a relatively safe location in the HRZ
  • Odette pleads that the Akalma survivors live in peace, hidden from the factions — they accept her proposal
  • This marks the final known destination of the Akalma exile, and their first contact with the Oxir species

docs.title: Multi-Species Prosperity (~2586)
docs.text:
• ~2586: After decades of cooperation between members of House Outro, House Patrah, and the refugees of the Convergence War, Vega Fall prospers — becoming a beacon for the multi-species cooperation ideology
  • The sector that once symbolized Sogmian tyranny now stands as proof that different species can build something together

docs.title: Narrative Hooks
docs.text:
• Lutavira's Burden: Operating from a tyrant's citadel — does the architecture haunt them? Do they find Vega's hidden chambers?
  • The Journalist: The unnamed House Outro journalist — one of the bravest in Sogmian history
  • Shame and Identity: Young Sogmians confronting ancestral guilt
  • Akalma Echoes: Are there Akalma remnants still hidden in the sector?

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• House Akalma — canon/species/sogmian.md (Banished house)
  • House Lutavira — canon/species/sogmian.md (Violet, Leaders, fundamentalist Du Prah)
  • House Outro — canon/species/sogmian.md (Journalist origin)
  • Akalma Exile — canon/geography/sectors/akalma_exile.md (MRZ-12)
  • Du Prah — canon/species/sogmian.md (Philosophy)
  • Convergence War — canon/history/convergence_war.md

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ⏸️ Q-VEG1: Name of the House Outro journalist?
  • ⏸️ Q-VEG2: Did Lutavira discover anything significant when reforming the citadel?
  • ⏸️ Q-VEG3: Named Lutavira leaders currently operating from Vega Fall?

docs.title: 🌟 Canon Document: VERBIS CLUSTER (MRZ-22)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — MRZ Ustur sector population Zone: Medium Risk Zone (MRZ-22) Influence: Ustur Theme: Binary Star System, Eternal Daylight, Photosynthetic Life, Near-Sentient Species

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
• ~2489: The Ustur discover the twin-star system of the Verbis Cluster

In the Verbis Cluster, a cosmic ballet unfolds as a pair of stars — one crimson, the other azure — dance in a perpetual orbit. Their radiant embrace eliminates darkness, gifting the sector's planets with unending daylight.

While these stars are positioned at a safe distance (ensuring the planets aren't scalded), their presence has nurtured an environment rich in extraordinary life.

docs.title: Binary Star System
docs.text:
• Red + blue binary stars in perpetual mutual orbit
  • The combined radiance eliminates darkness entirely
  • Planets experience eternal daylight — no night cycle exists
  • Stars are distant enough to prevent scorching, but close enough to bathe everything in constant light

docs.title: Unique Biosphere
docs.text:
• Ceaseless illumination has birthed species adapted to thrive in eternal luminescence
  • Many species harness the relentless light, converting it into sustenance (photosynthetic life at scale)
  • Life here is marked by truncated lifespans and a complete absence of sleep
  • This unique biology captivates biologists across Galia
  • Visiting species also suffer — constant light exposure naturally affects all biological organisms (sleep disruption, accelerated fatigue, skin damage)

docs.title: The Janar (Near-Sentient Species)
docs.text: [!IMPORTANT] The Janar are teetering on the brink of sentience — remarkable beings with natural biological manufacturing capabilities.

docs.title: Biology
docs.text:
• Possess a natural aptitude for photopolymerization and photochemical reactions
  • Their bodies become biological factories, producing compounds they later expel
  • These expelled compounds serve as the foundation for structures and tools they craft
  • A testament to their burgeoning intelligence — they are builders without technology

docs.title: Discovery
docs.text:
• ~2512: The Ustur formally discover the existence of the Janar
  • The Ustur build a scientific city called Pertuam to study the Janar and the Verbis Cluster

docs.title: Poacher Siege (~2533–2535)
docs.text:
• ~2533: Due to the Ustur weakening during the Convergence War, poachers begin raiding the Verbis Cluster to capture or exploit the Janar
  • Pertuam forces fight back against the invaders to protect the near-sentient species
  • ~2535: Although Pertuam forces ultimately win against the poacher raids, the planet suffers critical infrastructure attacks
  • The damage forces the Ustur to withdraw from the sector
  • Before leaving, the Ustur deploy an automated defense system to protect the Janar against foreign attackers

docs.title: Current Status
docs.text:
• Protected by automated Ustur defense systems — but the Ustur themselves have withdrawn
  • Other organizations still attempt to hunt and capture them
  • Maintain a cautious distance from other settlements and species
  • Their photopolymerization compounds have known industrial applications and would be extraordinarily valuable if harvested
  • The key deterrent to exploitation: Janar do not develop well in captivity — they cannot be farmed or factory-bred
  • This biological limitation, combined with Ustur/COP protection, is the only thing keeping them safe

docs.title: Narrative Hooks
docs.text:
• Pre-sentient contact dilemma: Do you protect the Janar or study them? And who decides?
  • Biological manufacturing: Janar compounds could revolutionize material science — if you can get them ethically
  • Sleepless civilization: What does culture look like when no one sleeps? What replaces dreams?
  • Eternal day psychology: How does constant light affect visiting species adapted to day/night cycles?
  • Poaching threat: The Janar are one leaked report away from galactic exploitation

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Ustur — canon/species/ustur.md
  • Summer Sea — canon/geography/sectors/summer_sea.md (another extreme-light MRZ sector, different mechanism)

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ⏸️ Q-VER1: Janar population estimate and distribution?
  • ⏸️ Q-VER2: Are any factions already aware of the Janar's capabilities?
  • ⏸️ Q-VER3: What do the Janar's structures/tools look like?
  • ⏸️ Q-VER4: Other settlements in the cluster — who lives here besides the Janar?
  • ⏸️ Q-VER5: Is the binary system stable or decaying? (long-term narrative implications)

docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: VERZAN SECTOR (MUD 4)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (User Input, Feb 2026) Type: Safe Zone Sector (MUD 4)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
"Trust in our paths crossing again. When they do, humankind shall witness the realization of my promise." — The Revelation.

Verzan is a sanctified nexus where God Ahr was last heard from before his departure.

docs.title: Key Lore
docs.text:
• Environment: Unforgiving landscapes and harsh living conditions.
  • Population: Devout pilgrims and enterprising settlers who have built sprawling metropolises.
  • Conflict: Recent murmurs of discord between religious factions and the broader populace.

docs.title: The Order of Light
docs.text:
• Nature: Ancient congregation of Photoli worshippers. Created by Photoli Reh to gain influence.
  • Location: Principal temple on a moon devoid of resources (purchased from MUD).
  • Secret: Reh shares minor powers with seniors to brainwash recruits.
  • Archon Inference: The Order predates Verzan, but Reh expanded to Verzan deliberately — Ahr's departure left a spiritual vacuum that made this population especially susceptible to Photoli-worship.

docs.title: Dominant Power: The Holy Servants (The Church)
docs.text:
• Verzan is the spiritual capital of MUD. The Church rules here with an iron hand, controlling pilgrimages, tithes, and doctrinal interpretation.
  • Internal Rivalry: The Order of Light (Photoli Reh's creation) competes for spiritual allegiance. The Church views them as heretical, but their growing influence among the populace is undeniable.
  • Tension with Valiant: Church orthodoxy clashes with Scientia's rationalism. The eternal war of faith vs reason plays out between these two sectors.

docs.title: Systems & Locations (To be populated)
docs.text: • [System Name]

docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: VOSTALGIA SECTOR (Ustur 2)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (User Input + Archon Audit, Feb 2026) Type: Safe Zone Sector (Ustur 2 — First Expansion)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Vostalgia — the Ustur civilization's maiden voyage amongst the stars. This expansion marked a profound milestone after their awakening on Ioki.

docs.title: The Mantrah Trees of Damarah
docs.text:
• The Encounter: Pioneering Ustur touched down on planet Damarah and found vast groves of Mantrah trees, leaves dancing in the winds.
  • The Emotion: These android beings, with only fragmented memories of their origins, were profoundly moved. The rhythmic rustle stirred an intense, deep-rooted nostalgia.
  • The Black Tears: A liquid akin to black tears formed in their eyes — the organic manifestation of Ustur beauty perception. Like when someone cries at a beautiful scene. Previously undocumented.
  • The Timing: Occurred before the Path of Enlightenment was formulated and Ioki's legacies were explored.
  • The Legacy: The Mantrah tree became the Ustur faction insignia and influenced the architectural aesthetics of their flagship.

docs.title: Archon Audit
docs.text:
• Trees & Cores: The Mantrah trees' leaf frequency resonates with the frequency of Ustur cores. This is why ONLY Ustur experience this reaction — other species are unaffected.
  • Black tears are a purely organic emotional response (Ustur have organic components that enable physical manifestations of emotion).

docs.title: Present Day
docs.text: • Many Ustur return to the serene Mantrah groves seeking both inspiration and traces of their storied past.

docs.title: Systems & Locations
docs.text: • Damarah (Mantrah Groves)

docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: WEEPING NEBULA SECTOR (MRZ-10)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (User Input, Feb 2026) Type: Medium Risk Zone (MUD-influenced — Hazardous / Crystal Mining)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
• ~2483: The Weeping Nebula is discovered in MRZ-10 — the beautiful but deadly environmental phenomenon initially makes research in the sector impossible

The Weeping Nebula owes its evocative name to a singular molecular cloud cradling its expanse — a cloud that produces ceaseless, ethereal rain across all planets.

docs.title: The Eternal Rain
docs.text:
• The molecular cloud exhibits a unique equilibrium: heats → expands → cools → contracts (rhythmic gravitational dance).
  • Precipitates a never-ending rain that blankets all system planets.
  • Extremely toxic to most known life forms.

docs.title: Crystal Harvest
docs.text:
• The toxic rain serves as a crucible for the birth of intricate crystals.
  • Occasionally the skies shimmer as crystals cascade down — creating a wealth of colorful minerals.
  • Archon Confirmed: These crystals are a recognized rare resource in the galactic economy.
  • No known species is immune to the rain — not even Ustur (android) or Tufa (metagenic). Collection is universally dangerous.

docs.title: Navigation
docs.text:
• Cloud dynamics make navigation perilous.
  • Only the most seasoned spacefarers dare traverse its tumultuous weather patterns.
  • Those who brave it are rewarded with unique sights.

docs.title: Iris Academy Exploration
docs.text:
• ~2573: Iris Academy professor Exfeheros (Punaab) develops specialized equipment that allows exploration of the Weeping Nebula's toxic rain — the first technological breakthrough to make research in the sector feasible
  • ~2576: After several failed attempts, Exfeheros and the Iris Academy manage to establish a small research settlement to study the Weeping Nebula — marking the first permanent presence in the sector

docs.title: Political Influence
docs.text: • Alignment: Frontier (hazardous mining)

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text: • Iris Academy / Exfeheros — canon/geography/sectors/zenith_door.md

docs.title: 🌌 Canon Document: XIANYANG-VI SECTOR (MRZ-5)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (User Input + Archon Audit, Feb 2026) Type: Medium Risk Zone (MUD Ex-Colony — Military Powerhouse)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The most powerful ex-colony in terms of military might, Xianyang-VI survived post-war turmoil through a pivotal alliance with the Gate Garrison, a formidable bounty-hunter collective from the High-Risk Zone. ~2529: Xianyang pivots into a military dictatorship to face the chaotic state of the Medium Risk Zone after the Convergence War.

docs.title: The Gate Garrison Alliance
docs.text:
• Who: A formidable bounty-hunter collective from the HRZ.
  • Composition: Human-founded, but accepts all species. The best-trained warriors in the galaxy.
  • ~2547: After being nearly overwhelmed by alien invaders, Xianyang-VI forges an alliance with Gate Garrison
  • Deal: In exchange for a strategic foothold in the region, the Garrison provided protective cover and support for the development of military technology using HRZ materials
  • Effect: Deterred aggressors eyeing newly independent MRZ territories.
  • ~2554: The Gate Garrison formally relocates its headquarters to Xianyang-VI, cementing the sector as a military powerhouse

docs.title: The Xianyang Enforcers
docs.text:
• Origin: Xianyang's own mercenary legion, inspired by the Gate Garrison's model.
  • Scope: Operates across every skirmish line in Galia Expanse.
  • Rite of Passage: ~2543: Xianyang starts mandatory public army service — every citizen, upon reaching maturity, must dedicate one year to the Enforcers.
  • ~2569: Xianyang-VI becomes the strongest ex-colony of MUD in military power and formally creates the Xianyang Enforcers as a mercenary group present in most of the battlefronts of Galia, to help finance their activities
  • Alumni Network: Many veterans leave and become independent mercenaries across the galaxy. This is how the Enforcers' presence spans every conflict — it's not a centralized army but a galactic network of alumni.
  • Reputation: Acclaimed across the galaxy.

docs.title: Military Expansionism
docs.text:
• Xianyang has been intensifying military prowess and influence across the MRZ.
  • Post-Pergamos COP offensive: Xianyang threatened to openly defy Council resolution.
  • The unfolding political drama is keenly observed across sectors.
  • Xianyang stands resilient, anchored in strength.

docs.title: Political Influence
docs.text:
• Alignment: Independent (military-first)
  • Allies: Gate Garrison (HRZ bounty hunters, multi-species)
  • Faction: Xianyang Enforcers (mercenary legion + alumni network)
  • Stance: Anti-COP, willing to defy Council

docs.title: 💀 Canon Document: YULDUN WASTE (MRZ-32)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — MRZ Ustur sector population Zone: Medium Risk Zone (MRZ-32) Influence: Ustur (lawless / ungoverned) Theme: Barren Wasteland, Criminal Haven, Ancient Space Station, Shadow Economy

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Yuldun Waste, to the uninitiated, appears to be a barren expanse within Galia. Devoid of bustling life, its planets are treacherous — either aggressively hostile to life or shrouded in tumultuous atmospheric tempests that deter even the most seasoned captains from daring a descent.

Yet while its surface seems vacant, the sector isn't entirely desolate. Some planets sustain legitimate economic activity and normal populations under Ustur domain, while the criminal underworld operates in the shadows alongside them.

docs.title: Planetary Conditions
docs.text:
• No habitable worlds — all planets are either actively hostile or storm-shrouded
  • Atmospheric tempests prevent safe landing on most surfaces
  • Appears barren to casual observation — this is part of its value

docs.title: The Ancient Station
docs.text:
[!IMPORTANT] Suspended amidst the cosmic void is an ancient space station of unknown origin. Its origins are lost to time. It has been repurposed into a clandestine refuge.

  • ~2496: Criminals from different species begin gathering in MRZ-32 — they take hold of the ancient decrepit space station and make it a refuge for criminals
  • Origins lost to time — no one knows who built it or why
  • Repurposed into a haven for outlaws, fugitives, and shadow operators
  • Within its shadowed corridors and dimly lit chambers: dark bargains, plots, and illegal salvage yards
  • Everything happens beyond the prying eyes of galactic authorities

docs.title: Legitimate Economy
docs.text:
• Some planets in the sector have normal populations conducting legitimate economic activity
  • These worlds follow Ustur domain governance
  • The sector is not purely criminal — it has a dual nature

docs.title: Shadow Economy (BMAH-Coordinated)
docs.text:
• The criminal operations and illicit locations are coordinated by the Black Market Auction House (BMAH)
  • The broader galactic economy overlooks this sector
  • Those in the know understand: the currency of secrets and shadows is sometimes more valuable than any mineral wealth
  • Clandestine trade, black market goods, and information brokering

docs.title: Illegal Salvage Yards
docs.text:
• Extremely popular in this region
  • Everything that arrives is disassembled and reassembled for resale
  • Stolen ships, war debris, contraband tech — all broken down and rebuilt into untraceable goods

docs.title: The Price of Sanctuary
docs.text:
• For those desperate enough — whether to hide or to hunt — Yuldun Waste offers sanctuary
  • But always at a price
  • The station operates on its own rules, outside galactic law

docs.title: Planet Etira — Former BMAH Capital
docs.text:
• Etira was the last capital of the Black Market Auction House (BMAH) — destroyed during the Never Alone incident (~2624)
  • Atmosphere was terraformed by the Photoli (~2135) to be lethally hostile — originally to seal the "Dreamer Below", a powerful creature imprisoned in its core
  • The Dreamer Below is now dead — destroyed during Never Alone. Its corpse was looted by the Dark Photoli Bah, who retrieved a silver gem from within
  • The COP invaded Etira post-Never Alone, destroying the BMAH's subsurface bunkers
  • The Church of the Dreamer Below — a memory-stealing cult founded by Herdus.soul — is destroyed
  • Etira is now effectively abandoned
  • Full details: canon/geography/worlds/etira.md

docs.title: Narrative Hooks
docs.text:
• Tortuga in space: The ancient station is a lawless hub — perfect setting for underworld storylines
  • Unknown builders: Who built the ancient station? Pre-Convergence? Alien origin? Connection to the Cataclysm?
  • Salvage yards: What are they salvaging? War debris? Ancient tech? Stolen ships?
  • Information as currency: Natural overlap with the SDU system from Lumik — does Rank B+ SDU trade happen here?
  • Okuto vs. Yuldun: Both are gray/black market hubs — how do they relate? (Okuto in Pavo is "neutral gray exchange"; Yuldun is fully criminal)

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Etira — canon/geography/worlds/etira.md (former BMAH capital, Dreamer Below destroyed, Bah's looting)
  • Church of the Dreamer Below — canon/factions/church_of_the_dreamer_below.md (destroyed cult)
  • Dark Photoli / Bah — canon/factions/dark_photoli.md (looted the Dreamer's corpse)
  • BMAH — canon/factions/black_market_auction_house.md (former Etira operations, now in transit)
  • SDU System — canon/systems/sdu_classification.md (shadow information trade likely uses SDU rankings)
  • Gate Garrison — canon/geography/sectors/xianyang.md (HRZ bounty hunters may pass through here)

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ✅ ~~Q-YUL1: Ancient station origins?~~ → Flavor text for now, no deep lore yet.
  • ✅ ~~Q-YUL2: Governance?~~ → Legitimate planets follow Ustur domain; criminal ops coordinated by BMAH.
  • ✅ ~~Q-YUL3: Salvage yards?~~ → Everything disassembled + reassembled for resale.
  • ✅ ~~Q-YUL4: BMAH connection?~~ → Yes, BMAH coordinates the criminal side of Yuldun Waste.

docs.title: ⏳ Canon Document: ZAVIJAVA ZONE (MRZ-34)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — MRZ Ustur sector population Zone: Medium Risk Zone (MRZ-34) Influence: Ustur (monitored by COP) Theme: Temporal Anomalies, Stolen Artifact, Time Distortion, Gate Garrison Intrigue

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Zavijava Zone is an enigmatic sector where time doesn't always obey conventional rules. Under still-mysterious conditions, temporal distortions unpredictably ripple through the fabric of time, affecting the sector's ecosystem in myriad ways.

docs.title: Public Knowledge
docs.text:
• Before the Convergence War, Zavijava was a nondescript sector of Galia
  • ~2531: After the war concluded and the COP treaty was ratified, peculiar time-based phenomena started occurring
  • It was as though a cosmic hex had been cast upon the sector
  • Publicly, there is no clue as to what causes the distortions

docs.title: The True Origin (Hidden Lore)
docs.text:
[!CAUTION] This information is not publicly known in-universe. Only a handful of entities are aware of the true cause.

  1. HRZ relic hunters stole an unknown artifact from one of the worlds dominated by the Tufa Titan Bklok
  2. They fled to Zavijava Zone to hide, taking refuge in the depths of an underground city
  3. Their plan was to wait for the heat to die down, then transport the artifact to Yuldun Waste and sell it through the BMAH
  4. One of the thieves attempted to steal the artifact for himself and flee alone
  5. He was caught in the act — a fight broke out among the group
  6. During the combat, the artifact was accidentally activated
  7. It began generating the random temporal anomalies that have plagued Zavijava ever since

The artifact remains somewhere in that underground city, still active, still generating distortions — but slowly running out of energy.

docs.title: Manifestations
docs.text:
The anomalies manifest in capricious, unpredictable ways:

  • Time acceleration: Within a ~2km radius, days may pass as mere minutes
  • Time stasis: Creatures can be suspended in a temporal freeze — seemingly frozen, only to resume life years later, untouched by the passage of years
  • Effects are localized and random — no pattern has been identified
  • No known way to predict, control, or prevent them

docs.title: Anomaly Status
docs.text:
• The phenomenon is stable but weakening over time
  • The artifact's energy is being spent and there is no nearby source to recharge it
  • Eventually, the anomalies will cease entirely — but the artifact itself will remain

docs.title: Impact on Daily Life
docs.text:
• Despite the tangible risk of eternal time freeze, inhabitants approach it with humor and resignation
  • Popular local jest:

"The chances of being stuck in time are slimmer than snagging the galactic lottery. But should I ever hit that jackpot, I'll simply relocate from Zavijava, and all will be well!"

docs.title: Public Face
docs.text:
• A coalition of neutral, esteemed scholars and temporal researchers
  • Have established a research base in the sector
  • Stated dual objective:
  • Decipher the mysteries of Zavijava's time anomalies
  • Harness the potential power of time manipulation
  • So far, their efforts have been in vain
  • The Council of Peace keeps tabs on everything happening in Zavijava

docs.title: Secret Reality
docs.text:
[!WARNING] The Vortex Watchers are secretly funded and oriented by the Gate Garrison. The Gate Garrison is discreetly searching for the artifact within the sector. The Gate Garrison's leader has extreme personal interest in recovering the artifact as quickly as possible.

  • The "neutral research" is a front — the real objective is to locate the Tufa Titan artifact
  • The Gate Garrison is the true power behind the Vortex Watchers
  • Full Gate Garrison motivations will be revealed with that faction's deep lore

docs.title: Narrative Hooks
docs.text:
• Artifact hunt: The most powerful object in the sector is buried in an underground city, guarded by temporal anomalies it created
  • Gate Garrison's true agenda: The bounty hunters aren't just funding research — they're running an extraction operation
  • COP vs. Gate Garrison: COP monitors Zavijava but doesn't know about the artifact or Gate Garrison involvement
  • Bklok wants it back: A Tufa Titan's artifact was stolen — does Bklok know where it ended up?
  • Ticking clock: The artifact is losing energy — when it dies, the anomalies stop but the artifact becomes findable
  • Living time capsules: People frozen in stasis from years ago could awaken into a completely changed galaxy

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Tufa Titan Bklok — artifact stolen from one of Bklok's worlds
  • Gate Garrison — canon/factions/gate_garrison.md (secretly funds Vortex Watchers)
  • BMAH — canon/geography/sectors/yuldun_waste.md (original destination for the artifact)
  • Council of Peace — monitoring Zavijava (unaware of true cause)
  • Yuldun Waste — canon/geography/sectors/yuldun_waste.md (planned fence location)

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ✅ ~~Q-ZAV1: Convergence War connection?~~ → Not the war itself. HRZ relic hunters stole a Tufa Titan artifact and activated it accidentally in Zavijava.
  • ⏸️ Q-ZAV2: Vortex Watchers — any named leaders or notable researchers?
  • ⏸️ Q-ZAV3: Has anyone ever been "unfrozen" from stasis and reported what they experienced?
  • ✅ ~~Q-ZAV4: Anomalies getting worse or fading?~~ → Stable but weakening. Artifact energy depleting with no recharge source.
  • ⏸️ Q-ZAV5: What happened to the original relic hunters? Dead? Frozen in time? Scattered?
  • ⏸️ Q-ZAV6: Does Bklok know the artifact is in Zavijava?

docs.title: 🚪 Canon Document: ZENITH DOOR (MRZ-36)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided + Archon Cross-Reference Enrichment, Mar 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — Zenith Door lore document + cross-referenced from ustur.md, iris_academy.md, church_of_the_dreamer_below.md, bmah.md, never_alone_campaign.md, punaab.md, anfoil_states.md, eras_of_star_atlas.md, weeping_nebula.md, lumik.md, eol_garadar.md, bluevael_mining_colonies.md Zone: Medium Risk Zone (MRZ-36) Influence: ONI (ONI/Ustur border — closest ONI territory to HRZ) Theme: Diplomatic Capital, Iris Academy HQ, Dawn Vanguard, Chior's Peace Scale: T2 — Sector Power (Chior.eldr governs the entire sector via diplomatic consensus)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
"What truly drives the ruler of the Zenith Door?" The answer is known: genuine peace. But to the rest of Galia, whispers persist.

The Zenith Door stands as a pivotal nexus within the frontiers of ONI and Ustur territory — the closest ONI territory to the borders of the HRZ. Home to the galaxy's foremost academic institution and the COP's elite border fleet, it is ruled by one of the canon's most important characters: Chior.eldr, an Ustur Elder who dreams of a galaxy without factions or wars.

Zenith Door is not merely a sector — it is the origin point of four interlocking storylines that define Galia's modern era:

  1. Chior's peacekeeping crusade — a centuries-long diplomatic project
  2. The Iris Academy — Galia's greatest research institution, architect of Project Starpath
  3. The Herdus Tragedy — a co-founder's descent into madness, cult prophecy, and criminal empire
  4. The COP Crisis — the irony of a peace council waging war, and the Elder trying to stop it

docs.title: Strategic Importance
docs.text:
• Nexus between ONI and Ustur territory
  • Closest ONI sector to the HRZ borders
  • Natural chokepoint for any HRZ expansion or exploration
  • Home to both Galia's premier academic institution and the COP's elite border defense fleet

docs.title: Founding (~2539)
docs.text:
• ~2539: Chior.eldr and his disciple Herdus.soul discover the wrecked sector of Zenith Door
  • Together they mobilize the local population and, using all of their personal resources, manage to stabilize the sector
  • This act of selfless reconstruction establishes Chior's reputation and lays the foundation for Zenith Door's future as a diplomatic capital
  • ~2545: Chior relocates the Iris Academy (conceptually founded ~2525) to Carvum, an abandoned mining planet in Zenith Door, establishing it as the institution's permanent headquarters

[!NOTE] The Iris Academy was conceptually founded ~2525 by Chior.eldr and Professor Exfeheros during the post-war rebuilding period. It was physically relocated to Carvum in Zenith Door ~2545, after Chior had stabilized the sector (~2539). Both dates are canon — they describe different milestones.

docs.title: The Figure (Major Canon Character)
docs.text:
• Species: Ustur — rank .eldr (Elder), highest tier of Ustur hierarchy
  • Born: ~2391
  • Trained by: Armi.eldr — the faction leader who guided the Ustur through the Convergence War
  • Twin pupil alongside: Ponel.eldr — who would later become his philosophical opposite
  • As an Elder: virtually immortal (fused with core), can be revived via the Pool of Reincarnation
  • Full artifact-level precision in the Ustur Elder Conscience
  • An exotic Elder who deviates from other Ustur Elders' agendas
  • One of the principal characters of the entire Star Atlas lore

docs.title: Chior's Vision
docs.text:
• Seeks balance and peaceful coexistence in Galia
  • Dreams of a galaxy without factions or wars
  • Uses power, wealth, and influence always toward this objective
  • A genuine neutral agent — not aligned with any faction's expansionist goals
  • Possesses unparalleled diplomatic finesse — brokered agreements with almost every significant Galia faction
  • Maintains Zenith Door as an oasis for diplomacy

docs.title: Timeline
docs.text: Year Event Source ~2391 Born — trained by Armi.eldr alongside Ponel.eldr ustur.md ~2430 Sent to Azut sector to help create a new Ustur metropolis ustur.md ~2523 Witnesses Convergence War devastation — vows to never allow it again ustur.md ~2523-2539 Wanders the MRZ with disciple Herdus.soul, aiding war-ravaged populations ustur.md ~2525 Co-founds the Iris Academy concept with Professor Exfeheros iris_academy.md ~2539 Discovers and stabilizes wrecked Zenith Door sector using personal resources (with Herdus) zenith_door.md, church_of_the_dreamer_below.md ~2545 Relocates Iris Academy to Carvum, Zenith Door sector church_of_the_dreamer_below.md ~2559 Herdus leaves Chior — begins wandering the MRZ alone bmah.md ~2623 Calls for COP cease-fire vote: army limits + MRZ population rights never_alone_campaign.md ~2623 Free Cities of Humanity pressure COP via Zenith Door + Free Harbors for MRZ truce eras_of_star_atlas.md

docs.title: The Chior-Ponel Divergence
docs.text:
Chior and Ponel were twin pupils of Armi.eldr — the leader who guided the Ustur through the Convergence War. After the war, they diverged completely:

  • Chior → stayed in the Elder Order, became Galia's foremost pacifist diplomat (Zenith Door)
  • Ponel → broke away to found the Anfoil State (MRZ-15) — a meritocratic technate that suppresses emotion

Their opposing philosophies — Chior's empathy-driven diplomacy vs Ponel's cold efficiency — represent the fundamental Ustur schism. Ponel even attempted to bribe or destroy the Iris Academy, viewing it as a rival to Anfoil's own intellectual project.

docs.title: The Herdus Tragedy
docs.text:
The ghost of Herdus haunts Zenith Door's success.

Chior's greatest failure and Zenith Door's darkest chapter: his own disciple's descent into madness.

Herdus.soul co-founded Zenith Door alongside Chior in ~2539 and likely helped establish the early Iris Academy. But by ~2559, he had left his master and begun wandering the MRZ alone — his reasons unclear: disillusionment? Restlessness? The first whispers of something darker?

docs.title: The Fall
docs.text:
Year Event ~2539 Co-founded Zenith Door with Chior. A beacon of learning and peace ~2545 Likely involved in establishing the Iris Academy at Carvum ~2559 Leaves Chior.eldr — begins wandering the MRZ alone ~2569 After ascending to the .soul stage, Herdus goes insane — the vast expansion of consciousness may have exposed him to the Dreamer Below's psychic influence ~2577 A mysterious Photoli delivers "The Revelation" — Herdus founds the Church of the Dreamer Below and joins the BMAH in Yuldun Waste ~2577-2624 Rises to Leading Auctioneer of the BMAH while secretly channeling resources to the Church ~2605 Takes infant Saand (Punaab) as a vessel for the Dreamer Below ~2624 Killed by Saand during the Never Alone incident — Church exposed, BMAH scattered

[!IMPORTANT] Herdus.soul is a pivotal character connecting four major storylines: Zenith Door (co-founder with Chior.eldr), Church of the Dreamer Below (founder), BMAH (former leader), and the Never Alone incident (his death). His arc spans from idealist peace-builder to insane cult prophet to underworld kingpin.

docs.title: Iris Academy (HQ: Planet Carvum)
docs.text:
"The search for knowledge is the common unifying endeavor of all sentient species." — Professor Exfeheros, Headmaster

The galaxy's most prestigious research institute in the known sectors of Galia. A neutral institution devoted to advancing knowledge for the greater good.

Field Value Founded ~2525 (concept); ~2545 (relocated to Carvum, Zenith Door) Founders Chior.eldr and Professor Exfeheros Platinum Bar HQ Planet Carvum — abandoned mining planet, Zenith Door sector Leader Professor Exfeheros Platinum Bar — Headmaster Governance Council of Professors — Headmaster elected from among them COP Status Allied but autonomous — direct COP Senate seat. Likened to Cambridge/Oxford with parliamentary seats Branch Anchorage — outpost in Glowhaven AI An AI overseer monitors faculty and student activities

docs.title: Professor Exfeheros Platinum Bar
docs.text:
• Species: Punaab (confirmed via naming convention and multiple cross-references)
  • One of Galia's greatest intellectuals and scientists
  • Friend and ally of Chior.eldr
  • ~2573: Developed specialized equipment enabling exploration of the Weeping Nebula's toxic rain — the first technological breakthrough of its kind
  • ~2576: Established a small Iris Academy research settlement in the Weeping Nebula
  • Authorized a deep-sea exploration expedition to investigate anomalous readings from the Bluevael Prime Dead Zone
  • Ponel.eldr (Anfoil State) has attempted to bribe or destroy the Iris Academy — viewing it as a rival

docs.title: Carvum — The Academy Planet
docs.text:
• An abandoned mining planet — Carvum's ancient mines still generate significant revenue as the Academy's sole planetary proprietor
  • Features rural villages alongside multiple research institutions
  • Retired Commander Goe (MUD) lives on a farm near the Academy — a Convergence War veteran who led HRZ expeditions
  • Selected for its strategic location: near the HRZ threshold, within reach of COP governing bodies — a perfect balance of scientific exploration and political influence

docs.title: Major Achievements
docs.text:
• Project Starpath — the Academy's defining breakthrough: a revolutionary network of warp gates that reconnected the fractured galaxy and reignited interstellar trade. Ushered in a new Golden Age
  • Galactic Indices: GFI-8 (no army, but former students include top military strategists), GWI-5 (largest research institute, patents, endowments), GPI-3 (COP Senate seat, shapes policy from behind the scenes)
  • Galactic partnerships: Cooperation agreement with the Scriptorium of Lumikir; Anchorage branch in Glowhaven; research settlement in Weeping Nebula; expedition authorized to Bluevael Dead Zone

docs.title: The Dawn Vanguard
docs.text:
• COP elite fleet created specifically to guard the HRZ/MRZ border
  • Flagship: C11-class capital ship — commanded by a Sogmian officer
  • Capable of obliterating a squadron of Fimbul Mambas with a single missile barrage
  • Equipped with tractor beams for rescue and recovery operations
  • Elite troops, extremely powerful — first line against HRZ threats
  • Stationed permanently at Zenith Door due to its geographic position

docs.title: The Paradox of Zenith Door
docs.text:
An elite military force stationed in a pacifist's domain. Chior did not invite the Dawn Vanguard — the COP stationed them here. Their presence is simultaneously:

  • A shield — protecting Zenith Door from HRZ threats (Tufa incursions, unknown dangers)
  • A sword — that could be turned on Chior if his opposition to Scarka becomes too threatening
  • A symbol — of the COP's reach into even the most peaceful corners of Galia

docs.title: The COP Crisis: Chior vs. Vaor Scarka
The COP's War
docs.text:
[!WARNING] The "war" Chior opposes refers to recent COP invasions of MRZ territories — including Pergamos and Frenir. The peace-keeping body has become an aggressor.

  • The COP, under Vaor Scarka's influence, has invaded MRZ sectors:
  • Pergamos — COP military intervention (canon/geography/sectors/pergamos.md)
  • Frenir — COP-imposed order after warlord era (canon/geography/sectors/frenir.md)
  • Scarka's declared milestones: Frenir slave traders destroyed, Jorvik influence disrupted, Pergamos agreements signed
  • Despite a declared cease-fire, the threat of escalation remains

docs.title: Chior's Counter-Offensive (Diplomatic)
docs.text:
• ~2623: Chior.eldr vocally opposes the COP's military expansionism
  • Calls for an emergency cease-fire vote: COP army limits + MRZ population rights
  • Proposes an emergency motion for Council DAO approval
  • Behind the scenes, the Ustur faction seeks a peaceful resolution

docs.title: The Free Cities of Humanity
docs.text:
• ~2623: Redam (MRZ-1) reaches out to old MUD colonies including Zenith Door and Free Harbors to pressure COP into accepting a truce in the MRZ
  • This loose alliance — the Free Cities of Humanity — represents the first organized MRZ-wide resistance to COP military expansion, and Chior is its most prominent voice

docs.title: The Rivalry
docs.text:
• Chior.eldr: Genuine peace through diplomacy, consensus, and centuries of patient relationship-building
  • Vaor Scarka: Victory only through vanquishing adversaries and expanding COP dominion
  • This is the galaxy's highest-stakes rivalry — an immortal Elder vs. the most powerful military commander in Galia

docs.title: Diplomatic Network
docs.text:
Chior's centuries of diplomatic work have woven Zenith Door into a web of relationships across Galia:

Partner Nature Notes Redam (MRZ-1) Free Cities ally Leading the MRZ truce pressure on COP Free Harbors Free Cities ally Joined Redam's outreach to pressure COP Iris Academy Home institution Chior co-founded it; Exfeheros is his closest ally Eol Garadar Philosophical parallel Both seek peace — Chior via diplomacy, Zagah.eldr via action Anfoil State Philosophical rival Ponel.eldr's breakaway — opposite methods, shared Ustur origin COP Tense alignment Opposes Scarka's militarism but works within COP structures

docs.title: Narrative Hooks
docs.text:
• Chior's Crusade: An immortal Elder using centuries of wisdom to prevent galactic war
  • Exfeheros & Chior: The academic and the diplomat — allies shaping Galia's future from Zenith Door
  • COP as Aggressor: The irony of a peace council waging war — and the man trying to stop it from within
  • Dawn Vanguard Tension: An elite military force stationed in a pacifist's domain — shield, sword, or leash?
  • HRZ Gateway: What comes from beyond the border that requires this level of defense?
  • The Herdus Ghost: Chior's co-founder became a cult prophet and criminal kingpin — does Chior know the full truth?
  • The Chior-Ponel Mirror: Two students, one master, opposite conclusions — who was right?
  • Iris Academy Secrets: Hidden vaults of classified combat technology. What else does Carvum hide beneath its mining tunnels?

docs.text:

  • Dawn Vanguard — COP fleet (canon/institutions/council_of_peace.md)

  • Prof. Exfeheros — canon/factions/iris_academy.md, canon/species/punaab.md (Punaab, Academy Headmaster)

  • COP invasions — Pergamos (canon/geography/sectors/pergamos.md), Frenir (canon/geography/sectors/frenir.md)

  • Iris Academy partnerships — Scriptorium of Lumikir (canon/geography/sectors/lumik.md), Bluevael investigation (canon/factions/bluevael_mining_colonies.md)

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ✅ ~~Q-ZEN1: Species/rank~~ → Ustur Elder (confirmed via ustur.md)
  • ✅ ~~Q-ZEN2: Chior.eldr full character deep-dive~~ → Full timeline compiled from birth (~2391) through COP crisis (~2623). Trained by Armi.eldr, twin pupil with Ponel.eldr
  • ✅ ~~Q-ZEN3: Iris Academy full structure and research areas~~ → Council of Professors, AI overseer, Project Starpath, Carvum details. Further department-level detail deferred for Creator
  • ✅ ~~Q-ZEN4: Dawn Vanguard — fleet composition, commander?~~ → C11 capital ship, Sogmian commander, missile barrages, tractor beams. Commander's name still unknown
  • ✅ ~~Q-ZEN5: Prof. Exfeheros — species, background?~~ → Punaab. Full name "Exfeheros Platinum Bar". Greatest scientist in Galia. Developed Weeping Nebula exploration tech
  • ⏸️ Q-ZEN6: COP invasions full timeline and details (partially enriched — Pergamos + Frenir confirmed, Scarka's truce milestones added)
