  • ⏸️ Q-ZEN11: What is the sector's economy beyond the Iris Academy?
  • ⏸️ Q-ZEN12: What other factions operate in or pass through Zenith Door?

docs.title: 🪐 Worlds
docs.text:
Notable planets, stations, and locations.

  • Cathris
  • Crushspire
  • Etira
  • Okuto
  • Tollhaven
  • Velloris
  • Veshmora

docs.title: 👑 Canon Document: CATHRIS (Capital World of Pavo Passage)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — World Sheet 01 Type: Capital World Sector: Pavo Passage (MRZ-17) Function: Seat of governance, bureaucratic power center Named after: Queen Cathris (Crumon Dynasty), who disappeared during "The Night"

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Cathris is the capital world of Pavo Passage: polished authority built atop old grief. It is where toll power becomes law, where commerce becomes governance, and where the sector pretends it is stable. Everything here is designed to look permanent — because everyone remembers how quickly permanence died during The Night.

docs.title: Environment / "Feel"
docs.text: Cathris is a world of controlled spaces: administrated districts, sealed corridors, curated skylines, and heavily monitored transit arteries. The atmosphere is less "natural planet" and more "capital machine" — a place where you feel watched even when you can't see cameras, and where silence in the wrong hallway can mean you're already late.

docs.title: Governance & Faction Presence
docs.text: Faction Presence Ruling Conclave Home territory. Appointments, permissions, and quiet deals radiate from here Crumon Dynasty Dynastic legitimacy clings (the name itself is propaganda + mourning). Varn Crathis holds institutional power Ustur Regency Present through compliance doctrine and audit pressure ("stability without occupation") MTC / Synod Not openly sovereign, but embedded in contracts, credit gravity, and trade politics VHE Liaison presence defending clean passage and citizen-facing legitimacy Order of Seasons / Duskreach Not visible. Cathris is paranoid about ghosts — the best place to hide is where everyone assumes they're secure

docs.title: Economy & Exports
docs.text:
Cathris exports permission: stamps, classifications, approvals, exceptions, and "legal reality."

The physical economy rides on high-margin services:

  • Administrative licensing
  • Arbitration
  • Security contracting
  • Financial routing

The real commodity is access — to corridors, to schedules, to exceptions, to outcomes.

docs.title: 1) The Gilded Citadel
docs.text:
The seat of the Conclave: a fortress-palace of procedure, where decisions are dressed as votes and power is distributed across chairs. Everyone enters as a petitioner and leaves as a debtor — or doesn't leave at all.

Control: Conclave (institutional), dynastic influence (contested)

docs.title: 2) The Appointment Gallery
docs.text:
A public-facing hall of legitimacy: carved lists, rotating seals, recorded oaths. Where "responsibility holders" are announced, so the sector knows exactly who to blame when a route collapses or a permit vanishes.

Control: Conclave

docs.title: 3) The Stampworks District
docs.text:
A dense bureaucracy-industrial zone where permits, classifications, and compliance artifacts are physically produced, archived, and authenticated. Most citizens will never see its inner floors — only the output that determines their life.

Control: Conclave machinery; Ustur audit pressure

docs.title: 4) Citadel Undercorridors ("The Quiet Miles")
docs.text:
Restricted service corridors beneath official routes: maintenance lanes, sealed doors, dead cameras, and old access points nobody admits exist. Rumors insist some passages are older than the current regime — and that some are not entirely human in their logic.

Control: Officially "no one"; practically, whoever knows the doors

[!NOTE] The Quiet Layer Night — when doors seal, sublevels go silent, and "maintenance" takes priority — is believed to be when these corridors are actively used for deniable operations.

docs.title: 5) The Ledger Court
docs.text:
A formal chamber for disputes: fines, seizures, toll conflicts, and claims. Where wealth is laundered into legality and where smaller operators come to die slowly — unless they have the right advocate.

Control: Conclave (formal); MTC/VHE influence (economic pressure)

docs.title: 6) The Skychain Transit Spine
docs.text:
A high-capacity transit artery linking capital nodes: cargo gates, diplomatic lanes, and heavily monitored movement corridors. The world's visible proof that Cathris "works" — and the easiest place to detect a disruption.

Control: Operators under Conclave appointments; Ustur oversight standards

docs.title: 7) The Queen's Absence Memorial
docs.text:
A sanctioned memorial to Queen Cathris and The Night: simultaneously mourning and warning. People leave offerings, but also watch who leaves offerings — because grief is political on Cathris.

Control: Civic; heavily monitored

docs.title: 8) The Pale Band Shelter
docs.text:
A discreet Winter Fist Path sanctuary corridor for mediation and emergency shelter. No violence inside the threshold. Many citizens treat it as the only place on Cathris where power can't bargain.

Control: Winter Fist Path (Ustur sect presence), tolerated by the Conclave

docs.title: Travel Hazards
docs.text:
• Administrative disappearance: You don't get arrested; your permit stops existing
  • Reclassification shock: A cargo becomes "risk," a ship becomes "suspicious," a crew becomes "liable"
  • Audit storms: Ustur pressure can freeze operations without firing a shot
  • Quiet layer exposure: Wrong hallway, wrong door, wrong camera minute — and you become a rumor
  • Ghost panic: If fear of the Order spikes, the city overcorrects — more checkpoints, more detentions, more "accidents"

docs.title: Story Fuel
docs.text:
• Cathris is a world where legality is manufactured, and morality is negotiated in hallways
  • The name "Cathris" is both homage and weapon: it keeps The Night alive in public memory
  • Every faction maintains an office here, but the real fight is for workflow control: appointments, exceptions, routing priority, and settlement outcomes

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Ruling Conclave — ruling_conclave.md
  • Crumon Dynasty — crumon_dynasty.md
  • TAPP — tapp.md
  • Winter Fist Path — winter_fist_path.md (Pale Band Shelter)
  • Queen Cathris — disappeared during "The Night" (crumon_dynasty.md)
  • Pavo Passage — pavo_passage.md

docs.title: 👑 Canon Document: CRUSHSPIRE (Arco World / Dynastic Stronghold)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — World Sheet 03 Type: Industrial planet / dynastic stronghold Sector: Pavo Passage (MRZ-17) Function: Resource-backed power — where dynasty becomes infrastructure Primary Export: Arco Crystal Dynastic Head: Ike Pavo (operates from here)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Crushspire is the sector's most dangerous kind of power: resource-backed. Where Cathris rules through procedure and Tollhaven rules through flow, Crushspire rules through what everyone needs — industrial output, hard labor, and the dynastic wealth still pulsing through Arco Mines.

It is the center of gravity for Crumon loyalists, and the place where Ike Pavo can convert nostalgia into leverage.

docs.title: Environment / "Feel"
docs.text:
Crushspire is harsh, industrial, and vertical: quarry cities, mining towers, slag fields, pressure-sealed tunnels, and worker habs stacked around extraction shafts.

The world feels like constant vibration — machinery, drilling, convoy traffic, and the tension of people who know that one shutdown could starve half the sector.

docs.title: Governance & Faction Presence
docs.text: Faction Presence Crumon Dynasty Strongest here — dynastic base, loyalist recruitment pool, seat of practical wealth Ike Pavo Operates as head of royal family from Crushspire; access to dynastic wealth via Arco Mines Ruling Conclave Present through permits, extraction quotas, directors trying to keep Arco flowing without letting Dynasty become monopoly-state Ustur Regency Audits and standards; watches for militarization and destabilizing hoarding MTC / Synod Seeks to bind Arco into contracts/credit networks, turning raw wealth into dependency VHE Smaller footprint than MTC, but culturally important where it provides fair employment and transparent shipping Order of Seasons Never claimed; rumors persist about "sealed shafts" and sanctums hidden in infrastructure

docs.title: Economy & Exports
docs.text:
Primary export: Arco Crystal (via the Arco Mines), refined industrial outputs, and heavy-material supply chains.

[!NOTE] Arco Crystal Properties: "The fleeting Arco crystal has the ability to retain its sturdiness under high temperatures while also being efficient in dissipating kinetic energy." — Used in armor, shields, heat-resistant hull components, and kinetic dampening systems. A rare mineral with wide strategic applications. Crushspire is the largest source of Arco Crystal in Galia Expanse (not exclusive, but dominant).

Secondary outputs: Mining equipment, fabricated parts, repair services for extraction fleets.

True export: Leverage. Whoever influences Arco supply influences shipbuilding, bargaining, and survival across the corridor.

Economic reality: The Arco Mines are profitable and keep the Crumon Dynasty financially alive — but they could be vastly more lucrative if not for the Spire Foundries bleeding the dynasty's profits through ruinous maintenance costs. The Foundries are a financial anchor on what should be even greater wealth.

docs.title: 1) The Arco Mines
docs.text:
The dynastic wealth anchor: an extraction complex so old and expanded it feels like a nation beneath the ground. Some sections are pristine and guarded; others are patched, dangerous, and "temporarily" occupied by desperate labor.

Control: Royal family access via Ike Pavo; contested oversight via Conclave/Ustur audits

docs.title: 2) The Spire Foundries
docs.text:
[!IMPORTANT] The Spire Foundries were built to mimic the Ustur Elder Spire structure on Ioki. At the height of Crumon arrogance, the Dynasty spent an enormous fortune on them — less for efficiency and more to impress the galaxy with a monument-factory that looked like power made permanent.

It worked. They were genuinely impressive.

Now, the Foundries are a decaying industrial complex with ruinously expensive maintenance. They bleed the royalty's profits, but the Dynasty cannot abandon them without admitting decline — so the Foundries remain: a prestige engine kept alive to preserve the appearance of success.

Control: Contractors + dynastic influence; constant interest from MTC contracting teams; quietly scrutinized by Ustur observers for the symbolism it carries

docs.title: 3) Loyalist Quarter ("The Old Banner District")
docs.text:
A district where Crumon symbols are treated as daily life, not nostalgia. Recruitment, oaths, and family networks are stronger than law here.

Control: Dynastic loyalists (Ike-aligned), watched by Conclave security

docs.title: 4) The Pressure-Cathedral
docs.text:
A sealed, resonant chamber carved into old infrastructure — part memorial, part oath-site, part warning. People speak of Queen Cathris here without using her name — they call her "The Absent Queen."

Control: Civic/dynastic culture (informal), tolerated so long as it stays non-military

docs.title: 5) The Quota Office
docs.text:
A brutal administrative node where extraction quotas, shutdown orders, and "safety" closures are issued. One stamp here can starve a district or collapse a convoy schedule.

Control: Conclave directors; heavily audited by Ustur; pressured by Dynasty and trade operators

docs.title: 6) The Worker Stair
docs.text:
A vertical labyrinth of hab stacks and stairwells where the working class lives close to danger. It breeds pride, anger, and solidarity — and it remembers who pays wages on time.

The Winter Fist Path Hardship Shrine is located nearby — close to the industrial quarters where accidents and exploitation are most common.

Control: Civic; VHE influence through employment legitimacy; MTC influence through debt-contract offers

docs.title: 7) The Silent Shaft (Rumored)
docs.text:
A sealed extraction shaft that "should" be decommissioned, yet sometimes shows signs of activity. Some claim it is black-market Arco. Some claim it is Duskreach.

Control: Unknown (rumor anchor)

docs.title: Travel Hazards
docs.text:
• Quota shock: A sudden shutdown triggers riots, shortages, and convoy failures
  • Debt capture: Contracts turn miners into collateral without firing a shot
  • Industrial accidents: Sabotage can look like routine collapse
  • Loyalist flare-ups: Symbolism can harden into mobilization — exactly the line Ustur watches
  • Arco convoys: Anything carrying Arco attracts predation, political interference, and "paper holds"

docs.title: Story Fuel
docs.text:
• Crushspire is where dynasty stops being myth and becomes infrastructure
  • It is the sector's temptation: whoever controls Arco can force everyone else to negotiate
  • The Conclave needs Crushspire stable; Ike Pavo needs Crushspire proud; MTC needs Crushspire bound by contract; Ustur needs Crushspire to never become a militarized throne

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Crumon Dynasty — crumon_dynasty.md (Ike Pavo base)
  • Arco Crystal — primary mineral resource of the sector
  • Spire Foundries → Ustur Elder Spire on Ioki (architectural imitation)
  • TAPP — tapp.md (Quota Office = extraction quotas)
  • Winter Fist Path — winter_fist_path.md (Hardship Shrine on Crushspire)
  • Pavo Passage — pavo_passage.md

docs.title: 🌑 Canon Document: ETIRA (Planet — Yuldun Waste, MRZ-32)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Aeon Timeline + Creator clarification, Feb 2026) Source: Aeon Timeline Event 18, Creator corrections Location: Yuldun Waste Sector (MRZ-32) Influence: Ustur Zone (ungoverned / BMAH territory) Theme: Sealed Cosmic Horror, Memory Theft, Fanatical Cult, Terraformed Prison

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Planet Etira is the last capital of the Black Market Auction House (BMAH) and one of the most deliberately isolated worlds in Galia. Beneath its hostile, terraformed atmosphere lies a secret so old that only the Photoli know the full truth — and they have ensured that no one else ever reaches it.

docs.title: Discovery (~2135)
docs.text:
• During their early exploration of Galia, the Photoli discovered a strange and powerful creature buried in the heart of planet Etira
  • The creature possessed abilities the Photoli had never encountered
  • They could not destroy it — not because it was truly immortal, but because the Photoli did not know how to kill it
  • The Photoli decided to seal the creature instead

docs.title: The Sealing
docs.text:
• The Photoli stole the creature's memories, stripping away its consciousness and identity
  • They confined it to a subterranean pool deep within Etira's core
  • To prevent anyone from reaching the creature, the Photoli terraformed the planet's atmosphere to be lethally hostile to all known species
  • This is the third confirmed case of Photoli cosmological manipulation (alongside draining Night Window's star and casting the Bluvael illusion)

docs.title: The Name
docs.text:
• The creature's name in Galia is the "Dreamer Below"
  • Even stripped of its memories, the creature's psychic influence bleeds through — indirect, dreamlike, unsettling
  • The creature is not truly immortal — the Photoli simply lacked the means to destroy it

docs.title: Current State
docs.text:
• The Dreamer Below is dead. During the Never Alone incident (~2624), the entity was destroyed
  • The Dark Photoli Bah visited the corpse afterward, looting a silver gem from within the Dreamer's remains and storing it in a pocket dimension
  • The psychic influence that once leaked from the sealed creature has ceased
  • Etira is now a hostile world with a dead god rotting in its core

docs.title: Origin
docs.text:
• Over the centuries, the Dreamer Below's indirect psychic influence has reached susceptible minds across the sector
  • A fanatical cult of memory stealers was founded — inspired unconsciously by the creature's nature
  • The cult seeks to resurrect the Dreamer Below by gathering enough stolen memories to reconstruct its consciousness

docs.title: Methods
docs.text:
• Cult members practice memory theft — extracting memories from unwilling victims
  • They believe that by feeding enough memories to the creature, they can awaken it
  • Their practices mirror the Photoli's original act of sealing the Dreamer — a dark echo of the method used to imprison it

docs.title: Threat Level
docs.text:
• The cult operated in the shadows of Yuldun Waste's criminal underworld
  • Their true purpose was unknown to most — even the BMAH considered them eccentric
  • Post-Never Alone: Herdus was killed by Saand, the Church was exposed, and the Dreamer was destroyed. The cult is effectively defunct

docs.title: Origin (~2515)
docs.text:
• ~2515: During the Convergence War, Eknok, a notorious war criminal, crash-lands on planet Etira
  • He and his crew discover how to bypass the planet's atmospheric storm
  • They establish a black market hidden from the rest of the galaxy, exploiting Etira's hostile atmosphere as natural security
  • This operation grows into the Black Market Auction House (BMAH)

docs.title: Destruction (~2624)
docs.text:
• During the Never Alone incident, Saand sabotaged the BMAH gathering, killed Herdus.soul, and exposed the Church
  • The Council of Peace invaded Etira with military-grade atmospheric shielding, destroying the BMAH's subsurface bunkers
  • The BMAH is now in transit — scattered across Galia, searching for a new permanent base under Garonthe's leadership
  • Etira is effectively abandoned — no longer a functioning capital for any organization

docs.title: The Never Alone Story
docs.text: [!NOTE] The full story of the Dreamer Below is depicted in the "Never Alone" narrative. Key outcomes: Herdus.soul killed by Saand, Church exposed, COP invades Etira, BMAH destroyed, Dreamer destroyed, corpse looted by Dark Photoli Bah.

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Yuldun Waste — yuldun_waste.md (host sector, BMAH coordination)
  • Photoli — photoli.md (sealed the Dreamer, terraformed atmosphere)
  • Black Market Auction House — BMAH, criminal institution
  • Night Window — night_window.md (Photoli star draining — parallel manipulation)
  • Bluvael — bluvael.md (Photoli illusion — parallel manipulation)

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ⏸️ Q-ETI1: What species IS the Dreamer Below? Pre-Convergence? Ancient?
  • ⏸️ Q-ETI2: Full details from the "Never Alone" story (Creator to share)
  • ⏸️ Q-ETI3: What exactly is the silver gem Bah looted from the Dreamer's corpse?
  • ⏸️ Q-ETI4: Has the Dreamer's destruction ended all psychic influence on Yuldun Waste, or does residual energy linger?
  • ⏸️ Q-ETI5: Is Etira now permanently abandoned, or will new inhabitants claim it?

docs.title: 👑 Canon Document: OKUTO (Dwarf Planet / Neutral Gray Exchange)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — World Sheet 05 Type: Dwarf planet / neutral gray exchange Sector: Pavo Passage (MRZ-17) Local Name: Formerly known as "Riftmarket" Function: Pressure valve — where opportunity moves at a price Governance: Self-governed (not Conclave-controlled) Role Division: Tollhaven moves ships; Velloris moves legitimacy; Okuto moves opportunity

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Okuto (formerly known locally as "Riftmarket") is a dwarf planet on the far edge of the Pavo Passage system — remote, hard to police, and perfectly placed to become the sector's pressure valve. It is a neutral, self-governed hub where salvage, gray exchange, and quiet conversion thrive: the place people go when they can't wait for approvals, can't survive an audit storm, or can't afford to be clean.

If Tollhaven moves ships and Velloris moves legitimacy, Okuto moves opportunity — at a price.

docs.title: Environment / "Feel"
docs.text:
Okuto feels improvised and alive: modular bazaars, scaffold cities, patchwork landing yards, and neighborhoods that expand or vanish overnight.

  • Cold edge-world climate and isolation give everything a harsher tone
  • More heaters, thicker doors, tighter circles
  • The air is loud with bargaining, engine heat, and encrypted comms

It's freer than Cathris, less procedural than Velloris, and less controlled than Tollhaven — yet it has its own rules, enforced by reputation and consequence.

docs.title: Governance & Faction Presence
docs.text: Faction Presence Neutral Self-Governance Not run by the Conclave; self-governed through local broker councils, yard bosses, and enforcement crews whose authority rests on reputation Ruling Conclave Present only in thin layers — permits for "legitimate" operators, selective crackdowns, quiet influence attempts Ustur Regency Limited visibility; focuses on preventing catastrophic fraud or weaponization, not policing every gray deal MTC / Synod Operates through intermediaries, credit traps, and "salvage financing"; buys influence by buying desperation VHE Avoids deep gray entanglement publicly, but maintains observers because Okuto can destabilize Tollhaven's throughput overnight Crumon Dynasty Uses Okuto to source deniable parts and keep loyalist supply chains alive without asking permission Order of Seasons Rumors of brokers who "sell information that kills." Nobody proves it; everyone fears it

docs.title: Economy & Exports
docs.text:
Okuto exports:

  • Salvage and refit parts
  • Gray-market logistics
  • "Unofficial" route intelligence
  • Conversion services (turning broken ships and seized cargo into functioning assets)

docs.title: Pricing Canon
docs.text:
[!IMPORTANT] Goods on Okuto are more expensive than normal due to the dwarf planet's unique status:

  • Distance inflates transport and storage costs
  • Risk premiums are baked into every sale
  • Buyers pay for deniability as much as for the item itself

docs.title: Black Market Auction House (BMAH) Connection
docs.text:
Okuto has deep ties to the Black Market Auction House (BMAH) and functions as the practical connector between BMAH networks and the Pavo Passage economy:

  • Brokers on Okuto source, stage, and "sanitize" goods before they touch the corridor's visible lanes
  • Information and inventory move through Okuto as a buffer layer, keeping true origins obscured

Okuto is where BMAH becomes usable inside Pavo Passage.

docs.title: 1) The Patchwork Yards
docs.text:
A sprawl of refit docks made from old frames and scavenged gantries. Ships get rebuilt here that should not fly again.

Control: Independent yard bosses; Conclave permits reach only the surface layer

docs.title: 2) The Free Ledger Bazaar
docs.text:
A market district where deals are struck through private wallets, escrow proxies, and reputation brokers. Nothing is "illegal" if nobody can prove it — and proof is expensive.

Control: Reputation networks; quietly influenced by MTC financiers

docs.title: 3) The Wreckline Fields
docs.text:
A surrounding zone of hulks, debris-lanes, and salvage claims where crews compete for fortunes and die for mistakes.

Control: Whoever can hold a claim long enough to enforce it

docs.title: 4) The BMAH Exchange Gate (Connector Node)
docs.text:
A deniable logistics and broker zone where BMAH-linked inventory is staged, split, repackaged, and routed into Pavo Passage via intermediaries.

Control: Broker councils and "quiet partners"; officially unlisted

docs.title: 5) The Quiet Auction (Rumored)
docs.text:
A rotating, invite-only auction for rare parts, sealed crates, and "unlisted" items. People argue whether it is a local myth or a BMAH instrument.

Canon truth (never publicly confirmed): The Quiet Auction is a special event drawing criminals from across Galia Expanse. It operates in absolute secrecy — no one has factually confirmed its existence. It is also the channel through which many offerings to Iris Dusk are sold within Pavo Passage, connecting the Order of Seasons' shadow economy to Okuto's gray markets.

Control: Unknown; the thing everyone denies knowing

docs.title: 6) The Riftwatch Perch
docs.text:
A lookout district of comm towers and spotter crews tracking incoming convoys, patrol shifts, and inspection weather.

Control: Local intel crews; courted by every faction

docs.title: 7) The Claims Chapel
docs.text:
A neutral-ish mediation space where disputes are settled without Conclave courts — often by restitution, sometimes by blood. Even criminals prefer rules when profit is at stake.

Control: Civic elders and broker councils (informal)

docs.title: 8) The Smelter Lanes
docs.text:
Industrial lanes where salvage is stripped, melted, and repurposed. It's where evidence disappears and parts reappear as "new stock."

Control: Independent industrial crews; periodic Ustur scrutiny

docs.title: Travel Hazards
docs.text:
• Price shock: Everything costs more than planned — fuel, parts, labor, information
  • Reputation collapse: One bad deal can blacklist you across Okuto
  • Salvage violence: Claims disputes turn lethal fast
  • Counterfeit proofs: Fake attestations that work — until they don't
  • Predatory financing: MTC-aligned credit can own a captain for life
  • BMAH heat: Get too close to the connector node and you inherit enemies you can't name

docs.title: Story Fuel
docs.text:
• Okuto isn't chaos — it's a neutral order built on necessity and distance
  • It keeps the sector from starving when official systems choke, but ensures no reform ever fully wins
  • Okuto is the corridor's shadow economy — and the bridge that lets the BMAH touch Pavo Passage without showing its face

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• BMAH (Black Market Auction House) — galactic gray/black market network
  • Tollhaven — tollhaven.md (throughput counterpart)
  • Velloris — velloris.md (proof/credential counterpart)
  • MTC — mtc.md (predatory financing through intermediaries)
  • Crumon Dynasty — crumon_dynasty.md (deniable supply chains)
  • Order of Seasons — order_of_seasons.md (rumored "information that kills")
  • Pavo Passage — pavo_passage.md

docs.title: 👑 Canon Document: TOLLHAVEN (Orbital Gate-Station of Cathris)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — World Sheet 02 Type: City-sized orbital station (mothership-scale) Sector: Pavo Passage (MRZ-17) Orbits: Cathris (capital world) Function: Gate-station — where law becomes motion Population: ~1 million workers Origin: Crumon Dynasty relic from their brightest era

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Tollhaven is a city-sized orbital station circling Cathris — a mothership-scale relic of the Crumon dynasty's brightest era that still keeps the sector breathing. Nearly a million workers live inside its decks, feeding the Toll Machine with shifts, stamps, repairs, inspections, and endless waiting.

If Cathris turns commerce into law, Tollhaven turns law into motion: ships that clear, ships that stall, and ships that vanish into "pending" until their captains learn what power really costs.

docs.title: Environment / "Feel"
docs.text:
Tollhaven is layered civilization under pressure:

Upper rings (Crumon-era opulence):

  • Polished halls, ceremonial docks, old prestige signage
  • Architecture designed to impress travelers into obedience
  • The part visitors remember — and the Conclave wants photographed

Lower decks (service corridors):

  • Patched bulkheads, dead sections, improvised living blocks
  • Lingering consequences of past civil conflict
  • The present government's struggle to keep everything moving

It smells of lubricant, recycled air, hot metal, and overcrowding. Everything is timed, tagged, scanned, and watched — yet the most important things still happen in blind spots between shifts.

docs.title: Governance & Faction Presence
docs.text: Faction Presence Ruling Conclave Present through directors and toll appointments; where Conclave decisions become real as operations (queues, holds, priorities), not where legitimacy is authored Ustur Regency Strongest practical impact through inspection standards and enforcement audits; deeper credential-proof validity is anchored off-station (Velloris) MTC / Synod Heavy influence through contracts, insurance, credit-driven prioritization; aims to become the default carrier network by making "priority" feel inevitable at dock level VHE Major hub presence; strongest local reputation (employs citizens, keeps crews alive) Crumon Dynasty Living relic of their era — symbolic leverage, old family loyalties, nostalgia embedded in structure Order of Seasons Never openly present; rumors around sealed decks, missing night-shift workers, corridors from the Crumon monarchy era that "should not connect"

docs.title: Economy & Exports
docs.text:
Tollhaven's economy is physical throughput:

  • Docking and storage at scale
  • Inspection execution and reclassification handling
  • Starpath route windows/slots administration
  • Convoy coordination and beacon routing
  • Repairs, refits, provisioning
  • Labor contracts (the station's bloodstream)

Its export is motion — the ability to clear ships through the corridor.

Proof/credential validity and registry truth are primarily anchored on Velloris, not Tollhaven.

docs.title: 1) The Starpath Auction Hall
docs.text:
Where Starpath route usage, time windows, and priority slots are bid for. Competition becomes ritual — public enough to look fair, controlled enough to remain political.

Control: Conclave calendar + operator execution; Ustur audits fairness/compliance Core rule: No single operator may hold >3 active routes while the other has not secured ≥1 in the cycle.

docs.title: 2) The Gilded Concourse (Crumon Legacy Ring)
docs.text:
A grand public ring of ceremonial docks, legacy architecture, and "civilized" commerce — the part visitors remember, and the part the Conclave wants photographed.

Control: Conclave-facing administration; prestige contested by Dynasty symbolism

docs.title: 3) Queue City ("The Stacks")
docs.text:
A vertical sprawl of temporary living: crews waiting on holds, families displaced by delays, and contract workers living between shifts. Rumors spread faster than official notices.

Control: Nobody fully; VHE legitimacy is strongest here

docs.title: 4) The Inspection Spine
docs.text:
Gantry corridors where cargo becomes destiny: reclassified, delayed, cleared, or sealed. People fear it because it can turn one mistake into bankruptcy.

Control: Operators execute; Ustur standards rule; Conclave directors set priorities

docs.title: 5) Beaconworks Ridge
docs.text:
Routing beacons and coordination towers that feed convoy schedules into the Toll Machine. When Beaconworks stutters, the sector chokes.

Control: Contractors; heavily audited; quietly contested by MTC vs VHE logistics teams

docs.title: 6) VHE Workforce Commons
docs.text:
Hiring boards, training bays, injury support, and family relief coordination — visible proof of VHE's "people's company" posture.

Control: VHE civic apparatus (employment-first doctrine)

docs.title: 7) Meridian Contract Pier
docs.text:
A sleek dock complex where "priority" feels like a private language: better berths, smoother paperwork, faster clearances — paid for in credit and obligation.

Control: MTC influence through contracts/insurance gravity

docs.title: 8) The Rest House (Winter Fist Path)
docs.text:
A quiet shelter for injured workers and stranded crews. No violence inside. No bargaining over mercy.

Control: Winter Fist Path; tolerated by Conclave because it reduces unrest

docs.title: Travel Hazards
docs.text:
• Hold-loop purgatory: Your ship isn't seized, it's "pending," and pending can last forever
  • Slot sabotage: Losing a window cascades into weeks of delay and debt
  • Audit freezes: Ustur crackdowns can immobilize whole bays overnight
  • Deck unrest: One unjust seizure can ignite a labor flashpoint
  • Night-shift vanishings: People disappear from lower corridors; paperwork stays clean

docs.title: Story Fuel
docs.text:
• Tollhaven is where the sector becomes human: labor, overcrowding, survival, and fragile dignity
  • It is also where Crumon grandeur still stands — opulence over rot — making the station a daily argument about what Pavo was and what it became
  • Tollhaven moves ships; Velloris moves legitimacy. Together they form the corridor's chokehold.

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Cathris — cathris.md (Tollhaven orbits Cathris)
  • Velloris — velloris.md (proof/credential counterpart)
  • TAPP — tapp.md (Starpath Auction Hall)
  • MTC — mtc.md (Meridian Contract Pier)
  • VHE — vhe.md (Workforce Commons)
  • Winter Fist Path — winter_fist_path.md (Rest House)
  • Crumon Dynasty — crumon_dynasty.md (Gilded Concourse)
  • Starpath — chain of warp gates linking Galia Expanse regions
  • Pavo Passage — pavo_passage.md

docs.title: 👑 Canon Document: VELLORIS (Ledger World / The Proof Spine)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — World Sheet 04 Type: Administrative planet / ledger world Sector: Pavo Passage (MRZ-17) Function: Proof infrastructure — where legality becomes provable Biome: Dual-hemisphere — agricultural belt + administrative cities Role Division: Tollhaven moves ships; Velloris moves legitimacy

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Velloris is where Pavo Passage turns life into proof. It does not move ships — that is Tollhaven's job. Velloris moves validity: the attestations, registry anchors, and wallet-bound credentials that decide whether a vessel can be insured, whether a cargo can be classified, and whether an identity can transact at all.

In a sector ruled by permits and audits, Velloris is the quiet back-end that makes the Toll Machine believable — state affairs, middle-class work, and the institutions that keep "truth" consistent across the corridor.

docs.title: Environment / "Feel"
docs.text:
Velloris is calmer than most of Pavo Passage — orderly, breathable, and intentionally livable.

Agricultural hemisphere:

  • Wide fields, managed terraces, irrigation grids, and harvest towns
  • Keeps crews and lower decks across the sector fed when commerce stutters

Administrative hemisphere:

  • Compact cities and administrative districts
  • Clean streets, quiet comms etiquette
  • Culture that treats stability as a civic duty

The vibe is "chill competence," with an undercurrent of fear: polite clerks can erase you without raising their voices.

docs.title: Governance & Faction Presence
docs.text: Faction Presence Ruling Conclave Uses Velloris to make authority portable — Conclave decisions become enforceable when recorded as durable proofs tied to identity credentials Ustur Regency Treats Velloris as a strategic compliance anchor; audits here ripple outward through standards, not dockside crackdowns MTC / Synod Strongest here through escrow rails, credit instruments, and proof-based contract enforcement; wins by controlling what "valid commerce" looks like VHE Maintains visible compliance liaisons to protect clean passage reputations and defend citizens from predatory proof traps Crumon Dynasty Leverages legacy archives and lineage proofs as political ammunition; Velloris can validate a claim — or bury it behind missing attestations Order of Seasons Never official; rumors persist that some sealed vaults hide proofs too dangerous to surface

docs.title: Economy & Exports
docs.text:
Velloris exports verification services and proof infrastructure:

  • Registry anchoring (vessel/crew/cargo identity validity)
  • Notarized wallet attestations and credential issuance
  • Dispute evidence bundles (time-stamped, signed, preserved)
  • Compliance tooling, credentialing, and integrity guarantees

Its hidden commodity is selective absence: what never receives a valid attestation becomes un-auditable — because the corridor has nothing to reference.

docs.title: 1) The Grand Registry Spires
docs.text:
Secure stacks of identity vaults, vessel registries, cargo-class histories, and permit proofs — mirrored across redundancies and bound to wallet-based credentials.

Control: Conclave-appointed directors; constant Ustur audit pressure

docs.title: 2) The Attestation Exchange
docs.text:
Verification houses where signatures, credential chains, and compliance proofs are minted and cross-checked. A clean proof opens doors everywhere; a broken chain follows you like a curse.

Control: Mixed contractors; heavily audited; under constant faction pressure

docs.title: 3) The Audit Cloister
docs.text:
A Ustur-adjacent complex that interprets compliance doctrine and issues standards updates ("proof weather") that later manifest as inspections and holds in Tollhaven.

Control: Ustur audit doctrine; tolerated as necessary, resented as interference

docs.title: 4) Witness Row (Civic Notary Quarter)
docs.text:
A civic quarter for sworn statements, biometric confirmations, wallet attestations, and sealed dispute filings — where people come to make reality count.

Control: Conclave bureaucracy; exploited by every faction

docs.title: 5) The Contract Basin
docs.text:
Escrow rails, route-bonding agreements, claims processing, and payment logic. This is where "legal commerce" is engineered — quietly, permanently.

Control: MTC gravity strongest; VHE maintains defensive presence

docs.title: 6) The Quiet Court Annex
docs.text:
A chamber for cases too delicate to leave clean records: sealed hearings, redacted rulings, wallet-locked evidence.

Control: Conclave influence; fertile ground for corruption

docs.title: 7) The Sealed Proof Vault (Rumored)
docs.text:
An indexed-but-inaccessible vault believed to contain proofs tied to civil conflict, The Night, and the administrative disappearances that made modern Pavo possible.

Control: Officially decommissioned; practically unknown

docs.title: 8) Southbelt Harvest Arc
docs.text:
A managed agricultural corridor built to stabilize the sector's baseline food supply — kept boring on purpose because boring prevents riots.

Control: Local civic councils under Conclave oversight; quietly protected by everyone who fears unrest

docs.title: Travel Hazards
docs.text:
• Credential suppression: You don't get arrested; your wallet credentials stop validating
  • Proof traps: Contradictory attestations freeze you everywhere, not just at a dock
  • Audit cascades: One flagged proof chain can force sector-wide compliance tightening
  • Sealed truths: Requesting the wrong proofs gets your access revoked — or your name "corrected"
  • Ledger ghosts: The system insists something never happened, and the corridor agrees because the reference never existed

docs.title: Story Fuel
docs.text:
• Velloris is the sector's quiet weapon: it destroys people with paperwork that never raises alarms
  • Tollhaven moves ships; Velloris moves legitimacy — together they form the corridor's chokehold
  • The most valuable treasure here isn't data — it's provable data, and the power to deny proof

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Tollhaven — tollhaven.md (physical throughput counterpart)
  • Cathris — cathris.md (law-authoring center)
  • TAPP — tapp.md (proof/credential dependency)
  • MTC — mtc.md (Contract Basin + escrow rails)
  • VHE — vhe.md (compliance liaisons)
  • Pavo Passage — pavo_passage.md

docs.title: 👑 Canon Document: VESHMORA (Quiet World / Disappearance Refuge)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — World Sheet 06 Type: Quiet world / disappearance refuge Sector: Pavo Passage (MRZ-17) Local Name: Formerly called "Hushmoor" in corridor slang Function: Absence — where people go to stop being measured Role Division: Cathris = authority; Tollhaven = throughput; Velloris = proof; Okuto = opportunity; Veshmora = absence

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Veshmora (formerly called "Hushmoor" in corridor slang) is where Pavo Passage goes to be forgotten. It is a quiet world of sparse settlements and deliberate silence, valued less for what it produces and more for what it allows: distance from the corridor's gaze.

If Cathris is authority, Tollhaven is throughput, Velloris is proof, and Okuto is opportunity, then Veshmora is absence — a place where people can live without being constantly measured.

docs.title: Environment / "Feel"
docs.text:
Veshmora feels muted and wide: low-light skies, mist-heavy basins, broad plains broken by wetlands, and settlement lights that look like candles from orbit.

  • Infrastructure is functional but minimal, built to endure rather than impress
  • Less noise — less advertising, fewer scanners, fewer eyes
  • That quiet is exactly what makes it valuable

docs.title: Governance & Faction Presence
docs.text: Faction Presence Ruling Conclave Present at the edges only — enough to claim the world on paper, not enough to dominate. Tolerated because it reduces pressure elsewhere Ustur Regency Minimal footprint; watches for weapons trafficking and militarization, otherwise avoids turning Veshmora into a policing problem MTC / Synod Light but persistent; offers credit and supply contracts to remote settlements, quietly turning dependence into leverage VHE Trusted here for employment and fair supply chains; "people's company" reputation travels well to quiet places Crumon Dynasty Low visibility; uses Veshmora as a deniable transit refuge and a place to hide loyalist movement without spectacle Order of Seasons Fertile ground for hidden sanctums — because nobody looks too closely unless something goes wrong

docs.title: Economy & Exports
docs.text:
Veshmora exports modest, practical goods:

  • Staple foods from wetlands and plains
  • Basic industrial materials
  • Repair labor for ships that don't want attention

Its true export is privacy — paid for in favors, silence, and selective hospitality.

docs.title: 1) The Moorlight Settlements
docs.text:
Clusters of low-rise towns built along wetlands and ridgelines. They live off local supply cycles and avoid drawing corridor-scale attention.

Control: Local councils; lightly overseen by Conclave registries

docs.title: 2) Quietport Landing Fields
docs.text:
Low-signature landing pads and storage yards designed to look boring. Ships come here to cool off from heat, wait out audits, or disappear for a week without being noticed.

Control: Local port crews; quietly influenced by whoever pays reliably

docs.title: 3) The Lantern Road
docs.text:
A transit route marked by old beacons and manual navigation cues — used when you don't want automated systems to remember you too clearly.

Control: Civic; guarded by custom, not law

docs.title: 4) The Reedvault Granaries
docs.text:
Flood-safe granaries and reserve depots that keep Veshmora stable through corridor disruptions. When Tollhaven stalls, Veshmora becomes quietly important.

Control: Civic councils; courted by VHE and MTC supply teams

docs.title: 5) The Listening Marsh
docs.text:
A wetland basin where sound carries strangely. People claim you can hear engines and voices that aren't present — stories of ghosts, old war machines, or Duskreach whispering through buried infrastructure.

Canon clarification: The "whispering" is metaphorical — the Listening Marsh is where Duskreach contacts, scouts, and sources operate. The eerie reputation keeps outsiders away, making it perfect cover for real intelligence networks.

Control: None; superstition keeps most away

docs.title: 6) The Disappeared Chapel
docs.text:
A small, unofficial memorial site where families leave names that never became official records. It exists because Velloris can erase proof — but grief still needs a place to stand.

Control: Civic; quietly watched

docs.title: 7) The Sublevel Door (Rumored)
docs.text:
A sealed access point in an abandoned utility complex. Some say it leads to smugglers' caches. Some say it leads to a Duskreach anchor. No one admits testing it twice.

Canon clarification: Schrödinger's dungeon — it exists and doesn't until the narrative needs it. Deliberately left ambiguous as a rumor anchor.

Control: Unknown (rumor anchor)

docs.title: Travel Hazards
docs.text:
• Soft capture: Isolation makes settlements vulnerable to predatory contracts and "help" that becomes ownership
  • Quiet violence: Disputes don't escalate loudly — they vanish into silence
  • Supply fragility: Storms or corridor freezes can starve remote towns fast
  • Ghost risk: If Duskreach is present, it is present in the worst way — unverifiable until it matters
  • False safety: People come to disappear and discover someone else already did

docs.title: Story Fuel
docs.text:
• Veshmora is the sector's hiding place — and therefore one of its most strategic assets
  • It shows the human cost of a corridor built on control: people don't run here for profit, they run here for air
  • If the sector ever militarizes, Veshmora becomes the first thing everyone tries to "secure" — and the first thing that stops being quiet

docs.text:

  • Tollhaven — tollhaven.md (Reedvault supplies when throughput stalls)

  • MTC — mtc.md (predatory supply contracts in remote settlements)

docs.text:
The major eras and events that shaped the galaxy.

docs.title: 📜 Canon Document: CHAPTER 2 HOLOSIM — THE BALIFA GROVE CAMPAIGN
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Feb 2026) Source: Chapter 2 Holosim campaign scripts (MUD, ONI, Ustur perspectives) Type: Historical Event — Holosim Simulation Scenario Context: Holosim-generated scenario. Historical events referenced (Frenir, Old Grove, ECOS raids, Convergence War backstories) are confirmed as real. The simulation reconstructs these events and projects future escalation scenarios.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
"Holosim added her to the simulation in hope of trying to understand another doomsday scenario caused by the ECOS terrorists. She is both the cause of suffering and the key to understanding whether civilization's destruction is avoidable."

Chapter 2 of the Holosim scenario pits the player (Captain) against the Balifa Grove — the radical ECOS sub-faction led by Arch Druidess Andreza Liora. The scenario unfolds across three faction perspectives (MUD, ONI, Ustur), each offering a unique lens on the same escalating conflict.

The core narrative follows a consistent arc: the Captain builds infrastructure in the Safe Zone, expands into the Medium Risk Zone (MRZ), encounters Balifa Grove raids, captures and eventually releases Martuce Breeze, faces the mid-game offensive of General Thaena Corvax, and confronts Andreza's Superphoenix titan in a final climactic battle.

Canon Note: The events depicted in the simulation mirror real galactic history. Holosim constructed this scenario to test whether faction leadership could prevent ECOS-driven civilizational collapse. The Balifa Grove's tactics, fleet composition, and ideological positions are drawn from actual Star Atlas intelligence.

docs.title: Shared Arc
docs.text:
All three campaigns follow the same macro-structure:

Phase Event Description Phase 1 Safe Zone Build-Up Captain recruits crew, mines resources, builds fleet in home territory Phase 2 MRZ Expansion Captain pushes into Medium Risk Zone, establishes starbases Phase 3 First Contact Martuce Breeze contacts Captain, warns against exploitation Phase 4 ECOS Raids Balifa Grove attacks convoys and mining operations Phase 5 Martuce Captured Combat engagement results in Martuce's capture Phase 6 Corvax's Mourning War Thaena Corvax declares "Mourning War" to avenge Martuce Phase 7 Escalation Capital ship construction, rare resource mining, starbase fortification Phase 8 Martuce Exchange Captain trades Martuce for captured crew; ethical dilemma Phase 9 Final Battle Superphoenix arrives from the HRZ; Andreza leads full Mourning Fleet

docs.title: Companion: Deacon Howard Dawn
docs.text:
"I killed her world once. She returned the favor at Ashlara-3. And now Holosim wants me to face her again."

Dawn serves as the Captain's first officer and moral compass — a man haunted by the C11 Bombardment and the Ashlara-3 tragedy. His personal history with Andreza creates the campaign's emotional spine.

docs.title: Key Narrative Beats
docs.text:
• Dawn's Guilt: Dawn reveals he ordered the C11 Bombardment that destroyed an ECOS factory colony — a bombardment that a young Andreza survived. When the surviving enclave reached his outpost at Ashlara-3, Dawn showed mercy. Three days later, the ECOS survivors detonated a terraforming bomb, destroying the outpost and killing millions. Dawn has never forgiven himself.
  • First Contact with Martuce: Martuce contacts the Captain during MRZ expansion, pleading for sustainable mining practices. Dawn recognizes ECOS rhetoric and warns the Captain.
  • Martuce's Capture: After a combat engagement, Martuce is taken prisoner. Dawn advocates for her humane treatment, seeing echoes of the mercy he once extended.
  • Corvax's Assault: Corvax launches coordinated raids across MUD territory, declaring a Mourning War.
  • Corvax's Death: In the MUD campaign, Corvax is cornered during a decisive battle. Rather than surrender, she detonates her own ship — "I'll see you in the vines, Deacon" — scattering spores across the battlefield. A final act of ecological warfare disguised as martyrdom.
  • The Exchange: Dawn supports trading Martuce for captured MUD crews, framing it as "the only mercy that costs nothing."
  • Superphoenix Battle: Andreza arrives and addresses Dawn directly: "You made me, Deacon. Every root I plant grew from the ash you left behind." Dawn's response reveals his acceptance: "Then let me be the one to end what I started."

docs.title: Thematic Core
docs.text: Mercy, guilt, and the cycles of violence. Dawn's arc explores whether compassion creates or prevents more suffering. The campaign asks: can the people who started a war be the ones to end it?

docs.title: Companion: Commander Seeikolobe Mineefastu
docs.text:
"You don't wait for proof when you've seen what they do. I watched them turn a whole ocean red on Virel's Rest while we debated reports."

Seeikolobe is a gruff, aging Punaab veteran of the Convergence War — a survivor of Frenir's terraforming and a man who believes the only answer to zealotry is overwhelming force.

docs.title: Key Narrative Beats
docs.text:
• Seeikolobe's Trauma: Reveals he was stationed in Frenir when Balifa Grove attacked. One day a fortress, the next, vines wrapping around towers. He lost 3,000 civilians and responded by burning three worlds in retaliation.
  • Zar Funnu's Convoys: Punaab mechanic Zar Funnu at the Golden Finger workshop in Hakarabet reports convoys destroyed by ships bearing "the green mark." Investigation confirms Balifa Grove.
  • SAGE Hides Martuce: After Martuce is captured, SAGE secretly classifies her medbay as "biohazard quarantine" and hides Martuce from Seeikolobe and ONI Command. This sets up the campaign's moral crisis.
  • Seeikolobe Discovers the Deception: When SAGE proposes exchanging Martuce for captured ONI crews, Seeikolobe discovers she's been hidden aboard the whole time. He calls it treason: "You hide a top ECOS asset from ONI Command, then try to trade her back to the terrorists?"
  • The Moral Dilemma: Seeikolobe orders the Captain to glass captured ECOS outposts — including biodomes with civilians. SAGE and the Captain refuse. "Maybe the Convergence should've burned hotter" — Seeikolobe's bitterest moment.
  • Seeikolobe's Redemption: After seeing MRZ civilians volunteer for ONI service because "at least you stayed and repaired the damage," Seeikolobe grudgingly acknowledges the Captain's restraint was right: "Maybe that was the right call. For them, anyway."
  • Superphoenix Battle: All ONI capital ships engage. Seeikolobe apologizes for calling the Captain a traitor: "You're a loyal citizen of the Consortium, Captain. I'll put that in my report myself."

docs.title: Thematic Core
docs.text: The cost of pragmatism. Seeikolobe embodies "necessary evil" doctrine — the soldier who does terrible things because the alternative seems worse. The campaign challenges whether scorched-earth tactics actually win wars, or just create the next generation of Andrezas.

docs.title: Companion: Nova.tchr
docs.text:
"Perhaps enlightenment was never about climbing a ladder. Perhaps it is about realizing there is no ladder at all."

Nova.tchr is a high-ranking Ustur disciple of Mother Craven, tasked with evaluating the Captain for the Golden Star Path. Her personal arc — a crisis of faith that leads to genuine spiritual breakthrough — is the campaign's emotional backbone.

docs.title: Key Narrative Beats
docs.text:
• The Golden Star Path: Nova teaches the Captain the Ustur philosophy of enlightenment through stages (Bod → Tcher → Soul → Elder). She is stuck at the Tcher stage, unable to progress despite decades of perfect adherence.
  • Philosophical Contrast: Where Seeikolobe uses force and Dawn uses guilt, Nova frames every operation through Ustur philosophy. Mining becomes meditation. Ship construction becomes alignment. Combat becomes defense of harmony.
  • Nova's Stagnation: Mid-campaign, Nova reveals she cannot advance in the Path. She's followed every tenet of the Golden Star perfectly — "I'm a direct disciple of the first Ustur, walking the perfect path taught by the perfect teacher" — yet feels nothing.
  • SAGE's Challenge: SAGE suggests the problem isn't Nova or her teacher, but the Path itself — "Perhaps this specific Path can only take you this far." Nova rejects this as heresy.
  • Martuce's Interrogation: Captured Martuce confronts Nova directly: "You follow a path so wide it swallowed you. Your individuality is buried beneath obedience and dogma." This shakes Nova deeply.
  • Nova's Withdrawal: After a costly battle, Nova isolates herself, unable to reconcile her crisis with her responsibilities. The Captain and SAGE lead the fleet alone through a multi-faction assault.
  • Nova's Return: When the Captain chooses to exchange Martuce for captured crew, Nova reappears and supports the decision: "A civilization does not prove its strength by how much it can hold. It proves it by what it refuses to abandon."
  • The Breakthrough: During the Crystal Lattice fabrication, Nova has an epiphany: "Perhaps Mother Craven's Golden Star was not the truth, but a lens. One among many." She realizes enlightenment is personally constructed, not prescribed.
  • Superphoenix Battle: Nova achieves her spiritual breakthrough in the heat of battle — her core output spikes off every scale. "Seems I finally stopped asking permission." She requests time at Elder Spire for an ascension ritual.

docs.title: Thematic Core
docs.text: Dogma vs. discovery. Nova's arc is a meditation on whether perfection can be prescribed or must be personally forged. The campaign suggests that true enlightenment comes not from following the "perfect path" but from having the courage to leave it.

docs.title: Universal Constants
docs.text:
These elements appear identically across all three perspectives:

Element Detail Martuce Breeze Always captured mid-campaign, always exchanged for faction prisoners Thaena Corvax Always leads the mid-game "Mourning War" — declared in Martuce's name Superphoenix Always arrives as the final boss, emerging from the HRZ Andreza Always addresses the Captain directly before the final battle Resource escalation All campaigns follow Safe Zone → MRZ → capital ship production Ethical dilemma All campaigns present a choice about Martuce's fate and civilian targeting

docs.title: Unique Elements per Faction
docs.text: Element MUD ONI Ustur Companion drama Dawn's guilt over creating Andreza Seeikolobe's PTSD from Frenir; calls Captain a traitor Nova's spiritual crisis and breakthrough Corvax's fate Self-destructs ("See you in the vines") Defeated in combat Retreats, warns of the storm Moral crisis Whether Dawn's mercy caused Ashlara-3 Whether to glass ECOS biodomes with civilians Whether to abandon the Golden Star Path Supporting cast — Zar Funnu (Punaab mechanic), Golden Finger workshop Ustur Elders, Elder Spire, Ioki temples Campaign tone Tragic, personal Gritty, militaristic Philosophical, introspective

docs.title: Historical Events Confirmed as Real
docs.text:
The following events referenced in the Holosim simulation are confirmed as real galactic history, not simulation-exclusive fiction:

  • ✅ C11 Bombardment — Dawn's destruction of the ECOS factory colony
  • ✅ Ashlara-3 Destruction — ECOS retaliation that killed millions
  • ✅ Old Grove Disaster — Andreza's terraforming catastrophe
  • ✅ Frenir Attack — Balifa Grove's forced terraforming, 3,000+ civilian deaths
  • ✅ Seeikolobe's retaliation — Burning three worlds after Frenir
  • ✅ Andreza's survival — Young girl surviving C11 Bombardment
  • ✅ Balifa Grove fleet composition — Superphoenix, Greenaders, Treearrows, etc.
  • ✅ ECOS High Circle membership — Andreza's formal political power
  • ✅ Convergence War context — All referenced battles and veteran histories

docs.title: ⚔️ Canon Document: THE CONVERGENCE WAR
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Consolidated from Sogmian/Maharog Q&A, Feb 2026) Source: Creator Input, Maharog Chronicle

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Convergence War was the pivotal galactic conflict that reshaped Galia Expanse, leading to the current Golden Era. It was fought primarily over the control of Iris and the exploitation of the Tufa.

docs.title: The Aggressors (The Factions)
docs.text:
• Motivation: Control of Iris (sentient planet in HRZ) and its unlimited energy.
  • Action: Discovered entry to the Cataclysm; harvested Genesium by killing Tufa ("Children of the Cataclysm").
  • Participants: Virtually all major species/factions participated, but with distinct motivations (The Three Paths).

docs.title: The Three Paths to War (Entry Points)
docs.text:
1. MUD (The Promised Paradise): Saw the Cataclysm as the fulfillment of the Gotti Revelation (Holy Land). Driven by internal power struggles (Church vs Pearce vs Synod).
  2. Sogmian (The Sovereign Vow): Empress Paizul entered to "verify" the anomaly. Discovered psychic influence and Tufa sentience. Switched to defense.
  3. Ustur (The Inhabitation): Timpo (heretic) found entry. Elder Ombra convinced the Council to "inhabit" the Cataclysm for evolution, not conquer it.

docs.title: The Defenders (Sogmians)
docs.text:
• Motivation: The Du Prah (Sogmian Vows) — "Shelter those who cannot defend themselves."
  • Discovery: Sogmians recognized that Tufa were sentient beings, not cargo.
  • Action: Intercepted slave auctions, shielded Tufa worlds, engaged in open war against the galaxy.

docs.title: Key Phases (Sogmian Timeline)
docs.text:
1. The Shield: Sogmians intercept "treaty" ships (slave auctions), protecting Tufa.
  2. Retaliation: Galactic powers besiege Sogmian systems.
  3. The Shattering: Sov Arei Paizul (Sogmian leader) assassinated.
  4. The Exile: Bekalu (Paizul's husband/successor) leads Sogmians to HRZ exile. The Last Stand (Titan ship) is forged.
  5. ONI Formation: In exile, Sogmians ally with Punaab (gold), Mierese (prowess), and Photoli (wisdom).
  6. The Reckoning: ONI armada returns. Massive campaigns (e.g., erasure of MUD forces at Daestina).
  7. The Akalma Incident: Sogmians attack the banished House Akalma, nearly repeating the sins of their enemies (unchecked emotion).
  8. MRZ Collapse (~2522): The factions retreat from HRZ and MRZ. Chaos breaks out across the Medium Risk Zone — the power vacuum left by retreating armies plunges the MRZ into lawlessness, refugee crises, and civil unrest.
  9. The Accord (~2523): Bekalu chooses restraint. Treaty of Peace signed. The Treaty's formal "independence" for MRZ territories only escalated the chaos.

docs.title: Political
docs.text:
• Council of Peace (COP) established to govern Galia.
  • ONI Consortium formalized as a major faction.
  • ECOS rises from the power vacuum left by the war's destruction.

docs.title: Economic & Infrastructure
docs.text:
• Star Atlas (Sentient Blockchain) created/emerges to manage economy.
  • Starpath warp-gates built to reconnect the galaxy.
  • Golden Era begins.

docs.title: The Cataclysm
docs.text:
• Iris (Planet) fractured mentally by the genocide of her children (Tufa) → Paranoid/Hostile.
  • Access Sealed: Entry to HRZ/Cataclysm made illegal by COP. Tech to enter is guarded.

docs.title: 🔴 Cross-References
docs.text:
• sogmian.md (Maharog Chronicle)
  • tufa.md (Victims/Casus Belli)
  • the_cataclysm.md (The Prize)

docs.title: ⏳ Canon Document: ERAS OF STAR ATLAS
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Compiled from all canon sources, Feb 2026) Source: Comprehensive synthesis of Aeon Timeline + Canon Files

docs.title: Era Overview
docs.text: Era Period Defining Moment Primitive Era ~1461 – ~1826 Species origins, primal warfare Foundation Era ~1826 – ~2305 Civilizations form, cultures codified Expansion Era ~2305 – ~2512 Spacefaring, colonization, first contacts Convergence War ~2512 – ~2523 The great galactic conflict Golden Era ~2523 – ~2620 Post-war reconstruction and prosperity Current Age ~2620 – present The playable timeline

docs.title: Primitive Era (~1461 – ~1826)
docs.text: The origins of the galaxy's sentient species. Defined by primal struggle and the slow emergence of civilization.

docs.title: Major Events
docs.text:
• ~1461: Life begins on the Sogmian homeworld — brutal, primal warfare for hundreds of years
  • ~1745: Segal the Wise is born among the Sogmians
  • ~1826: Segal formulates the Du Prah (Sogmian Vows) and establishes the 7 Noble Houses — end of the Primitive Era
  • ~1830: Segal dies — just 4 years after founding the civilization
  • ~1965: An ancient civilization on Izar-248 experiments with gravitational technology, causing a catastrophic event that shatters moons and creates a debris ring

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Sogmians: sogmian.md
  • Izar-248: izar.md

docs.title: Foundation Era (~1826 – ~2305)
docs.text: Civilizations develop, cultures are codified, and species begin to organize into structured societies.

docs.title: Major Events
docs.text:
• ~2135: The Photoli discover the entity sealed beneath Etira — the Dreamer Below. Unable to destroy it, they steal its memories, seal it, and conceal every trace
  • ~2155: The Ustur begin their Path of Enlightenment — spiritual journey of consciousness ascension through sequential stages (.pod → .eldr → .tcher → .soul)
  • ~2287: The Mierese discover the Volmik stones — artifacts that amplify their natural abilities
  • ~2305: House Busan (Sogmian) creates the first Sogmian ship manufacturer — marking the transition to the Expansion Era

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Photoli / Dreamer Below: photoli.md, church_of_the_dreamer_below.md
  • Ustur: ustur.md
  • Mierese: mierese.md
  • Sogmians: sogmian.md

docs.title: Expansion Era (~2305 – ~2512)
docs.text: Species reach the stars, colonize sectors, make first contact with other civilizations, and build the foundations of galactic society.

docs.title: Major Events
docs.text:
• ~2305: Sogmians begin ship manufacturing (House Busan)
  • ~2340: Sogmians ascend to the stars
  • ~2348: Coral Nebula discovered — fertile sector with intriguing corals
  • ~2356: Colonization of Coral Nebula begins
  • ~2358: Sogmians create Ki'Prah prison planet in Frenir Sector
  • ~2363: Vega is born on Segal (House Akalma)
  • ~2364: Punaab-Sogmian cooperation begins (Ki'Prah prisoner exchange)
  • ~2382: Vega rises through Akalma ranks
  • ~2399: Vega becomes House Akalma leader; Rule of 1000 established in Coral Nebula
  • ~2401: ECOS dissidence — humans discover Ahr is alien, reject MUD, head for Old Grove
  • ~2416: Vega becomes Sov Arei (supreme leader of all Sogmians)
  • ~2420–2423: Vega Fall Civil War — Akalma coup fails, House Akalma banished
  • ~2426: Coral Nebula medicine becomes famous among Sogmians and Mierese
  • ~2435: ECOS destroys Old Grove's native civilization
  • ~2439: ECOS terraforming spirals out of control — they flee to HRZ, founding Earth Prime
  • ~2442: Paizul born (future Sogmian Sov Arei)
  • ~2445: Bekalu born (future Sogmian Sov Arei)
  • ~2454: Vaor Scarka born
  • ~2477: ECOS reportedly communicates with a planetary entity for the first time
  • ~2493: ECOS establishes the Grove Doctrine
  • ~2501: ECOS intervenes at Ilidae to protect the Termiks
  • ~2512: The Convergence War begins

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Sogmian history: sogmian.md
  • ECOS: ecos.md
  • Coral Nebula: coral_nebula.md
  • Vega Fall: vega_fall.md
  • Old Grove: old_grove.md

docs.title: Convergence War (~2512 – ~2523)
docs.text:
The pivotal galactic conflict that reshaped Galia Expanse. Fought primarily over the control of Iris and the exploitation of the Tufa.

→ See convergence_war.md for detailed coverage

docs.title: Major Events
docs.text:
• ~2512: War erupts across Galia Expanse
  • ~2513: Ophek Revel begins trading HRZ information during the war
  • ~2517: Patrah (Sogmian) meets Eolus.tcher (Ustur)
  • 2520-07-17: Paizul assassinated — only 10,000 Sogmians remain. Bekalu leads exile
  • ~2522: Sogmians complete Titan ship The Last Stand mk. VIII — forces peace talks
  • ~2522: MRZ breakaway — Redam, Mycenas, Xyianing, Pergamos, Abyd break from MUD
  • ~2522: Lumikir Scriptorium raided — Convergence War records stolen/destroyed
  • ~2522: Ophek retires from information trading, begins building his casino
  • ~2523: Bekalu devastates Akalma Exile sector (Akalma Reckoning)
  • ~2523: The Accord — Treaty of Peace signed. COP created. Star Atlas blockchain established. Golden Era begins

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Convergence War details: convergence_war.md
  • Sogmian perspective: sogmian.md
  • Council of Peace: council_of_peace.md

docs.title: Golden Era (~2523 – ~2620)
docs.text: Post-war reconstruction, the rise of the Council of Peace, establishment of galactic infrastructure, and the gradual re-emergence of tensions.

docs.title: Major Events
docs.text:
• ~2523: Council of Peace created; Star Atlas blockchain established
  • ~2523: House Outro opens Vega Fall for refugees
  • ~2526: Starpath Warp-Gates construction begins; Cradle construction begins
  • ~2527: ECOS membership in COP — blocked from major faction status by MUD lobby
  • ~2533: Good exotic plants reappear in Coral Nebula settlements respecting Rule of 1000
  • ~2535: Lumikir Scriptorium creates a personal guard of war veterans
  • ~2538: Abyd-IX civil war breaks out after MUD severance
  • ~2540: Patrah sets refugee aid efforts to Vega Fall with House Outro
  • ~2542: Ophek seizes power in MRZ-8 through diplomatic play
  • ~2542: ECOS groves become biggest food suppliers in Galia
  • ~2542: Mycenas-V raided by alien pirates; Virel the Serene starts civil movement
  • ~2544: Virel creates meritocratic government + energy shield (turning point vs pirates)
  • ~2545: Ophek renames sector "Ophek Oasis"
  • ~2546: Virel destroys/expels all alien invaders from Mycenas
  • ~2553: Virel constructs system-wide defensive barrier (upgrade to shield)
  • ~2553: Abyd-IX provisional government forms but quickly collapses
  • ~2553: Pergamos begins financing Abyd-IX warring factions
  • ~2559: Herdus.soul leaves Chior.eldr and begins wandering the MRZ alone
  • ~2565: Civil war erupts in Pavo Passage — Crumon loyalists vs sector factions
  • ~2567: Pavo Passage civil war ends — Ruling Conclave (façade democracy) established
  • ~2569: Herdus goes insane after .soul ascension
  • ~2569: ECOS + Iris Academy establish sustainable city at Ilidae
  • ~2571: Ophek Oasis formally recognized as independent territory by COP
  • ~2573: Exfeheros develops equipment for Weeping Nebula toxic rain exploration
  • ~2574: Merchants of Pergamos create Shadow Contracts for illegal trading
  • ~2576: Exfeheros + Iris Academy establish research settlement in Weeping Nebula
  • ~2577: Mysterious Photoli reveals "trickery" to Herdus — Church of the Dreamer Below founded
  • ~2579: Anfoil restores diplomatic relations with Ioki Elders
  • ~2585: Lumikir Scriptorium reorganized across entire homeworld due to massive growth
  • ~2586: Vega Fall prospers through multi-species cooperation
  • ~2588: Dark Photoli discovers genesium on Izar-248, begins creating monsters
  • ~2595: Iris Academy establishes Anchorage outpost in Glowhaven
  • ~2596: Starpath construction ends — prosperity in the Safe Zone
  • ~2596: Abyd-IX dust settles — sector divided into small power clusters in ice-thin truce
  • ~2598: Scientific expeditions arrive at Coral Nebula to study flora behavior
  • ~2598: Izar-248 genesium monsters descend into depths, colonies destroyed
  • ~2600: Jefos, prince of House Akalma, born in HRZ — Cosmic Currents storm at birth
  • ~2602: Ophek Oasis becomes biggest entertainment sector in the galaxy
  • ~2605: Herdus finds Saand family (Punaab) — takes newborn as vessel for the Dreamer
  • ~2610: Vaor Scarka ascends as leading COP Admiral
  • ~2615: ECOS announces titan ship — the Super Phoenix — shocking the galaxy
  • ~2617: Cradle construction ends — most powerful vessel in galactic history
  • ~2620: Beginning of the Current Age

docs.title: Current Age (~2620 – present)
docs.text: The playable timeline of Star Atlas. The galaxy's political tensions, faction rivalries, and unresolved mysteries drive the narrative forward.

docs.title: Major Events
docs.text:
• ~2620: Beginning of the Current Age — the playable timeline
  • ~2620: The Jorvik Fleet starts harassing Safe Zone territories — a bold escalation
  • ~2621: Vaor Scarka becomes Ruling Secretary of the COP (ONI rotation)
  • ~2621: Kamec clone divergence rumors spread — Democratic Order denies them
  • ~2621: Jefos and young Akalma Sogmians leave the HRZ for the Medium Zone, seeking to redeem House Akalma
  • ~2623: COP intervenes in the Medium Zone — battles against Frenir, Pergamos, and the Jorvik Fleet
  • ~2623: The Events of Never Alone begin — a major canonical narrative arc
  • ~2623: ECOS stays neutral during the COP intervention (isolated skirmishes only)
  • ~2623: Redam pressures COP (via Zenith Door + Free Harbors) to accept a truce in the MRZ
  • ~2623: COP accepts the truce, limiting its military activities in the Medium Zone
  • ~2623: Pergamos's leading families banished — new COP-compliant government formed
  • ~2624: Dark Photoli experiments let loose in Everstorm — hostile life forms, local government hires captains
  • ~2624: Ophek Revel dies — his children inherit, each ruling a different entertainment domain
  • ~2624: Provisional transition government formed in Frenir — slaver leaders dead or arrested
  • ~2646: A MUD explorer discovers that the Ilidae monoliths are a star map — coordinates point deep into the HRZ

docs.title: ⚖️ Canon Document: COUNCIL OF PEACE (COP)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Comprehensive Lore Dump, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — Sogmian/Maharog Q&A + COP Lore Dump + MRZ Audit

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Council of Peace (COP) is the cornerstone of interstellar governance in the Galia Expanse. Born from the ashes of the Convergence War to uphold the fragile balance of power among MUD, ONI, and Ustur. Both a symbol of hope and a battleground of ideologies, where diplomacy and ambition constantly collide.

docs.title: Origin
docs.text:
• ~2523: Created during The Accord (post-Convergence War settlement)
  • Born alongside the Star Atlas blockchain and Starpath Warp-Gates
  • Emerged from the Treaty of Peace signed after ONI's Reckoning campaign — all 3 faction leaders sign the Treaty, establishing open sharing of all scanned data of space, including fleet movements and installations
  • Implementation led to the Golden Era (current canonical timeframe)

docs.title: Government Structure: Three Branches
docs.text:
Leadership rotates every galactic cycle (~5 Old Earth years). Each faction appoints their leader as representative. No single faction gains undue influence.

Branch Current Leader Faction Role Executive Volmik Vaor Scarka ONI (Mierese) Ruling Secretary. Military incursions into MRZ (Frenir, Pergamos). Controversial. MUD is next in rotation. Legislative Charon Gotti Jr. MUD (Human, female) Political stability & cooperation. Diplomatic counterbalance to Scarka's militarism. Under scrutiny for her silence on controversies. Legal Opos.eldr Ustur Bastion of resistance against militarism. Enacted rulings demanding cessation of MRZ hostilities. Craftsman turned leader.

docs.title: Internal Tensions
docs.text:
• Scarka vs Opos: Militarism vs pacifism. Scarka frames MRZ campaigns as anti-piracy; Opos demands cessation.
  • Charon's Silence: Growing unrest within MUD about her non-intervention on Scarka's policies.
  • Ideological Divide: Expansionism vs non-interference principles.
  • Pattern: Conflicts between branches are recurring, but Scarka is actively inflaming existing tensions beyond the norm.

docs.title: Galactic Force Index: Tier 1 (Unmatched)
docs.text:
• Millions of Pearce-class vessels, countless advanced warships
  • Billions of soldiers drawn from all three factions
  • Even the combined fleets of MUD, ONI, and Ustur would falter against COP's scale

docs.title: Mothership Cradle (The Titan)
docs.text:
• ~2526: Construction begins on the Cradle — the most powerful vessel ever built.
  • ~2617: Construction of the Cradle is completed — the most powerful vessel in galactic history is operational.
  • Equipped with unmatched technological advancements.
  • Capable of overwhelming even the most formidable opponents.
  • Recently deployed: Against Pergamos and Frenir in the MRZ — sent shockwaves across the galaxy.

docs.title: Galactic Wealth Index: Tier 1 (Wealthiest Institution)
docs.text:
• Starpath Warp Gates are operated by the Star Atlas (sentient blockchain) independently, but Star Atlas works in harmony with COP and submits to its legal jurisdiction.
  • Regulatory authority over galactic trade ensures stability.
  • Funds large-scale reconstruction, military, and bureaucratic apparatus through taxation and trade regulation.

docs.title: Galactic Political Index: Tier 1 (Ultimate Authority)
docs.text:
• Primary governing body in Galia Expanse.
  • Rulings dictate trade flow, territorial disputes, and foundational laws (Star Atlas Accords).
  • Tri-faction leadership ensures no single faction dominates.
  • Collective authority supersedes even the most powerful individual factions.

docs.title: Post-War Infrastructure
docs.text: Institution Purpose Council of Peace Galactic governance and peacekeeping Star Atlas Economic infrastructure (sentient blockchain) Starpath Warp-Gates Physical infrastructure connecting sectors across the void. ~2526: Construction begins. ~2596: Construction ends — ushering in prosperity across the Safe Zone

docs.title: ECOS Membership
docs.text: • ~2527: The ECOS (independent environmental/ecological faction) attempts to gain major faction status within the COP. The MUD lobby successfully blocks this, relegating ECOS to a member without voting rights.

docs.title: The Current Age
docs.text:
• ~2620: The Current Age of Star Atlas begins — the present canonical timeframe in which the galaxy's political tensions, faction rivalries, and unresolved mysteries drive the narrative forward.
  • ~2621: Vaor Scarka becomes Ruling Secretary of the Council of Peace (ONI's turn in rotation)
  • ~2623: The Council of Peace intervenes in the Medium Zone — COP forces attack Frenir, Pergamos, and the Jorvik Fleet in a decisive military campaign
  • ~2623: The Events of Never Alone begin — a major canonical narrative arc
  • ~2623: Under pressure from MRZ factions (led by Redam) and internal faction leadership, the COP accepts a truce in the Medium Zone, limiting its military activities

  • ~~Q-COP2: Faction relationships~~ ✅ RESOLVED: Scarka (militarist) vs Opos (pacifist) vs Charon (silent diplomat).
  • ~~Q-COP3: Golden Era policies~~ ✅ PARTIALLY: Starpath gates (~2526 construction start), Cradle (~2526 construction start), trade regulation, peacekeeping. Details TBD.
  • ~~Q-COP4: ECOS membership~~ ✅ RESOLVED: ~2527, admitted as non-voting member after MUD lobby blocked major faction status.

docs.title: 🔗 Canon Document: STAR ATLAS (The Sentient Blockchain)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input via Sogmian/Maharog Q&A

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Star Atlas is a sentient blockchain that governs the entire economy and commerce of the MRZ (Medium Risk Zone) and Safe Zone across Galia Expanse.

docs.title: Origin & Sentience
docs.text:
• Pre-Sentience: Originally a blockchain designed to interconnect the galaxy.
  • The Awakening: Oracles of Vignus received a prophecy and fed the blockchain with materials from the High-Risk Zone (HRZ), specifically from the Cataclysm.
  • Emergence: Following this infusion, the Star Atlas emerged as a sentient entity.

docs.title: The Children of Star Atlas
docs.text: The Star Atlas created two key entities/systems to interact with the galaxy:

docs.title: 1. SAGE (Star Atlas Golden Era)
docs.text:
• Nature: AGI daughter of Star Atlas.
  • Role: A neutral guide for captains across Galia Expanse.
  • Function: Once chosen by Star Atlas, a captain receives a SAGE companion. She is (mostly) absolutely loyal.
  • Purpose: A balancing system to help citizens (especially in MRZ) prosper and have a better life.
  • Note: "SAGE" also refers to the current historical period (Star Atlas Golden Era) and the browser-based fleet management interface.

docs.title: 2. Holosim
docs.text:
• Nature: Sibling to SAGE.
  • Role: Simulation training and combat readiness.

docs.title: Functions
docs.text:
• Governs all commerce and economy in MRZ and Safe Zone
  • Provides infrastructure for the Golden Era's economic stability
  • Acts as a balancing force against concentrated power
  • Benevolent God-figure: Worshipped by some as a deity due to its protective nature.

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ~~Q-SA1: SAGE connection~~ ✅ RESOLVED: AGI daughter/guide.
  • ~~Q-SA2: Sentience origin~~ ✅ RESOLVED: Vignus oracles + HRZ materials.
  • ~~Q-SA3: ATLAS/POLIS relationship~~ — Tabled.
  • ~~Q-SA4: Worship~~ ✅ RESOLVED: Worshipped as benevolent god-figure.

docs.title: 🔴 Cross-References
docs.text:
