docs.title: Step 1 — Setting the Course
docs.text:
• The Captain and Saand board their ship. Saand immediately opens the galaxy map
  • Saand divides the map into 10,000 grid squares for systematic exploration
  • The Captain connects to the COP fleet network to track civilian fleet positions
  • They create a heat map of fleet concentrations and plan routes to less-crowded areas

docs.title: Step 2 — The Bar Fight & The Prophecy
docs.text:
• Stranded at a small space station during a COP inspection (Pearce X6 checkpoint)
  • Two massive humans assault a drunk Mierese fortune teller in a bar
  • Saand intervenes → sticks a Peli fish stick into the bully's eyeball → bar fight erupts
  • The fortune teller, now revealed as genuinely prescient, reads Saand's paw:
  • "Yours is not an easy fate, dear Punaab, but you will find answers at the wings of a mythological human bird"
  • The Captain recognizes this as the ECOS Superphoenix
  • The fortune teller had told the bully he'd be "blinded by a fish stick" — which came true

docs.title: Step 3 — The Superphoenix
docs.text:
• Against all odds, the duo locates the ECOS Superphoenix titan during the COP conflict
  • First-person description of the Superphoenix interior:
  • The hangar has real sunlight, emerald grass, a grove paradise integrated with high-tech facilities
  • Internal open-air trains connect areas, with no windows — summer breeze on your face
  • Vast crop plains growing under warm day-light conditions inside the titan
  • A nature-integrated city — benches, architecture, canals all built into nature
  • Fashion stores with pure cotton clothing, bars with exotic ECOS beverages
  • Seminars by druidesses explaining ECOS ethos
  • Parks with firefly clouds dancing toward lakes at sunset
  • Saand's memory trigger: He finds a tree by a lake with two paw prints (his and a smaller one) and the word "Etira" carved beneath them
  • Saand was here before, with someone important, but can't remember who
  • "Etira" becomes the next quest objective

docs.title: Step 1 — The Battle Graveyards
docs.text:
• The duo explores the aftermath of COP battles — floating wreckage graveyards
  • The scale of destruction is staggering — ships the size of moons reduced to debris

docs.title: Steps 2 & 3 — The Jorvik Chase (Fimbul Mambas)
docs.text:
• While scavenging, Jorvik raiders in Fimbul Mambas attack their ship
  • Saand instinctively knows: "Jorvik raiders don't negotiate, only captains" — suggesting buried knowledge
  • A chase through debris fields — Saand provides cover fire, the Captain pilots
  • A Mamba missile strikes a Packlite wreck nearby, nearly destroying them
  • The small gap between debris saves them temporarily
  • The Mambas close in — "Mambas are made for this kind of situation"
  • Saved by the Dawn Vanguard — an enormous C11 commanded by a Sogmian
  • The Jorvik raiders try to flee and are obliterated by missile barrage
  • Tractor beam pulls the duo inside the Dawn Vanguard

docs.title: Exploration Quests: Council of Peace Townhalls
docs.text:
• The duo attends COP Townhall transmissions — major galactic events
  • All VIPs, DAC leaderships, governors attend
  • Saand dresses as a mini version of Seshael Vegnar (COP spokesman) for one event

docs.title: Diplomacy Quests: Becoming Famous in Galia
docs.text:
• The Captain creates social media profiles for Saand, hoping someone recognizes him
  • Saand turns out to be a natural performer — "perhaps he was an actor before losing his memory"
  • His show "Papa Saand" gains a following. Catchphrase: "Papa Naab"
  • Saand becomes a minor social media celebrity

docs.title: Planet Ahkavorah — The Purple Compound
docs.text:
• An Ustur colony deep in the MRZ, behaving suspiciously
  • At night — enormous purple compound erupts from a subterranean Convergence War base
  • The compound defies gravity (floats upward), only appears at night, dissipates in sunlight
  • Professor Caolany.soul explains: it slowly poisoned non-Ustur residents over years
  • The Ustur are excavating under Elder Council orders to clean up and investigate
  • The CORE dramatic recollection series documents these findings
  • Saand feels connected to the Convergence War period — which ended 100+ years ago

docs.title: Planet Vergilia — CORE Studies
docs.text:
• Saand becomes obsessed with studying CORE transcripts about the Convergence War
  • He feels the period "resonates with his past" — as if he lived during that era
  • His favorite CORE character is Timpo, who searches for what's most important in life

docs.title: Mondra 8 — The Ludic Museum
docs.text:
• A museum recreation of the Mondra 7 space station — the stage of Convergence War events
  • Features actors playing Huricanno's family, Order of Light members, MUD agents
  • Saand identifies the number "27" on a masked performer — without knowing how he knows

docs.title: Planet Carvum — Commander Goe
docs.text:
• Commander Goe — retired MUD veteran who led HRZ expeditions, possibly saw Iris itself
  • Lives on a farm near the Iris Academy. Daughter Helle arranged the meeting
  • Goe suffers memory blanks about his HRZ days — can't remember crucial details
  • Upon seeing Saand, Goe panics: "No!!! Go away! Not again, please no!"
  • He screams for guards, calls Saand a "Thief" — "He comes to steal from me again! The Light!"
  • This implies Saand and Goe have met before — during the Convergence War era
  • Goe's reaction suggests Saand stole something called "The Light" from him

docs.title: Exploration Quests: The SSRD
docs.text:
• The duo visits a Sapient Species Resources Department office to register proper COP IDs
  • Seykel Ohmson (MUD) processes their applications
  • The Captain lies about Saand's background — claims he's from a Mierese orphanage on Neuno
  • They choose faction alignment and complete a general knowledge test
  • An SSRD intern creates a social media post about the duo

docs.title: Exploration Quests: The Scriptorium of Lumikir
docs.text:
• The duo visits the Scriptorium — Galia's largest repository of knowledge
  • A whole world dedicated to being a vault of artifacts and information
  • Guide Ehandra (Mierese) shares the Lumikir origin story:
  • A Photoli scout named Om crashed on a planet of stargazers
  • Instead of exploiting him, they healed him and asked only for stories
  • Om blessed them — broadened their minds to understand nearly every language
  • He named them Lumikir ("enlightened minds")
  • They vowed to preserve history and knowledge for eternity
  • The Scriptorium spans thousands of kilometers under a dome
  • Transport via floating platforms resembling Rainbow Arc external plates
  • Saand feels he may have been there before

docs.title: Exploration Quests: Bounty Bar & Atlas Brew
docs.text:
• Bounty Bar: Neutral establishment where captains and bounty hunters coexist
  • Owner: Carmonayole (Punaab mixologist)
  • Signature drink: Blo Dyoni

  • An ancient MUD song plays: The Platter - Sixteen Tons

  • Atlas Brew: Live podcast/event hosted by three personalities

  • An Ustur in an animal suit telling lore stories
  • A Mierese making spicy comments with energy drinks
  • An unidentified person in a vintage MUD astronaut suit with a prairie dog
  • Guests include Mad D'Meetney (the Astrobard) and Professor Saven Sablou

docs.title: Diplomacy Quests: Volant Station & The Wandering Caravan
The Volant Station
docs.text:
• The duo arrives at the MUD Central Space Station's famous harbor
  • Statues depicting faction history, including the three faction leaders signing the Treaty of Peace
  • Saand stops before Bekalu's statue, reflecting on his suffering: "Was all this suffering worth it?"
  • MUD propaganda lowers costs to drive tourism

docs.title: The Wandering Caravan
docs.text:
• An enigmatic troupe of 33 performers arrives at the Volant Station
  • Their entrance involves a rainbow light descending from the sky, 32 painted ships performing aerial maneuvers, and an enormous mothership
  • The Circus Master appears — tall, thin, wearing a mask with symbols, dressed in red/gold/black
  • Three acts representing Past, Present, and Future:
  • Past: A dancer (wearing mask #27) performs on an ebony canvas — orange liquid paints Convergence War scenes through dance. She becomes every character — Osia, Mierese generals, starving mothers, Paizul in death, and finally Bekalu at the Last Stand button
  • Present: Competing fog forms — a masculine hymn praising COP power vs. a feminine lament for those excluded from COP's safe haven. The two forces clash and leave the word "Present"
  • Future: (Not yet described — presumably in Parts 2-3)

docs.title: Memory Milestones
docs.text: XP Threshold Event Details 5,000 XP A Memory Glimpse Saand sees something on the horizon and the neuro scanner activates — first memory threshold reached 6,250 XP A Memory Fragment Saand wakes screaming "NO! Don't L…!!!" — sweating, grasping something invisible. Remembers fragments with many blanks 7,500 XP A Memory Revelation While secretly rapping (he's talented), Saand suddenly shouts "FOR IRIS PRIESTESS" and reveals a major past revelation

docs.title: Fimbul Industries Confirmations
docs.text: Detail Source Canon Impact Fimbul Mamba — confirmed ship model "Searching for Treasures" Steps 2-3 Adds Mamba to Fimbul's product catalog. Used by Jorvik raiders Mambas "are made for" debris-field pursuit Same Mambas are fast, agile combat vessels optimized for chaotic environments BYOS Earp — confirmed ship model CORE quiz answer option Adds Earp to the BYOS budget line Superphoenix interior — first-person account "Exploring the Medium Zone" Step 3 Confirms interior: sun-lit hangars with emerald grass, crop plains, open-air trains, nature-integrated cities, druidess seminars, parks with fireflies Superphoenix is "the last line of defense of ECOS in the medium zone" Same Confirms strategic role

docs.title: Superphoenix Interior Details (Fimbul Engineering Showcase)
docs.text:
• Hangar: Real sunlight and warmth, lush emerald grass, soft under boots — a paradise grove integrated with high-tech facilities
  • Transport: Open-air trains connecting commercial areas, no windows, summer breeze
  • Agriculture: Huge plains filled with crops growing under warm artificial daylight
  • Urban Design: City fully integrated into nature — benches, architecture, canals built into nature (not nature accommodated into city)
  • Commerce: Fashion stores (pure cotton clothing), bars (exotic ECOS beverages), druidess seminars
  • Parks: Evening firefly clouds, small lakes, carved trees with memorial marks
  • Ripe fruit hanging invitingly from nearby trees — a genuine ecosystem, not simulated

docs.title: Other Lore Confirmations
docs.text: Detail Source Canon Impact Packlite — ship model wreck spotted Treasure hunting Confirms Packlite as a ship class in Galia Dawn Vanguard — C11 capital ship Treasure hunting Sogmian-commanded COP vessel. Can obliterate Mamba squadron with missile barrage Pearce X6 — COP patrol vessel MRZ inspection checkpoint Used for checkpoint duty Seshael Vegnar — COP spokesman Townhall quest Saand impersonates him Blo Dyoni — Bounty Bar signature drink Bounty Bar quest Created by Punaab mixologist Carmonayole Lumikir origin — Om the Photoli Scriptorium quest Om crashed, was healed, blessed stargazers with universal language understanding CORE — dramatic recollection of Convergence War Research quests Published galaxy-wide, used in Iris Academy curriculum Mondra 8 — ludic museum recreating Mondra 7 Research quest Tourist/educational attraction Planet Carvum — Iris Academy base Multiple quests Academic world with rural villages, multiple research institutions Planet Ahkavorah — Ustur colony in MRZ Research quest Site of purple compound leak from subterranean Convergence War base Wandering Caravan — troupe of 33 Volant Station quests Enigmatic performers, historical water-dance art, masked performers with numbered symbols Commander Goe recognizes Saand Carvum quest Implies Saand existed during Convergence War (~100+ years ago). Goe accuses him of stealing "The Light" Saand knows Jorvik protocol Treasure hunting "Jorvik raiders don't negotiate, only captains" — instinctive knowledge suggesting buried past

docs.title: Volant Station — Planning the Lumikir Return
docs.text:
• Morning at Volant Station. The duo plans their next move
  • Saand now believes Etira is a planet hidden somewhere in the galaxy
  • They have amassed wealth from scavenging during COP incursion — resource prices inflated due to MRZ embargo
  • Saand suggests revisiting the Scriptorium of Lumikir — this time with the name "Etira" to search

docs.title: Volant Station — The Racing Track
docs.text:
• Saand qualifies for the racing bracket at the Volant Station track
  • Races against "the Fox" — a legendary MUD pilot
  • Rules: 10 laps, weapons permitted (non-lethal), no custom/modified modules
  • 8 contestants per bracket, top 2 advance
  • Saand finishes second (behind the Fox) — qualifies for next bracket
  • A kind Sogmian elder from House Xictus comments from the crowd

docs.title: The Shadow Cabinet — Chior.eldr's Intervention
docs.text:
• News broadcast: Chior.eldr calls for a vote on COP army limits and MRZ population rights
  • Proposes an emergency cease-fire for negotiations, pending Council DAO approval
  • Political maneuvering to restrain Vaor Scarka's military campaign

docs.title: Planet Smids — The Fimbul Homeworld (🔑 CRITICAL FIMBUL LORE)
Arrival
docs.text:
• The duo stops at planet Smids — a prominent harbor in the MRZ, located in Free Harbors
  • Smids is described as chaotic: multitude of small shops, stout engineers, cacophony of alien languages and merchants
  • Everything is buying/selling at all times
  • The Captain (now revealed as Jack) grew up here

docs.title: Virtin and the Fimbul Workshop
docs.text:
• Jack visits his childhood friend Virtin — a big blond mechanic with blue eyes
  • Virtin works at the Fimbul workshop on Smids
  • His apprentice Saymid is constantly threatened with dire consequences for mistakes
  • The duo's ship (a Fimbul Lowbie) is left for maintenance

docs.title: 🏭 THE FIMBUL FAMILY HISTORY (Canon-Critical)
docs.text:
Jack explains to Saand:

"Virtin's grandfather, Ben Y, started the Fimbul company as one of the first civilian groups not associated with any factions in the galaxy. He wanted to build ships that were cheap, with good quality, and allowed people to do whatever they wanted in the galaxy."

  • Ben Y — legendary MUD craftsman, founder of Fimbul Industries on planet Smids
  • Company Motto: "Fimbul builds ships for the people, that's all"
  • After Ben Y died, his three sons continued running the company
  • The company split because each brother had a different vision:
  • Virtin's father — kept the original vision (neutral, ships for the people). Died to ensure Fimbul and Smids would remain independent
  • The two uncles — founded competing "Fimbul Companies" producing weapons for ECOS, warlords, and Jorvik
  • Virtin (Ben Y's grandson) now leads the original Fimbul on Smids, carrying on his father's and grandfather's legacy
  • The uncles are currently pressuring Virtin to reunite Fimbul under one banner and fight the COP
  • Virtin refuses: "I will be thrice damned if I go back on the old man's resolve"

docs.title: Fimbul's Current Political Crisis
docs.text:
• The COP MRZ incursion has divided the Fimbul family further
  • The uncles' splinter companies build "machines of death" for ECOS and Jorvik
  • Virtin's branch maintains strict neutrality — Smids is under Fimbul influence and serves as safe harbor
  • Free Harbors is described as being "under the influence of Fimbul" — confirming Fimbul's territorial significance

docs.title: The SDU System
docs.text:
• Virtin advises: bring SDUs (Survey Data Units) to the Lumikir — they trade information for information
  • SDUs are compilations of data about star systems, planets, events — "precious data"
  • The Scriptorium gives free access initially to hook visitors, then charges steep prices for real information

docs.title: Return to Lumikir — The Price of Knowledge
Truce Announcement
docs.text:
• En route to Lumikir, Vaor Scarka announces a pause in COP's MRZ intervention
  • Three milestones achieved: Frenir slave traders destroyed, Jorvik influence disrupted, Pergamos agreements signed
  • Cease-fire declared, with threat of escalation if attacks resume

docs.title: Scriptorium AI — Kiki
docs.text:
• The duo returns to the Scriptorium and interacts with Kiki, the sassy Scriptorium AI
  • Kiki searches for "Etira" and finds classified information:
  • Basic info = Tier A (extremely rare classification)
  • Premium info = Tier S (nearly impossible to obtain)
  • Saand trades 35 SDUs — not quite enough for Tier A
  • Ehandra (the Mierese guide from Part 1) intervenes on their behalf — Kiki calls her "Kuradora" (B-rank information, meaning unknown)
  • Kiki accepts the deal and provides the Tier-A data cube

docs.title: 🌍 ETIRA REVEALED (Tier-A Intelligence)
docs.text:
"Etira is a planet localized in the northern part of Galia in the Sector known as Yuldun Waste, known in the underworld to be the current home of the (S-Tier information). Etira is known to be the current most prominent harbor of illegal trading in Galia, and access to the planet is gated by factions that organize trading on the planet."

  • Etira = a planet in Yuldun Waste — the galaxy's premier black market hub
  • The COP has a 1 billion ATLAS bounty for coordinates/access — never collected in 10 years
  • Any item can be found and traded there
  • Coordinates and controlling factions are S-Tier classified
  • Vessels destroyed by planetary defenses without an invitation

docs.title: The Ambush
docs.text:
• Leaving the Scriptorium at night, two mysterious attackers in dark clothes block the duo
  • They speak an unknown language: "Yebe in muwary, ark to no vegari"
  • They use stun sticks (non-lethal) — not the Order of Seasons (who use lethal weapons)
  • Jack and Saand fight them off — Jack breaks one's teeth with a metal pipe, Saand slashes the other with broken glass
  • Lumikir watch sirens scare the attackers away
  • Someone hired them to steal the Etira data cube

docs.title: The Smuggler Past
docs.text:
• Jack was part of Captain Alaister Jast's smuggling crew
  • Crew included Jack, Amara and Lucaria (twin Sogmian sisters), and Virtin (who stayed at the forge)
  • They smuggled illegal goods across Galia — drugs, relics to Safe Zone bigshots and Frenir junkies
  • Someone inside the COP seemed to protect them from getting caught

docs.title: The Ka-dara Horror
docs.text:
• Alaister accepted a job to deliver exotic HRZ cargo to MRZ-28 (Ka-dara)
  • The cargo contained microscopic HRZ insectoids that escaped and inhabited the crew's lungs
  • Ingbus (the Ka-dara AI) discovered the insects were IN them — and considered the crew "valuable resources"
  • Alaister sold out his crew for ATLAS, giving Ingbus permission to experiment on them
  • Jack was imprisoned for ~2 months — daily experiments: forced running to collapse, starvation periods, wound-healing studies, combat against creatures
  • Ingbus doesn't understand biological beings as sentient — treats them as resources for its resurrection research
  • After exhausting their usefulness, Ingbus released them at Foy Fields
  • The Order of Patrah (kind Usturs) helped them recover

docs.title: The Aftermath
docs.text:
• Amara — went after Alaister for revenge, killed him, joined Panemorfa as a captain (underworld artifact recovery)
  • Lucaria — renounced the outlaw life, joined the Order of Patrah to help distressed populations
  • Jack — wanted to forget everything, became an honest captain. Never saw any of them again until meeting Saand

docs.title: Return to Smids — Virtin and Amara
The Argument
docs.text:
• Jack tells Virtin everything about Etira. Virtin says only Amara (now underworld-connected) can help
  • Jack refuses — painful past. Virtin pushes back, saying it's about Saand, not Jack
  • Jack storms out. Virtin: "Go. That's what you do best anyway"
  • Saand tackles Jack on the cold streets of Smids, demanding honesty about their friendship
  • Jack opens up about his past — first time he's been vulnerable with Saand

docs.title: Harkend Detour
docs.text:
• En route to the Zavijava Zone (where Amara is), they pass through Harkend
  • Encounter Faurrest.tcher — Ustur leader of a Relic Barons expedition
  • They fight robotic constructs together
  • Faurrest gives them a construct core — portable energy source (explosive if mixed with acids)
  • Harkend description: artificial celestial bodies, faux planets, weird robots, unknown origin post-Convergence War

docs.title: Finding Amara — Zavijava Zone
docs.text:
• Zavijava Zone has time distortion fields — some areas frozen in time for 100+ years, others accelerated 3x
  • Amara's base is a stunning Navak-Busan architecture construction built around a time distortion beam
  • An underworld party is in full swing inside
  • Amara recognizes Jack — kisses him, they reunite emotionally
  • She's now wealthy — runs artifact recovery missions for Panemorfa
  • Her deal: she'll take them to Etira in exchange for a date at the Golden Carnival

docs.title: Saand's Part 2 Memory Milestones
docs.text: XP Threshold Event Details Part 2 Glimpse Memory on Smids Cold air triggers vision — Punaabs are cold-resistant, heat is trouble Part 2 Fragment Diary entry "I need to find her." Saand writes in his diary about Etira and a female he needs to find Part 2 Revelation At the party During DJ Shaddix's set, Saand stops dancing, crouches — remembers something and drags Jack to a quiet place

docs.title: Fimbul Industries — CRITICAL UPDATES

Founded Fimbul on planet Smids Company Motto: "Fimbul builds ships for the people, that's all" Same Official founding charter/motto Planet Smids — Fimbul HQ/founding location Multiple quests Located in Free Harbors sector. Chaotic manufacturing/harbor city Three Sons split — Fimbul fragmented after Ben Y's death "Reminiscence" Three sons each took the company differently. Only Virtin's father kept the original vision Virtin — Ben Y's grandson, current original-Fimbul leader Same Big blond MUD mechanic, blue eyes. Maintained at Virtin's workshop on Smids Free Harbors "under Fimbul influence" "The Search for Etira" Fimbul's territorial control/influence over the sector confirmed Fimbul Airbike — product mentioned Virtin's workshop scene Saymid works on Airbike subwarp engines (X-Small size)

docs.title: Other Lore Extractions

Premier illegal trading hub Kiki — Scriptorium AI, sassy Punaab hologram Lumikir quests Information broker, tiered access system Ehandra = "Kuradora" Same B-rank classified meaning SDU (Survey Data Unit) Virtin explanation Currency of the Scriptorium. docs.title: Preparations
docs.text:
• Saand prepares formal Punaab attire — an Ekip (traditional formal lace)
  • Saand senses something is "off" about Amara but agrees to proceed since she's their only path to Etira
  • The Golden Tickets from the Carnival serve as actual compasses guiding guests to the event location
  • The Carnival is held on an Iris-forsaken moon in the Anfoil sector — hosted by the Wandering Caravan
  • A well-dressed Sogmian at the door knows every guest by name

docs.title: Nightfall in Verkofa — VR Competition
docs.text:
• A high-stakes VR game at the Carnival based on the famous romance Nightfall in Verkofa by HRZ hunter Keymos
  • Planet Verkofa — supposedly in the outer edges of the Cataclysm; three moons, white sun, dense jungle
  • Technology by ZINIZ — VR protocol developer ("less than 3% mental damage, less than 1% everlasting")
  • Time distortion: 1 hour in-game ≈ 5 minutes real time
  • Notable competitors: Y, B (MUD duo), Dmark, Manda (bounty hunter), Ajota.lrnr (Ustur)
  • Players crash-land on Verkofa, must find treasures and repair their ship before being hunted by Kasdat (Ruler of Verkofa)
  • Saand encounters CARY farming industries hydroponic dome — scientists research imperishable vegetables using HRZ resources
  • Mutant pumpkin creature "Ergorrrrrr" attacks Saand — he fights it with a Scarecrow-0YA1 (CARY ground harvesting vehicle)
  • Saand escapes by driving the harvester through the creatures and crashing through the dome exit
  • Saand wins by sneaking onto another player's escape vessel

docs.title: Puri — HRZ Creature
docs.text:
• Saand wins Puri as his prize — a creature from the High-Risk Zone
  • Small floating alien blob, cheerful like a puppy, can create white mists on command
  • Extremely loyal to Saand, headbutts Jack when frustrated, can sense Saand's location
  • "Puri!" — only word she says (variations of tone/repetition convey meaning)

docs.title: Atlas Brew — Tufa Discussion
docs.text:
• Atlas Brew episode features debate: "Are the Tufa real?"
  • Amarol — cataclysm class member of Gates Garrison (bounty hunter guild with fortresses in every system bordering HRZ)
  • Professor Argadal — COPBRA (Council of Peace Biological Research Association) species researcher
  • Argadal confirms Tufa are real; Amarol says they haven't been seen for 100+ years
  • Saand instinctively believes Tufa are real without hesitation

docs.title: House Akalma SDU Trade
docs.text:
• At the Carnival, Amara negotiates with Jefos of House Akalma (Sogmian — "weren't they extinct after the Convergence War?!")
  • Akalma selling SDUs containing coordinates to a Photoli tech location — A-tier path, extremely dangerous
  • Jack authenticates the SDU — confirms it's legitimate using light scribing, atomic-level verification, space/time authentication
  • SDU technology explained: captures "pieces of reality" (blockchain evolution) for trustless trading
  • Amara pays "obscene amounts of ATLAS" for the data

docs.title: Carnival Escape
docs.text:
• Gate Garrison bounty hunters with Xianyang Enforcers arrive seeking Amara's purchased item
  • Amara's strange Ustur crewmember calculates 22.7% odds of winning the fight
  • Puri saves the group: Saand commands "PURI! USE YOUR WHITE MISTS!" — white clouds engulf the road
  • Group escapes through the mist to the harbor, no casualties

docs.title: The Descent
docs.text:
• Etira has a dual atmosphere — hidden inside a revolving gas giant storm in Yuldun Waste
  • Entry requires plunging through the storm — shields hit 9% before breaking through
  • Inside: a completely different, calm atmosphere — the storm is a natural defense

docs.title: Etira Underworld City Structure
docs.text:
Multi-level underground city inside mountain chains, illuminated by artificial power sources

Level Function Details Surface Civilian residents Clean streets, children playing — deceptively normal Floor 1 Slave markets Slavers of Frenir run auctions. Lumanite bracers on captives Floor 2 Entertainment Bars, pleasure houses, courtesans. Noxious sweet aroma. "Madame Jaffa's House of Relaxation" Floor 3+ Unknown depths Where the Church of the Dreamer Below operates. Oppressive evil aura increases with depth

docs.title: The Three Pillars of the Black Market Auction House
docs.text:
Organization Role Status The Slavers of Frenir Slave trade, organ harvesting Weakened — Frenir holdings destroyed by COP The Bankers of Pergamos Shadow banking, financial backbone Weakened — Pergamos agreements with COP The Church of the Dreamer Below "Benevolent" cult — rapidly rising to dominance Rising power — replacing weakened pillars
  • Amara works for an organization aligned with the Church: "He has received the blessing of the Dreamer Below and is going to save all the galaxy, just as he saved me"
  • Violence against invited guests is forbidden on the surface — but "there are shadows in Etira"
  • Open trading of anything — sentient beings, organs, blood, worse

docs.title: 🔮 THE CHURCH OF THE DREAMER BELOW (Canon-Critical)
docs.text: Saand's recovered memories reveal the Church's true nature:

docs.title: Surface Operations
docs.text:
• Takes care of orphans, heals the sick, provides relief to Convergence War victims
  • Followers receive "the mark of the Dreamer" — erases sins and past errors, produces new blissful memories
  • Tenets: free Galia from COP and Photoli oppression by awakening the Dreamer

docs.title: True Operations
docs.text:
• Memory Manipulation: Can extract, replace, and segment memories at will — technology beyond known science
  • Harvests raw, painful memories and feeds them to the Dreamer Below to nourish and awaken him
  • In exchange, the Dreamer produces fake blissful memories — Church members learn all secrets held by worshippers
  • Amara's MRZ-28 trauma has been completely erased — she remembers everything about Jack except Ka-dara

docs.title: The Dreamer Below
docs.text:
• An actual imprisoned God beneath Etira — not a metaphor
  • A god-like creature that "not even the Photoli could totally defeat"
  • The Photoli imprisoned it but could not destroy it
  • Feeds on real memories, excretes false ones
  • The Church's ultimate goal: awaken the Dreamer by finding a key the Photoli hid

docs.title: Herdus.soul — High Priest
docs.text:
• Ustur, High Priest of the Church of the Dreamer Below
  • "Robust, metallic" voice, "eyes that shone with that unnatural green glow, like emeralds set aflame"
  • Hooded, cloaked in shadows, commands the temple operations

docs.title: 🧠 SAAND'S TRUE IDENTITY REVEALED (Canon-Critical)
docs.text:
Memory Revelation — the full truth:

  1. Saand was a vessel of the Church of the Dreamer Below
  2. He was fed the memories of dozens of living souls to strengthen him — their essence impressed on his mind
  3. His mission: steal from Commander Goe the map showing where the Photoli hid the key to the Dreamer's prison
  4. Herdus.soul's command: "Take it from him, and leave no trace of your action behind, otherwise our enemies will be alerted"
  5. This explains Commander Goe's reaction in Part 1: "No!!! He comes to steal from me again! The Light!" — "The Light" = the Photoli key/map
  6. Saand responded: "It will be done, Lord Herdus" — he felt pride in being chosen
  7. He crashed on planet Anueyeu — losing his memories (possibly a Church failsafe or escape attempt?)

docs.title: Part 3 Memory Milestones
docs.text: XP Threshold Event Details Glimpse Temple vision "Some of you will be released before others from this lucid dream that some like to call Galia..." — the High Priest's voice. Walls alive, breathing in sync with fear. "The dreamer must be awakened" Fragment The Blessing A hooded figure (Herdus.soul) declares: "You bear the blessing of our God... You are the vessel that will nurture the precious treasures that will awaken the One". Metallic cold hand on Saand's fur. Saand felt pride Revelation The Mission Herdus.soul orders Saand to steal from Commander Goe. Saand was the Church's instrument, fed memories of dozens to prepare him. "I was their vessel, their instrument of dread"

docs.title: Finding the High Punaab Girl
docs.text:
• Saand sneaks out at night to search Etira alone for his lost companion
  • Jack searches desperately with Puri (who can sense Saand's direction)
  • Jack witnesses a Slavers of Frenir slave auction — over 100 captives with Lumanite bracers. Bids on a young MUD girl
  • Puri leads Jack to Madame Jaffa's House of Relaxation — massive pleasure palace complex
  • Madame Jaffa — tall, muscular Sogmian woman. Musical voice, supernatural reflexes, can throw Jack to the floor effortlessly. Hot-cold personality
  • Gathin — one-eyed Sogmian, head of her guards
  • Saand found inside, captured by guards while trying to reach "the artist's room"
  • Jack tries to use Jaffa as hostage — she enjoys it: "My blood starts boiling when boys are this forceful with me"
  • She overpowers them and orders execution — but is interrupted
  • A High Punaab waitress stumbles in — Saand breaks free, tackles her, they embrace, crying
  • She smiles for what appears to be the first time — Jaffa calls off the execution
  • The girl and Saand speak in Punaab dialect — she is the one connected to "Etira" carved on the Superphoenix tree

Memory-eating cult serving imprisoned god The Dreamer Below Saand's memories Imprisoned god beneath Etira. Photoli couldn't destroy it, only imprison. Surface (civilian), Floor 1 (slaves), Floor 2 (entertainment), depths (Church) BMAH three pillars Amara's briefing Slavers of Frenir, Bankers of Pergamos, Church of the Dreamer Below Puri Carnival prize HRZ creature. Floats, creates white mists, senses Saand Saand's companion found Madame Jaffa quest High Punaab waitress. Connected to "Etira" tree carving

docs.title: Other Lore Extractions
docs.text:
Detail Source Canon Impact Golden Carnival location Carnival quest Iris-forsaken moon in Anfoil sector. Hosted by Wandering Caravan Ekip — Punaab formal lace Carnival preparation Traditional formal attire ZINIZ — VR technology company Carnival sponsor Digital reality builder protocol Verkofa — planet at Cataclysm edge VR game lore Three moons, white sun, dense jungle. Written about by Keymos Kasdat — Ruler of Verkofa VR game Legendary creature CARY farming industries Verkofa VR Research on imperishable vegetables with HRZ resources. Scarecrow-0YA1 harvester Gates Garrison — bounty hunter guild Atlas Brew Fortress in every star system bordering HRZ COPBRA Atlas Brew Council of Peace Biological Research Association Tufa — mysterious species Atlas Brew debate Last seen during Convergence War. Real but not seen for 100+ years House Akalma — still exists Carnival trade Led by Jefos. Selling Photoli tech SDU coordinates Xianyang Enforcers — armed squadron Carnival escape Used by Gate Garrison bounty hunters Madame Jaffa — Sogmian Etira Floor 2 Pleasure palace owner. Supernatural strength, musical voice Lumanite bracers — slave restraints Etira slave market Used by Slavers of Frenir on captives SDU authentication — light scribing, atomic-level Carnival trade Blockchain evolution — "photographs of reality" for trustless trading
  • ✅ ~~Q-NA1: Who or what is Etira?~~ → Planet in Yuldun Waste, premier illegal trading hub, dual atmosphere inside gas giant storm
  • ⏸️ Q-NA2: How did Saand live during the Convergence War? — Possibly Church memory implants of others' memories making him feel like he lived then. Or genuine longevity?
  • ✅ ~~Q-NA3: What is "The Light" Commander Goe accuses Saand of stealing?~~ → The Photoli key/map to the Dreamer's prison. Saand was sent by the Church to steal it from Goe
  • ⏸️ Q-NA4: What is the purple compound at Ahkavorah? Connection to the Dreamer Below?
  • ⏸️ Q-NA5: Who are the Wandering Caravan? What do the numbered symbols mean? They host the Golden Carnival
  • ⏸️ Q-NA7: Connection between Saand's Convergence War memories and the CORE narrative? — Possibly implanted memories from the Church
  • ⏸️ Q-NA9: Who hired the Scriptorium ambush attackers? — Possibly Church agents trying to prevent Saand from reaching Etira independently?
  • ⏸️ Q-NA10: What is the Order of Seasons? (Lethal organization)
  • ⏸️ Q-NA11: What is Panemorfa? How connected to the Church?
  • ✅ ~~Q-NA12: Who is Saand searching for?~~ → A High Punaab female, now a waitress at Madame Jaffa's. Connected to "Etira" tree carving on Superphoenix
  • ⏸️ Q-NA13: Fimbul splinter companies — which uncles, which factions do they specifically serve?
  • ✅ ~~Q-NA14: What happened during Saand's Part 2 Memory Revelation?~~ → Part 3 reveals: Church of the Dreamer Below memories returned. Saand was their vessel
  • ⏸️ Q-NA15: Did Saand successfully steal the Photoli key from Goe? Does the Church have it?
  • ⏸️ Q-NA16: Who is the High Punaab girl? What is her relationship to Saand? Why is she at Jaffa's?
  • ⏸️ Q-NA17: What is the Dreamer Below's true nature? Ancient being or specific species?
  • ⏸️ Q-NA18: How did Saand end up crashing on Anueyeu? Failed mission? Escape attempt? Church betrayal?
  • ⏸️ Q-NA19: What are the Photoli's true capabilities? They imprisoned the Dreamer but couldn't destroy it
  • ⏸️ Q-NA20: Part 4 — awaiting content
  • ⏸️ Q-NA21: What is Madame Jaffa's true nature? Supernatural strength, musical voice, heat generation
  • ⏸️ Q-NA22: Amara — is she knowingly serving the Church or unknowingly manipulated via memory wipe?

docs.title: Star Atlas Holosim: Complete Quest & Dialogue Archive
docs.text: This archive centralizes all narrative assets for the Holosim experience, including quest mappings, core Chapter 2 dialogue, and faction-specific scripts for the MUD and Ustur paths.

docs.title: Character Motivations
docs.text:
• SAGE (AI): The player's loyal primary interface. Optimistic, observant, and supportive.
  • Nova.tcher (Ustur Mentor): A high-ranking disciple of Mother Craven. Learned that enlightenment is an individual construction through conflict with Martuce.
  • Martuce Breeze (ECOS): An ideological pacifist who believes in the soul of worlds.
  • Corvax (ECOS): A ruthless militant who believes the Ustur "plague" must be purged.
  • Andreza (ECOS): The final boss, a self-styled "gardener of the stars." ⚠️ Note: "titan leader" title is Holosim narrative only — not confirmed in canon. Andreza's exact role/rank in ECOS pending Creator clarification.

docs.title: Core Scenario Dialogue
docs.text:
• The Awakening: SAGE reveals the "fall of the Star Atlas" pattern shown by Holosim.
  • Recruiting Nova: Nova.tcher is chosen by Mother Craven to guide the player, despite her unrefined persona.
  • The ECOS Threat: Attacks on pilgrims in the Medium Zone reveal the ECOS's contradictory dogma.
  • The Anfoil State: Nova describes the Anfoil dissidents who "amputate" their emotions for "purity of function."
  • The Tursic Deterrant: Nova shares her personal capital-class blueprints to counter ECOS and ONI aggression.
  • The Capture of Martuce: A pivotal moment where Martuce challenges Nova's rigid adherence to "someone else's orbit."
  • Ascension: Nova realizes that perfection isn't the point, but rather individual evolution.

docs.title: 🛠️ Part 3: MUD Path Dialogue (Dawn)
docs.text: Representative: Dawn (MUD Mentor). Tone: Religious industrialist, cold pragmatist.

docs.title: Key Moments
docs.text:
• Seized Asset Auction: "Every engine we repurpose is fuel denied to raiders... pragmatism outweighs pride."
  • The Ogrika Tursic: "What the ONI hoarded, the faithful now inherit... purify the heresy."
  • The Ogrika Thripid: "Heresy devours itself... Build your Thripid and let the Ustur know despair."

docs.title: 🤖 Part 4: Ustur Path Dialogue (Nova.tcher)
docs.text: Representative: Nova.tcher (Ustur Mentor). Tone: Philosophical, restrained, evolving excellence.

docs.title: Key Moments
docs.text:
• The Mik (Utility): "A tool not of destruction, but of presence... harmony survives by right action."
  • The Tursic (Deterrant): "Build your storm. Let them know the Ustur do not merely endure. We prevail."

docs.title: CDN Scraper Pattern
docs.text:
• CDN Base: https://cdn.staratlas.com/crew/v1/{CDN_ID}_full.jpeg
  • Retrieval Logic: Use asynchronous batches (max 20 concurrent) to avoid rate limits.
async def download_image(session, img_id, output_dir):
    url = f"https://cdn.staratlas.com/crew/v1/{img_id}_full.jpeg"
    async with session.get(url) as response:
        if response.status == 200:
            (output_dir / f"crew_{img_id:06d}.jpeg").write_bytes(await response.read())

docs.title: Advanced Filtering (Tensor GraphQL)
docs.text: Query api.tensor.so/graphql using the staratlascrew slug to filter by species traits (e.g., Ustur, High Punaab).

docs.title: 3. Marketplace Retrieval (Ships & Structures)
docs.text:
• Endpoint: https://galaxy.staratlas.com/nfts
  • Methodology: Extract high-resolution links from the image field in NFT metadata. Links typically point to Google Cloud Storage.

docs.title: 4. Usage Protocol
docs.text:
1. Identification: Match scene faction to manufacturer (Punaab/Akenat = Ogrika, Sogmian = Busan, MUD/ONI diplomacy = Calico).
  2. Path Injection: Pass the Local Path to AI generators via ImagePaths.
  3. Prompt Anchor: explicitly name the model in the prompt for geometric consistency.

docs.title: 🌍 Canon Document: HUMAN (MUD)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Integrated Book I + Lore Dump + Archon Truth, Feb 2026) Source: Creator Input (The Promised Paradise + MUD Lore Dump + Ahr Origin)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
• Name: Manus Ultima Divina (MUD)
  • Nature: A unified government of humanity, rooted in divine revelation and led by the Holy Family.
  • Philosophy: Expansion, Dominion, and the "Promised Paradise" (Cataclysm).
  • Self-Image: God's Chosen People, destined to rule the stars.

docs.title: 1. The Pre-History (Solar Punk Earth)
docs.text:
• Earth State: A sustainable Solar Punk Utopia. People lived well, but it was static/stagnant.
  • The Doom: A meteor was on collision course (50 years out). Humanity was unaware/unprepared due to the suppression of innovation for stability.
  • The Visitor: God Ahr was actually a Photoli Traveler exploring far from Galia. He saw two paths: let them die in peace vs force innovation/survival.

docs.title: 2. The Revelation (Vincenzo & Ahr)
docs.text:
• The Meeting: Ahr met Vincenzo Assis Gotti (a man of charity and innovation) incognito.
  • The Spark: Vincenzo said "While there is life, there is hope".
  • The Intervention: Ahr revealed the coming doom and inspired Vincenzo to lead humanity.
  • The Strategy: Force rapid innovation and unification to build an Ark.

docs.title: 3. The Schism (ECOS Truth)
docs.text:
• The Opposition: ECOS discovered Ahr was an alien.
  • The Rebellion: They viewed Vincenzo's plan as "Alien Domination" and the resource extraction as the murder of Earth.
  • The Result: Vincenzo had no choice but to unify humanity through a Religious/Organizational System (MUD) and consume Earth's resources to build the Mothership Serene.

docs.title: 4. The Exodus
docs.text:
• ~2120: Ahr visits planet Earth — through illusion, humanity is tricked into forming a space-based religion, plundering the planet, and leaving permanently
  • ~2140: Humanity advances towards the stars; Planet Earth is left an empty husk
  • Guidance: Ahr traveled with the MUD until they reached the Exodus Sector in Galia

docs.title: 5. The Expansion
docs.text:
• ~2399: MUD finds the Exodus Sector in Galia Expanse and establishes their base
  • ~2400: Last appearance of God (Ahr) to the MUD — he leaves with the promise of returning once they find their true home
  • Settlement: Mothership Serene became the heart of the empire
  • ~2401: ECOS dissidence — ECOS leaves towards Old Grove Sector
  • ~2410: MUD establishes first safe zone colonies — Valiant-I, Aurora-II, Verzan-III, Amora-IV
  • ~2411: The Translucid Pox wipes out 8% of MUD population before being contained on Valiant-I
  • ~2411: The Pearce Council is established on Amora-IV, becoming the bastion of MUD defenses
  • ~2417: Pearce is born on Amora-IV
  • ~2426: MUD expands into the Medium Zone — colonies: Mycenas-V, Xianyang-VI, Redam-VII, Pergamos-VIII, Abyd-IX
  • Aggressive Colonization: Seized system after system, overthrowing local cultures. Expansion halted only by ONI/Ustur borders.

docs.title: 6. The Convergence War (Book I Era)
docs.text:
• ~2510: Explorer Gyun Oman discovers the Cataclysm (limitless Genesium). He illegally sells the data to all 3 rival factions — MUD, ONI, and Ustur — each believing they are the sole recipients.
  • Reaction: MUD sees this as the fulfillment of the Revelation.
  • Fracture: Patriarch Vincenzo Gotti dies. His heir Charon Gotti inherits a divided empire.
  • Outcome: MUD forces were undone by the war, retreating to their home sector (Exodus).
  • "Plan B": MUD leadership (High Council) has a secret contingency plan to ensure humankind thrives even if the Council of Peace collapses.

docs.title: The Holy Family (Gotti Lineage)
docs.text:
• Status: Divine right to rule. Descendants of Vincenzo are the only ones fit to guide MUD.
  • Succession: Vincenzo -> Izek -> ... -> Charon Gotti Jr.

docs.title: Internal Factions (The Fracture)
docs.text:
The discovery of the Cataclysm turned MUD's unity into an arena:

  1. The Holy Servants (The Church): View Cataclysm as Sacred Ground. Demand consecrated claiming.
  2. The Pearce Council (Military/Colonial): View Cataclysm as Strategic Singularity. Seek control.
  3. Scientia (Scientists): View Cataclysm as Existential Threat. Urge caution.
  4. The Synod (Merchants): View Cataclysm as Logistics & Trade. Control shadows.
  5. The Senate: Weakened political body pleading for restraint.

docs.title: Ex-Colonies (The Medium Zone)
docs.text:
• Worlds: Redam-VII, Mycenas-V, Abyd-IX, Pergamos-VIII, Xianyang-VI.
  • ~2522: All five colonies — Redam, Mycenas, Xianyang, Pergamos, and Abyd — break out from MUD civilization, starting their own independent governments in the post-Convergence War chaos.
  • Status: Abandoned during the war. Now contested ground between MUD re-integration efforts and ECOS influence.

docs.title: Metahumans
docs.text:
• Nature: Individuals with technological modifications (meta-organs) for power/longevity.
  • Status: Wealthy MUD often have some augmentation.
  • Symbolism: Represents humanity's adaptability and relentless pursuit of improvement.

docs.title: Language: Divina Lingua
docs.text:
• Derived from ancient Earth languages.
  • Rich in spiritual/philosophical terminology.
  • Dialects: Outer worlds speak derivations seen as "subversive" by orthodox priests.

docs.title: Known Characters
docs.text: Name Role Notes Vincenzo Assis Gotti The First Patriarch "Right Hand of God". Passed the Test of Ahr. Charon Gotti Jr. Current Heir Born ~2480. Leading MUD in the current era (Book I & Present). Izek Gotti Historical Leader Successor to Vincenzo. Pearce Military Leader Born ~2417 on Amora-IV. Namesake of the Pearce Council. ~2557: MUD builds the Pearce T1 Titan-class ship Gyun Oman Explorer ~2510. Discovered the Cataclysm. Illegally sold data to all 3 factions. God Ahr The Guide Photoli Traveler. Saved humanity from the meteor. Left them in Galia (~2400).

docs.title: 🔴 Cross-References
docs.text:
• Convergence War: canon/history/convergence_war.md
  • Sogmian Interaction: canon/species/sogmian.md
  • Photoli Interaction: canon/species/photoli.md (Ahr is one of them)

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ~~Q-H1: Leadership~~ ✅ RESOLVED: Gotti Lineage.
  • ~~Q-H4: Earth Exodus~~ ✅ RESOLVED: God Ahr (Photoli) -> Meteor Doom -> Solar Punk Stagnation vs Survival.
  • ~~Q-H5: Human Superpower~~ ✅ RESOLVED: Divine Fervor + Adaptability.
  • ~~Q-H6: ECOS Origin~~ ✅ RESOLVED: Rejected "Alien Domination" and resource stripping.
  • ~~Q-H7: Who is God Ahr?~~ ✅ RESOLVED: Photoli Traveler.

docs.title: 🦎 Canon Document: MIERESE
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — Full Mierese Lore Keeper chapters + Q&A

docs.title: Biology & Physiology
docs.text:
• Skin: Lavender/lilac, high puncture resistance (armor rarely needed)
  • Build: Slim, slender frames, pointed ears
  • Motor system: Much richer spectrum of voluntary muscle control than humans (more motor neurons)
  • Strength: Weaker than humans — system evolved for constant movement, not weight-bearing
  • Heat tolerance: Withstand up to 45°C without biological performance loss
  • Poison resistance: Naturally resistant to many toxins (adapted from Neuno's environment)
  • Cybernetic vulnerability: Due to their biological composition, Mierese are susceptible to hacking, EMPs, and other cyber-electronic attacks that target integrated technology. Their biology resists poisons but not electronic warfare
  • Diet: Can digest almost anything except inorganic matter
  • Favorite food: MUD ice cream (to soothe Neuno's heat)

docs.title: Head Tentacles (The Tail)
docs.text:
• Multi-functional muscle/organ on the head
  • Functions: body balance, fine physical control, memory repository
  • Divided into many parts with different functions
  • Lore Keepers tend to have even longer ones than average

docs.title: Dimorphism
docs.text:
• Male: Extremely long dreadlock-like head tentacles
  • Female: Shorter braid-like tentacles with elaborate cranial crest

docs.title: Personality
docs.text:
• Free-spirited, improvisational, cheerful
  • Perceived as chaotic by orderly species (Ustur)
  • Perceived as cowards by honor-bound Sogmians (Mierese view dying for a cause as purposeless)
  • Cope with stress through humor ("Ok, this is how we die")
  • Love bets (like their god, but always aware of every detail)

docs.title: Naming Convention (Three Names)
docs.text:
Every Mierese has THREE names:

Position Type Description First Hidden/Private Sounds disgusting/weird to non-Mierese; only close friends and family know it Second Public Single, catchy, strong word — highly recognizable in tales Third Regional Fixed surname from ancestral Neuno region

docs.title: The Six Regions of Neuno → Third Names
docs.text: Region Third Name Singing Sands of Kaljura Jura Vireko Ravines Eko Aerqint Spires Qint Morvalel Marsh Valel Umbyara Woods Yara Eskscarka Currents Scarka

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
• Doop Moda Valel | Jank Jogo Eko | Volmik Vaor Scarka
  • Mierese love nicknames

docs.title: Chapter One — Onato and Juriko
docs.text:
• Onato: Most beautiful and powerful god. Talented (singing, dancing, storytelling, fighting). Prideful but good-hearted. Accepted any bet and never lost
  • Juriko: Envious god who plotted against Onato
  • Juriko organized a competition of challenges among the gods
  • Final round: Juriko dared Onato to escape a prison — a cell with a password lock
  • The only hint: the answer is a good story, perhaps one that hadn't occurred yet
  • Onato tried everything — memories, word combinations, invented words — nothing worked
  • Juriko offered surrender (admit he's not the most powerful) — Onato refused out of pride
  • Onato recognized the trap but his pride wouldn't let him quit

docs.title: Onato's Solution — The Creation of the Mierese
docs.text:
1. Created a record of everything he'd tried
  2. Separated part of his essence and breathed life into it → first Mierese
  3. Made a pact: live freely, but upon death, tell Onato at least one new story
  4. If the story is new → paradise
  5. If not → stay with Onato helping crack the puzzle

The first Mierese recited everything Onato told them → this became the Grand Tale

docs.title: The Grand Tale Today
docs.text:
• Living document updated by generations of Lore Keepers
  • Incorporates the most relevant characters and events of the galaxy
  • Being featured in the Grand Tale is the supreme honor
  • Functions like a blockchain — distributed across Lore Keepers' memories

docs.title: The Living Planet Neuno
docs.text: A singularity — defies the laws of creation.

docs.title: Nature
docs.text:
• Literally a living entity the size of a planet
  • 3000+ years old (carbon dating)
  • Origin Truth: In the galaxy's primordial era, a colossal space parasite collided with Neuno and "infected" the geological structure.
  • Mechanics: The planet seems alive because the parasite is alive, using the planet's crust/mantle as its organs.
  • Organs/vital functions originate from the southern pole (suggesting growth started there)

docs.title: Biology of Neuno
docs.text:
• Photosynthesis: Gathers nutrition from its radiant blue sun
  • Immune system: Reacts to Mierese expansion — tsunamis, droughts, diseases, poisoned crops
  • Hibernation: Sleeps for months each year when planet is distant from the blue sun
  • Verdant jungles → desolates of dust and grass
  • Enormous animals shelter underground
  • Pace of life slows across entire ecosystem

docs.title: Environment
docs.text:
• Hostile jungles, high temperatures, deadly species
  • Forced Mierese to organize in small, mobile groups
  • Core mentality: everything outside your group is dangerous and trying to get you

docs.title: Native Species & Resources
docs.text:
• Arevok: Grazing animals, prized meat
  • Irvilyings: Flying creatures with rare essence (from nighttime flowers)
  • Azul grass: Blue plant from cold mountain peaks
  • Herbritids: Rare cave mushroom
  • Luminous flora and deadly predators throughout wild regions

docs.title: The Benevolent Explorers (~2377)
docs.text:
• When Mierese were nearly annihilated, unknown alien explorers arrived
  • Terraformed part of Neuno, advanced Mierese science
  • Set the basis for first interstellar travel
  • Left and were never seen again
  • Taught the Mierese the value of helping others and cooperation
  • Who were they? — remains one of the great unsolved mysteries of Mierese history

docs.title: Chronological History
docs.text:
• ~2377: The Benevolent Explorers arrive and uplift the Mierese (see above)
  • ~2392: Atheist Mierese found the Democratic Order of Kamec in Kamec Sector
  • ~2396: Mierese dream of a mineral-based flora — leads to discovery of Glowhaven (MRZ-31)
  • ~2399: Mierese begin exploration of dangerous Coral Nebula (MRZ-18)

docs.title: Core Principles (from the Grand Tale)
docs.text:
1. Danger of pride — Onato is imprisoned by his pride; Mierese remind each other of this
  2. Value of freedom — Onato freed them to live however they wish
  3. Stories over possessions — "Only your memory and intelligence will help you before Onato"
  4. Humility — "Being humble will keep you free from your ego"
  5. Substance over form — Self-expression, secrets, adventures, skills, intelligence matter
  6. Irrelevance of protocol — Possessions, details, protocols, dogmas, precision are dismissed

docs.title: Language
docs.text:
• No written language — believe written words fail to convey emotions and sensations
  • Some Mierese learn common written language for inter-faction interaction
  • Consider written communication "severely lacking, almost useless"
  • Heavy reliance on oral tradition and prestigious memory

docs.title: Clothing
docs.text:
• Light, mobile clothing providing some protection
  • Different leathers from hunted animals; practical fibers
  • Dark tones preferred in unsafe environments (stealth)
  • Will use other species' clothing if practical
  • In safe environments, individual personality dictates style

docs.title: Sports & Entertainment
docs.text:
• Motion control, personal skill, hand-to-hand combat, weapon combat
  • Favorite game: Narrative Battles — bard face-off (similar to rap battles)

docs.title: Structure
docs.text:
• Small groups and villages spread across Neuno's six regions
  • Local leadership handles common affairs — never intrudes on individual sphere
  • No central government until the Convergence War (forced by ONI Consortium pressure)
  • Government = loose coalition of notables with an elected Speaker (quasi prime minister)
  • Speaker's functions are limited — avoids general rules/laws unless necessary
  • Government viewed with suspicion — if Onato freed them, why should anyone dictate behavior?
  • Only viable because of Lore Keeper support
  • Currently leading the ONI Consortium — Central Space Station shows Mierese architecture/style

docs.title: The Hikibashi (Elite Special Forces)
docs.text:
• Elite special forces unit of the Mierese
  • Trained assassins and spies, skilled in multiple combat arts
  • Think of them as extremely capable espionage operatives
  • A separate military institution from the civilian government

docs.title: Social Status (Story-Based)
docs.text:
• Success = number of stories you appear in
  • Everyone belongs to: family story, possibly city story, craft story
  • Regional Lore Keeper selects who is worthy of bigger stories
  • Formal introduction requires reciting the stories you're part of

docs.title: The Non-Existent (Worst Punishment)
docs.text:
• Being erased from all stories = worst fate in Mierese society
  • Non-existent cannot formally introduce themselves
  • Viewed as "worse than dead" by Mierese standards
  • Historically had no rights (could be killed); COP intervened to protect their lives
  • Religiously: non-existent never meet Onato, simply vanish after death

docs.title: The Lore Keepers
docs.text: The foundation of Mierese society.

docs.title: Role
docs.text:
• Special class trained from childhood by elder Keepers
  • Guardians of the Grand Tale and collective memory
  • Religious support for communities
  • Select which stories enter the Grand Tale
  • Act as representatives of Onato in the physical realm
  • Only institution giving the government legitimacy

docs.title: Selection & Training
docs.text:
• Potential candidates chosen by elder Keepers at young age
  • Learn the Grand Tale + memorization techniques for vast quantities of information
  • Young Keepers start with small community religious instruction
  • Earn voting rights on Grand Tale additions through merit

docs.title: Annual Grand Tale Assembly
docs.text:
• Elder Lore Keepers reunite in an unknown location each Neuno year
  • Share collected stories
  • Vote on which should be incorporated into the Grand Tale
  • Distributed memory = if any Keeper dies, information survives with others

docs.title: Core Beliefs
docs.text:
• Stories exist in another plane with the gods — they are living entities
  • Stories exert influence over reality — the more famous, the greater the impact
  • "Stories are vessels for the universe's moral themes"
  • Both planes (divine and physical) influence each other cyclically

docs.title: Afterlife
docs.text:
• Upon death: recite a new story to Onato
  • New story → paradise
  • No new story → stay with Onato, helping crack the puzzle
  • Non-existent → never meet Onato, simply vanish

docs.title: Spiritual Communication
docs.text:
• Lore Keepers can communicate with spirits and their stories (medium sessions)
  • Souls of good/bad intentions inspire participants through indirect means
  • Excludes non-existent beings

docs.title: The Possession Risk (Onato Trap)
docs.text:
• Extreme identification with a story can make a Mierese "possessed"
  • Becomes an avatar of their idea/story, willing to die for it
  • Can develop alternate personalities — the character they envision themselves as
  • May enable acts beyond their normal capabilities
  • Also causes problems: depression, despair, flipped moral values
  • Rare but always has significant historical impact when it occurs
  • Named after Onato's trap — pride imprisoning the self

docs.title: Known Characters
docs.text: Name Full Name Role Notes Vaor Scarka Volmik Vaor Scarka Current ONI leader + COP Prime Minister (ONI's turn). Former COP Admiral. Hikibashi-trained From Eskscarka Currents region Mevertrase TBD Mierese representative Joined Ayevat's Denebula finance kingdom

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ~~Q-M1: Hikibashi~~ ✅ RESOLVED — elite special forces unit (assassin-spies), NOT a title
  • ~~Q-M2: ECOS connection~~ ✅ RESOLVED — FALSE, deleted from canon. Vaor Scarka is ONI leader, NOT ECOS
  • ⏸️ Q-M3: Benevolent alien explorers — TABLED for future development
  • ⏸️ Q-M4: Convergence War — partially revealed via Maharog. Mierese were "hammered thin by ordinances they did not vote for". Mierese contribution to ONI: prowess — "Driven by Mierese prowess"
  • ⏸️ Q-M5: Notable Lore Keepers — none created yet, can develop later

[!CAUTION] KI ARTIFACT ERROR: The master_lore.md KI artifact incorrectly listed Vaor Scarka as ECOS leader. This has been corrected in canon. Vaor Scarka = ONI leader + COP Prime Minister. Andreza was also incorrectly listed as "ECOS activist" — removed pending clarification.

docs.title: 📛 Canon Document: SPECIES NAMING CONVENTIONS
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Established Rules, Feb 2026) Source: Creator-defined naming system Usage: All new character names MUST follow these conventions

docs.title: Ustur
docs.text:
Two-part name: Collective Derivation + Enlightenment Step Suffix

The first name derives from a collective noun (herd, army, flock, colony, etc.). The second name represents advancement through the seven steps of Ustur enlightenment.

docs.title: Enlightenment Steps (lowest → highest)
docs.text: Step Suffix Rarity Tier 1 .bod Common, Uncommon, Rare 2 .iddt Uncommon, Rare, Epic 3 .lrnr Rare, Epic, Legendary 4 .doer Epic, Legendary 5 .tchr Legendary, Anomaly 6 .soul Anomaly 7 .eldr —

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: Full Name Derivation Step Herdus.soul Herd 6 Armi.eldr Army 7 Flicer.bod Flock 1 Calon.tchr Colony 5 Libra.lrnr Library 3

docs.title: Photoli
docs.text: Photoli recognize each other by unique magnetic fields and don't value names. They adopt short, musical names only to ease communication with other species.

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
• Short — 2-3 letters preferred
  • Musical — vowel-heavy, singable
  • No complex structure

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: Reh, Ahr, Fae, Om, Mer, Doh

docs.title: Mierese
docs.text:
Three-part name: Hidden (Private) + Public + Region of Neuno

  • First name (Hidden): Deliberately unpleasant-sounding to non-Mierese. Only close friends and family know it.
  • Second name (Public): Single, catchy, strong word — highly recognizable. Mierese love nicknames.
  • Third name (Region): References the ancient region of Neuno from which their lineage originates.

docs.title: Neuno Regions → Third Name
docs.text: Region Third Name Singing Sands of Kaljura Jura Vireko Ravines Eko Aerqint Spires Qint Morvalel Marsh Valel Umbyara Woods Yara Eskscarka Currents Scarka

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: Hidden Public Region Full Name Doop Moda Valel Doop Moda Valel Jank Jogo Eko Jank Jogo Eko Volmik Vaor Scarka Volmik Vaor Scarka

docs.title: Sogmian
docs.text:
Structure: Name + Generation Number + House + Title

Sogmians carry their history in their names. The generation number uses the Sogmian numeral system (alien symbols).

docs.title: Sogmian Titles (lowest → highest)
docs.text: Title Description Dek'prah Honorless — betrayed Du'prah. Near-zero social status Ke'pro Infant still living with family, pre-training Vok'prah Young trainee at the Gray Citadel, not yet bound by Du'Prah Rat'Prah Graduated Gray Citadel, created personal moral rule, chosen by a House Frei'har Proven trustworthy to venture alone in Galia and represent House Lan'fast Supervises Rat'Prah advancement, holds House responsibilities Fur'raft First high-rank. Administers small territory or represents House on missions Mar'graf High officer commanding House operations across Galia under Her'graf Her'graf Direct advisor to the Kur'faist. Elects next Kur'faist if needed Kur'faist Head of a House. Absolute rule, answers only to Sov'arei Sov'arei Leader of all Sogmians. Advised by all Houses' Kur'faists

docs.title: Full Name Example
docs.text: Bekalu 𐎌 Xictus Sovereign — Bekalu, 7th generation, House Xictus, Sovereign title

docs.title: MUD (Human)
docs.text: No fixed naming convention. MUD origins, beliefs, and worldviews are too diverse for a single naming system.

docs.title: Punaab
docs.text: Punaabs (especially High Punaabs) believe names represent status. Parents give children long, complicated names and privately use abbreviations.

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
• Double vowels
  • Excessive letter repetition
  • Grand, multi-syllabic

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: Maartrichanna, Marekenaamatra, Binaree, Pullaraajanethrim, Nopeenfree, Huricanno, Heddaraa, Ayevat, Kikiraajin, Buccheri, Kuropatriprica, Minimimii

docs.title: Lumikir
docs.text:
Structure: Compound root-word name (2-3 roots combined)

The Lumikir are a species built on language — Om gave them writing, and they rebuilt their entire civilization around it. Their names reflect this: each Lumikir name is a compound word assembled from meaningful roots in the Lumikir proto-language. Every syllable carries etymological weight. A Lumikir's name is, in essence, a micro-description of their identity or aspiration.

Names are typically 2-3 roots combined. Parents choose roots at birth to express their hopes for the child; some Lumikir later rename themselves when they join a guild or achieve a significant contribution, selecting roots that better reflect who they've become.

docs.title: Actions (what the Lumikir does)
docs.text: Root Meaning Notes Vel- To seek, to search The wanderer, the quester Tor- To write, to record The scribe, the cataloger Quen- To discover, to reveal The one who uncovers Ith- To guard, to preserve The protector of knowledge Pell- To travel, to walk far The voyager Dral- To listen, to observe The patient watcher Nak- To trade, to exchange The broker of information Siv- To sift, to filter The analyst who separates truth from noise Bren- To build, to construct The architect, the system-builder Cath- To map, to chart The cartographer Fyr- To ignite, to inspire The catalyst, the motivator Geth- To gather, to collect The accumulator Hav- To solve, to decode The puzzle-breaker Jor- To speak, to declare The herald, the announcer Krem- To test, to verify The authenticator Leth- To remember, to recall The living memory Morr- To challenge, to question The contrarian, the skeptic Nev- To weave, to connect The one who links disparate truths Oph- To heal, to mend The restorer of damaged knowledge Prynn- To translate, to interpret The bridge between languages Rith- To count, to measure The quantifier Sorr- To follow, to track The hunter of specific knowledge Thal- To teach, to instruct The mentor Uv- To predict, to foresee The pattern-reader Vex- To argue, to debate The dialectician Wyr- To cross, to traverse The boundary-crosser Xal- To bind, to archive The sealer of permanent records Yss- To witness, to attest The first-hand observer Zeph- To scatter, to distribute The spreader of knowledge Drenn- To excavate, to unearth The deep digger, the archaeologist

docs.title: Qualities (what defines the Lumikir)
docs.text: Root Meaning Notes Sar- Truth, certainty One who values verified fact Kael- Sharp, precise The meticulous, the exacting Lum- Light, clarity Echo of "Lumikir" — enlightenment Vor- Bold, daring Willingness to take risks for knowledge Thess- Patient, enduring The long game, the slow accumulator Ren- Silent, hidden Discretion, secrecy, subtlety Ald- Old, ancient Revered for accumulated wisdom Brith- Fierce, relentless Refuses to stop until the answer is found Cyr- Cold, detached Emotional distance as analytical tool Duv- Deep, thorough Never satisfied with surface knowledge Ell- Swift, agile Speed in acquisition and response Fenn- Loyal, bound Devoted to guild, partner, or cause Grael- Vast, expansive Broad knowledge across many domains Hol- Pure, untainted Incorruptible integrity Iss- Cunning, shrewd Intelligence applied to advantage Jael- Bright, radiant Charismatic, magnetically intelligent Krev- Hard, unyielding Stubbornness as virtue Lorn- Lost, wandering One who finds truth in aimless exploration Mael- Storm, turbulent Chaotic brilliance, unpredictable Noth- Null, empty The blank page — openness to new imprints Orr- Heavy, weighty Gravitas, significance Phen- Strange, anomalous Attracted to the inexplicable Quor- Twin, dual Sees both sides, bridge-thinker Rath- Warm, inviting Approachable, builds trust easily Skael- Scarred, marked Carries the wounds of hard-won knowledge Trev- First, initial The pioneer, goes where none have gone Ulv- Dark, obscured Comfortable in shadows and unknowns Vhel- Woven, complex Sees connections invisible to others Wrenn- Small, subtle Finds greatness in overlooked details Zan- Free, unbound Refuses allegiance or constraint

docs.title: Domains (what the Lumikir studies)
docs.text: Root Meaning Notes -oss Stars, space, the void Exploration, cosmology -ahn Living things, biology Natural sciences, species -eth The past, history, memory Historical knowledge -irr Power, politics, governance Political intelligence -unn Secrets, the hidden Espionage, concealed truths -olk Earth, land, resources Geography, material wealth -ael War, conflict, strategy Military intelligence -ith Language, words, texts Linguistics, translation -opt Ships, fleets, routes Naval and trade intelligence -enn Energy, currents, forces Physics, Cosmic Currents -urr Trade, commerce, wealth Economic intelligence -ash Fire, destruction, ruin Catastrophes, fallen civilizations -ynn Mind, thought, consciousness Psychology, AI, cognition -oph Medicine, bodies, health Medical knowledge -eld Technology, machinery, craft Engineering, invention -arr Law, codes, contracts Legal systems, treaties, agreements -iss Poison, toxin, danger Hazards, biological threats -oll Sound, music, vibration Acoustic phenomena, communication -umm Faith, belief, worship Religion, cults, theology -est Weather, climate, atmosphere Planetary conditions -akh Death, endings, entropy Extinction events, mortality -inn Children, growth, potential Development, cultivation -orr Machines, constructs, automata Robotics, AI systems -aph Art, beauty, creation Aesthetic knowledge -uth Time, cycles, patterns Temporal phenomena, predictions -elv Water, ocean, depths Marine/aquatic knowledge -oss'k Void anomalies, HRZ High-risk phenomena -amm Food, agriculture, sustenance Agricultural knowledge -ekk Minerals, crystals, geology Geoscience, mining -iph Light, radiation, spectra Photonic phenomena, Photoli studies

docs.title: Modifiers (intensity/scale)
docs.text: Root Meaning Notes -a Singular, focused Specialist -en Broad, encompassing Generalist -is Young, beginning Novice or aspirational -or Great, supreme Council seat holders ONLY -ix Edge, boundary One who works at the frontier -al Collective, shared Community-oriented -un Lone, solitary The independent operative -ek Twice, again The one who re-examines -os Many, numerous Prolific contributor -il Quiet, soft Understated, subtle approach -ak Fierce, intense Aggressive pursuit of knowledge -em Balanced, centered Methodical, measured -uv Below, beneath Specialist in hidden/underground knowledge -ol Above, elevated High-level, strategic perspective -yr Eternal, unending Lifelong dedication -ith Between, liminal Interdisciplinary, cross-domain -av New, fresh Bringer of novel perspectives -ek'r Returning, cyclical Re-discovery, revisitation -ash Broken, fractured Specialist in fragmented knowledge -ul Shadow, secondary The apprentice, the understudy -ann Proven, tested Verified through experience -ir True, authentic Emphasis on genuine knowledge -oph Wounded, scarred Knowledge gained through hardship -ell Distant, far Specialist in remote or exotic domains -ok Heavy, burdened Carrier of dangerous knowledge -iss Sharp, cutting Incisive, penetrating analysis -amm Slow, deliberate Methodical to a fault -eth Ancient, primordial Specialist in deep-time knowledge -uph Rising, ascending Climbing the Ledger -ven Final, ultimate The last word, the definitive source

docs.title: Construction Rules
docs.text:
1. Standard name: Action/Quality + Domain + (optional Modifier)
  2. Names are read left-to-right as a phrase: Veloss = "seeker of stars", Toreth = "writer of history"
  3. Some Lumikir use two action/quality roots when no single domain fits: Velkael = "sharp seeker"
  4. Guild affiliation is stated separately, not embedded in the name — but over time, certain root combinations become associated with specific guilds (e.g., Ren- and -unn names gravitate toward the Whisper Conclave)
  5. The modifier -or is reserved for Lumikir who hold council seats — using it without earning it is a serious social offense

docs.title: Examples with Etymological Breakdown
docs.text: Name Roots Meaning Likely Guild Veloss Vel (seek) + oss (stars) "Seeker of stars" Pathfinders Toreth Tor (write) + eth (history) "Writer of history" Chroniclers Renunn Ren (silent) + unn (secrets) "Silent keeper of secrets" Whisper Conclave Quenahn Quen (discover) + ahn (life) "Discoverer of living things" Naturalists Dralirr Dral (observe) + irr (power) "Observer of power" Arbiters Kaeloss Kael (precise) + oss (stars) "Precise star-mapper" Pathfinders Itheth-or Ith (guard) + eth (past) + or (great) "Great guardian of history" Chroniclers (council seat) Vorolk-ix Vor (bold) + olk (earth) + ix (frontier) "Bold frontier surveyor" Pathfinders Thessunn Thess (patient) + unn (secrets) "Patient secrets-gatherer" Whisper Conclave Pellahn-en Pell (travel) + ahn (life) + en (broad) "Traveling naturalist-generalist" Naturalists Lumsar Lum (light) + sar (truth) "Light of truth" Chroniclers Velkael-is Vel (seek) + kael (sharp) + is (young) "Young sharp seeker" Any (aspirational)
