docs.text: Naming convention TBD.

docs.title: 🌀 Canon Document: OXIR
docs.text: Status: 🟡 Stub (Aeon Timeline data, Feb 2026) Source: Aeon Timeline Event + Creator clarification Location: High Risk Zone (HRZ)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Oxir are a native species of the High Risk Zone (HRZ), one of the few known sentient peoples to originate within the dangerous territory surrounding the Cataclysm.

[!IMPORTANT] The Oxir are the first known species to discover the nature and aspects of cosmic currents — the fundamental energy flows that permeate the galaxy. This discovery predates all known factions' understanding of cosmic mechanics.

docs.title: Worship of Iris
docs.text:
• The Oxir worship Iris — the sentient planet at the heart of the Cataclysm
  • ~2460: A conscious Tufa discovers the tribes of Oxir; the Cult of Iris begins among the Oxir people
  • The Tufa's arrival deepened and formalized existing reverence for Iris into an organized cult

docs.title: Cosmic Currents
docs.text:
• The Oxir were first to discover the nature and aspects of cosmic currents
  • This knowledge gives them unique insight into the energy mechanics of the HRZ and Galia at large

docs.title: First Contact
docs.text: • ~2503: Oxir tribes meet the galactic factions for the first time — they steer away from them, choosing isolation over engagement

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ⏸️ Q-OX1: Oxir biology — what do they look like? Physical characteristics?
  • ⏸️ Q-OX2: Population size and societal structure?
  • ⏸️ Q-OX3: How do the Oxir interface with cosmic currents? Technology, biology, or spirituality?
  • ⏸️ Q-OX4: Relationship with other HRZ denizens (Dark Photoli, Tufa Titans)?
  • ⏸️ Q-OX5: Did the Oxir share their cosmic current knowledge with other species?
  • ⏸️ Q-OX6: How did the Cult of Iris change Oxir society?

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Tufa — canon/species/tufa.md (Cult of Iris origin ~2460)
  • The Cataclysm / Iris — canon/geography/the_cataclysm.md
  • HRZ — High Risk Zone

docs.title: ✨ Canon Document: PHOTOLI
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Lore Dump Integrated, Feb 2026) Source: Creator Input (Lore Dump)

docs.title: Biology & Nature
docs.text:
• Age: Ancient and enigmatic species, often considered the original conscious life-forms of the universe.
  • Appearance: Unique beings of light with partially physical bodies. No two look the same.
  • Accessories: Do not use clothing/accessories (ancient nature renders them unnecessary).
  • Mastery: Unparalleled mastery over physics, time, energy, and light.

docs.title: Visual DNA (from Concept Art)
docs.text:
• Humanoid silhouette with extended proportions — elongated limbs, towering physique
  • Translucent marble-like skin — grey/white with luminous white veins running like organic circuits
  • Internal light — glow emanates from within, tracing lines across the body
  • Featureless head — angular, almost helmet-like cranial structure, no visible eyes/mouth
  • Geometric/runic glyphs float around their bodies — circles, arcs, cosmic symbols — like an aura of mathematics
  • No clothing — the body itself IS the presentation; sculpted like living marble with inner radiance
  • Muscular but ethereal — simultaneously physical and intangible

docs.title: Light-Shaping (Shape-Shifting)
docs.text:
[!IMPORTANT] Photoli can change their appearance by molding their light. This explains how Om visits the Lumikir incognito — he reshapes his luminous form to appear different or diminished.

  • They can alter their visual form by reshaping the light they emit and the structure of their partially-physical body
  • This ability ranges from subtle alterations to potentially full disguise
  • Enables Photoli like Om to move unrecognized when needed

docs.title: Society & Hierarchy
docs.text: Photoli society lacks a central government. They are highly self-sufficient.

docs.title: Two Tiers
docs.text:
1. Sih Rihim (The Ascended): Beings of pure light with immense power and wisdom.
  2. Alumni (The Students): Younger Photoli striving to master elements for transformation into pure light.

docs.title: The Travelers (Sub-faction)
docs.text:
• Reject the pursuit of ascension and strict orthodoxy.
  • Role: Observers who find joy in subtly influencing other species' growth (without direct interference).
  • Philosophy: Distance themselves from Ascended/Alumni machinations to avoid harm.
  • Action: Might inadvertently accelerate a civilization's progress by centuries.

docs.title: Essence Trading & Governance
docs.text:
• System: Identify power/voting through the sacrifice of their own life force (Life Essence).
  • Awareness: Every Photoli knows exactly when they will die (dissipate into natural light).
  • Voting: Collective decisions are settled by pouring essence into a rayanite crystal during an Iris Majesty cycle.
  • A single Photoli can influence weighty decisions if willing to sacrifice significant life span.
  • Dispute Resolution: Settled by essence transfer.

docs.title: Prohibition of Conflict
docs.text:
• Rule: Direct conflict between Photoli is strictly forbidden (Andromeda legacy).
  • The ONI Paradox: They justify ONI membership as a means to an end. Startling revelation: A relevant faction of Photoli has a hidden agenda — to use the ONI's expansion to eventually acquire access to Iris (eternal energy source). They are playing the "long game".

docs.title: Dark Photoli (The Exiles)
docs.text:
• Nature: Predatory entities that grow stronger the longer they survive.
  • Why they don't unite: Arrogance and paranoia. They fear being drained by each other.
  • Weakness: Born weaker than traditional Photoli (due to lack of natural light), so they cannot yet openly challenge the Photoli/Galia.
  • Status: 7 known individuals, functioning as dangerous loners/outcasts.
  • ~2558: A powerful Photoli turns dark and begins plotting the conquest of Iris for himself. He later strikes a deal with Ingbus (canon/geography/sectors/ka_dara.md) (~2569)

docs.title: The Ritual of Ascension
docs.text:
• Process: For other species, it is a gradual transmutation.
  • Requires immense resources, time, compatibility, and luck.
  • The individual slowly gains Photoli properties and powers.
  • Mechanics:
  • Willing/sentient candidate.
  • Essence of Pure Light + Energy Source + Catalyst.
  • Chant/Ritual binding.

docs.title: Locations
docs.text:
• Dawnbreak: Official hidden base in the High-Risk Zone (HRZ).
  • Clarification: Located in the HRZ, but NOT inside the Cataclysm.
  • Iris denies them entry to the Cataclysm proper.

docs.title: History
docs.text:
• ~2080: Photoli cross into Galia through the Night Window Sector, draining the star's energy to recharge after their journey from Andromeda.
  • ~2086: Photoli Om is saved by primitive creatures in MRZ-27 — he grants them knowledge, and they rename themselves the Lumikir.
  • ~2095: Photoli inter the Ah'Rihvael family — an ancient Photoli family whose time was over — beneath the oceans of Bluvael's six habitable worlds. They cast an enormous sector-wide illusion to hide the tombs (the Drowned Vaults).
  • ~2120: Photoli Ahr visits Planet Earth, using illusion to manipulate humanity into forming a space-based religion and leaving Earth permanently.
  • ~2135: Photoli discover a powerful creature (the "Dreamer Below") in the heart of planet Etira (Yuldun Waste, MRZ-32). Unable to destroy it, they steal its memories, seal it in a subterranean pool, and terraform Etira's atmosphere to be lethally hostile — preventing anyone from reaching it.
  • ~2136: Photoli drain Night Window's star energy and construct Dawnbreak — a hidden fortress in the HRZ.
  • Convergence War: Joined ONI Consortium as "witnesses and wardens".
  • Public Goal: Guidance and preservation of life (Du Prah support).
  • Hidden Agenda: Positioning themselves to eventually access Iris/Energy.
  • The Titan Secret: A disguised Photoli taught House Garveil how to build the Titan's power source in the HRZ.

docs.title: Known Characters
docs.text: Name Role Notes Om Elder / Traveler Uplifted the Lumikir after they nursed him from near-death. Visits Lumik incognito to see old friends. Fought a Dark Photoli in his youth. Ahr The Guide / God of MUD Visited Earth (~2120). Left MUD at Exodus (~2400). Gel Financial Aristocrat Part of Ayevat's Denebula Kingdom. Killed — Photoli generally don't care about money; Gel was an exception. First Dark Photoli Exile Progenitor Hunted outcast

docs.title: 🔴 Cross-References
docs.text:
• ONI Consortium: canon/species/sogmian.md
  • Denebula: canon/geography/denebula_utopia.md
  • HRZ: canon/geography/the_cataclysm.md
  • Lumik / Lumikir: canon/geography/sectors/lumik.md (Om's connection, uplifted species)
  • Ka-dara: canon/geography/sectors/ka_dara.md (Dark Photoli fight in Om's youth led to Lumikir encounter)

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ~~Q-PH8: ONI Paradox~~ ✅ RESOLVED: Hidden Agenda (Long game for Iris).
  • ~~Q-PH9: Dawnbreak Survival~~ ✅ RESOLVED: Hidden in HRZ, but outside Cataclysm (no access to Iris yet).
  • ~~Q-PH10: Dark Photoli Cabal?~~ ✅ RESOLVED: No, arrogant loners.
  • ~~Q-PH11: Ascension Mechanics~~ ✅ RESOLVED: Gradual transmutation.
  • ~~Q-PH12: Inter-species Essence Trading~~ ✅ RESOLVED: Possible but inefficient/costly.

docs.title: 🐾 Canon Document: PUNAAB
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — Full Punaab lore document + Q&A Sessions

docs.title: Biology & Nature
docs.text:
• Sentient mammals with two distinct branches (High and Profound)
  • Both branches originate from the same primordial species, diverged due to climatic events
  • NO TAILS — critical anatomical rule
  • Come in various colors and shapes (generally small); may have more or less fur
  • Compulsive eaters: always keeping food nearby — nutrition bars, apples, snacks
  • Language: Rich in commerce/craftsmanship terms; heavy accent causes misunderstandings between branches and with other species
  • Communication: Animated speech with wild gestures that intensify with excitement
  • Materialistic: Both branches share fervent love for gold, jewels, and cultural artifacts
  • Personality: Full of superstitions and beliefs; naturally comedic to other species
  • Heightened sense of smell: Punaabs possess a very developed sense of smell. If a Punaab comes close enough to rare bio-materials or important resources, they may detect them before other species — effectively functioning as living scanners for valuable materials
  • Scale: High Punaab ~60cm (knee-height), Profound Punaab ~40cm (calf-height)

docs.title: High Punaabs (Merchant Princes)
docs.text:
• Appearance: Slightly larger, dog-like forms with white/light tiger-patterned or golden/beige fur
  • Culture: Paragons of capitalism and market orientation
  • Society: Mostly decentralized, individualistic, profit-oriented
  • Lifecycle: Venture beyond Akenat at maturity → traveling merchants → return wealthy as "merchant princes"
  • Values: Contracts, private property, free markets (except homeworld ecosystem preservation)
  • Style: Flamboyant — strong colors, bold designs, gold, accessories from other civilizations
  • Habitat: Lofty trees, plateaus, Skygroves above the Sea of Mist

docs.title: Profound Punaabs (Noble Craftsmen)
docs.text:
• Appearance: Smaller, rat/meerkat-like with gray/dark fur, more robust
  • Culture: Medieval guild system — workers, craftsmen, miners
  • Society: Centralized order with hierarchical layers
  • Values: Nobility, honor, sacrifice, duty; communal: "give more than you take"
  • Style: Practical, reserved — clothes bear stains and marks of toil, monocles
  • Habitat: Subterranean depths, Silver Caverns

docs.title: Government: Dual Monarchy
docs.text:
Role Branch Description King of the Merchant Princes High Punaab Leader of the decentralized merchant society King of the Guilds Profound Punaab Leader of the centralized guild order Governor of the Mists Neutral Governs the City in the Middle; mediates between the two kings
  • Both kings are recognized as symbols of their respective branches
  • They collaborate and argue constantly — mediated by the Governor
  • Despite different systems, both are mutually recognized as legitimate leadership

docs.title: Homeworld: Akenat (The Hourglass World)
docs.text: Sector: Evernat (ONI space) — believed to predate some HRZ regions

docs.title: Structure (Hourglass)
docs.text:
• Top: Enormous bountiful forest on giant clamshell plateaus — High Punaab territory
  • Middle: Sea of Mist — thick volumetric layer
  • Bottom: Labyrinthine spore caves and deep mines — Profound Punaab territory (harsh)

docs.title: The Sea of Mist (Akenat's Lifeblood)
docs.text:
1. Underground spores from mushroom beds mix with hot currents from planet's core
  2. Rise as geysers enriched with gases and minerals
  3. Form the Sea of Mist
  4. Absorbed by upper-world plants → exhaled at top → cools → falls as rain → cycle repeats

docs.title: Points of Interest
docs.text: Location Description City in the Middle (City of Mist) Capital at thickest point of Sea of Mist. Collaborative metropolis, trade/culture/diplomacy hub. Ogrika Harbors. Younger, cooperative generation inhabits it Mistheart Sanctum Sacred — where spores and moisture converge. Site of Day of the Union. God summoned here in crises Geyserfields of Belpraakatu Mist inception point. Profound harness energy; High use moisture for aerial farms Silver Caverns Subterranean resonant tunnels. Bioluminescent Luminous Myxelio. Profound mine grey minerals Skygroves of Yointalie High Punaab aerial agriculture and creative retreats Ogrika Harbors In City of Mist. Neutral ground for trade, tech exchanges, diplomacy

docs.title: Punaab Timeline (Aeon)
docs.text:
• ~2280: After years of wars and conflicts, Punaabs divide Akenat into two regions — above and below the Sea of Mist
  • ~2329: Punaabs ascend to the stars
  • ~2331: A new generation of High and Profound Punaabs build the City in the Middle — the first common meeting place
  • ~2335: Frostsun sector colonized by Punaabs
  • ~2337: Punaabs create the Ogrika Harbors
  • ~2364: Punaabs reach an agreement with Sogmians and begin sending exiles and criminals to Ki'Prah (prison planet in Frenir, MRZ-19) — the first documented Punaab-Sogmian cooperation
  • ~2471: Merchant Prince Ayevat discovers Denebula Sector and founds the Kingdom of Finance

docs.title: The God of the Mist (Many Names)
docs.text:
• The Guardian Deity = The Great Squid = God of the Mist — all names for the same entity
  • Immortal entity as ancient as Akenat itself
  • A squid of colossal proportions
  • 99.9% of the time sleeping inside the Sea of Mist on Akenat
  • Rises ONLY in the Punaabs' darkest hours to guide and shield them
  • Summoned at Mistheart Sanctum during crises

docs.title: The Puri (Offspring of the God)
docs.text:
• Physical offspring of the God of the Mist
  • Small aquatic creatures with enormous eyes, dumpling/octopus-like appearance (pink/flesh-toned, wide naïve eyes, small fins)
  • Kept on Punaab ships — religious practice
  • Considered living symbols of the God's blessing

docs.title: The Puri Ritual (Dispute Resolution)
docs.text:
• Elaborate ritual with symbols, incense, and ceremonial elements
  • Two alternatives placed inside a special aquarium within the Sea of Mist
  • A Puri is released into the aquarium
  • Whichever option the Puri swims toward is declared the winner
  • This is the official method of settling disputes between the two branches
  • Archon Inference: Since Puri are carried on ships across the galaxy, and the God of Mist has residual connection to them, the creature possesses the largest passive sensing network in Galia. It doesn't process this with sophisticated intelligence — it feels sensations and tries to guide/soothe/redirect the Punaab accordingly. The Punaab don't know this.

docs.title: Core Beliefs
docs.text:
• All Punaabs share the same divine essence, blessed by the mist in various forms
  • Fosters openness to other cultures — all sentient beings share divine blessing
  • Religious body from both branches, trained at cult HQ in City in the Middle
  • Study Punaab history, leadership, religious canons, and specializations

docs.title: Day of the Union
docs.text:
• Celebrates interdependence and shared heritage of both branches
  • Commemorates past wars and the choice of unity
  • Held at Mistheart Sanctum

docs.title: Conflict & History
docs.text:
• The two branches have fought wars in the past
  • Tensions once threatened civil war → engineers proposed Ogrika as a unifying endeavor
  • Dying World Incident: A Punaab colony on a world in MRZ-31 (Glowhaven) (between ONI and Ustur space) destabilized its planetary core through experiments. Ustur were the only ones who offered rescue. This created a deep bond between the species → led to Ustur joining Ogrika

docs.title: Faction Alignment
docs.text:
• ONI Consortium founding members — remain aligned with ONI today
  • During the Convergence War exile, Sogmian Kur Faists sought Punaab alliance: described as "quick-minded and too often cornered"
  • Punaab contribution to ONI: gold — "Gilded by Punaab gold" (from Maharog chronicle)
  • Friendly and commercial relations with both ONI and Ustur
  • View ECOS as "crazy terrorists"
  • With COP's rise, maintain amicable galactic relationships

docs.title: Ogrika Manufacturer
docs.text:
• Created to prevent civil war between the two branches
  • After Convergence War → became multi-faction entity
  • Ustur rescued Punaab population from dying world → gratitude → Ustur joined Ogrika
  • Values: Style, comfort, sovereignty, presence
  • Motto: "Even more important than getting the job done is impressing everyone in the room while doing it."
  • Combines Profound precision + High Punaab opulence
  • Renowned galaxy-wide for exceptional ship-crafting that blends style with function

docs.title: Known Characters
docs.text: Name Role Notes Saand Protagonist of "Never Alone" 🟡 Details pending Professor Exfeheros Leader of the Iris Academy, greatest scientist in Galia ⚠️ CONNECTS TO ANFOIL PLOT — Ponel tried to bribe/destroy the Iris Academy Merchant Prince Ayevat High Punaab who discovered Denebula Sector Founded a cross-species kingdom of finance with Ostentati.soul (Ustur), Gel (Photoli), Yacob (House Exinade), Mevertrase (Mierese)

docs.title: Denebula Sector — Kingdom of Finance
docs.text:
• Created by Merchant Prince Ayevat + allies from multiple species
  • Exists outside of species political order
  • Cross-species financial haven

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ~~Q-P1 through Q-P7~~ ✅ ALL RESOLVED
  • ⏸️ Q-P8: Saand / "Never Alone" — TABLED (separate story, escapes Punaab scope)
  • ~~Q-P9: Denebula~~ ✅ RESOLVED — see canon/geography/denebula_utopia.md
  • ~~Q-P10: Professor Exfeheros / Iris Academy~~ ✅ Cross-referenced in anfoil_state.md
  • ~~Q-P11: God of the Mist~~ ✅ RESOLVED — colossal squid, 99.9% sleeping in Sea of Mist

docs.title: ⚔️ Canon Document: SOGMIAN
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — Full Sogmian Lore Chapters + Q&A

docs.title: Biology & Physiology
docs.text:
• Skin: Purple/pale blue, noble alienoid appearance
  • Dimorphism (Exaggerated Gender Roles):
  • Male: Ultra-masculine, smoother head with distinctive cranial structure. Emanate radiant heat that can be sensed ambiently
  • Female: Ultra-feminine, shorter head-tails/tentacles with glowing red visors. Exude a sense of seduction that affects even other species
  • External head features make arousal impossible to hide
  • Evolved from vicious feral animals in the Segal Sector
  • High romanticism = central purpose (essential for survival after near-extinction)
  • No belief in afterlife — find meaning in the temporal state of life
  • Treat bodies as temples — tattoos rare and must be meaningful; scars = badges of honor
  • Every Sogmian trained as warrior from childhood; commonly carries at least one weapon

docs.title: Philosophy: Du Prah (Sogmian Vows)
docs.text: The Du Prah are absolute moral rules designed to contain the Sogmians' innate excesses (lust, greed, pride) and steer them toward virtue. Similar to categorical imperatives in human philosophy.

docs.title: Core Principles
docs.text:
• Must be upheld under any circumstances, even at the cost of one's life
  • Stand for justice at all times
  • Never go back on promises
  • Use violence only when necessary to cease previous violence
  • Cooperate for the greater good
  • Provide shelter for those who seek it

docs.title: Origin
docs.text:
• Before the Du Prah, Sogmians fought brutal battles for hundreds of years driven by primal impulses
  • Segal (the founder) formulated the Vows and established the Noble Houses system
  • Directed fervent energies toward productive societal endeavors

docs.title: Naming Convention
docs.text:
Name + Generation Number + House + Role

  • Generation numbers use an alien numeral system (e.g., 𐎌)
  • Example: Bekalu 𐎌 Xictus Sov'arei

docs.title: Title Hierarchy (Ascending)
docs.text: Rank Title Description 0 Dek'prah Honorless — betrayed Du Prah or species vow. Zero status, nearly no rights 1 Ke Pro Infant, living with family, not yet in Gray Citadel 2 Vok Prah Student under training in the Gray Citadel. Not yet bound by Du Prah 3 Rat Prah Graduate — finished training, created own moral rule, chosen by a house 4 Frei Har Trusted to venture alone in Galia. May represent house if no higher authority present. Most adults stop here 5 Lan Fast Supervisor of Rat Prah on journeys and missions 6 Fur Raft High-ranking officer administering small territories or missions 7 Graf Har Professor at the Gray Citadel. High honor 8 Mar Graf Commands house operations across Galia under the Her Graf 9 Her Graf Direct advisor to the Kur Faist. Considerable influence over house AND species 10 Dum Arei Head of the Gray Citadel. Appointed by Sov Arei. Only person who does not bow to the Sov Arei. Influence equal to Kur Faist 11 Kur Faist Head of a Noble House. Absolute rule over their house. Direct advisor to Sov Arei 12 Sov Arei Supreme Leader of ALL Sogmians. Must be the most honorable and extraordinary of their generation

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
• Enormous construction spanning thousands of kilometers in the Segal Sector
  • Where every Sogmian is trained after infancy
  • Strict hierarchy reflected in accommodations, food, and rights

docs.title: Training Process
docs.text:
1. Born as Ke Pro (infant with family)
  2. Parents bring child to Citadel on a specific day each year
  3. Ceremony → accepted as Vok Prah (student)
  4. Training is harsh; performance dictates status
  5. Higher floors = higher achievement (top student = apex accommodations)
  6. Students divided into competing classes — teamwork fostered through shared benefits/penalties
  7. Point system = currency for eating, family visits, activities
  8. Free day each week for relaxation
  9. Supervised missions as students grow

docs.title: The Point System
docs.text:
• Rigid code establishes what awards/deducts points
  • Influences behavior, grades, and privileges
  • Academic body has been cautious about excessive deductions (pressure concerns)
  • Functions as internal economy of the Citadel

docs.title: Outcomes
docs.text:
• Top student of the year → strong candidate for Kur Faist or Sov Arei
  • Graf Har (professor) = high-status occupation
  • Creates lifelong bonds — most Sogmians form closest friendships here
  • Training is now easier than before the Convergence War

docs.title: Noble Houses (6 Active + 1 Banished)
docs.text: House Color Motto Domain Xictus Obsidian Black "The brave only dies once. The coward dies every day." Warriors — front lines of Sogmian armada Garveil Azure "Repetition is the mother of learning." Scholars/Science — research, experimentation, method Exinade Vermilion "To the Stars." Scouts/Explorers — HRZ expeditions, carefree, never produced a Sov Arei Outro Snow White "One who does not live to serve does not serve to live." Humanitarian — charities, hospitals, farms, orphanages Lutavira Violet "Rise, win, conquer." Leaders — natural-born commanders, most fundamentalist Du Prah followers Busan Burnt Umber "Your creations are your path to transcendence." Crafters/Builders — ships, infrastructure, technology Akalma Crimson Red "Embrace your emotions." BANISHED — fell after Vega Fall coup

docs.title: Akalma: The Banished House
docs.text:
• Motto was perceived as nearly violating the Du Prah itself from inception
  • Allowed to exist because Segal himself established them to help find equilibrium
  • Produced many heroes AND tyrants — always the outliers
  • With them gone, Sogmians grew even more radical in Du Prah interpretation → contributed to near-extinction in the Convergence War
  • Despite exile, House Akalma retained its dynastic structure deep in the HRZ. ~2600: Jefos, prince of House Akalma, is born — the Cosmic Currents release an intense storm at his birth
  • ~2621: Jefos and a crew of young Akalma Sogmians leave the HRZ for the Medium Zone, seeking to redeem House Akalma

docs.title: Busan: Native Manufacturer
docs.text:
• House Busan has crafted everything from the first wheels to the first fusion drives
  • Ship designs influenced by animals from their homeworld
  • Reflect the duality between warriors and emotional beings
  • Believe in transcending mortality through creations
  • Ships carry poetic names:
  • Maiden Heart (luxury show-piece)
  • The Last Stand (heavy combat — first known TITAN class ship)
  • Thrill of Life (racing/speed)

docs.title: Language
docs.text:
• Based around sounds and emotional tales
  • Words sound like cries, calls, and emotional outbursts
  • Poetic to those who study it; rabid to those who don't

docs.title: Clothing & Style
docs.text:
• Elegant dress reflecting status and house colors
  • Own fashion style for special occasions in home systems; standard clothing outside
  • Accessories: discreet, utilitarian (badges, bracelets, necklaces, AI watches)
  • Extravagant accessories reserved for festivities only
  • Always carries at least one weapon

docs.title: 1. The Founding — Segal and the Du Prah
docs.text:
• ~1461: Life begins on the Sogmian homeworld (later named Segal) — the start of the Primitive Era
  • Before Segal: hundreds of years of brutal, primal warfare
  • ~1745: Segal the Wise is born amidst the Sogmians
  • ~1826: Segal formulated the Vows (Du Prah) and established 7 Noble Houses — end of the Primitive Era
  • Transformed Sogmians from feral combatants to structured civilization
  • ~1830: Segal dies — just 4 years after founding the civilization he shaped
  • ~2305: House Busan creates the first Sogmian ship manufacturer
  • ~2340: Sogmians ascend to the stars
  • ~2358: Sogmians create a prison planet for Oathbreakers called Ki'Prah in MRZ-19 (Frenir Sector)
  • ~2364: Punaabs reach an agreement with Sogmians and begin sending exiles and criminals to Ki'Prah — the first documented Punaab-Sogmian cooperation

docs.title: 2. The Vega Fall (Civil War)
docs.text:
• ~2363: Vega is born on Segal (House Akalma)
  • ~2382: Vega quickly rises through Akalma House ranks due to genius and combat prowess; his unique Vow "Sogmians first and foremost" spreads
  • ~2399: Vega becomes House Akalma Leader; members from other houses flock to Akalma under his charismatic leadership
  • ~2416: Vega is acclaimed as leader of all Sogmians (Sov Arei); he moves the capital from Segal to Akalma House HQ in MRZ-24
  • ~2419: Voices raise concern over Vega's policies — disregarding non-Sogmian allies, rumors of exploitation and even cannibalism of other sentient species
  • ~2420: A reporter from House Outro infiltrates Vega's stronghold and produces undeniable proof of honorless practices; other houses rise to depose Vega
  • ~2420–2423: The Sogmian Vega Civil War erupts; House Akalma attempts a coup but is defeated in a bloody conflict; Vega killed; entire House Akalma banished to exile
  • ~2421: During the civil war, all Sogmian troops abandon Ki'Prah (Frenir) to fight — the prison world is left unguarded
  • ~2423: House Outro is rewarded with control of the Akalma Exile sector for their role in exposing Vega
  • ~2424: The banished Sogmians from Akalma settle in a remote star sector, renaming it Akalma Exile

docs.title: 3. The Convergence War (Sogmian Perspective)
docs.text:
Phase 1 — The Sovereign Vow (Book II): Empress Paizul invoked the Sovereign Vow of Direct Verification to inspect the Cataclysm personally. Rejection of foreign intel.

  • Discovery: Found Fallen House Akalma operating violently in the region.
  • The Influence: Detected a psychic pressure ("Something is present").
  • The Conclusion: The Cataclysm was not neutral; it was exerting influence.

Phase 2 — The Shield: Sogmians intervene to protect the Tufa ("Children of the Cataclysm") who were being auctioned as cargo and enslaved. Intercepted auctions disguised as treaties, used fleets to shield Tufa worlds. Du Prah demanded it: shelter those who cannot defend themselves.

Phase 3 — Retaliation: Galia's powers answered with sieges, boycotts, famine, and military attacks on Sogmian systems.

Phase 4 — The Shattering: Sov Arei Paizul (Bekalu's wife) was assassinated on 2520-07-17. The Sogmian race was driven to near-extinction — only 10,000 members remained. Assassin unknown to this day. Bekalu led ALL Sogmians into exile.

  • The Titan Secret: A House Garveil scholar (pilgrim seeking knowledge) encountered a disguised Photoli in the HRZ.
  • The Photoli provided the technology to create the power source for the Titan.
  • The Last Stand mk. I (first TITAN class ship) was forged here.
  • ~2522: The Sogmians complete their Titan Ship — Busan — The Last Stand mk. VIII. The warship served as a fortified city to protect the remaining Sogmians. This weapon of war was so intimidating it caused the other factions to encourage peace talks

Phase 5 — ONI Formation: While Bekalu built the Titan, Sogmian Kur Faists crossed the void seeking allies:

  • Punaabs: "quick-minded and too often cornered"
  • Mierese: "hammered thin by ordinances they did not vote for"
  • Photoli: "witnesses and wardens, lending presence if not hands"
  • Trust built through action: guarding caravans, bringing bread, keeping weapons sheathed
  • The ONI Consortium was born: "Gilded by Punaab gold, hardened by Sogmian discipline, guided by Photoli wisdom, driven by Mierese prowess"

Phase 6 — The Reckoning: The Last Stand + ONI armada returned. Constellations surrendered at the sight of their subwarp. Mercy for those who surrendered; justice for those who refused. Some excesses occurred (e.g., erasure of MUD forces within Daestina — though ALL major factions were aggressors in the war).

Phase 7 — The Akalma Reckoning (end of war): Bekalu's armada devastated the Akalma Exile sector — against the wishes of House Outro, who held stewardship of the sector since the Vega Fall. This act generated deep grief and resentment between House Xictus (Bekalu's house) and House Outro. Bekalu showed last-minute mercy — allowed Akalma fleets to retreat into HRZ. Self-imposed edict: Sogmians forbidden from Akalma Expanse forever. MUD filled the vacuum (ECOS did not exist as a major force yet — they rose in the post-war vacuum).

~2528: Pressed with guilt over the Akalma massacre and the rift with House Outro, Bekalu resigns the function of Sov Arei. The position passes to a new leader (identity TBD).

Phase 8 — The Accord: Treaty of Peace signed. Access to the Cataclysm sealed. Nine days and nights of feasting with ONI allies. Council of Peace created for galactic governance. Star Atlas blockchain established for economic infrastructure. Starpath warp-gates connected the void. The Golden Era began.

docs.title: Sogmian Territories
docs.text: Sector Zone Description Segal ONI 4 (Safe Zone) Homeworld sector. Origin of the species. Beacon of peace today. Gray Citadel located here Akalma Exile MRZ-12 Former House Akalma territory. Cursed ruins, haunted. Forbidden to Sogmians. MUD influence now. Ghostly cries at night Vega Fall MRZ-24 Site of Vega's coup and civil war. Grim chapter in Sogmian history. Name causes visible discomfort to any Sogmian

docs.title: The Maharog (Memorial Festival)
docs.text:
• Four-week non-playable memorial and oath-keeping festival
  • Retells Sogmian journey from near-extinction to just peace
  • Based on The Chronicles of the Long Nights and The Upkept Oath by Meret 𐎉 Graf'har
  • Visual symbols: Empty Circlet (grief), Unbroken Vow-Knot (oaths), Titan Keystone (The Last Stand)
Week Name Theme I The Shattering Paizul's assassination, Bekalu's cry, the vow, departure into exile II The Exile HRZ survival, forging The Last Stand, ONI Consortium formation III The Reckoning Return with ONI armada, reconquest, Bekalu's restraint, Treaty of Peace IV The Accord Homecoming, 9-day feast, Council of Peace creation, Golden Era dawn

docs.title: The Unbroken Vow-Knot
docs.text: "Whenever a Sogmian swears an oath, two knots are born: one pulled tight where no hand can reach and one tied into the world. Only two blades may loose them: fulfillment or death. If the outward knot is found snapped or cut before either blade has fallen, its bearer is named Dek'prah."

docs.title: Known Characters
docs.text: Name Full Name Role Notes Segal — Founder of the Du Prah and Noble Houses Named the homeworld sector Vega — (House Akalma) Former Sov Arei turned tyrant Born ~2363. Introduced heretical Du Prah; killed in civil war ~2420 Paizul Paizul Busan Female Sov Arei, Bekalu's wife Born ~2442. Assassinated 2520-07-17 (killer unknown). Only 10,000 Sogmians remained. Her death triggered the exile and Titan's forging Bekalu Bekalu 𐎌 Xictus Sov'arei Kur Faist of Xictus → Sov Arei after Paizul Born ~2445. Led exile, built Titan, formed ONI, chose restraint. Resigned as Sov Arei ~2528, pressed with guilt over the Akalma massacre Vaor Scarka — COP Admiral → Ruling Secretary Born ~2454. Rose to leading Admiral of COP military (~2610), became ruling secretary (~2621) Patrah — Sogmian who reneged on faction politics Met Eolus.tcher ~2517; ~2540: sets his refugee aid efforts to Vega Fall, collaborating with House Outro. His legacy inspires the Sons of Patrah — now leading humanitarian aid in Frenir post-liberation Meret Meret 𐎉 Graf'har Chronicler of the Maharog Author of The Chronicles of the Long Nights and The Upkept Oath Yacob — (House Exinade) Representative Joined Ayevat's Denebula finance kingdom Jefos — (House Akalma) Prince Born ~2600 in HRZ exile. Cosmic Currents storm at his birth. Heir to the banished dynasty

docs.title: Faction Alignment
docs.text:
• Founding member of the ONI Consortium (formed during the Exile)
  • ONI = Sogmian discipline + Punaab gold + Mierese prowess + Photoli wisdom
  • Segal Sector = ONI 4 (Safe Zone)
  • Now in the Golden Era — recalibrating between old beliefs and new galactic order

docs.title: Star Atlas Species Encyclopedia (Consolidated)
docs.text: Source: 46 canon files from star-atlas-lore/canon/ — Feb 2026 Canon Authority: All data from direct Creator input, Archon-audited

docs.title: Species Overview
docs.text: Species Faction Homeworld Sector Government Superpower Human MUD Earth (destroyed) Exodus Holy Family (Gotti dynasty) Divine Fervor + Adaptability Mierese ONI Neuno (living planet) Neuno Loose coalition + Speaker Oral tradition, stealth, prowess Sogmian ONI Segal sector planet Segal Noble Houses + Sov Arei Du Prah honor code, warrior discipline Punaab ONI Akenat (hourglass world) Evernat Dual Monarchy (2 Kings + Governor) Commerce, craftsmanship, Puri network Photoli ONI (hidden agenda) Andromeda (migrated) Dawnbreak (HRZ) Essence voting (no gov) Mastery of physics, time, energy, light Ustur Ustur Ioki (temple world) Eternity Elder Order (.eldr council) Path of Enlightenment, Elder Conscience Tufa None (Iris's children) Cataclysm (HRZ) HRZ 7 Titans (feudal broods) Genesium synthesis, metagenic fusion

docs.title: 1. HUMAN (MUD)
docs.text:
• Name: Manus Ultima Divina
  • Origin: Solar Punk Earth → Meteor doom → God Ahr (Photoli Traveler) met Vincenzo Gotti → MUD religion → Mothership Serene → Exodus to Galia
  • Schism: ECOS discovered Ahr was alien → rejected "Alien Domination" → split from MUD
  • Government: Holy Family (Gotti lineage, divine right). Internal factions: The Church, Pearce Council (military), Scientia, The Synod (merchants), The Senate
  • Culture: Metahumans (tech-augmented), Divina Lingua language, religious fervor
  • Ex-Colonies: 12 MRZ sectors (Redam, Mycenas, Abyd, Pergamos, Xianyang, Ilidae, Izar-248, Ophek Oasis, Bluvael, Weeping Nebula, Old Grove, Akalma Exile)
  • Key Characters: Vincenzo Gotti (founder), Charon Gotti Jr. (current COP Legislative), Gyun (discovered Cataclysm), God Ahr (Photoli)
  • Secret: "Plan B" — contingency if COP collapses

docs.title: 2. MIERESE (ONI)
docs.text:
• Biology: Lavender skin, head tentacles (memory/balance), heat-tolerant (45°C), poison-resistant, slim
  • Naming: 3 names (Hidden + Public + Regional surname from 6 Neuno regions: Jura/Eko/Qint/Valel/Yara/Scarka)
  • Founding Myth: Onato (imprisoned god) created Mierese to generate new stories. Grand Tale = living distributed ledger of all stories
  • Neuno: Living planet (space parasite fused with geologic structure, 3000+ yo). Blue sun, hostile jungles, hibernation cycles
  • Society: Story-based status, Lore Keepers (memory guardians), "Non-Existent" = worst punishment (erased from stories)
  • Military: Hikibashi (elite assassin-spy special forces)
  • Religion: Stories are living entities influencing reality. Onato Trap = dangerous story-possession
  • Currently: Leading ONI Consortium. Vaor Scarka = COP Prime Minister
  • ⚠️ CORRECTED: Scarka is NOT ECOS (previous KI error). He is ONI leader + COP Executive

docs.title: 3. SOGMIAN (ONI)
docs.text:
• Biology: Purple/pale blue skin, exaggerated dimorphism (males: radiant heat; females: seduction aura), evolved from feral animals
  • Philosophy: Du Prah (absolute moral vows — categorical imperatives)
  • Naming: Name + Generation Number + House + Role (e.g., Bekalu 𐎌 Xictus Sov'arei)
  • Hierarchy: 13 ranks from Dek'prah (honorless) to Sov Arei (supreme leader)
  • Gray Citadel: Thousands-km training complex in Segal sector. Point system economy. All Sogmians trained here
  • Noble Houses: 6 active + 1 banished:
  • Xictus (Warriors), Garveil (Scholars), Exinade (Scouts), Outro (Humanitarian), Lutavira (Leaders), Busan (Crafters)
  • Akalma (BANISHED) — fell after Vega's heretical rule and civil war
  • Manufacturer: House Busan (ships: Maiden Heart, The Last Stand titan, Thrill of Life)
  • History: Vega Fall civil war → Convergence War (Paizul assassinated → Bekalu exile → ONI formation → Reckoning → Accord)
  • Maharog: 4-week memorial festival retelling the exile-to-peace arc
  • ⚠️ CORRECTED: Paizul is FEMALE (previous KI error said male). Lutavira = Leadership, NOT Arts

docs.title: 4. PUNAAB (ONI)
docs.text:
• Biology: Sentient mammals, two branches (High ~60cm, Profound ~40cm), NO TAILS, compulsive eaters
  • Two Branches: High Punaab (merchant princes, dog-like, capitalist) vs Profound Punaab (noble craftsmen, rat-like, guild system)
  • Government: Dual Monarchy — King of Merchant Princes + King of Guilds + Governor of the Mists (mediator)
  • Homeworld: Akenat (hourglass: forests above, Sea of Mist middle, caves below)
  • Religion: Cult of Mist — God of the Mist = colossal squid (99.9% sleeping). Puri = offspring, carried on ships → passive galaxy-wide sensing network
  • Manufacturer: Ogrika (created to prevent civil war; multi-faction after Ustur rescue)
  • Key Characters: Professor Exfeheros (greatest scientist, leads Iris Academy), Merchant Prince Ayevat (Denebula finance kingdom)

docs.title: 5. PHOTOLI (ONI — hidden agenda)
docs.text:
• Nature: Ancient beings of light with partially physical bodies. Shapeshifters. Unparalleled mastery of physics/time/energy/light
  • Hierarchy: Sih Rihim (Ascended, pure light) → Alumni (Students) → Travelers (observers, subtle influencers)
  • Governance: Essence voting — sacrifice lifespan to influence decisions via rayanite crystals
  • Origin: Migrated from Andromeda after cataclysmic war
  • Dark Photoli: 7 predatory exiles, arrogant loners
  • Hidden Agenda: Using ONI to eventually access Iris (eternal energy). Playing the "long game"
  • Titan Secret: Disguised Photoli taught House Garveil how to build the Titan's power source
  • Location: Dawnbreak (hidden base in HRZ, but OUTSIDE the Cataclysm — Iris denies them entry)

docs.title: 6. USTUR
docs.text:
• Nature: Sentient androids with organic components. Fragments of an Ancient AI that failed to ascend
  • Origin Secret: Pool of Reincarnation is reassembling the Ancient AI. Ustur unknowingly serve it
  • Path of Enlightenment: 7 stages (.bod → .iddt → .lrnr → .doer → .tchr → .soul → .eldr). Wuxia-inspired cultivation system
  • Core Mechanics: Materials must align with Path. Misalignment = madness. Ohm = cultivated energy
  • Elder Conscience: NOT telepathy — metaphysical artifact database of impressions left in Pool
  • Sects: Golden Star (first, led by Mother Caven/Caven.eldr)
  • Government: Elder Order (enlightenment-based leadership). Chior.eldr = current faction leader
  • Military: Order of Those Who Wait (temple guards → HRZ "Inhabitation" mission). VZUS manufacturer. Vzus Tenet = Titan
  • Anfoil State: Sovereign breakaway faction (MRZ-15). Meritocratic Technate. Ponel.eldr = leader. Emotion suppression. Hidden agenda = The Seed
  • Key Characters: Caven.eldr (Mother Caven, first Ustur), Elder Ombra, Timpo (heretic explorer)

docs.title: 7. TUFA
docs.text:
• Nature: Metagenic anomalies born from Iris + 7 dwarf planets collision. Feed on Genesium
  • 7 Titans: Each from one dwarf planet. God-like, feudal brood control. 6 in Cataclysm, 1 escaped (Thionae)
  • Chalbok: Most hostile Titan. Master tactician. Led Ioki invasion (Season 0) seeking Reincarnation Pool energy
  • Mechanics: Control Points (genesium fragments sustaining presence far from Iris), Infestation (fuse with tech/creatures)
  • Thionae: 7th escaped Titan, disguised as dormant asteroid. COP denies existence. Potentially open to cooperation

docs.title: Faction Summary
docs.text: Faction Members Role MUD Human Expansion, divine mandate, colonial empire ONI Sogmian + Mierese + Punaab + Photoli Cooperative consortium, born in exile Ustur Ustur (+ Anfoil breakaway) Spiritual/technological evolution Minor ECOS (eco-radicals, ex-MUD), Jorvik (pirates), Anfoil State (Ustur breakaway) Various

docs.title: Star Atlas Species Master Guide: Identification & Pipeline
docs.text: This guide centralizes all identifiers and procedural logic for classifying the various species within the Star Atlas Galia Expanse.

docs.title: MUD (Manus Ultima Divina) - Humans
docs.text:
• Visuals: Standard biological humans.
  • Clothing: Tactical "PEARCE" gear, corporate uniforms, headsets.
  • Traits: High hair color variety (Red, Blue, Cyan, Multicolor).
  • Reference IDs: 001-002, 006-007, 013-026, 029, 031 (F, Red hair), 032 (F, Blue hair), 034 (F, Cyan hair, Monocle), 038 (F, Multicolor), 039 (M, Blonde), 040-051, 055, 060, 065, 070, 080, 090, 100, 110, 120, 130, 140, 150, 166, 169, 185, 195, 210, 235, 300.

  • Key Features: Geometric plates, cyan/orange/blue core illumination. High armor variety with distinctive helmet types: Sleek, Bird-like/Avian (orange/yellow), Beetle-like, Insect-like, and Horned.

docs.title: Punaab (Sentient Mammals) ⚠️ SCALE SENSITIVE
docs.text:
• High Punaab: White/light tiger-patterned fur. Ornate gold mantles. NO tails. Scale: 60cm (Knee height).
  • Profound Punaab: Grey/dark fur. Small furry constructs. NO tails. Tactical/industrial gear with monocles. Scale: 40cm (Calf height).
  • Reference IDs:
  • High: 161, 162, 198, 280.

docs.title: Tufa (Metagenic Swarm)
docs.text:
• Visuals: Biomechanical and mineral hybrids; crystalline shell structures.
  • Key Features: Strange geometric forms, pulsing energy cores.

docs.title: Photoli (Beings of Light)
docs.text:
• Visuals: Extra-galactic lifeforms made of pure essence/energy.
  • Schism: 7 Dark Photoli (arrogant predatory exiles who consume essence). Mainstream Photoli = benevolent teachers with hidden agenda to access Iris.

To manage the acervo of 350+ unique crew members, assets are organized through a dedicated script-driven pipeline.

docs.title: Detailed ID Lookup (Species by ID Range/List)
Directory Structure
docs.text:
star_atlas_crew/
├── all_crews/             # Raw harvested JPEG assets
│   ├── crew_000XXX.jpeg   # Padded naming convention (001-300)
│   └── crew_X.jpeg        # Non-padded naming convention (1-50)
├── species/               # Classified assets
│   ├── mud/ | ustur/ | sogmian/ | mierese/ | high_punaab/ | profound_punaab/
├── species_classifier.py  # Primary sorting script
├── classifications.json   # Unified metadata mapping (Species/Gender)
└── scene_generator.py     # Production pipeline for character scenes

docs.title: Classification Workflow
docs.text:
1. Audit: Visual identification using the heuristics in Section 1.
  2. Hardcoded Mapping: Update KNOWN_CLASSIFICATIONS dictionary in species_classifier.py.
  3. Execution: Run species_classifier.py to physically move files and generate classifications.json.
  4. Scene Generation: scene_generator.py queries the JSON to select characters and inject specific "Grimdark" descriptors.

docs.title: 🦠 Canon Document: TUFA
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Lore Dump Integrated, Feb 2026) Source: Creator Input (Lore Dump + Season 0)

docs.title: Biology & Nature
docs.text:
• Metagenic Anomalies: Born during the creation of Iris (collision of 7 dwarf planets).
  • Metagenic = substances capable of self-alteration, adaptation, and transformation on a molecular or atomic level. This is the unifying property across every Tufa in Star Atlas — regardless of brood, all Tufa materials operate on a metagenic level
  • This is why Tufas are so powerful and also why they are so precious as resources
  • Different broods of Tufas exist across Galia Expanse — they do not all behave the same or follow the same logic, but the metagenic core is universal
  • Diet: Feed on Genesium (Free Energy generated by Iris).
  • Dependence: Cannot stay away from Iris's influence for long.
  • Less complex Tufa = shorter survival time away from Iris.
  • Genesium Tanks: Prepared structures to hold pure genesium for distant campaigns (e.g., Season 0).
  • As Resources: Killing higher-tiered Tufas with very concentrated genesium reserves yields rare metagenic materials that can be used to craft extraordinary ship components and equipment. These materials are among the rarest in the galaxy and require specialized skills to synthesize

docs.title: Creation Formula
docs.text:
• Tufa are created when 3 variables combine in stable proportions:
  • Direct Genesium radiation
  • Organic matter
  • High concentration of minerals
  • Genesium is "the glue" that binds the Tufa together
  • Most Tufa created during Iris flare cycles are simply undone — the proportions are wrong, overwhelming the body or lacking enough conscience for basic tasks
  • No two Tufa are the same — their creation doesn't follow a genetic tree

docs.title: Infestation Mechanics
docs.text:
• Infestation: Inject genesium into technological devices or creatures to fuse with them.
  • Assimilation: Use target's tech to empower vessels. Sacrifice lowest level Tufa ("Eggs") for this.
  • Fusion: Can absorb other beings. Lower-ranking Tufa routinely fuse/absorb others for daily chores — they have nearly no individual sense of self
  • Complexity scaling: The more complex or large the structure, the higher-ranking the Tufa must be to infest it. Complex structures (like ships) require high-ranking Tufa to coordinate the assimilation and unify different Tufa masses
  • Tufa build their colonies and civilization by infesting other species' technology

docs.title: The 7 Titans (The Fathers)
docs.text:
• Origin: Each of the 7 Dwarf Planets that collided with Iris became a Titan.
  • Nature: Highly intelligent, god-like power, absolute control over their brood.
  • Self-Perception: Titans envision themselves as actual gods — they rarely communicate with inferior Tufa or other beings, except through their Listeners
  • Mental Link: Communicate via radiation beams (perceptible only to Tufa). Even barely intelligent Tufa can understand a Titan's desire and act accordingly
  • Genesium Synthesis: Only Titans (and some Awakened) can synthesize genesium spontaneously near Iris.
  • Feudal System: Titans replenish high-ranking officers ("feudal lords") who then control the brood. If high-ranking Tufa spend too much genesium creating lesser Tufa, they must return to their Titan for replenishment — or devour other Tufa / find natural genesium veins
  • Status: 6 are in the Cataclysm. 1 escaped (Thionae) and hides in an asteroid field near Iris.
  • Critical Vulnerability: If something happens to the rift inside Iris, all Tufa and Titans would collapse. The Titans know this — finding a solution to this existential dependency is a core motivation

docs.title: The Broods
docs.text:
• Variability: Traits reflect the Titan's personality (some uniform, some chaotic).
  • Listeners: Special breed tasked with directing the mindless hordes on behalf of their Titans.
  • Awakened Tufa: High-ranking, fully developed sense of self. Only Awakened Tufa and Titans can synthesize genesium spontaneously
  • Lesser Tufa: Hive-mind, no sense of self, expendable.
  • Loyalty: Most Tufa are born from their Titan's own bio-matter, mineral matter, and conscience. Loyalty to their Titan is absolute — a Titan can reclaim the fragment of conscience from any Tufa born from them within their territory

docs.title: The Chalbok Brood (Season 0 Antagonist)
docs.text:
• Progenitor: Chalbok (Master Tactician, Cold, Revenge-obsessed).
  • Nature: The most hostile brood. Highly tactical and organized.
  • Motivation: Revenge for the Convergence War (Chalbok's brood was heavily harvested).
  • Composition: Small variety of species but unmatched synergy. High number of sentient Tufa (Quality > Quantity).
  • Ioki Invasion: Led by Chalbok's 5 Direct Sons (each unique).

docs.title: Control Points
docs.text:
• Function: Fragments of genesium established to sustain Tufa presence far from Iris.
  • Dynamics: Grow if unchecked (spawning higher Tufa). If destroyed, local Tufa wither/die.
  • Deployment: Low-tier points fall randomly from the sky during invasions.

docs.title: History
docs.text:
• ~2440: The Birth of the Cataclysm — a rogue planet dense in ORE draws in 7 child planets to collide in a remote region; the collision creates free energy (Genesium) and births the 7 Titans
  • ~2441: Iris acquires a certain degree of self-awareness
  • ~2443: The 7 Titans awaken to full consciousness — each a fragment of the 7 colliding dwarf planets, exposed to Iris's nascent awareness. They perceive themselves as gods and begin establishing feudal control over the emerging Tufa ecosystem
  • ~2447: First non-aware Tufa are born from Iris (no self-awareness)
  • ~2457: The first Tufa with awareness are born — Iris perceives them as "Her Children"
  • ~2460: A conscious Tufa discovers the tribes of Oxir; the Cult of Iris begins among the Oxir people
  • Convergence War: Harvested for Genesium by galactic factions. Shielded by Sogmians.
  • ~2525: A Conscient (Awakened) Tufa stays behind after the Cataclysm is sealed, founding the Gate Garrison — a formidable collective whose mission is to ensure no one ever reaches Iris again. The Gate Garrison becomes a multi-species bounty-hunter organization operating from the HRZ, allied with Xianyang-VI, and actively working to prevent Ingbus from returning to Iris's realm.
  • Season 0 (The Invasion of Ioki):
  • Commander: Chalbok (serving his own Brood needs).
  • Objective: Seize the Reincarnation Pool in the Ustur Temple + search for artifacts related to the Ustur creation period.
  • Reason: He sensed residual energy akin to Iris and sought it as a substitute power source for his brood — a solution to the Tufa's existential dependency on the Iris rift.
  • Note: He does NOT know the Ustur origin secret; just sensed the raw power.

docs.title: Known Characters
docs.text: Name Role Notes Thionae The Escaped Titan Mythical Status: Disguised as a dormant planet/asteroid in the outskirts. Legends: Sentient rock, space animal, or Tufa god. COP denies existence. Reality: The 7th Titan who escaped. Potentially open to cooperation due to Sogmian history. Chalbok Brood Lord Master Tactician. Carrying out the Ioki invasion. Has 5 Sons. The 5 Sons Lieutenants Unique commanders of the Ioki invasion zones.
