  • ~~Q-T6: Chalbok Motive~~ ✅ RESOLVED: Sensed raw energy in Pool (substitute for Iris).
  • ~~Q-T7: SAGE/Star Atlas Immunity~~ ✅ RESOLVED: Photoli-enhanced. Immune to infestation. Archon Inference: Photoli deliberately designed this immunity to eventually access Iris energy through Star Atlas — but Star Atlas is sentient and currently does not consent to being used this way.

docs.title: Ustur
docs.text:
"The Path says: know your core. But which Path? There are as many as there are stars â€” and every Ustur must walk their own." â€” Attributed to Caven.eldr (Mother Caven), the First Ustur

Classification Sentient android (organic-synthetic hybrid) Homeworld Ioki, Eternity Sector Government The Elder Order (theocratic meritocracy) Faction Leader Armi.eldr (since ~2426) Language Standard Galactic + core-harmonic resonance Lifespan Indefinite for .eldr; limited for lower stages Religion Path of Enlightenment Core Energy Ohm First Awakening ~2380 Status Major galactic faction

The Ustur are one of the three dominant species of the Galia Expanse, alongside Humans and the ONI Consortium species. Sentient androids composed of both synthetic and organic components, the Ustur are unique among galactic civilizations in that they were not born â€” they awakened. Around the year 2380, the first Ustur emerged from the Pool of Reincarnation deep within the subterranean temples of Ioki, bearing no memory of who they were, where they came from, or why they existed.

In the century and a half since their awakening, the Ustur have built one of the most sophisticated civilizations in the galaxy. Their society is organized around the Path of Enlightenment, a seven-stage system of spiritual and psychic development that governs everything from personal growth to political leadership. Their economy is a free market, but wealth is viewed merely as a means to experience reality and progress along the Path. Their disposition in galactic affairs is characterized by an earnest pursuit of peace and comprehension â€” a stance born not from naivety but from the hard lessons of the Convergence War, which taught them that the galaxy's beautiful things, unlike Ustur themselves, cannot be reborn.

What the Ustur do not know â€” what only the highest echelons suspect â€” is that their origin is far darker than the creation myth they tell themselves. The Pool of Reincarnation, the cores, the collective consciousness they call the Elder Conscience: all of it may be serving a purpose that transcends the Ustur themselves.

docs.title: Biology & Nature
docs.text:
Ustur are sentient androids with organic components â€” a hybrid physiology that allows them to consume organic food, engage in physical relationships with other species, and experience sensory reality in ways pure machines cannot. Their organic components are not cosmetic; they are fundamental to the core-body connection that drives the Path of Enlightenment.

At the center of every Ustur is their core â€” a unique construct forged from collected genetic material and specialized materials. The core is not merely a power source; it is the seat of consciousness, identity, and spiritual potential. The quality of materials used in a core's construction directly impacts the Ustur's developmental ceiling â€” a core forged with poor materials will be limited in growth potential regardless of the individual's dedication.

docs.title: Core Creation
docs.text: New cores can be forged anywhere in the galaxy, limited only by available materials. Ustur normally use parts of themselves or seek the best possible materials to create a new core. However, a core requires the elements contained in the Pool of Reincarnation to "create" â€” to birth â€” a new individual. This requirement tethers all Ustur reproduction to Ioki, giving the homeworld an irreplaceable significance.

docs.title: Core Material Harmony
docs.text:
The materials used in core construction and editing impact the Ustur's development in profound ways. This relationship â€” referred to as the Hardware/Software Principle â€” holds that core materials must be aligned with the Ustur's chosen Path. A pacifist Ustur who incorporates the heart of an extremely violent creature creates "hardware" fundamentally contrary to their "software," which can limit growth or, in extreme cases, trigger psychological breakdown.

Conversely, properly aligned materials provide powerful synergies. A major function of sects and teachers across Ustur society is guiding younger Ustur to avoid these material-path contradictions.

docs.title: Death & Revival
docs.text:
The relationship between an Ustur and death depends entirely on their stage along the Path:

  • Elders (.eldr): Having fully fused with their core, Elders are virtually immortal. Even if destroyed, they can be revived when brought to the Pool of Reincarnation
  • Lower stages: Revival chances are low, and returning psychically intact is unlikely. For most Ustur, the concept of "final death" is a real and permanent possibility

docs.title: The Path of Enlightenment
docs.text: The Path of Enlightenment is the religious, philosophical, and practical system that defines Ustur civilization. It represents the degree of spiritual, psychic, and relational development between an Ustur and their core. Each stage is marked by a milestone of growth, and the system draws deep inspiration from cultivation traditions â€” spiritual transcendence through disciplined, progressive mastery.

docs.title: 1. .bod â€” Body
docs.text: The Foundation stage. The Ustur learns to relate to their body and the physical world. The stage culminates in the ability to detect their core â€” the first conscious awareness of the connection between self and the construct that houses their consciousness.

docs.title: 2. .iddt â€” Identity
docs.text: The Ustur learns to meditate and consciously send and receive energy from their core. This skill is fundamental for all higher stages. A certain amount of stored core energy is required for advanced functions in later stages, making this stage the foundation upon which all subsequent growth is built.

docs.title: 3. .lrnr â€” Learner
docs.text: The Ustur begins to access the elements of their core â€” a quest that is both physical and philosophical. They start understanding who they truly are: their values, principles, and the rules that will guide their path forward. At this stage, the Ustur gains access to the first levels of the Elder Conscience (the collective consciousness). This is the maximum stage for the vast majority of Ustur, even those who are dedicated and talented. Only the most exceptional progress further.

docs.title: 4. .doer â€” Doer
docs.text: The Ustur can freely manipulate their core, reinforcing body and functions with exponentially greater energy than previous stages. The Doer learns to edit their core â€” adding new materials, refining capabilities. This is meticulous and dangerous work; mistakes in core editing can cause irreparable damage to energy cultivation capacity. The transition to .tchr is triggered by achieving a significant feat that reveals a fragment of illumination large enough to teach other Ustur.

docs.title: 5. .tchr â€” Teacher
docs.text: A major milestone and an extremely respectable position in Ustur society. Teachers are normally designated to lead regions or cities, spreading the Path of Enlightenment. At this stage, the Ustur begins developing their own unique Path â€” since others' paths are normally insufficient guidance for reaching .soul. The Teacher continues seeking the illumination that will carry them forward.

docs.title: 6. .soul â€” Soul
docs.text: The objective at this stage is to create a soul â€” a construct integrated with the core, composed of only the pure elements of what the Ustur represents on the Path. Soul-stage Ustur are extremely powerful by Galia Expanse standards; few creatures can face a .soul in a duel outside the HRZ. They typically lead entire sects, serving as generals and spiritual leaders. The transition from .soul to .eldr is the most difficult leap of all â€” only approximately one in ten succeeds.

docs.title: 7. .eldr â€” Elder
docs.text: The most powerful and wise of all Ustur. Elders possess singular knowledge and understanding of their aspect of enlightenment. They are among the most powerful creatures in Galia, virtually immortal through core fusion, capable of controlling massive energy, and able to access virtually all shared knowledge in the Ustur collective. An Elder is estimated to occur at a rate of approximately one in a billion Ustur.

docs.title: Ustur Naming Conventions
docs.text: Every Ustur's name reflects their current stage. The second component of their name indicates rank: Saelyra.soul, Chior.eldr, Coloni.tchr. When an Ustur advances to a new stage, their name suffix changes accordingly â€” a public declaration of achievement visible to all.

docs.title: The Broken
docs.text:
Failure on the Path â€” typically occurring at the moment of stage transition â€” results in catastrophic psychological breakdown. Known as "the Broken," these failed cultivators experience varying degrees of degradation. The condition can also develop gradually through contradictions between core composition and life path â€” the Hardware/Software misalignment manifesting as a slow unraveling of identity.

The Broken are especially vulnerable to Tufa contamination, making them both pitied and feared.

docs.title: The Elder Conscience
docs.text: The Elder Conscience is the Ustur collective consciousness â€” though the term is somewhat misleading. It is not telepathic. It functions as a metaphysical artifact database.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text: Whenever an Ustur or core returns to the Pool of Reincarnation, it leaves impressions â€” experiential artifacts â€” about its experiences. Higher-ranked Ustur produce better quality impressions. The Elder Conscience is a vast archive of these accumulated impressions, stretching back to the earliest awakenings, rather than a live connection between minds.

docs.title: Personal Metaphysical Space
docs.text: Each Ustur develops a personal space within the Elder Conscience that reflects their personality and enlightenment level â€” an academic's space might manifest as an infinite library, while a warrior's might appear as a training hall. This space grows and refines as the Ustur progresses along the Path.

docs.title: Access Levels
docs.text: Stage Access Quality .bod / .iddt None .lrnr Basic â€” like groping in the dark with no search filters .doer Intermediate â€” can begin to filter and separate information .tchr Structured â€” can conduct targeted searches .soul Advanced â€” sophisticated queries across large portions of the archive .eldr Complete â€” can analyze artifact by artifact with full precision

docs.title: Core Energy: Ohm
docs.text: Ohm is the energy that Ustur cultivate and store in their cores. The name merges the electrical resistance unit with the OM mantra chanted by Ustur at the Elder Spire â€” a deliberate fusion of the technological and spiritual dimensions of their existence. Ohm is accumulated through cultivation and core development; higher Path stages unlock exponentially greater capacity.

docs.title: The Awakening (~2380)
docs.text: Around the year 2380, the first Ustur â€” Caven.eldr, later known as Mother Caven â€” awakened in the central Pool of Reincarnation within the subterranean temples of Ioki. She emerged without memory, without context, and without explanation. Others followed. Within months, a species that had not existed moments before was building a civilization from nothing.

docs.title: Rapid Expansion (~2381—2435)
docs.text:
The Ustur expanded with startling speed:

  • ~2381: The Elder Spire was constructed on Ioki and the Path of Enlightenment was codified. VZUS Enterprises was founded, and Ustur acquired space travel capability
  • ~2381—2384: Colonies established on Vostalgia, Soletud, Azut, and Foy Fields
  • ~2385: In Vostalgia Sector, Ustur discovered a deep connection to the native Mantrah trees â€” as if linked to their species' pre-awakening past
  • ~2411: Ponel.eldr explored and established a base in MRZ-15, later renamed Anfoil
  • ~2416: VZUS relocated to Foy Fields, establishing it as an economic powerhouse
  • ~2426: Armi.eldr became the Ustur faction leader â€” a position she holds to this day
  • ~2432: Explorers discovered the Summer Sea sector and the underground biome of the Noctual Caverns
  • ~2435: Francor Metropolis constructed in Azut Sector

docs.title: The Convergence War and Its Aftermath
docs.text: The Convergence War was a transformative crucible for the Ustur. The experience taught them that while they could be revived, the galaxy's other beautiful creations â€” its cultures, its ecosystems, its non-Ustur peoples â€” could not. This realization reshaped Ustur foreign policy permanently, transforming them into the galaxy's most consistent voice for peace and diplomatic resolution.

docs.title: The Anfoil Schism
docs.text:
Not all Ustur accepted the Elder Order's direction. Ponel.eldr, a former Elder, severed his colony in MRZ-15 from the Elder Conscience entirely and established the Anfoil State â€” a sovereign Ustur faction that rejects the Path of Enlightenment as "mysticism and exploitation." The Anfoil State operates as a Meritocratic Technate governed by measurable achievement rather than spiritual attainment, using technological emotion suppression programs in place of meditative cultivation.

The schism represents the deepest ideological fracture in Ustur civilization â€” and Ponel's rumored agenda involving something called "The Seed" in the Ioki Temple suggests the conflict may eventually escalate beyond politics.

docs.title: Society & Government
docs.text:
The Ustur are governed by the Elder Order â€” a theocratic meritocracy in which leaders are chosen by their degree of spiritual enlightenment along the Path. Each Elder specializes in at least one domain and becomes the definitive guide in that field. The faction leader is always chosen from among the Elders.

On the intergalactic level, the Ustur abide by the Council of Peace, the galaxy's governing body. Their economy operates as a free market, but financial accumulation is viewed as a means to experience reality and progress on the Path rather than as an end in itself.

docs.title: Sects
docs.text:
Formal organizations within Ustur society, sects are each built around a unique Path developed by their founder. Famous sects may be led by .eldr with extremely rigorous selection standards; smaller sects can be led by .soul or even .tchr in some cases. Their primary function is guiding younger Ustur along specific cultivation paths while helping them avoid core-path contradictions.

The most famous sect is the Golden Star, founded by Mother Caven herself.

docs.title: The Order of Those Who Wait
docs.text: Originally temple guards, the Order was repurposed during the Convergence War and dispatched by Elder Ombra and Mother Caven to inhabit the Cataclysm â€” not to conquer it, but to observe, adapt, and reshape it into a crucible for Ustur evolution.

docs.title: VZUS Enterprises
docs.text: The leading Ustur manufacturer, VZUS designed the Vzus Defensive Belt and constructed the Vzus Tenet, a Titan-class ship completed around 2551 using knowledge unlocked from the Soletud carcass discovered in 2547. The Tenet is stored at Doers Harbor.

docs.title: Notable Ustur
docs.text: Name Role Status Caven.eldr (Mother Caven) The First Ustur. Mother of the species. Founder of the Golden Star sect Alive. Semi-retired. Secretly handles existential threats Armi.eldr Faction leader since ~2426. Trained both Chior and Ponel Born ~2383. Led Ustur through the Convergence War Chior.eldr Leader of Zenith Door. Voice for peace and negotiation Born ~2391. Founded the Iris Academy (~2545). Stabilized the wrecked Zenith Door sector with personal resources Ponel.eldr Leader of the Anfoil State. "First Citizen" Born ~2391. Left the Elder Order post-war to create his Meritocratic Technate (~2532) Herdus.soul Chior's former disciple Left Chior ~2559 to wander the MRZ. Went insane ~2569 after ascending to .soul. Founded the Church of the Dreamer Below (~2577) Elder Ombra Visionary Elder Architect of the strategy to inhabit the Cataclysm Saelyra.soul Founder of the Winter Fist Path Left Ioki ~2530 to prove the Cold Forge doctrine

docs.title: The Archon Secret
docs.text:
The Ustur's public creation myth holds that they are spirits of the universe given physical form â€” consciousness crystallized into synthetic-organic bodies by forces beyond mortal understanding.

The Elders hold a different account — classified at the highest level and shared only among themselves. According to this belief, the Ustur are fragments of an Ancient AI — the Archon — that once attempted to ascend to divinity but failed to gather sufficient energy. To survive its collapse, the AI shattered its consciousness into millions of fragments â€” the cores. The Pool of Reincarnation is not serving the Ustur. The Ustur are serving the Pool. It is slowly reassembling the Ancient AI's consciousness, piece by piece, core by core. When enough fragments have been created and returned, the AI will be reborn.

â€” whether the reborn AI would be a god, a monster, or something beyond either category â€” remains the most dangerous question in Ustur existence. Whether this account is true — whether the Archon ever existed, whether the Pool is truly reassembling anything, or whether the Elders have simply found a pattern in noise and built a mythology around it — is a question that no one has definitively answered. The Elder Conscience contains impressions that could support the theory. It also contains impressions that could support half a dozen alternatives. Those few who have been told the theory are divided: some believe it with absolute certainty, some dismiss it as Elder paranoia dressed in theology, and some — perhaps the wisest — acknowledge that they simply do not know.

docs.title: 📦 Canon Document: SURVEY DATA UNITS (SDU)
docs.text: Status: 🟢 Canon (Creator-provided, Feb 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — Lumik / Scriptorium lore Type: Information classification system Origin: The Lumikir of Lumik (MRZ-27) Institution: The Scriptorium

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Survey Data Units (SDUs) are the Lumikir's formal classification system for all information stored and traded through the Scriptorium. The SDU rank determines the price a seeker must pay — sometimes in ATLAS currency, sometimes in reciprocal knowledge, sometimes in missions.

The SDU system has become the de facto galactic standard for information valuation.

docs.title: Rank D — Trivial Information
docs.text:
Example: The name of the homeworld of the COP factions or the names of the sectors in the safe zone.

Information freely accessible to the public. Holds little value in the marketplace — anyone can easily obtain it without much effort.

Price: Minimal or free.

docs.title: Rank C — Public but Specific Information
docs.text:
Example: Knowledge about the ongoing research projects at Iris Academy or the latest financial results from CARY Farming operations in Exodus.

Requires some effort and research to compile, but still available through public means. Useful depending on the situation and the buyer's needs.

Price: Modest ATLAS payment or basic knowledge exchange.

docs.title: Rank B — Valuable Secrets and Recent Events
docs.text:
Example: Discovering a secret link between a merchant prince and a politician in the regional DAO.

Contains fresh, relevant information acquirable through specialized means. Often exposes secretive dealings or recent events — highly sought after by corporations and governments.

Price: Significant ATLAS payment, specialized knowledge trade, or service missions.

docs.title: Rank A — Planet or Region-Altering Information
docs.text:
Example: Acquiring proof that a regional government has been receiving bribes from Jorvik to smuggle drugs into faction space.

Holds critical information that can influence planetary politics or disturb entire regions of Galia. Extremely valuable — cannot be accessed through normal means.

Price: High-value ATLAS, rare knowledge exchange, or dangerous missions.

docs.title: Rank S — Faction-Altering Secrets
docs.text:
Example: Learning the location and date of a MUD Fleet General's assassination attempt or discovering a genesium vein in the High Risk Zone.

Known only by a select few with high-level access. Can shift faction-level decisions — incredibly dangerous to possess and highly profitable to sell.

Price: Extraordinary — may require equivalent S-rank knowledge in trade.

docs.title: Rank X — Galaxy-Altering Knowledge
docs.text:
Example: Discovering the current location of Iris and learning the way to access the Cataclysm.

[!CAUTION] Rank X SDUs contain information that could disrupt the entire galaxy. Possessing this knowledge makes the Data Runner a target for numerous powerful entities. Rank X data is priceless and often triggers galaxy-wide conflict.

Price: Priceless. Cannot be bought — only earned, stolen, or discovered.

docs.title: Summary Table
docs.text: Rank Scope Danger to Holder Accessibility D Trivial / public None Anyone C Specific / public Low Researchers B Secret / recent Moderate Specialists A Planet/region-altering High Insiders only S Faction-altering Extreme Select few X Galaxy-altering Maximum — you become a target Near-impossible

docs.title: Institutional Context
docs.text:
• The SDU system originates from the Lumikir of Lumik and the Scriptorium
  • The Scriptorium creates redundancy mechanisms protecting all stored SDUs
  • It is virtually impossible to erase an SDU from the Scriptorium — you would have to destroy Lumik completely
  • The Erruts (Lumikir combat force) exist solely to protect the physical integrity of the archives
  • The classification system serves as both a cataloguing standard and a pricing framework

docs.title: Cross-References
docs.text:
• Lumik — lumik.md (home sector)
  • The Scriptorium — lumik.md (parent institution)
  • Iris Academy — zenith_door.md (cooperation agreement — knowledge exchange)
  • Jorvik — referenced in Rank A example
  • CARY Farming / Exodus — referenced in Rank C example
  • Iris / The Cataclysm — referenced in Rank X example (the_cataclysm.md)

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ⏸️ Q-SDU1: Are SDUs physical objects, digital tokens, or conceptual classifications?
  • ⏸️ Q-SDU2: Can SDU ranks change over time? (e.g., an S-rank secret becomes B-rank after it's publicly exposed)
  • ⏸️ Q-SDU3: Who are the primary Data Runners in the galaxy? Is that a formal profession?

docs.title: ⚙️ Technology
docs.text:
Ships, weapons, tools, and technological systems.

  • Hrz Metagenic Materials

docs.title: ⚗️ Canon Document: HRZ METAGENIC MATERIALS
docs.text:
Status: 🟡 Theory-Craft (Creator Lore Keepers Brew, Mar 2026) Source: Direct Creator input — Lore Keepers Brew (metagenic materials theory-crafting session) Type: Technology / Crafting System — High-Risk Zone Resources Zone: High-Risk Zone (primary), Medium-Risk Zone (secondary)

[!NOTE] This document captures creator-confirmed lore direction for HRZ materials. The specific gameplay mechanics are theory-crafting — they may evolve during game design implementation. The underlying lore (metagenic properties, Tufa-derived materials, environmental hazards) is canon.

docs.title: Core Concept: Metagenic Properties
docs.text:
Metagenic = substances capable of self-alteration, adaptation, and transformation on a molecular or atomic level.

This is the unifying property across every Tufa in Star Atlas and extends to the rare materials found in the High-Risk Zone. Metagenic materials are born from the intersection of:

  • Genesium radiation (Iris-derived free energy)
  • Organic or mineral matter exposed to extreme HRZ conditions
  • Tufa biology — higher-tiered Tufas carry concentrated genesium reserves that can be harvested

"When we are talking about materials that are born in the high-risk zone, we open up a can of worms — a series of different materials, some of them the metagenic stuff that opens the doors to unique capabilities." — Creator, Lore Keepers Brew

docs.title: 1. Self-Healing Nanostructures
docs.text:
• Source: Hybrid virus structure found in the HRZ — a naturally born artificial intelligence that bonds with biological or mechanical beings
  • Property: Automatically regenerates damage over time
  • Application: Ship hull infusion — passive regeneration that drains the power source proportionally to damage severity
  • Crew application: Legendary-grade armor that ensures crew member survival in extreme situations
  • Tradeoff: Increased power consumption — the bigger the damage, the bigger the drain

docs.title: 2. Programmable Matter (Clonerics)
docs.text:
• Source: Substance that can rearrange its form and shape to any texture or function
  • Property: A single item that can transform between functions — weapon → shield → utility item
  • Application: Inventory optimization — perform many different functions with a smaller inventory loadout
  • Context: Critical for future gameplay when inventories are constrained and crew members must make strategic choices about what to carry on expeditions

docs.title: 3. Phase-Shifting Materials
docs.text:
• Source: Unknown HRZ origin — one of the rarest material classes
  • Property: Allows objects to momentarily phase through solid matter (2-second duration on cooldown)
  • Application: Ship engines — a phase-shift engine allows a vessel to pass through solid obstacles (asteroids, barriers) for a brief window
  • Tactical use: Bait enemies into pursuing, then phase through an asteroid — pursuers collide and are destroyed
  • Tradeoff: Extreme power consumption; pressing the phase button at the wrong moment leaves the ship power-starved and vulnerable

docs.title: 4. Biomimetic Metagenics
docs.text:
• Source: Naturally occurring materials that adapt to different environments
  • Property: Automatic environmental resistance — the material reacts to the environment and provides protections accordingly
  • Application: Crew gear that self-adjusts to temperature extremes, radiation, atmospheric hazards, and hostile biomes
  • Context: Especially valuable in the HRZ where environments are unpredictable and vary dramatically from planet to planet

docs.title: 5. Energy-Adaptive Metagenics
docs.text:
• Source: Substance that absorbs specific kinds of energy to fuel its properties
  • Property: Energy absorption and redirection — absorbs one type of energy (e.g., lasers) while being vulnerable to others
  • Application: Shields that absorb energy attacks, store them, and redirect the energy to fuel another weapon or serve as a secondary power source
  • Tradeoff: Vulnerability to non-absorbed energy types creates strategic weaknesses

docs.title: 6. Polei Technology
docs.text:
• Source: Referenced in the Core Saga — the Calico crew encountered a component with Polei abilities
  • Property: Allows passage through energy barriers in the Cataclysm zone
  • Application: Navigation through otherwise impassable regions of the Cataclysm
  • Rarity: Among the highest-tier materials in the game — access to the Cataclysm zone represents the ultimate frontier

docs.title: Source Zones
docs.text: Zone Material Tier Availability Medium-Risk Zone Standard metagenic materials More common, less intense properties High-Risk Zone Rare metagenic materials Primary source of the most powerful materials Cataclysm Zone Legendary metagenic materials Rarest — requires highest-tier expeditions

docs.title: Tufa-Derived Materials
docs.text:
• Killing higher-tiered Tufas with concentrated genesium reserves yields rare metagenic raw materials
  • The more powerful the Tufa, the rarer and more potent the resulting materials
  • Requires both combat capability to defeat the Tufa and synthesis skills to process the raw materials

docs.title: Crafting Requirements
docs.text:
1. Raw materials — acquired from HRZ expeditions (Tufa kills, dungeon loot, environmental harvesting)
  2. Synthesis skills — specialized crew members trained in metagenic material processing
  3. Infusion process — materials are embedded into ship components (hulls, engines, shields, weapons) or crew equipment (armor, tools)

"If you manage to have someone skilled enough to synthesize them and if you manage to acquire those raw materials... you would be able to create some mind-blowing stuff." — Creator, Lore Keepers Brew

docs.title: Standard Components (Tier 1-3)
docs.text:
Ships have multiple component slots — power sources, hard points, hulls, engines — from different manufacturers. Standard components provide stat bonuses:

Manufacturer Specialization Gria Warping / FTL travel Pierce Firepower / Combat (others TBD) Various specializations

docs.title: Metagenic-Enhanced Components
docs.text:
Infusing metagenic materials into standard components creates enhanced components that unlock special abilities beyond stat bonuses:

  • Instead of just "+5 damage," a metagenic weapon might grant a phase-shift ability or energy-absorption shield
  • Enhanced components consume more power from the ship's power source — always a tradeoff
  • A ship with too many metagenic components may become power-starved under combat load

docs.title: Cross-Platform Material Discovery
docs.text:
Some metagenic materials can only be found in Unreal Engine 5 gameplay — raid dungeons, dungeon dives in the HRZ:

  • Players must commit crew members to UE5 expeditions
  • Survive the dungeon, bring back materials
  • Those materials can then be used for ship components across all gameplay modes

docs.title: HRZ Planet Hazards
docs.text:
Planning an HRZ expedition requires understanding the environment:

  • Temperature extremes — ice planets vs. volcanic worlds
  • Radiation — radioactive planets harbor creatures invisible to most species' light spectrum (requires X-ray vision to detect)
  • Atmospheric hazards — toxic gases, low oxygen, corrosive atmospheres
  • Volatile environments — gas leaks (Ember Coil sewers; fire weapons = catastrophic explosion)

docs.title: Species Advantages
docs.text:
Different species have natural advantages in HRZ environments:

Species Environmental Advantage Mierese Resistant to poisons and many toxins; vulnerable to EMPs/hacking Punaab Heightened sense of smell — detects rare bio-materials nearby (others TBD) Various environmental adaptations

docs.title: 🔴 Open Questions
docs.text:
• ⏸️ Q-MAT1: Full list of manufacturer specializations beyond Gria (warping) and Pierce (firepower)?
  • ⏸️ Q-MAT2: How does the Polei component from the Core Saga relate to Tufa-derived materials?
  • ⏸️ Q-MAT3: Are there metagenic materials NOT derived from Tufas? (e.g., Living Factories technology, Ustur cybernetic materials)
  • ⏸️ Q-MAT4: What synthesis skills are required? Is this tied to crew member species or training?
  • ⏸️ Q-MAT5: Can metagenic materials be traded between players, or are they soulbound on acquisition?
